<!-- Source: https://napari.org/stable/index.html -->
# napari: a fast, interactive viewer for multi-dimensional images in Python[#](#napari-a-fast-interactive-viewer-for-multi-dimensional-images-in-python "Link to this heading")
* **view and explore** 2D, 3D, and higher-dimensional arrays on a canvas;
* **overlay** derived data such as *points*, *polygons*, *segmentations*, and
  more;
* **annotate** and **edit** derived datasets, using standard data structures
  such as NumPy or Zarr arrays, allowing you to
* **seamlessly weave** exploration, computation, and annotation together in
  imaging data analysis.
[**Get started**](getting_started/quick_start.html#napari-quick-start)
[
![napari viewer showing a 4D image of a developing Tribolium embryo.  Dataset Fluo-N3DL-TRIF from the [cell tracking challenge](http://celltrackingchallenge.net/3d-datasets/) by Dr. A. Jain, MPI-CBG, Dresden, Germany.](_static/images/tribolium.png "your browser does not support the video tag")
](_static/images/tribolium.webm)
Install with Python
Install napari in a Python environment for the most customizable experience.
[Install as Python package](getting_started/installation.html#install-python-package)
Download napari app
A standalone installer for when you want napari without setting up Python first.
[Install as an application](getting_started/installation.html#installation-bundle-conda)
[Community chat](https://napari.zulipchat.com/)
[Calendar](community/meeting_schedule.html)
[Release notes](release/index.html)
[Troubleshooting](troubleshooting.html)
[Roadmap](roadmaps/index.html)
[Blog](https://napari.org/island-dispatch)
### [Add image](gallery/add_image.html)
Display an image in napari and explore the viewer with a minimal example.
[Examples gallery](gallery.html)
Show another example
[![Screenshot preview of the add image example](_images/sphx_glr_add_image_001.png)](gallery/add_image.html)
## funding[#](#funding "Link to this heading")
If you like napari and want to support our [mission](community/mission_and_values.html#mission-and-values),
please consider a [donation](https://numfocus.org/donate-to-napari) to our
efforts.
napari is a fiscally sponsored project of [NumFOCUS](https://numfocus.org), a US-based non-profit
supporting open science and open source scientific software.
We gratefully acknowledge funding support from the [Chan Zuckerberg
Initiative](https://chanzuckerberg.com) (CZI) and [NumFOCUS](https://numfocus.org).
[![CZI logo](_images/czi-logo.png)](https://chanzuckerberg.com)

* napari.layers
# napari.layers[#](#module-napari.layers "Link to this heading")
Layers are the viewable objects that can be added to a viewer.
Custom layers must inherit from Layer and pass along the
[visual node](https://vispy.org/api/vispy.scene.visuals.html)
to the super constructor.
Classes
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`Image`](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.Image") | Image layer. |
| [`Labels`](napari.layers.Labels.html#napari.layers.Labels "napari.layers.Labels") | Labels (or segmentation) layer. |
| [`Layer`](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.Layer") | Base layer class. |
| [`LayerLock`](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.LayerLock") | Flags describing which UI operations are locked on a layer. |
| [`Points`](napari.layers.Points.html#napari.layers.Points "napari.layers.Points") | Points layer. |
| [`Shapes`](napari.layers.Shapes.html#napari.layers.Shapes "napari.layers.Shapes") | Shapes layer. |
| [`Surface`](napari.layers.Surface.html#napari.layers.Surface "napari.layers.Surface") | Surface layer renders meshes onto the canvas. |
| [`Tracks`](napari.layers.Tracks.html#napari.layers.Tracks "napari.layers.Tracks") | Tracks layer. |
| [`Vectors`](napari.layers.Vectors.html#napari.layers.Vectors "napari.layers.Vectors") | Vectors layer renders lines onto the canvas. |
[previous
Modules](modules.html "previous page")
[next

# napari.layers.Image[#](#napari-layers-image "Link to this heading")
class napari.layers.Image(*data: ArrayLike | Sequence[ArrayLike]*, *\**, *affine: npt.ArrayLike | [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *attenuation: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 0.05*, *axis\_labels: Sequence[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *blending: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = 'translucent'*, *cache: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *colormap: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = 'gray'*, *contrast\_limits: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d: npt.NDArray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *depiction: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = 'volume'*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *gamma: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1.0*, *interpolation2d: InterpolationStr = 'nearest'*, *interpolation3d: InterpolationStr = 'linear'*, *iso\_threshold: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *metadata: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *multiscale: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *opacity: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1.0*, *plane: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *projection\_mode: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = 'mean'*, *rendering: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = 'mip'*, *rgb: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *rotate: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") | Sequence[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] | npt.NDArray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *scale: Sequence[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *shear: Sequence[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] | npt.NDArray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *translate: Sequence[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *units: Sequence[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | pint.Unit] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *visible: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/image/image.html#Image)[#](#napari.layers.Image "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `IntensityVisualizationMixin`, `ScalarFieldBase`
    Image layer.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*array* *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *array*) – Image data. Can be N >= 2 dimensional. If the last dimension has length
          3 or 4 can be interpreted as RGB or RGBA if rgb is True. If a
          list and arrays are decreasing in shape then the data is treated as
          a multiscale image. In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen
          automatically based on the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution
          scale is displayed by default. The resolution level can be locked
          via `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
          controls UI.
        * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
          Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
          provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        * **attenuation** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Attenuation rate for attenuated maximum intensity projection.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Dimension names of the layer data.
          If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
        * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
          alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
          {‘translucent’, ‘translucent\_no\_depth’, ‘additive’, ‘minimum’, ‘opaque’}.
        * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
          Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        * **colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")*,* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Colormaps to use for luminance images. If a string, it can be the name
          of a supported colormap from vispy or matplotlib or the name of
          a vispy color or a hexadecimal RGB color representation.
          If a tuple, the first value must be a string to assign as a name to a
          colormap and the second item must be a Colormap. If a dict, the key must
          be a string to assign as a name to a colormap and the value must be a
          Colormap.
        * **contrast\_limits** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *(**2**,**)*) – Intensity value limits to be used for determining the minimum and maximum colormap bounds for
          luminance images. If not passed, they will be calculated as the min and max intensity value of
          the image.
        * **custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d** (*np.ndarray*) – Convolution kernel used with the ‘custom’ interpolation mode in 2D rendering.
        * **depiction** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – 3D Depiction mode. Must be one of {‘volume’, ‘plane’}.
          The default value is ‘volume’.
        * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
          Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
          Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
        * **gamma** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Gamma correction for determining colormap linearity; defaults to 1.
        * **interpolation2d** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Interpolation mode used by vispy for rendering 2d data.
          Must be one of our supported modes.
          (for list of supported modes see Interpolation enum)
          ‘custom’ is a special mode for 2D interpolation in which a regular grid
          of samples is taken from the texture around a position using ‘linear’
          interpolation before being multiplied with a custom interpolation kernel
          (provided with ‘custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d’).
        * **interpolation3d** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Same as ‘interpolation2d’ but for 3D rendering.
        * **iso\_threshold** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Threshold for isosurface.
        * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
        * **multiscale** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the data is a multiscale image or not. Multiscale data is
          represented by a list of array-like image data. If not specified by
          the user and if the data is a list of arrays that decrease in shape,
          then it will be taken to be multiscale. The first image in the list
          should be the largest. In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen
          automatically based on the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution
          scale is displayed by default. The resolution level can be locked
          via `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
          controls UI.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
        * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        * **plane** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *SlicingPlane*) – Properties defining plane rendering in 3D. Properties are defined in
          data coordinates. Valid dictionary keys are
          {‘position’, ‘normal’, ‘thickness’, and ‘enabled’}.
        * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions, but inside the thick Dims slice will
          be projected onto the viewed dimensions. Must fit to ImageProjectionMode
        * **rendering** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Rendering mode used by vispy. Must be one of our supported
          modes.
        * **rgb** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether the image is RGB or RGBA if rgb. If not
          specified by user, but the last dimension of the data has length 3 or 4,
          it will be set as True. If False, the image is interpreted as a
          luminance image.
        * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float, convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple, convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise, assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          ‘np.degrees’ if needed.
        * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
        * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
          If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
        * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
    data[#](#napari.layers.Image.data "Link to this definition")
    :   Image data. Can be N dimensional. If the last dimension has length
        3 or 4 can be interpreted as RGB or RGBA if rgb is True. If a list
        and arrays are decreasing in shape then the data is treated as a
        multiscale image. In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen
        automatically based on the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution
        scale is displayed by default. The resolution level can be locked
        via `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
        controls UI.
        Type:
        :   array or [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of array
    axis\_labels[#](#napari.layers.Image.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimension names of the layer data.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    metadata[#](#napari.layers.Image.metadata "Link to this definition")
    :   Image metadata.
        Type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    rgb[#](#napari.layers.Image.rgb "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the image is rgb RGB or RGBA if rgb. If not
        specified by user and the last dimension of the data has length 3 or 4
        it will be set as True. If False the image is interpreted as a
        luminance image.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    multiscale[#](#napari.layers.Image.multiscale "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the data is a multiscale image or not. Multiscale data is
        represented by a list of array like image data. The first image in the
        list should be the largest. In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen
        automatically based on the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution
        scale is displayed by default. The resolution level can be locked
        via `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
        controls UI.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    mode[#](#napari.layers.Image.mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Interactive mode. The normal, default mode is PAN\_ZOOM, which
        allows for normal interactivity with the canvas.
        In TRANSFORM mode the image can be transformed interactively.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    colormap[#](#napari.layers.Image.colormap "Link to this definition")
    :   The first is the name of the current colormap, and the second value is
        the colormap. Colormaps are used for luminance images, if the image is
        rgb the colormap is ignored.
        Type:
        :   2-tuple of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [napari.utils.Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")
    colormaps[#](#napari.layers.Image.colormaps "Link to this definition")
    :   Names of the available colormaps.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    contrast\_limits[#](#napari.layers.Image.contrast_limits "Link to this definition")
    :   Color limits to be used for determining the colormap bounds for
        luminance images. If the image is rgb the contrast\_limits is ignored.
        Type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") (2,) of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    contrast\_limits\_range[#](#napari.layers.Image.contrast_limits_range "Link to this definition")
    :   Range for the color limits for luminance images. If the image is
        rgb the contrast\_limits\_range is ignored.
        Type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") (2,) of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    gamma[#](#napari.layers.Image.gamma "Link to this definition")
    :   Gamma correction for determining colormap linearity.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    interpolation2d[#](#napari.layers.Image.interpolation2d "Link to this definition")
    :   Interpolation mode used by vispy. Must be one of our supported modes.
        ‘custom’ is a special mode for 2D interpolation in which a regular grid
        of samples are taken from the texture around a position using ‘linear’
        interpolation before being multiplied with a custom interpolation kernel
        (provided with ‘custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d’).
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    interpolation3d[#](#napari.layers.Image.interpolation3d "Link to this definition")
    :   Same as ‘interpolation2d’ but for 3D rendering.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    rendering[#](#napari.layers.Image.rendering "Link to this definition")
    :   Rendering mode used by vispy. Must be one of our supported
        modes.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    depiction[#](#napari.layers.Image.depiction "Link to this definition")
    :   3D Depiction mode used by vispy. Must be one of our supported modes.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    iso\_threshold[#](#napari.layers.Image.iso_threshold "Link to this definition")
    :   Threshold for isosurface.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    attenuation[#](#napari.layers.Image.attenuation "Link to this definition")
    :   Attenuation rate for attenuated maximum intensity projection.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    plane[#](#napari.layers.Image.plane "Link to this definition")
    :   Properties defining plane rendering in 3D. Valid dictionary keys are
        {‘position’, ‘normal’, ‘thickness’}.
        Type:
        :   SlicingPlane or [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    experimental\_clipping\_planes[#](#napari.layers.Image.experimental_clipping_planes "Link to this definition")
    :   Clipping planes defined in data coordinates, used to clip the volume.
        Type:
        :   ClippingPlaneList
    custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d[#](#napari.layers.Image.custom_interpolation_kernel_2d "Link to this definition")
    :   Convolution kernel used with the ‘custom’ interpolation mode in 2D rendering.
        Type:
        :   np.ndarray
    units[#](#napari.layers.Image.units "Link to this definition")
    :   Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of pint.Unit
    Notes
    \_data\_viewarray (N, M), (N, M, 3), or (N, M, 4)
    :   Image data for the currently viewed slice. Must be 2D image data, but
        can be multidimensional for RGB or RGBA images if multidimensional is
        True.

    Details
    property affine: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.layers.Image.affine "Link to this definition")
    :   Extra affine transform to go from physical to world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.transforms.Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property attenuation: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id0 "Link to this definition")
    :   attenuation rate for attenuated\_mip rendering.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#id1 "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    bind\_key(*key\_bind: [KeyBinding](https://app-model.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/types/#app_model.types.KeyBinding "(in App Model v0.0.0)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [EllipsisType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/types.html#types.EllipsisType "(in Python v3.14)")*, *func=<object object>*, *\**, *overwrite=False*)[#](#napari.layers.Image.bind_key "Link to this definition")
    :   Bind a key combination to a keymap.
        Parameters:
        :   * **keymap** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *str: callable*) – Keymap to modify.
            * **key\_bind** (*keybinding-like* *or* *...*) – Key combination.
              `...` acts as a wildcard if no key combinations can be matched
              in the keymap (this will overwrite all key combinations
              further down the lookup chain).
            * **func** (*callable**,* *None**, or* *...*) – Callable to bind to the key combination.
              If `None` is passed, unbind instead.
              `...` acts as a blocker, effectively unbinding the key
              combination for all keymaps further down the lookup chain.
            * **overwrite** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *keyword-only**,* *optional*) – Whether to overwrite the key combination if it already exists.
        Returns:
        :   **unbound** – Callable unbound by this operation, if any.
        Return type:
        :   callable or None
        Notes
        Key combinations are represented in the form `[modifier-]key`,
        e.g. `a`, `Control-c`, or `Control-Alt-Delete`.
        Valid modifiers are Control, Alt, Shift, and Meta.
        Letters will always be read as upper-case.
        Due to the native implementation of the key system, Shift pressed in
        certain key combinations may yield inconsistent or unexpected results.
        Therefore, it is not recommended to use Shift with non-letter keys. On OSX,
        Control is swapped with Meta such that pressing Command reads as Control.
        Special keys include Shift, Control, Alt, Meta, Up, Down, Left, Right,
        PageUp, PageDown, Insert, Delete, Home, End, Escape, Backspace, F1,
        F2, F3, F4, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10, F11, F12, Space, Enter, and Tab
        Functions take in only one argument: the parent that the function
        was bound to.
        By default, all functions are assumed to work on key presses only,
        but can be denoted to work on release too by separating the function
        into two statements with the yield keyword:
        ```
        @viewer.bind_key('h')
        def hello_world(viewer):
            # on key press
            viewer.status = 'hello world!'
            yield
            # on key release
            viewer.status = 'goodbye world :('
        ```
        To create a keymap that will block others, `` bind_key(..., ...)` ``.
    property blending: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.blending "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed.
        Blending.OPAQUE
        :   Allows for only the top layer to be visible and corresponds to
            depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False, blend=False.
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity
            and corresponds to depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False,
            blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT\_NO\_DEPTH
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity, but
            no depth testing is performed. Corresponds to `depth_test=False`,
            cull\_face=False, blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.ADDITIVE
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together with
            different colors and opacity. Useful for creating overlays. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one’), and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.MINIMUM
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together such that
            the minimum of each RGB component and alpha are selected.
            Useful for creating overlays with inverted colormaps. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_equation=(‘min’).
        Type:
        :   Blending mode
    click\_plane\_from\_click\_data(*click\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray, np.ndarray][#](#napari.layers.Image.click_plane_from_click_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data
        coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera.
        Parameters:
        :   * **click\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – click position in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – view direction in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – dimensions of the data array currently in view.
        Returns:
        :   **click\_plane** – tuple of (plane\_position, plane\_normal) in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
    property colorbar[#](#napari.layers.Image.colorbar "Link to this definition")
    :   Color Bar overlay.
    property colormap[#](#id2 "Link to this definition")
    :   colormap for luminance images.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")
    property colormaps[#](#id3 "Link to this definition")
    :   names of available colormaps.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property contrast\_limits[#](#id4 "Link to this definition")
    :   Limits to use for the colormap.
        Type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property contrast\_limits\_range[#](#id5 "Link to this definition")
    :   The current valid range of the contrast limits.
    classmethod create(*data: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *meta: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *layer\_type: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")[#](#napari.layers.Image.create "Link to this definition")
    :   Create layer from data of type layer\_type.
        Primarily intended for usage by reader plugin hooks and creating a
        layer from an unwrapped layer data tuple.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*Any*) – Data in a format that is valid for the corresponding layer\_type.
            * **meta** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dict of keyword arguments that will be passed to the corresponding
              layer constructor. If any keys in meta are not valid for the
              corresponding layer type, an exception will be raised.
            * **layer\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Type of layer to add. Must be the (case insensitive) name of a
              Layer subclass. If not provided, the layer is assumed to
              be “image”, unless data.dtype is one of (np.int32, np.uint32,
              np.int64, np.uint64), in which case it is assumed to be “labels”.
        Raises:
        :   * [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If `layer_type` is not one of the recognized layer types.
            * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If any keyword arguments in `meta` are unexpected for the
              corresponding add\_\* method for this layer\_type.
        Examples
        A typical use case might be to upack a tuple of layer data with a
        specified layer\_type.
        ```
        >>> data = (
        ...     np.random.random((10, 2)) * 20,
        ...     {'face_color': 'blue'},
        ...     'points',
        ... )
        >>> Layer.create(*data)
        ```
    property cursor: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.cursor "Link to this definition")
    :   String identifying cursor displayed over canvas.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property cursor\_size: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.cursor_size "Link to this definition")
    :   Size of cursor if custom. None yields default size.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    property data: LayerDataProtocol | MultiScaleData[#](#id6 "Link to this definition")
    :   Data, possibly in multiscale wrapper. Obeys LayerDataProtocol.
    property data\_level: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.data_level "Link to this definition")
    :   Current level of multiscale, or 0 if image.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    property data\_raw: LayerDataProtocol | Sequence[LayerDataProtocol][#](#napari.layers.Image.data_raw "Link to this definition")
    :   Data, exactly as provided by the user.
    data\_to\_world(*position*)[#](#napari.layers.Image.data_to_world "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from data coordinates to world coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in data coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Position in world coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")
    property depiction[#](#id7 "Link to this definition")
    :   The current 3D depiction mode.
        Selects a preset depiction mode in vispy
        :   * volume: images are rendered as 3D volumes.
            * plane: images are rendered as 2D planes embedded in 3D.
              :   plane position, normal, and thickness are attributes of
                  layer.plane which can be modified directly.
    property downsample\_factors: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.downsample_factors "Link to this definition")
    :   Downsample factors for each level of the multiscale.
        Type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    property editable: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.editable "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the current layer data is editable from the viewer.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property extent: Extent[#](#napari.layers.Image.extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Extent of layer in data and world coordinates.
        For image-like layers, these coordinates are the locations of the
        pixels in Layer.data which are treated like sample points that are
        centered in the rendered version of those pixels.
        For other layers, these coordinates are the points or vertices stored
        in Layer.data.
        Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
    get\_ray\_intersections(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Image.get_ray_intersections "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the start and end point for the ray extending
        from a point through the data bounding box.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** – the position of the point in nD coordinates. World vs. data
              is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – a unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD coordinates.
              World vs. data is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – a list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – True if the provided coordinates are in world coordinates.
              Default value is True.
        Returns:
        :   * **start\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point closest to the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
            * **end\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point farthest from the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
    get\_status(*position: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *value: Any | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Image.get_status "Link to this definition")
    :   Status message information of the data at a coordinate position.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
            * **value** (*Any*) – Pre-computed value. In some cases,
        Returns:
        :   **status\_dict** – Dictionary containing a information that can be used as a status update.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    get\_value(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.get_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Value of the data at a position.
        If the layer is not visible, return None.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:
        :   **value** – Value of the data. If the layer is not visible return None.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)"), None
    property help: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.help "Link to this definition")
    :   displayed in status bar bottom right.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")

        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property level\_shapes: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.level_shapes "Link to this definition")
    :   Shapes of each level of the multiscale or just of image.
        Type:
        :   array
    property loaded: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.loaded "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the layer has been loaded into memory.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property locked: [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.base._base_constants.LayerLock")[#](#napari.layers.Image.locked "Link to this definition")
    :   which UI operations are locked on this layer.
        `bool(layer.locked)` is truthy when any lock flag is set. Setting
        `True`/`False` is equivalent to `LayerLock.ALL`/`LayerLock.NONE`.
        Type:
        :   [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.LayerLock")
    property locked\_data\_level: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.locked_data_level "Link to this definition")
    :   Locked multiscale resolution level.
        When set to an integer, forces rendering at the given multiscale
        level instead of automatic level selection based on the viewport.
        Set to `None` to restore automatic behaviour.
        Added in version 0.7.1.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") or None
    property metadata: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id9 "Link to this definition")
    :   Key/value map for user-stored data.
    property mode: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id10 "Link to this definition")
    :   Interactive mode
        Interactive mode. The normal, default mode is PAN\_ZOOM, which
        allows for normal interactivity with the canvas.
        TRANSFORM allows for manipulation of the layer transform.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property mouse\_pan: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.mouse_pan "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive panning is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property mouse\_zoom: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.mouse_zoom "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive zooming is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.name "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique name of the layer.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property ndim: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Number of dimensions in the data.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    property opacity: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.opacity "Link to this definition")
    :   Opacity value between 0.0 and 1.0.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    projected\_distance\_from\_mouse\_drag(*start\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *end\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *vector: np.ndarray*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Image.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse
        clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   * **start\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – Starting point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **end\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – End point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – Vector defining the plane normal of the plane onto which the drag
              vector is projected.
            * **vector** (*np.ndarray*) – (3,) unit vector or (n, 3) array thereof on which to project the drag
              vector from start\_event to end\_event. This argument is defined in data
              coordinates.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – (3,) list of currently displayed dimensions
        Returns:
        :   **projected\_distance**
        Return type:
        :   (1, ) or (n, ) np.ndarray of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property projection\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Image.projection_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Mode of projection of the thick slice onto the viewed dimensions.
        The sliced data is described by an n-dimensional bounding box (“thick slice”),
        which needs to be projected onto the visible dimensions to be visible.
        The projection mode controls the projection logic.
    refresh(*event: [Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *thumbnail: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *data\_displayed: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *highlight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *extent: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.refresh "Link to this definition")
    :   Refresh all layer data based on current view slice.
    property rendering: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id11 "Link to this definition")
    :   Return current rendering mode.
        Selects a preset rendering mode in vispy that determines how
        volume is displayed. Options include:
        * `translucent`: voxel colors are blended along the view ray until
          :   the result is opaque.
        * `mip`: maximum intensity projection. Cast a ray and display the
          :   maximum value that was encountered.
        * `minip`: minimum intensity projection. Cast a ray and display the
          :   minimum value that was encountered.
        * `attenuated_mip`: attenuated maximum intensity projection. Cast a
          :   ray and attenuate values based on integral of encountered values,
              display the maximum value that was encountered after attenuation.
              This will make nearer objects appear more prominent.
        * `additive`: voxel colors are added along the view ray until
          :   the result is saturated.
        * `iso`: isosurface. Cast a ray until a certain threshold is
          :   encountered. At that location, lighning calculations are
              performed to give the visual appearance of a surface.
        * `average`: average intensity projection. Cast a ray and display the
          :   average of values that were encountered.
        Returns:
        :   The current rendering mode
        Return type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    reset\_contrast\_limits(*mode=None*)[#](#napari.layers.Image.reset_contrast_limits "Link to this definition")
    :   Scale contrast limits to data range
    reset\_contrast\_limits\_range(*mode: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['data', 'slice'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.reset_contrast_limits_range "Link to this definition")
    :   Scale contrast limits range to data type if dtype is an integer,
        or use the current maximum data range otherwise.
        Parameters:
        :   **mode** (*'data'* *or* *'slice'*) – If range should be calculated on whole data, or only visible slice.
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float64]][#](#napari.layers.Image.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   Rotation matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    save(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Image.save "Link to this definition")
    :   Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to open. Extensions may be used to
              specify output format (provided a plugin is available for the
              requested format).
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If `None` then all plugins
              corresponding to appropriate hook specification will be looped
              through to find the first one that can save the data.
        Returns:
        :   File paths of any files that were written.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property scale: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Image.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property scale\_factor[#](#napari.layers.Image.scale_factor "Link to this definition")
    :   Conversion factor from canvas coordinates to image coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property shear: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Image.shear "Link to this definition")
    :   Shear matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property thumbnail: npt.NDArray[np.uint8][#](#napari.layers.Image.thumbnail "Link to this definition")
    :   Integer array of thumbnail for the layer
        Type:
        :   array
    property translate: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Image.translate "Link to this definition")
    :   Factors to shift the layer by in units of world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property unique\_id: [Hashable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Hashable "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.unique_id "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique ID of the layer.
        This is guaranteed to be unique to this specific layer instance
        over the lifetime of the program.
    property units: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[pint.Unit, ...][#](#id12 "Link to this definition")
    :   List of units for the layer.
    property visible: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Image.visible "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the visual is currently being displayed.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    world\_to\_data(*position: npt.ArrayLike*) → np.ndarray[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], np.dtype[np.floating]][#](#napari.layers.Image.world_to_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from world coordinates to data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in world coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Position in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")
