* napari.layers.Labels
# napari.layers.Labels[#](#napari-layers-labels "Link to this heading")
class napari.layers.Labels(*data*, *\**, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *cache=True*, *colormap=None*, *depiction='volume'*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *features=None*, *iso\_gradient\_mode='fast'*, *metadata=None*, *multiscale=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=0.7*, *plane=None*, *projection\_mode='none'*, *properties=None*, *rendering='iso\_categorical'*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/labels/labels.html#Labels)[#](#napari.layers.Labels "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `ScalarFieldBase`
    Labels (or segmentation) layer.
    An image-like layer where every pixel contains an integer ID
    corresponding to the region it belongs to.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*array* *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *array*) – Labels data as an array or multiscale. Must be integer type or bools.
          In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen automatically based on
          the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution scale is displayed by
          default. The resolution level can be locked via
          `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
          controls UI.
        * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
          Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
          provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
          If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
        * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
          alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
          {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, and ‘additive’}.
        * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
          Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        * **colormap** ([*CyclicLabelColormap*](napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.html#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap "napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap") *or* [*DirectLabelColormap*](napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.html#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap "napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap") *or* *None*) – Colormap to use for the labels. If None, a random colormap will be
          used.
        * **depiction** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – 3D Depiction mode. Must be one of {‘volume’, ‘plane’}.
          The default value is ‘volume’.
        * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
          Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
          Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
        * **features** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**] or* *DataFrame*) – Features table where each row corresponds to a label and each column
          is a feature. The first row corresponds to the background label.
        * **iso\_gradient\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Method for calulating the gradient (used to get the surface normal) in the
          ‘iso\_categorical’ rendering mode. Must be one of {‘fast’, ‘smooth’}.
          ‘fast’ uses a simple finite difference gradient in x, y, and z. ‘smooth’ uses an
          isotropic Sobel gradient, which is smoother but more computationally expensive.
          The default value is ‘fast’.
        * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
        * **multiscale** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the data is a multiscale image or not. Multiscale data is
          represented by a list of array like image data. If not specified by
          the user and if the data is a list of arrays that decrease in shape
          then it will be taken to be multiscale. The first image in the list
          should be the largest. In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen
          automatically based on the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution
          scale is displayed by default. The resolution level can be locked
          via `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
          controls UI.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
        * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        * **plane** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *SlicingPlane*) – Properties defining plane rendering in 3D. Properties are defined in
          data coordinates. Valid dictionary keys are
          {‘position’, ‘normal’, ‘thickness’, and ‘enabled’}.
        * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
          be projected onto the viewed dimensions
        * **properties** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}* *or* *DataFrame*) – Properties for each label. Each property should be an array of length
          N, where N is the number of labels, and the first property corresponds
          to background.
        * **rendering** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – 3D Rendering mode used by vispy. Must be one {‘translucent’, ‘iso\_categorical’}.
          ‘translucent’ renders without lighting. ‘iso\_categorical’ uses isosurface
          rendering to calculate lighting effects on labeled surfaces.
          The default value is ‘iso\_categorical’.
        * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          np.degrees if needed.
        * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
        * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
          If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
        * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
    data[#](#napari.layers.Labels.data "Link to this definition")
    :   Integer label data as an array or multiscale. Can be N dimensional.
        Every pixel contains an integer ID corresponding to the region it
        belongs to. The label 0 is rendered as transparent. In 2D, the
        displayed resolution is chosen automatically based on the viewport.
        In 3D, the lowest resolution scale is displayed by default. The
        resolution level can be locked via `locked_data_level` or the
        resolution control in the layer controls UI.
        Type:
        :   array or [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of array
    axis\_labels[#](#napari.layers.Labels.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimension names of the layer data.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    multiscale[#](#napari.layers.Labels.multiscale "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the data is a multiscale image or not. Multiscale data is
        represented by a list of array like image data. The first image in the
        list should be the largest. In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen
        automatically based on the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution
        scale is displayed by default. The resolution level can be locked
        via `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
        controls UI.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    metadata[#](#napari.layers.Labels.metadata "Link to this definition")
    :   Labels metadata.
        Type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    num\_colors[#](#napari.layers.Labels.num_colors "Link to this definition")
    :   Number of unique colors to use in colormap. DEPRECATED: set
        `colormap` directly, using napari.utils.colormaps.label\_colormap.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    features[#](#napari.layers.Labels.features "Link to this definition")
    :   Features table where each row corresponds to a label and each column
        is a feature. The first row corresponds to the background label.
        Type:
        :   Dataframe-like
    properties[#](#napari.layers.Labels.properties "Link to this definition")
    :   Properties for each label. Each property should be an array of length
        N, where N is the number of labels, and the first property corresponds
        to background.
        Type:
        :   dict {str: array (N,)}, DataFrame
    color[#](#napari.layers.Labels.color "Link to this definition")
    :   Custom label to color mapping. Values must be valid color names or RGBA
        arrays. While there is no limit to the number of custom labels, the
        the layer will render incorrectly if they map to more than 1024 distinct
        colors. DEPRECATED: set `colormap` directly, using
        napari.utils.colormaps.DirectLabelColormap.
        Type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") of int to str or array
    seed[#](#napari.layers.Labels.seed "Link to this definition")
    :   Seed for colormap random generator. DEPRECATED: set `colormap`
        directly, using napari.utils.colormaps.label\_colormap.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    opacity[#](#napari.layers.Labels.opacity "Link to this definition")
    :   Opacity of the labels, must be between 0 and 1.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    contiguous[#](#napari.layers.Labels.contiguous "Link to this definition")
    :   If True, the fill bucket changes only connected pixels of same label.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    n\_edit\_dimensions[#](#napari.layers.Labels.n_edit_dimensions "Link to this definition")
    :   The number of dimensions across which labels will be edited.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    contour[#](#napari.layers.Labels.contour "Link to this definition")
    :   If greater than 0, displays contours of labels instead of shaded regions
        with a thickness equal to its value. Must be >= 0.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    brush\_size[#](#napari.layers.Labels.brush_size "Link to this definition")
    :   Size of the paint brush in data coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    iso\_gradient\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Labels.iso_gradient_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Method for calulating the gradient (used to get the surface normal) in the
        ‘iso\_categorical’ rendering mode. Must be one of {‘fast’, ‘smooth’}.
        ‘fast’ uses a simple finite difference gradient in x, y, and z. ‘smooth’ uses an
        isotropic Sobel gradient, which is smoother but more computationally expensive.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    selected\_label[#](#napari.layers.Labels.selected_label "Link to this definition")
    :   Index of selected label. Can be greater than the current maximum label.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    mode[#](#napari.layers.Labels.mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Interactive mode. The normal, default mode is PAN\_ZOOM, which
        allows for normal interactivity with the canvas.
        In PICK mode the cursor functions like a color picker, setting the
        clicked on label to be the current label. If the background is picked it
        will select the background label 0.
        In PAINT mode the cursor functions like a paint brush changing any
        pixels it brushes over to the current label. If the background label
        0 is selected than any pixels will be changed to background and this
        tool functions like an eraser. The size and shape of the cursor can be
        adjusted in the properties widget.
        In FILL mode the cursor functions like a fill bucket replacing pixels
        of the label clicked on with the current label. It can either replace
        all pixels of that label or just those that are contiguous with the
        clicked on pixel. If the background label 0 is selected than any
        pixels will be changed to background and this tool functions like an
        eraser.
        In ERASE mode the cursor functions similarly to PAINT mode, but to
        paint with background label, which effectively removes the label.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    plane[#](#napari.layers.Labels.plane "Link to this definition")
    :   Properties defining plane rendering in 3D.
        Type:
        :   SlicingPlane
    experimental\_clipping\_planes[#](#napari.layers.Labels.experimental_clipping_planes "Link to this definition")
    :   Clipping planes defined in data coordinates, used to clip the volume.
        Type:
        :   ClippingPlaneList
    units[#](#napari.layers.Labels.units "Link to this definition")
    :   Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of pint.Unit
    Notes
    \_selected\_color4-tuple or None
    :   RGBA tuple of the color of the selected label, or None if the
        background label 0 is selected.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `as_layer_data_tuple`() |  |
    | [`bind_key`](#napari.layers.Labels.bind_key "napari.layers.Labels.bind_key")(key\_bind[, func, overwrite]) | Bind a key combination to a keymap. |
    | [`block_history`](#napari.layers.Labels.block_history "napari.layers.Labels.block_history")() | Context manager to group history-editing operations together. |
    | `block_update_properties`() |  |
    | [`click_plane_from_click_data`](#napari.layers.Labels.click_plane_from_click_data "napari.layers.Labels.click_plane_from_click_data")(click\_position, ...) | Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera. |
    | [`create`](#napari.layers.Labels.create "napari.layers.Labels.create")(data[, meta, layer\_type]) | Create layer from data of type layer\_type. |
    | [`data_setitem`](#napari.layers.Labels.data_setitem "napari.layers.Labels.data_setitem")(indices, value[, refresh]) | Set indices in data to value, while writing to edit history. |
    | [`data_to_world`](#napari.layers.Labels.data_to_world "napari.layers.Labels.data_to_world")(position) | Convert from data coordinates to world coordinates. |
    | [`fill`](#napari.layers.Labels.fill "napari.layers.Labels.fill")(coord, new\_label[, refresh]) | Replace an existing label with a new label, either just at the connected component if the contiguous flag is True or everywhere if it is False, working in the number of dimensions specified by the n\_edit\_dimensions flag. |
    | [`get_color`](#napari.layers.Labels.get_color "napari.layers.Labels.get_color")(label) | Return the color corresponding to a specific label. |
    | [`get_ray_intersections`](#napari.layers.Labels.get_ray_intersections "napari.layers.Labels.get_ray_intersections")(position, ...[, world]) | Get the start and end point for the ray extending from a point through the data bounding box. |
    | `get_source_str`() |  |
    | [`get_status`](#napari.layers.Labels.get_status "napari.layers.Labels.get_status")([position, view\_direction, ...]) | Status message information of the data at a coordinate position. |
    | [`get_value`](#napari.layers.Labels.get_value "napari.layers.Labels.get_value")(position, \*[, view\_direction, ...]) | Value of the data at a position. |
    | `new_colormap`([seed]) |  |
    | [`paint`](#napari.layers.Labels.paint "napari.layers.Labels.paint")(coord, new\_label[, refresh]) | Paint over existing labels with a new label, using the selected brush shape and size, either only on the visible slice or in all n dimensions. |
    | [`paint_polygon`](#napari.layers.Labels.paint_polygon "napari.layers.Labels.paint_polygon")(points, new\_label) | Paint a polygon over existing labels with a new label. |
    | [`projected_distance_from_mouse_drag`](#napari.layers.Labels.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "napari.layers.Labels.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag")(...) | Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates. |

    Details
    property affine: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.affine "Link to this definition")
    :   Extra affine transform to go from physical to world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.transforms.Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#id0 "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    bind\_key(*key\_bind: [KeyBinding](https://app-model.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/types/#app_model.types.KeyBinding "(in App Model v0.0.0)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [EllipsisType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/types.html#types.EllipsisType "(in Python v3.14)")*, *func=<object object>*, *\**, *overwrite=False*)[#](#napari.layers.Labels.bind_key "Link to this definition")
    :   Bind a key combination to a keymap.
        Parameters:
        :   * **keymap** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *str: callable*) – Keymap to modify.
            * **key\_bind** (*keybinding-like* *or* *...*) – Key combination.
              `...` acts as a wildcard if no key combinations can be matched
              in the keymap (this will overwrite all key combinations
              further down the lookup chain).
            * **func** (*callable**,* *None**, or* *...*) – Callable to bind to the key combination.
              If `None` is passed, unbind instead.
              `...` acts as a blocker, effectively unbinding the key
              combination for all keymaps further down the lookup chain.
            * **overwrite** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *keyword-only**,* *optional*) – Whether to overwrite the key combination if it already exists.
        Returns:
        :   **unbound** – Callable unbound by this operation, if any.
        Return type:
        :   callable or None
        Notes
        Key combinations are represented in the form `[modifier-]key`,
        e.g. `a`, `Control-c`, or `Control-Alt-Delete`.
        Valid modifiers are Control, Alt, Shift, and Meta.
        Letters will always be read as upper-case.
        Due to the native implementation of the key system, Shift pressed in
        certain key combinations may yield inconsistent or unexpected results.
        Therefore, it is not recommended to use Shift with non-letter keys. On OSX,
        Control is swapped with Meta such that pressing Command reads as Control.
        Special keys include Shift, Control, Alt, Meta, Up, Down, Left, Right,
        PageUp, PageDown, Insert, Delete, Home, End, Escape, Backspace, F1,
        F2, F3, F4, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10, F11, F12, Space, Enter, and Tab
        Functions take in only one argument: the parent that the function
        was bound to.
        By default, all functions are assumed to work on key presses only,
        but can be denoted to work on release too by separating the function
        into two statements with the yield keyword:
        ```
        @viewer.bind_key('h')
        def hello_world(viewer):
            # on key press
            viewer.status = 'hello world!'
            yield
            # on key release
            viewer.status = 'goodbye world :('
        ```
        To create a keymap that will block others, `` bind_key(..., ...)` ``.
    property blending: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.blending "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed.
        Blending.OPAQUE
        :   Allows for only the top layer to be visible and corresponds to
            depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False, blend=False.
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity
            and corresponds to depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False,
            blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT\_NO\_DEPTH
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity, but
            no depth testing is performed. Corresponds to `depth_test=False`,
            cull\_face=False, blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.ADDITIVE
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together with
            different colors and opacity. Useful for creating overlays. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one’), and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.MINIMUM
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together such that
            the minimum of each RGB component and alpha are selected.
            Useful for creating overlays with inverted colormaps. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_equation=(‘min’).
        Type:
        :   Blending mode
    block\_history()[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/labels/labels.html#Labels.block_history)[#](#napari.layers.Labels.block_history "Link to this definition")
    :   Context manager to group history-editing operations together.
        While in the context, history atoms are grouped together into a
        “staged” history. When exiting the context, that staged history is
        committed to the undo history queue, and an event is emitted
        containing the change.
    property brush\_size[#](#id1 "Link to this definition")
    :   Size of the paint in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    click\_plane\_from\_click\_data(*click\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray, np.ndarray][#](#napari.layers.Labels.click_plane_from_click_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data
        coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera.
        Parameters:
        :   * **click\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – click position in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – view direction in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – dimensions of the data array currently in view.
        Returns:
        :   **click\_plane** – tuple of (plane\_position, plane\_normal) in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
    property contiguous[#](#id2 "Link to this definition")
    :   fill bucket changes only connected pixels of same label.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property contour: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id3 "Link to this definition")
    :   displays contours of labels instead of shaded regions.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    classmethod create(*data: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *meta: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *layer\_type: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.create "Link to this definition")
    :   Create layer from data of type layer\_type.
        Primarily intended for usage by reader plugin hooks and creating a
        layer from an unwrapped layer data tuple.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*Any*) – Data in a format that is valid for the corresponding layer\_type.
            * **meta** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dict of keyword arguments that will be passed to the corresponding
              layer constructor. If any keys in meta are not valid for the
              corresponding layer type, an exception will be raised.
            * **layer\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Type of layer to add. Must be the (case insensitive) name of a
              Layer subclass. If not provided, the layer is assumed to
              be “image”, unless data.dtype is one of (np.int32, np.uint32,
              np.int64, np.uint64), in which case it is assumed to be “labels”.
        Raises:
        :   * [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If `layer_type` is not one of the recognized layer types.
            * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If any keyword arguments in `meta` are unexpected for the
              corresponding add\_\* method for this layer\_type.
        Examples
        A typical use case might be to upack a tuple of layer data with a
        specified layer\_type.
        ```
        >>> data = (
        ...     np.random.random((10, 2)) * 20,
        ...     {'face_color': 'blue'},
        ...     'points',
        ... )
        >>> Layer.create(*data)
        ```

    data\_setitem(*indices*, *value*, *refresh=True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/labels/labels.html#Labels.data_setitem)[#](#napari.layers.Labels.data_setitem "Link to this definition")
    :   Set indices in data to value, while writing to edit history.
        Parameters:
        :   * **indices** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *arrays* *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Indices in data to overwrite. Must be a tuple of arrays of length
              equal to the number of data dimensions. (Fancy indexing in [[2]](#id6)).
            * **value** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *array* *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – New label value(s). If more than one value, must match or
              broadcast with the given indices.
            * **refresh** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *default True*) – whether to refresh the view, by default True
        References

        <https://numpy.org/doc/stable/user/basics.indexing.html>

    property depiction[#](#napari.layers.Labels.depiction "Link to this definition")
    :   The current 3D depiction mode.
        Selects a preset depiction mode in vispy
        :   * volume: images are rendered as 3D volumes.
            * plane: images are rendered as 2D planes embedded in 3D.
              :   plane position, normal, and thickness are attributes of
                  layer.plane which can be modified directly.
    property downsample\_factors: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.downsample_factors "Link to this definition")
    :   Downsample factors for each level of the multiscale.
        Type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    property editable: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.editable "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the current layer data is editable from the viewer.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property extent: Extent[#](#napari.layers.Labels.extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Extent of layer in data and world coordinates.
        For image-like layers, these coordinates are the locations of the
        pixels in Layer.data which are treated like sample points that are
        centered in the rendered version of those pixels.
        For other layers, these coordinates are the points or vertices stored
        in Layer.data.
        Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
    property features[#](#id7 "Link to this definition")
    :   Dataframe-like features table.
        It is an implementation detail that this is a pandas.DataFrame. In the future,
        we will target the currently-in-development Data API dataframe protocol [[1]](#id9).
        This will enable us to use alternate libraries such as xarray or cuDF for
        additional features without breaking existing usage of this.
        If you need to specifically rely on the pandas API, please coerce this to a
        pandas.DataFrame using features\_to\_pandas\_dataframe.
        References
        [[1](#id8)]
        <https://data-apis.org/dataframe-protocol/latest/API.html>
    fill(*coord: [Sequence](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Sequence "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *new\_label: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *refresh: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/labels/labels.html#Labels.fill)[#](#napari.layers.Labels.fill "Link to this definition")
    :   Replace an existing label with a new label, either just at the
        connected component if the contiguous flag is True or everywhere
        if it is False, working in the number of dimensions specified by
        the n\_edit\_dimensions flag.
        Parameters:
        :   * **coord** (*sequence* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position of mouse cursor in image coordinates.
            * **new\_label** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Value of the new label to be filled in.
            * **refresh** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether to refresh view slice or not. Set to False to batch paint
              calls.
    get\_color(*label*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/labels/labels.html#Labels.get_color)[#](#napari.layers.Labels.get_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the color corresponding to a specific label.
    get\_ray\_intersections(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Labels.get_ray_intersections "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the start and end point for the ray extending
        from a point through the data bounding box.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** – the position of the point in nD coordinates. World vs. data
              is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – a unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD coordinates.
              World vs. data is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – a list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – True if the provided coordinates are in world coordinates.
              Default value is True.
        Returns:
        :   * **start\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point closest to the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
            * **end\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point farthest from the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
    get\_status(*position: ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *view\_direction: ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *value: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/labels/labels.html#Labels.get_status)[#](#napari.layers.Labels.get_status "Link to this definition")
    :   Status message information of the data at a coordinate position.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:
        :   **status** – Dict containing a information that can be used in a status update.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    get\_value(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.get_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Value of the data at a position.
        If the layer is not visible, return None.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:
        :   **value** – Value of the data. If the layer is not visible return None.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)"), None
    property help: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.help "Link to this definition")
    :   displayed in status bar bottom right.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property iso\_gradient\_mode: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id10 "Link to this definition")
    :   Return current gradient mode for isosurface rendering.
        Selects the finite-difference gradient method for the isosurface shader. Options include:
        :   * `fast`: use a simple finite difference gradient along each axis
            * `smooth`: use an isotropic Sobel gradient, smoother but more
              computationally expensive
        Returns:
        :   The current gradient mode
        Return type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property level\_shapes: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.level_shapes "Link to this definition")
    :   Shapes of each level of the multiscale or just of image.
        Type:
        :   array
    property loaded: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.loaded "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the layer has been loaded into memory.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property locked: [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.base._base_constants.LayerLock")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.locked "Link to this definition")
    :   which UI operations are locked on this layer.
        `bool(layer.locked)` is truthy when any lock flag is set. Setting
        `True`/`False` is equivalent to `LayerLock.ALL`/`LayerLock.NONE`.
        Type:
        :   [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.LayerLock")
    property locked\_data\_level: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.locked_data_level "Link to this definition")
    :   Locked multiscale resolution level.
        When set to an integer, forces rendering at the given multiscale
        level instead of automatic level selection based on the viewport.
        Set to `None` to restore automatic behaviour.
        Added in version 0.7.1.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") or None
    property metadata: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id11 "Link to this definition")
    :   Key/value map for user-stored data.
    property mode[#](#id12 "Link to this definition")
    :   Interactive mode. The normal, default mode is PAN\_ZOOM, which
        allows for normal interactivity with the canvas.
        In PICK mode the cursor functions like a color picker, setting the
        clicked on label to be the current label. If the background is picked it
        will select the background label 0.
        In PAINT mode the cursor functions like a paint brush changing any
        pixels it brushes over to the current label. If the background label
        0 is selected than any pixels will be changed to background and this
        tool functions like an eraser. The size and shape of the cursor can be
        adjusted in the properties widget.
        In FILL mode the cursor functions like a fill bucket replacing pixels
        of the label clicked on with the current label. It can either replace
        all pixels of that label or just those that are contiguous with the
        clicked on pixel. If the background label 0 is selected than any
        pixels will be changed to background and this tool functions like an
        eraser.
        In ERASE mode the cursor functions similarly to PAINT mode, but to
        paint with background label, which effectively removes the label.
        Type:
        :   MODE
    property mouse\_pan: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.mouse_pan "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive panning is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property mouse\_zoom: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.mouse_zoom "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive zooming is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.name "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique name of the layer.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property ndim: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Number of dimensions in the data.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    property opacity: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id13 "Link to this definition")
    :   Opacity value between 0.0 and 1.0.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    paint(*coord*, *new\_label*, *refresh=True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/labels/labels.html#Labels.paint)[#](#napari.layers.Labels.paint "Link to this definition")
    :   Paint over existing labels with a new label, using the selected
        brush shape and size, either only on the visible slice or in all
        n dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   * **coord** (*sequence* *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position of mouse cursor in image coordinates.
            * **new\_label** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Value of the new label to be filled in.
            * **refresh** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether to refresh view slice or not. Set to False to batch paint
              calls.
    paint\_polygon(*points*, *new\_label*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/labels/labels.html#Labels.paint_polygon)[#](#napari.layers.Labels.paint_polygon "Link to this definition")
    :   Paint a polygon over existing labels with a new label.
        Parameters:
        :   * **points** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *coordinates*) – List of coordinates of the vertices of a polygon.
            * **new\_label** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Value of the new label to be filled in.
    property preserve\_labels[#](#napari.layers.Labels.preserve_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Defines if painting should preserve existing labels.
        Default to false to allow paint on existing labels. When
        set to true, existing labels will be preserved during painting.
    projected\_distance\_from\_mouse\_drag(*start\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *end\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *vector: np.ndarray*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Labels.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse
        clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   * **start\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – Starting point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **end\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – End point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – Vector defining the plane normal of the plane onto which the drag
              vector is projected.
            * **vector** (*np.ndarray*) – (3,) unit vector or (n, 3) array thereof on which to project the drag
              vector from start\_event to end\_event. This argument is defined in data
              coordinates.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – (3,) list of currently displayed dimensions
        Returns:
        :   **projected\_distance**
        Return type:
        :   (1, ) or (n, ) np.ndarray of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property projection\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Labels.projection_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Mode of projection of the thick slice onto the viewed dimensions.
        The sliced data is described by an n-dimensional bounding box (“thick slice”),
        which needs to be projected onto the visible dimensions to be visible.
        The projection mode controls the projection logic.
    property properties: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")][#](#id14 "Link to this definition")
    :   Properties for each label.
        Type:
        :   dict {str
        Type:
        :   array (N,)}, DataFrame
    refresh(*event: [Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *thumbnail: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *data\_displayed: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *highlight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *extent: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.refresh "Link to this definition")
    :   Refresh all layer data based on current view slice.
    property rendering[#](#napari.layers.Labels.rendering "Link to this definition")
    :   Return current rendering mode.
        Selects a preset rendering mode in vispy that determines how
        lablels are displayed. Options include:
        * `translucent`: voxel colors are blended along the view ray until
          the result is opaque.
        * `iso_categorical`: isosurface for categorical data.
          Cast a ray until a non-background value is encountered. At that
          location, lighning calculations are performed to give the visual
          appearance of a surface.
        Returns:
        :   The current rendering mode
        Return type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float64]][#](#napari.layers.Labels.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   Rotation matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    save(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Labels.save "Link to this definition")
    :   Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to open. Extensions may be used to
              specify output format (provided a plugin is available for the
              requested format).
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If `None` then all plugins
              corresponding to appropriate hook specification will be looped
              through to find the first one that can save the data.
        Returns:
        :   File paths of any files that were written.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property scale: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Labels.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates.

        :   array

    :   Swap between the selected label and the background label.

        Type:

    property unique\_id: [Hashable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Hashable "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.unique_id "Link to this definition")

        This is guaranteed to be unique to this specific layer instance

    property units: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[pint.Unit, ...][#](#id16 "Link to this definition")

    property visible: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Labels.visible "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the visual is currently being displayed.
        Type:

    world\_to\_data(*position: npt.ArrayLike*) → np.ndarray[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], np.dtype[np.floating]][#](#napari.layers.Labels.world_to_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from world coordinates to data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in world coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:

        Return type:

# napari.layers.Layer[#](#napari-layers-layer "Link to this heading")
class napari.layers.Layer(*data*, *ndim*, *\**, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *cache=True*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *metadata=None*, *mode='pan\_zoom'*, *multiscale=False*, *name=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *projection\_mode='none'*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*, *locked: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.LayerLock") = False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer)[#](#napari.layers.Layer "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `KeymapProvider`, `MousemapProvider`, [`ABC`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABC "(in Python v3.14)")
    Base layer class.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*array* *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *array*) – Data that the layer is visualizing. Can be N-dimensional.
        * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Number of spatial dimensions.
        * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
          Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
          provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
          If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
        * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
          alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
          {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, ‘translucent\_no\_depth’, ‘additive’, and ‘minimum’}.
        * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
          Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
          Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
          Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
        * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
        * **mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – The layer’s interactive mode.
        * **multiscale** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the data is multiscale or not. Multiscale data is
          represented by a list of data objects and should go from largest to
          smallest.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the layer. If not provided then will be guessed using heuristics.
        * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
          be projected onto the viewed dimensions. Must fit to cls.\_projectionclass.
        * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          np.degrees if needed.
        * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
        * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
          If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
        * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
    affine[#](#napari.layers.Layer.affine "Link to this definition")
    :   (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
        The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
        the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
        Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
        provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        Type:
        :   n-D array or [napari.utils.transforms.Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    axis\_labels[#](#napari.layers.Layer.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimension names of the layer data.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    blending[#](#napari.layers.Layer.blending "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed.
        * `Blending.OPAQUE`
          Allows for only the top layer to be visible and corresponds to
          `depth_test=True`, `cull_face=False`, `blend=False`.
        * `Blending.TRANSLUCENT`
          Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity and
          corresponds to `depth_test=True`, `cull_face=False`,
          `blend=True`, `blend_func=('src_alpha', 'one_minus_src_alpha')`,
          and `blend_equation=('func_add')`.
        * `Blending.TRANSLUCENT_NO_DEPTH`
          Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity, but
          no depth testing is performed. Corresponds to `depth_test=False`,
          `cull_face=False`, `blend=True`,
          `blend_func=('src_alpha', 'one_minus_src_alpha')`, and
          `blend_equation=('func_add')`.
        * `Blending.ADDITIVE`
          Allows for multiple layers to be blended together with different
          colors and opacity. Useful for creating overlays. It corresponds to
          `depth_test=False`, `cull_face=False`, `blend=True`,
          `blend_func=('src_alpha', 'one')`, and `blend_equation=('func_add')`.
        * `Blending.MINIMUM`
          :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together such that
              the minimum of each RGB component and alpha are selected.
              Useful for creating overlays with inverted colormaps. It
              corresponds to `depth_test=False`, `cull_face=False`, `blend=True`,
              `blend_equation=('min')`.
        Type:
        :   Blending
    cache[#](#napari.layers.Layer.cache "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
        Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    corner\_pixels[#](#napari.layers.Layer.corner_pixels "Link to this definition")
    :   Coordinates of the top-left and bottom-right canvas pixels in the data
        coordinates of each layer. For multiscale data the coordinates are in
        the space of the currently viewed data level, not the highest resolution
        level.
        Type:
        :   array
    cursor[#](#napari.layers.Layer.cursor "Link to this definition")
    :   String identifying which cursor displayed over canvas.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    cursor\_size[#](#napari.layers.Layer.cursor_size "Link to this definition")
    :   Size of cursor if custom. None yields default size
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | None
    help[#](#napari.layers.Layer.help "Link to this definition")
    :   Displayed in status bar bottom right.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    mouse\_pan[#](#napari.layers.Layer.mouse_pan "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive panning is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    mouse\_zoom[#](#napari.layers.Layer.mouse_zoom "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive zooming is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    multiscale[#](#napari.layers.Layer.multiscale "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the data is multiscale or not. Multiscale data is
        represented by a list of data objects and should go from largest to
        smallest.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    name[#](#napari.layers.Layer.name "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique name of the layer.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    ndim[#](#napari.layers.Layer.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimensionality of the layer.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    opacity[#](#napari.layers.Layer.opacity "Link to this definition")
    :   Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    projection\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Layer.projection_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
        be projected onto the viewed dimenions.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    rotate[#](#napari.layers.Layer.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
        angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
        pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
        assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
        np.degrees if needed.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), 3-tuple of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), or n-D array.
    scale[#](#napari.layers.Layer.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Scale factors for the layer.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    scale\_factor[#](#napari.layers.Layer.scale_factor "Link to this definition")
    :   Conversion factor from canvas coordinates to image coordinates, which
        depends on the current zoom level.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    shear[#](#napari.layers.Layer.shear "Link to this definition")
    :   Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
        ones along the main diagonal.
        Type:
        :   1-D array or n-D array
    source[#](#napari.layers.Layer.source "Link to this definition")
    :   source of the layer (such as a plugin or widget)
        Type:
        :   Source
    status[#](#napari.layers.Layer.status "Link to this definition")
    :   Displayed in status bar bottom left.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    translate[#](#napari.layers.Layer.translate "Link to this definition")
    :   Translation values for the layer.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    thumbnail[#](#napari.layers.Layer.thumbnail "Link to this definition")
    :   Array of thumbnail data for the layer.
        Type:
        :   (N, M, 4) array
    unique\_id[#](#napari.layers.Layer.unique_id "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique id of the layer. Guaranteed to be unique across the lifetime
        of a viewer.
        Type:
        :   Hashable
    visible[#](#napari.layers.Layer.visible "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    units[#](#napari.layers.Layer.units "Link to this definition")
    :   Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
        If not explicitly set, these default to pixel.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of pint.Unit
    z\_index[#](#napari.layers.Layer.z_index "Link to this definition")
    :   Depth of the layer visual relative to other visuals in the scenecanvas.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    Notes
    Must define the following:
    * \_extent\_data: property
    * data property (setter & getter)
    May define the following:
    * \_set\_view\_slice(): called to set currently viewed slice
    * \_basename(): base/default name of the layer
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |

    | [`click_plane_from_click_data`](#napari.layers.Layer.click_plane_from_click_data "napari.layers.Layer.click_plane_from_click_data")(click\_position, ...) | Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera. |
    | [`create`](#napari.layers.Layer.create "napari.layers.Layer.create")(data[, meta, layer\_type]) | Create layer from data of type layer\_type. |
    | [`data_to_world`](#napari.layers.Layer.data_to_world "napari.layers.Layer.data_to_world")(position) | Convert from data coordinates to world coordinates. |
    | [`get_ray_intersections`](#napari.layers.Layer.get_ray_intersections "napari.layers.Layer.get_ray_intersections")(position, ...[, world]) | Get the start and end point for the ray extending from a point through the data bounding box. |
    | `get_source_str`() |  |
    | [`get_status`](#napari.layers.Layer.get_status "napari.layers.Layer.get_status")([position, view\_direction, ...]) | Status message information of the data at a coordinate position. |
    | [`get_value`](#napari.layers.Layer.get_value "napari.layers.Layer.get_value")(position, \*[, view\_direction, ...]) | Value of the data at a position. |
    | [`projected_distance_from_mouse_drag`](#napari.layers.Layer.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "napari.layers.Layer.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag")(...) | Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates. |
    | [`refresh`](#napari.layers.Layer.refresh "napari.layers.Layer.refresh")([event, thumbnail, data\_displayed, ...]) | Refresh all layer data based on current view slice. |
    | [`save`](#napari.layers.Layer.save "napari.layers.Layer.save")(path[, plugin]) | Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin. |
    | `set_view_slice`() |  |
    | `update_highlight_visibility`(visible) |  |
    | `update_transform_box_visibility`(visible) |  |
    | [`world_to_data`](#napari.layers.Layer.world_to_data "napari.layers.Layer.world_to_data")(position) | Convert from world coordinates to data coordinates. |

    | [`editable`](#napari.layers.Layer.editable "napari.layers.Layer.editable") | Whether the current layer data is editable from the viewer. |

    | [`extent`](#napari.layers.Layer.extent "napari.layers.Layer.extent") | Extent of layer in data and world coordinates. |

    | `keymap` |  |
    | [`loaded`](#napari.layers.Layer.loaded "napari.layers.Layer.loaded") | Whether the layer has been loaded into memory. |

    | [`mode`](#napari.layers.Layer.mode "napari.layers.Layer.mode") | Interactive mode |

    | [`rotate`](#id12 "napari.layers.Layer.rotate") | Rotation matrix in world coordinates. |
    | [`scale`](#id13 "napari.layers.Layer.scale") | Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates. |
