    Details
    property affine: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#id0 "Link to this definition")
    :   Extra affine transform to go from physical to world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.transforms.Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#id1 "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    bind\_key(*key\_bind: [KeyBinding](https://app-model.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/types/#app_model.types.KeyBinding "(in App Model v0.0.0)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [EllipsisType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/types.html#types.EllipsisType "(in Python v3.14)")*, *func=<object object>*, *\**, *overwrite=False*)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.bind_key "Link to this definition")
    :   Bind a key combination to a keymap.
        Parameters:
        :   * **keymap** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *str: callable*) – Keymap to modify.
            * **key\_bind** (*keybinding-like* *or* *...*) – Key combination.
              `...` acts as a wildcard if no key combinations can be matched
              in the keymap (this will overwrite all key combinations
              further down the lookup chain).
            * **func** (*callable**,* *None**, or* *...*) – Callable to bind to the key combination.
              If `None` is passed, unbind instead.
              `...` acts as a blocker, effectively unbinding the key
              combination for all keymaps further down the lookup chain.
            * **overwrite** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *keyword-only**,* *optional*) – Whether to overwrite the key combination if it already exists.
        Returns:
        :   **unbound** – Callable unbound by this operation, if any.
        Return type:
        :   callable or None
        Notes
        Key combinations are represented in the form `[modifier-]key`,
        e.g. `a`, `Control-c`, or `Control-Alt-Delete`.
        Valid modifiers are Control, Alt, Shift, and Meta.
        Letters will always be read as upper-case.
        Due to the native implementation of the key system, Shift pressed in
        certain key combinations may yield inconsistent or unexpected results.
        Therefore, it is not recommended to use Shift with non-letter keys. On OSX,
        Control is swapped with Meta such that pressing Command reads as Control.
        Special keys include Shift, Control, Alt, Meta, Up, Down, Left, Right,
        PageUp, PageDown, Insert, Delete, Home, End, Escape, Backspace, F1,
        F2, F3, F4, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10, F11, F12, Space, Enter, and Tab
        Functions take in only one argument: the parent that the function
        was bound to.
        By default, all functions are assumed to work on key presses only,
        but can be denoted to work on release too by separating the function
        into two statements with the yield keyword:
        ```
        @viewer.bind_key('h')
        def hello_world(viewer):
            # on key press
            viewer.status = 'hello world!'
            yield
            # on key release
            viewer.status = 'goodbye world :('
        ```
        To create a keymap that will block others, `` bind_key(..., ...)` ``.
    property blending: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id2 "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed.
        Blending.OPAQUE
        :   Allows for only the top layer to be visible and corresponds to
            depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False, blend=False.
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity
            and corresponds to depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False,
            blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT\_NO\_DEPTH
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity, but
            no depth testing is performed. Corresponds to `depth_test=False`,
            cull\_face=False, blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.ADDITIVE
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together with
            different colors and opacity. Useful for creating overlays. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one’), and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.MINIMUM
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together such that
            the minimum of each RGB component and alpha are selected.
            Useful for creating overlays with inverted colormaps. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_equation=(‘min’).
        Type:
        :   Blending mode
    click\_plane\_from\_click\_data(*click\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray, np.ndarray][[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.click_plane_from_click_data)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.click_plane_from_click_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data
        coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera.
        Parameters:
        :   * **click\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – click position in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – view direction in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – dimensions of the data array currently in view.
        Returns:
        :   **click\_plane** – tuple of (plane\_position, plane\_normal) in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
    classmethod create(*data: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *meta: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *layer\_type: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Layer](#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.create)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.create "Link to this definition")
    :   Create layer from data of type layer\_type.
        Primarily intended for usage by reader plugin hooks and creating a
        layer from an unwrapped layer data tuple.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*Any*) – Data in a format that is valid for the corresponding layer\_type.
            * **meta** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dict of keyword arguments that will be passed to the corresponding
              layer constructor. If any keys in meta are not valid for the
              corresponding layer type, an exception will be raised.
            * **layer\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Type of layer to add. Must be the (case insensitive) name of a
              Layer subclass. If not provided, the layer is assumed to
              be “image”, unless data.dtype is one of (np.int32, np.uint32,
              np.int64, np.uint64), in which case it is assumed to be “labels”.
        Raises:
        :   * [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If `layer_type` is not one of the recognized layer types.
            * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If any keyword arguments in `meta` are unexpected for the
              corresponding add\_\* method for this layer\_type.
        Examples
        A typical use case might be to upack a tuple of layer data with a
        specified layer\_type.
        ```
        >>> data = (
        ...     np.random.random((10, 2)) * 20,
        ...     {'face_color': 'blue'},
        ...     'points',
        ... )
        >>> Layer.create(*data)
        ```
    property cursor: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id3 "Link to this definition")
    :   String identifying cursor displayed over canvas.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property cursor\_size: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id4 "Link to this definition")
    :   Size of cursor if custom. None yields default size.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    data\_to\_world(*position*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.data_to_world)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.data_to_world "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from data coordinates to world coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in data coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Position in world coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")
    property editable: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Layer.editable "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the current layer data is editable from the viewer.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property extent: Extent[#](#napari.layers.Layer.extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Extent of layer in data and world coordinates.
        For image-like layers, these coordinates are the locations of the
        pixels in Layer.data which are treated like sample points that are
        centered in the rendered version of those pixels.
        For other layers, these coordinates are the points or vertices stored
        in Layer.data.
        Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
    get\_ray\_intersections(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.get_ray_intersections)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.get_ray_intersections "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the start and end point for the ray extending
        from a point through the data bounding box.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** – the position of the point in nD coordinates. World vs. data
              is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – a unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD coordinates.
              World vs. data is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – a list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – True if the provided coordinates are in world coordinates.
              Default value is True.
        Returns:
        :   * **start\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point closest to the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
            * **end\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point farthest from the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
    get\_status(*position: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *value: Any | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.get_status)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.get_status "Link to this definition")
    :   Status message information of the data at a coordinate position.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
            * **value** (*Any*) – Pre-computed value. In some cases,
        Returns:
        :   **status\_dict** – Dictionary containing a information that can be used as a status update.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    get\_value(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.get_value)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.get_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Value of the data at a position.
        If the layer is not visible, return None.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:
        :   **value** – Value of the data. If the layer is not visible return None.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)"), None

    projected\_distance\_from\_mouse\_drag(*start\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *end\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *vector: np.ndarray*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → npt.NDArray[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse
        clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   * **start\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – Starting point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **end\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – End point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – Vector defining the plane normal of the plane onto which the drag
              vector is projected.
            * **vector** (*np.ndarray*) – (3,) unit vector or (n, 3) array thereof on which to project the drag
              vector from start\_event to end\_event. This argument is defined in data
              coordinates.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – (3,) list of currently displayed dimensions
        Returns:
        :   **projected\_distance**
        Return type:
        :   (1, ) or (n, ) np.ndarray of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property projection\_mode[#](#id11 "Link to this definition")
    :   Mode of projection of the thick slice onto the viewed dimensions.
        The sliced data is described by an n-dimensional bounding box (“thick slice”),
        which needs to be projected onto the visible dimensions to be visible.
        The projection mode controls the projection logic.
    refresh(*event: [Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *thumbnail: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *data\_displayed: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *highlight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *extent: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.refresh)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.refresh "Link to this definition")
    :   Refresh all layer data based on current view slice.
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float64]][#](#id12 "Link to this definition")
    :   Rotation matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    save(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.save)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.save "Link to this definition")
    :   Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to open. Extensions may be used to
              specify output format (provided a plugin is available for the
              requested format).
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If `None` then all plugins
              corresponding to appropriate hook specification will be looped
              through to find the first one that can save the data.
        Returns:
        :   File paths of any files that were written.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property scale: npt.NDArray[#](#id13 "Link to this definition")
    :   Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property scale\_factor[#](#id14 "Link to this definition")

    :   Unique ID of the layer.
        This is guaranteed to be unique to this specific layer instance

    property units: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[pint.Unit, ...][#](#id19 "Link to this definition")
    :   List of units for the layer.
    property visible: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id20 "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the visual is currently being displayed.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    world\_to\_data(*position: npt.ArrayLike*) → np.ndarray[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], np.dtype[np.floating]][[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/base.html#Layer.world_to_data)[#](#napari.layers.Layer.world_to_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from world coordinates to data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in world coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Position in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")

* napari.layers.LayerLock
# napari.layers.LayerLock[#](#napari-layers-layerlock "Link to this heading")
class napari.layers.LayerLock(*\*values*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/base/_base_constants.html#LayerLock)[#](#napari.layers.LayerLock "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Flag`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/enum.html#enum.Flag "(in Python v3.14)")
    Flags describing which UI operations are locked on a layer.
    Composable so future lock dimensions can be added without breaking the
    `Layer.locked` API. `bool(value)` is truthy when any flag is set.
    > * NONE: no operations are locked.
    > * DELETION: layer is protected from UI-driven delete; convert and
    >   stack split/merge are also blocked because they delete the
    >   original layer.
    > * ALL: every defined lock dimension is engaged.
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `NONE` |  |
    | `DELETION` |  |
    | `ALL` |  |
    Details

napari.layers.Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html "previous page")
[next
napari.layers.Points](napari.layers.Points.html "next page")

# napari.layers.Points[#](#napari-layers-points "Link to this heading")
class napari.layers.Points(*data=None*, *ndim=None*, *\**, *affine=None*, *antialiasing=1*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *border\_color='dimgray'*, *border\_color\_cycle=None*, *border\_colormap='viridis'*, *border\_contrast\_limits=None*, *border\_width=0.05*, *border\_width\_is\_relative=True*, *cache=True*, *canvas\_size\_limits=(2, 10000)*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *face\_color='white'*, *face\_color\_cycle=None*, *face\_colormap='viridis'*, *face\_contrast\_limits=None*, *feature\_defaults=None*, *features=None*, *metadata=None*, *n\_dimensional=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *out\_of\_slice\_display=False*, *projection\_mode='all'*, *properties=None*, *property\_choices=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shading='none'*, *shear=None*, *shown=True*, *size=10*, *symbol='o'*, *text=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/points/points.html#Points)[#](#napari.layers.Points "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Layer`](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")
    Points layer.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*array* *(**N**,* *D**)*) – Coordinates for N points in D dimensions.
        * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Number of dimensions for shapes. When data is not None, ndim must be D.
          An empty points layer can be instantiated with arbitrary ndim.
        * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
          Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
          provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        * **antialiasing** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Amount of antialiasing in canvas pixels.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
          If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
        * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
          alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
          {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, ‘translucent\_no\_depth’, ‘additive’, and ‘minimum’}.
        * **border\_color** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Color of the point marker border. Numeric color values should be RGB(A).
        * **border\_color\_cycle** (*np.ndarray**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to border\_color if a
          categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
        * **border\_colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")) – Colormap to set border\_color if a continuous attribute is used to set face\_color.
        * **border\_contrast\_limits** (*None**,* *(*[*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
          of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
          The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
          (property.min(), property.max())
        * **border\_width** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array*) – Width of the symbol border in pixels.
        * **border\_width\_is\_relative** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If enabled, border\_width is interpreted as a fraction of the point size.
        * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
          Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        * **canvas\_size\_limits** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Lower and upper limits for the size of points in canvas pixels.
        * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
          Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
          Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
        * **face\_color** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Color of the point marker body. Numeric color values should be RGB(A).
        * **face\_color\_cycle** (*np.ndarray**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to face\_color if a
          categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
        * **face\_colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")) – Colormap to set face\_color if a continuous attribute is used to set face\_color.
        * **face\_contrast\_limits** (*None**,* *(*[*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
          of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
          The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
          (property.min(), property.max())
        * **feature\_defaults** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Any**] or* *DataFrame*) – The default value of each feature in a table with one row.
        * **features** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**] or* *DataFrame*) – Features table where each row corresponds to a point and each column
          is a feature.
        * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
        * **n\_dimensional** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – This property will soon be deprecated in favor of ‘out\_of\_slice\_display’.
          Use that instead.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer. If not provided then will be guessed using heuristics.
        * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        * **out\_of\_slice\_display** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, renders points not just in central plane but also slightly out of slice
          according to specified point marker size.
        * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
          be projected onto the viewed dimensions. Must fit to cls.\_projectionclass.
        * **properties** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}**,* *DataFrame*) – Properties for each point. Each property should be an array of length N,
          where N is the number of points.
        * **property\_choices** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}*) – possible values for each property.
        * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          np.degrees if needed.
        * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
        * **shading** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Shading*) –
          Render lighting and shading on points. Options are:
          + ’none’
            No shading is added to the points.
          + ’spherical’
            Shading and depth buffer are changed to give a 3D spherical look to the points
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **shown** (*1-D array* *of* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether to show each point.
        * **size** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array*) – Size of the point marker in data pixels. If given as a scalar, all points are made
          the same size. If given as an array, size must be the same or broadcastable
          to the same shape as the data.
        * **symbol** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array*) – Symbols to be used for the point markers. Must be one of the
          following: arrow, clobber, cross, diamond, disc, hbar, ring,
          square, star, tailed\_arrow, triangle\_down, triangle\_up, vbar, x.
        * **text** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Text to be displayed with the points. If text is set to a key in properties,
          the value of that property will be displayed. Multiple properties can be
          composed using f-string-like syntax (e.g., ‘{property\_1}, {float\_property:.2f}).
          A dictionary can be provided with keyword arguments to set the text values
          and display properties. See TextManager.\_\_init\_\_() for the valid keyword arguments.
          For example usage, see /napari/examples/add\_points\_with\_text.py.
        * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
        * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
          If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
        * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
    data[#](#napari.layers.Points.data "Link to this definition")
    :   Coordinates for N points in D dimensions.
        Type:
        :   array (N, D)
    axis\_labels[#](#napari.layers.Points.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimension names of the layer data.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    features[#](#napari.layers.Points.features "Link to this definition")
    :   Features table where each row corresponds to a point and each column
        is a feature.
        Type:
        :   DataFrame-like
    feature\_defaults[#](#napari.layers.Points.feature_defaults "Link to this definition")
    :   Stores the default value of each feature in a table with one row.
        Type:
        :   DataFrame-like
    properties[#](#napari.layers.Points.properties "Link to this definition")
    :   Annotations for each point. Each property should be an array of length N,
        where N is the number of points.
        Type:
        :   dict {str: array (N,)} or DataFrame
    text[#](#napari.layers.Points.text "Link to this definition")
    :   Text to be displayed with the points. If text is set to a key in properties, the value of
        that property will be displayed. Multiple properties can be composed using f-string-like
        syntax (e.g., ‘{property\_1}, {float\_property:.2f}).
        For example usage, see /napari/examples/add\_points\_with\_text.py.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    symbol[#](#napari.layers.Points.symbol "Link to this definition")
    :   Array of symbols for each point.
        Type:
        :   array of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    size[#](#napari.layers.Points.size "Link to this definition")
    :   Array of sizes for each point. Must have the same shape as the layer data.
        Type:
        :   array (N,)
    border\_width[#](#napari.layers.Points.border_width "Link to this definition")
    :   Width of the marker borders in pixels for all points
        Type:
        :   array (N,)
    border\_width[#](#id0 "Link to this definition")
    :   Width of the marker borders for all points as a fraction of their size.
        Type:
        :   array (N,)
    border\_color[#](#napari.layers.Points.border_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Array of border color RGBA values, one for each point.
        Type:
        :   Nx4 numpy array
    border\_color\_cycle[#](#napari.layers.Points.border_color_cycle "Link to this definition")
    :   Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to border\_color if a
        categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
        Type:
        :   np.ndarray, [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    border\_colormap[#](#napari.layers.Points.border_colormap "Link to this definition")
    :   Colormap to set border\_color if a continuous attribute is used to set face\_color.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [napari.utils.Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")
    border\_contrast\_limits[#](#napari.layers.Points.border_contrast_limits "Link to this definition")
    :   clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
        of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
        The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
        (property.min(), property.max())
        Type:
        :   None, ([float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"))
    face\_color[#](#napari.layers.Points.face_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Array of face color RGBA values, one for each point.
        Type:
        :   Nx4 numpy array
    face\_color\_cycle[#](#napari.layers.Points.face_color_cycle "Link to this definition")
    :   Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to face\_color if a
        categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
        Type:
        :   np.ndarray, [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    face\_colormap[#](#napari.layers.Points.face_colormap "Link to this definition")
    :   Colormap to set face\_color if a continuous attribute is used to set face\_color.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [napari.utils.Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")
    face\_contrast\_limits[#](#napari.layers.Points.face_contrast_limits "Link to this definition")
    :   clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
        of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
        The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
        (property.min(), property.max())
        Type:
        :   None, ([float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"))
    current\_symbol[#](#napari.layers.Points.current_symbol "Link to this definition")
    :   Symbol for the next point to be added or the currently selected points.
        Type:
        :   Symbol
    current\_size[#](#napari.layers.Points.current_size "Link to this definition")
    :   Size of the marker for the next point to be added or the currently
        selected point.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    current\_border\_width[#](#napari.layers.Points.current_border_width "Link to this definition")
    :   Border width of the marker for the next point to be added or the currently
        selected point.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    current\_border\_color[#](#napari.layers.Points.current_border_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Border color of the marker border for the next point to be added or the currently
        selected point.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    current\_face\_color[#](#napari.layers.Points.current_face_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Face color of the marker border for the next point to be added or the currently
        selected point.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    out\_of\_slice\_display[#](#napari.layers.Points.out_of_slice_display "Link to this definition")
    :   If True, renders points not just in central plane but also slightly out of slice
        according to specified point marker size.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    selected\_data[#](#napari.layers.Points.selected_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Integer indices of any selected points.
        Type:
        :   [Selection](napari.utils.events.Selection.html#napari.utils.events.Selection "napari.utils.events.Selection")
    mode[#](#napari.layers.Points.mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Interactive mode. The normal, default mode is PAN\_ZOOM, which
        allows for normal interactivity with the canvas.
        In ADD mode clicks of the cursor add points at the clicked location.
        In SELECT mode the cursor can select points by clicking on them or
        by dragging a box around them. Once selected points can be moved,
        have their properties edited, or be deleted.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    face\_color\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Points.face_color_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Face color setting mode.
        DIRECT (default mode) allows each point to be set arbitrarily
        CYCLE allows the color to be set via a color cycle over an attribute
        COLORMAP allows color to be set via a color map over an attribute
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    border\_color\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Points.border_color_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Border color setting mode.
        DIRECT (default mode) allows each point to be set arbitrarily
        CYCLE allows the color to be set via a color cycle over an attribute
        COLORMAP allows color to be set via a color map over an attribute
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    shading[#](#napari.layers.Points.shading "Link to this definition")
    :   Shading mode.
        Type:
        :   Shading
    antialiasing[#](#napari.layers.Points.antialiasing "Link to this definition")
    :   Amount of antialiasing in canvas pixels.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    canvas\_size\_limits[#](#napari.layers.Points.canvas_size_limits "Link to this definition")
    :   Lower and upper limits for the size of points in canvas pixels.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    shown[#](#napari.layers.Points.shown "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether each point is shown.
        Type:
        :   1-D array of [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    units[#](#napari.layers.Points.units "Link to this definition")
    :   Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of pint.Unit
    Notes
    \_view\_dataarray (M, D)
    :   coordinates of points in the currently viewed slice.
    \_view\_sizearray (M, )
    :   Size of the point markers in the currently viewed slice.
    \_view\_symbolarray (M, )
    :   Symbols of the point markers in the currently viewed slice.
    \_view\_border\_widtharray (M, )
    :   Border width of the point markers in the currently viewed slice.
    \_indices\_viewarray (M, )
    :   Integer indices of the points in the currently viewed slice and are shown.
    \_selected\_view :
    :   Integer indices of selected points in the currently viewed slice within
        the \_view\_data array.
    \_selected\_boxarray (4, 2) or None
    :   Four corners of any box either around currently selected points or
        being created during a drag action. Starting in the top left and
        going clockwise.
    \_drag\_startlist or None
    :   Coordinates of first cursor click during a drag action. Gets reset to
        None after dragging is done.

    Details
    add(*coords: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/points/points.html#Points.add)[#](#napari.layers.Points.add "Link to this definition")
    :   Adds points at coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **coords** (*array*) – Point or points to add to the layer data.
    property affine: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.layers.Points.affine "Link to this definition")
    :   Extra affine transform to go from physical to world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.transforms.Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property antialiasing: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id1 "Link to this definition")
    :   Amount of antialiasing in canvas pixels.
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#id2 "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    bind\_key(*key\_bind: [KeyBinding](https://app-model.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/types/#app_model.types.KeyBinding "(in App Model v0.0.0)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [EllipsisType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/types.html#types.EllipsisType "(in Python v3.14)")*, *func=<object object>*, *\**, *overwrite=False*)[#](#napari.layers.Points.bind_key "Link to this definition")
    :   Bind a key combination to a keymap.
        Parameters:
        :   * **keymap** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *str: callable*) – Keymap to modify.
            * **key\_bind** (*keybinding-like* *or* *...*) – Key combination.
              `...` acts as a wildcard if no key combinations can be matched
              in the keymap (this will overwrite all key combinations
              further down the lookup chain).
            * **func** (*callable**,* *None**, or* *...*) – Callable to bind to the key combination.
              If `None` is passed, unbind instead.
              `...` acts as a blocker, effectively unbinding the key
              combination for all keymaps further down the lookup chain.
            * **overwrite** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *keyword-only**,* *optional*) – Whether to overwrite the key combination if it already exists.
        Returns:
        :   **unbound** – Callable unbound by this operation, if any.
        Return type:
        :   callable or None
        Notes
        Key combinations are represented in the form `[modifier-]key`,
        e.g. `a`, `Control-c`, or `Control-Alt-Delete`.
        Valid modifiers are Control, Alt, Shift, and Meta.
        Letters will always be read as upper-case.
        Due to the native implementation of the key system, Shift pressed in
        certain key combinations may yield inconsistent or unexpected results.
        Therefore, it is not recommended to use Shift with non-letter keys. On OSX,
        Control is swapped with Meta such that pressing Command reads as Control.
        Special keys include Shift, Control, Alt, Meta, Up, Down, Left, Right,
        PageUp, PageDown, Insert, Delete, Home, End, Escape, Backspace, F1,
        F2, F3, F4, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10, F11, F12, Space, Enter, and Tab
        Functions take in only one argument: the parent that the function
        was bound to.
        By default, all functions are assumed to work on key presses only,
        but can be denoted to work on release too by separating the function
        into two statements with the yield keyword:
        ```
        @viewer.bind_key('h')
        def hello_world(viewer):
            # on key press
            viewer.status = 'hello world!'
            yield
            # on key release
            viewer.status = 'goodbye world :('
        ```
        To create a keymap that will block others, `` bind_key(..., ...)` ``.
    property blending: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Points.blending "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed.
        Blending.OPAQUE
        :   Allows for only the top layer to be visible and corresponds to
            depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False, blend=False.
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity
            and corresponds to depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False,
            blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT\_NO\_DEPTH
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity, but
            no depth testing is performed. Corresponds to `depth_test=False`,
            cull\_face=False, blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.ADDITIVE
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together with
            different colors and opacity. Useful for creating overlays. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one’), and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.MINIMUM
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together such that
            the minimum of each RGB component and alpha are selected.
            Useful for creating overlays with inverted colormaps. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_equation=(‘min’).
        Type:
        :   Blending mode
    property border\_color: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#id3 "Link to this definition")
    :   Array of RGBA border colors for each point
        Type:
        :   (N x 4) np.ndarray
    property border\_color\_cycle: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#id4 "Link to this definition")
    :   Color cycle for border\_color.
        Can be a list of colors defined by name, RGB or RGBA
        Type:
        :   Union[[list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)"), np.ndarray]
    property border\_color\_mode: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id5 "Link to this definition")
    :   border color setting mode
        DIRECT (default mode) allows each point to be set arbitrarily
        CYCLE allows the color to be set via a color cycle over an attribute
        COLORMAP allows color to be set via a color map over an attribute
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property border\_colorbar[#](#napari.layers.Points.border_colorbar "Link to this definition")
    :   The colorbar associated with this layer’s color manager.
    property border\_colormap: [Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.colormaps.colormap.Colormap")[#](#id6 "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the colormap to be applied to a property to get the border color.
        Returns:
        :   **colormap** – The Colormap object.
        Return type:
        :   [napari.utils.Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")
    property border\_contrast\_limits: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#id7 "Link to this definition")
    :   contrast limits for mapping
        the border\_color colormap property to 0 and 1
        Type:
        :   None, ([float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"))
    property border\_width: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#id8 "Link to this definition")
    :   border\_width of all N points.
        Type:
        :   (N, D) array
    property border\_width\_is\_relative: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Points.border_width_is_relative "Link to this definition")
    :   treat border\_width as a fraction of point size.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property canvas\_size\_limits: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#id9 "Link to this definition")
    :   Limit the canvas size of points
    click\_plane\_from\_click\_data(*click\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray, np.ndarray][#](#napari.layers.Points.click_plane_from_click_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data
        coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera.
        Parameters:
        :   * **click\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – click position in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – view direction in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – dimensions of the data array currently in view.
        Returns:
        :   **click\_plane** – tuple of (plane\_position, plane\_normal) in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
    classmethod create(*data: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *meta: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *layer\_type: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")[#](#napari.layers.Points.create "Link to this definition")
    :   Create layer from data of type layer\_type.
        Primarily intended for usage by reader plugin hooks and creating a
        layer from an unwrapped layer data tuple.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*Any*) – Data in a format that is valid for the corresponding layer\_type.
            * **meta** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dict of keyword arguments that will be passed to the corresponding
              layer constructor. If any keys in meta are not valid for the
              corresponding layer type, an exception will be raised.
            * **layer\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Type of layer to add. Must be the (case insensitive) name of a
              Layer subclass. If not provided, the layer is assumed to
              be “image”, unless data.dtype is one of (np.int32, np.uint32,
              np.int64, np.uint64), in which case it is assumed to be “labels”.
        Raises:
        :   * [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If `layer_type` is not one of the recognized layer types.
            * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If any keyword arguments in `meta` are unexpected for the
              corresponding add\_\* method for this layer\_type.
        Examples
        A typical use case might be to upack a tuple of layer data with a
        specified layer\_type.
        ```
        >>> data = (
        ...     np.random.random((10, 2)) * 20,
        ...     {'face_color': 'blue'},
        ...     'points',
        ... )
        >>> Layer.create(*data)
        ```

    property editable: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Points.editable "Link to this definition")

    property extent: Extent[#](#napari.layers.Points.extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Extent of layer in data and world coordinates.
        For image-like layers, these coordinates are the locations of the
        pixels in Layer.data which are treated like sample points that are
        centered in the rendered version of those pixels.
        For other layers, these coordinates are the points or vertices stored
        in Layer.data.
        Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
    property face\_color: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#id16 "Link to this definition")
    :   Array of RGBA face colors for each point
        Type:

    property face\_color\_cycle: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#id17 "Link to this definition")
    :   Color cycle for face\_color
        Can be a list of colors defined by name, RGB or RGBA
        Type:
        :   Union[np.ndarray, cycle]
    property face\_color\_mode: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id18 "Link to this definition")
    :   Face color setting mode
        DIRECT (default mode) allows each point to be set arbitrarily
        CYCLE allows the color to be set via a color cycle over an attribute
        COLORMAP allows color to be set via a color map over an attribute
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")

    property face\_contrast\_limits: [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#id20 "Link to this definition")

    property feature\_defaults: pd.DataFrame[#](#id21 "Link to this definition")
    :   Dataframe-like with one row of feature default values.
        See features for more details on the type of this property.
    property features: pd.DataFrame[#](#id22 "Link to this definition")
    :   Dataframe-like features table.
        It is an implementation detail that this is a pandas.DataFrame. In the future,
        we will target the currently-in-development Data API dataframe protocol [1].
        This will enable us to use alternate libraries such as xarray or cuDF for
        additional features without breaking existing usage of this.
        If you need to specifically rely on the pandas API, please coerce this to a
        pandas.DataFrame using features\_to\_pandas\_dataframe.
        References
    get\_point\_info(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/points/points.html#Points.get_point_info)[#](#napari.layers.Points.get_point_info "Link to this definition")
    :   Retrieve all available information about a point at the given index.
        Parameters:
        :   **index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Index of the point.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary containing all relevant details of the point.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    get\_ray\_intersections(*position: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *view\_direction: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)"), [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")] | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/points/points.html#Points.get_ray_intersections)[#](#napari.layers.Points.get_ray_intersections "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the start and end point for the ray extending
        from a point through the displayed bounding box.
        This method overrides the base layer, replacing the bounding box used
        to calculate intersections with a larger one which includes the size
        of points in view.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** – the position of the point in nD coordinates. World vs. data
              is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – a unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD coordinates.
              World vs. data is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **dims\_displayed** – a list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – True if the provided coordinates are in world coordinates.
              Default value is True.
        Returns:
        :   * **start\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point closest to the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
            * **end\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point farthest from the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
    get\_status(*position: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *view\_direction: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/points/points.html#Points.get_status)[#](#napari.layers.Points.get_status "Link to this definition")
    :   Status message information of the data at a coordinate position.
        # Parameters
        # ———-
        # position : tuple
        # Position in either data or world coordinates.
        # view\_direction : Optional[np.ndarray]
        # A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
        # The default value is None.
        # dims\_displayed : Optional[List[int]]
        # A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
        # The default value is None.
        # world : bool
        # If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
        # and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        # Returns
        # ——-
        # source\_info : dict
        # Dict containing information that can be used in a status update.
        #
    get\_value(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Points.get_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Value of the data at a position.
        If the layer is not visible, return None.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:
        :   **value** – Value of the data. If the layer is not visible return None.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)"), None

    property locked: [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.base._base_constants.LayerLock")[#](#napari.layers.Points.locked "Link to this definition")
    :   which UI operations are locked on this layer.
        `bool(layer.locked)` is truthy when any lock flag is set. Setting
        `True`/`False` is equivalent to `LayerLock.ALL`/`LayerLock.NONE`.
        Type:
        :   [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.LayerLock")
    property metadata: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Points.metadata "Link to this definition")
    :   Key/value map for user-stored data.
    property mode: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id23 "Link to this definition")
    :   Interactive mode
        Interactive mode. The normal, default mode is PAN\_ZOOM, which
        allows for normal interactivity with the canvas.
        In ADD mode clicks of the cursor add points at the clicked location.
        In SELECT mode the cursor can select points by clicking on them or
        by dragging a box around them. Once selected points can be moved,
        have their properties edited, or be deleted.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property mouse\_pan: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Points.mouse_pan "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive panning is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property mouse\_zoom: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Points.mouse_zoom "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive zooming is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")

        :   **index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Index of the point to remove. Default is -1, which removes the last point.
