    get\_value(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.get_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Value of the data at a position.
        If the layer is not visible, return None.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:
        :   **value** – Value of the data. If the layer is not visible return None.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)"), None
    property help: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.help "Link to this definition")
    :   displayed in status bar bottom right.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    interaction\_box(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")[9], [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float32]] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.interaction_box)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.interaction_box "Link to this definition")
    :   Create the interaction box around a shape or list of shapes.
        If a single index is passed then the bounding box will be inherited
        from that shapes interaction box. If list of indices is passed it will
        be computed directly.
        Parameters:
        :   **index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *iterable* *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Index of a single shape, list of shapes or Selection around which to
            construct the interaction box
        Returns:
        :   **box** – 10x2 array of vertices of the interaction box. The first 8 points
            are the corners and midpoints of the box in clockwise order
            starting in the upper-left corner. The 9th point is the center of
            the box, and the last point is the location of the rotation handle
            that can be used to rotate the box
        Return type:
        :   np.ndarray
    property loaded: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.loaded "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the layer has been loaded into memory.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property locked: [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.base._base_constants.LayerLock")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.locked "Link to this definition")
    :   which UI operations are locked on this layer.
        `bool(layer.locked)` is truthy when any lock flag is set. Setting
        `True`/`False` is equivalent to `LayerLock.ALL`/`LayerLock.NONE`.
        Type:
        :   [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.LayerLock")
    property metadata: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.metadata "Link to this definition")
    :   Key/value map for user-stored data.
    property mode[#](#id10 "Link to this definition")
    :   Interactive mode. The normal, default mode is PAN\_ZOOM, which
        allows for normal interactivity with the canvas.
        The SELECT mode allows for entire shapes to be selected, moved and
        resized.
        The DIRECT mode allows for shapes to be selected and their individual
        vertices to be moved.
        The VERTEX\_INSERT and VERTEX\_REMOVE modes allow for individual
        vertices either to be added to or removed from shapes that are already
        selected. Note that shapes cannot be selected in this mode.
        The ADD\_RECTANGLE, ADD\_ELLIPSE, ADD\_LINE, ADD\_POLYLINE, ADD\_PATH, and
        ADD\_POLYGON modes all allow for their corresponding shape type to be
        added.
        Type:
        :   MODE
    property mouse\_pan: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.mouse_pan "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive panning is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property mouse\_zoom: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.mouse_zoom "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive zooming is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    move\_to\_back() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.move_to_back)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.move_to_back "Link to this definition")
    :   Moves selected objects to be displayed behind all others.
    move\_to\_front() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.move_to_front)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.move_to_front "Link to this definition")
    :   Moves selected objects to be displayed in front of all others.
    property name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.name "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique name of the layer.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property ndim: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Number of dimensions in the data.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    property nshapes[#](#id11 "Link to this definition")
    :   Total number of shapes.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    property opacity: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.opacity "Link to this definition")
    :   Opacity value between 0.0 and 1.0.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    pop(*index=-1*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.pop)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove and return the shape at the given index.
        Parameters:
        :   **index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Index of the shape to remove and return. Defaults to -1, which
            removes the last shape.
        Returns:
        :   Dictionary containing the removed shape’s data.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Any]
    projected\_distance\_from\_mouse\_drag(*start\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *end\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *vector: np.ndarray*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse
        clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   * **start\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – Starting point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **end\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – End point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – Vector defining the plane normal of the plane onto which the drag
              vector is projected.
            * **vector** (*np.ndarray*) – (3,) unit vector or (n, 3) array thereof on which to project the drag
              vector from start\_event to end\_event. This argument is defined in data
              coordinates.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – (3,) list of currently displayed dimensions
        Returns:
        :   **projected\_distance**
        Return type:
        :   (1, ) or (n, ) np.ndarray of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property projection\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.projection_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Mode of projection of the thick slice onto the viewed dimensions.
        The sliced data is described by an n-dimensional bounding box (“thick slice”),
        which needs to be projected onto the visible dimensions to be visible.
        The projection mode controls the projection logic.
    property properties: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")][#](#id12 "Link to this definition")
    :   Annotations for each shape
        Type:
        :   dict {str
        Type:
        :   np.ndarray (N,)}, DataFrame
    refresh(*event: [Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *thumbnail: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *data\_displayed: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *highlight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *extent: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.refresh)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.refresh "Link to this definition")
    :   Refresh all layer data based on current view slice.
    refresh\_colors(*update\_color\_mapping: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.refresh_colors)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.refresh_colors "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate and update face and edge colors if using a cycle or color map
        Parameters:
        :   **update\_color\_mapping** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If set to True, the function will recalculate the color cycle map
            or colormap (whichever is being used). If set to False, the function
            will use the current color cycle map or color map. For example, if you
            are adding/modifying shapes and want them to be colored with the same
            mapping as the other shapes (i.e., the new shapes shouldn’t affect
            the color cycle map or colormap), set update\_color\_mapping=False.
            Default value is False.
    refresh\_text()[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.refresh_text)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.refresh_text "Link to this definition")
    :   Refresh the text values.
        This is generally used if the properties were updated without changing the data
    remove(*indices: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.remove)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove any shapes at the given indices.
        Parameters:
        :   **indices** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – List of indices of shapes to remove from the layer.
    remove\_selected() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.remove_selected)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.remove_selected "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove any selected shapes.
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float64]][#](#napari.layers.Shapes.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   Rotation matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    save(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Shapes.save "Link to this definition")
    :   Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to open. Extensions may be used to
              specify output format (provided a plugin is available for the
              requested format).
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If `None` then all plugins
              corresponding to appropriate hook specification will be looped
              through to find the first one that can save the data.
        Returns:
        :   File paths of any files that were written.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property scale: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property scale\_factor[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.scale_factor "Link to this definition")
    :   Conversion factor from canvas coordinates to image coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property selected\_data: Selection[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#id13 "Link to this definition")
    :   set of currently selected shapes.
        Type:
        :   [Selection](napari.utils.events.Selection.html#napari.utils.events.Selection "napari.utils.events.Selection")
    property shape\_type[#](#id14 "Link to this definition")
    :   name of shape type for each shape.
        Type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property shear: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.shear "Link to this definition")
    :   Shear matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property text: TextManager[#](#id15 "Link to this definition")
    :   The TextManager object containing the text properties
        Type:
        :   TextManager
    property thumbnail: npt.NDArray[np.uint8][#](#napari.layers.Shapes.thumbnail "Link to this definition")
    :   Integer array of thumbnail for the layer
        Type:
        :   array
    to\_labels(*labels\_shape=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.to_labels)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.to_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Return an integer labels image.
        Parameters:
        :   **labels\_shape** (*np.ndarray* *|* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *|* *None*) – Tuple defining shape of labels image to be generated. If non
            specified, takes the max of all the vertiecs
        Returns:
        :   **labels** – Integer array where each value is either 0 for background or an
            integer up to N for points inside the shape at the index value - 1.
            For overlapping shapes z-ordering will be respected.
        Return type:
        :   np.ndarray
    to\_masks(*mask\_shape=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/shapes/shapes.html#Shapes.to_masks)[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.to_masks "Link to this definition")
    :   Return an array of binary masks, one for each shape.
        Parameters:
        :   **mask\_shape** (*np.ndarray* *|* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *|* *None*) – tuple defining shape of mask to be generated. If non specified,
            takes the max of all the vertices
        Returns:
        :   **masks** – Array where there is one binary mask for each shape
        Return type:
        :   np.ndarray
    property translate: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.translate "Link to this definition")
    :   Factors to shift the layer by in units of world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property unique\_id: [Hashable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Hashable "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Shapes.unique_id "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique ID of the layer.
        This is guaranteed to be unique to this specific layer instance
        over the lifetime of the program.

* napari.layers.Surface
# napari.layers.Surface[#](#napari-layers-surface "Link to this heading")
class napari.layers.Surface(*data*, *\**, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *cache=True*, *colormap='gray'*, *contrast\_limits=None*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *feature\_defaults=None*, *features=None*, *gamma=1.0*, *metadata=None*, *name=None*, *normals=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *projection\_mode='none'*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shading='flat'*, *shear=None*, *texcoords=None*, *texture=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *vertex\_colors=None*, *visible=True*, *wireframe=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/surface/surface.html#Surface)[#](#napari.layers.Surface "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `IntensityVisualizationMixin`, [`Layer`](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")
    Surface layer renders meshes onto the canvas.
    Surfaces may be colored by:
    :   * setting vertex\_values, which colors the surface with the selected
          colormap (default is uniform ones)
        * setting vertex\_colors, which replaces/overrides any color from
          vertex\_values
        * setting both texture and texcoords, which blends a the value from
          a texture (image) with the underlying color from vertex\_values or
          vertex\_colors. Blending is achieved by multiplying the texture
          color by the underlying color - an underlying value of “white” will
          result in the unaltered texture color.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*2-tuple* *or* *3-tuple* *of* *array*) –
          The first element of the tuple is an (N, D) array of vertices of
          mesh triangles.
          The second is an (M, 3) array of int of indices of the mesh triangles.
          The optional third element is the (K0, …, KL, N) array of values
          (vertex\_values) used to color vertices where the additional L
          dimensions are used to color the same mesh with different values. If
          not provided, it defaults to ones.
        * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
          Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
          provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
          If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
        * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
          alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
          {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, and ‘additive’}.
        * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
          Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        * **colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")*,* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Colormap to use for luminance images. If a string must be the name
          of a supported colormap from vispy or matplotlib. If a tuple the
          first value must be a string to assign as a name to a colormap and
          the second item must be a Colormap. If a dict the key must be a
          string to assign as a name to a colormap and the value must be a
          Colormap.
        * **contrast\_limits** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *(**2**,**)*) – Color limits to be used for determining the colormap bounds for
          luminance images. If not passed is calculated as the min and max of
          the image.
        * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
          Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
          Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
        * **feature\_defaults** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Any**] or* *Dataframe-like*) – The default value of each feature in a table with one row.
        * **features** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**] or* *Dataframe-like*) – Features table where each row corresponds to a shape and each column
          is a feature.
        * **gamma** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Gamma correction for determining colormap linearity. Defaults to 1.
        * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
        * **normals** (*None**,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *SurfaceNormals*) – Whether and how to display the face and vertex normals of the surface mesh.
        * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
          be projected onto the viewed dimenions.
        * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          np.degrees if needed.
        * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
        * **shading** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Shading*) –
          One of a list of preset shading modes that determine the lighting model
          using when rendering the surface in 3D.
          + `Shading.NONE`
            Corresponds to `shading='none'`.
          + `Shading.FLAT`
            Corresponds to `shading='flat'`.
          + `Shading.SMOOTH`
            Corresponds to `shading='smooth'`.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **texcoords** (*(**N**,* *2**)* *array*) – 2D coordinates for each vertex, mapping into the texture.
          The number of texture coords must match the number of vertices (N).
          Coordinates should be in [0.0, 1.0] and are scaled to sample the 2D
          texture. Coordinates outside this range will wrap, but this behavior
          should be considered an implementation detail: there are no plans to
          change it, but it’s a feature of the underlying vispy visual.
        * **texture** (*(**I**,* *J**) or* *(**I**,* *J**,* *C**)* *array*) – A 2D texture to be mapped onto the mesh using texcoords.
          C may be 3 (RGB) or 4 (RGBA) channels for a color texture.
        * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer
        * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
          If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
        * **vertex\_colors** (*(**N**,* *C**) or* *(**K0**,* *...**,* *KL**,* *N**,* *C**)* *array* *of* *color values*) – Take care that the (optional) L additional dimensions match those of
          vertex\_values for proper slicing.
          C may be 3 (RGB) or 4 (RGBA) channels..
        * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
        * **wireframe** (*None**,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *SurfaceWireframe*) – Whether and how to display the edges of the surface mesh with a wireframe.
    data[#](#napari.layers.Surface.data "Link to this definition")
    :   The first element of the tuple is an (N, D) array of vertices of
        mesh triangles. The second is an (M, 3) array of int of indices
        of the mesh triangles. The third element is the (K0, …, KL, N)
        array of values used to color vertices where the additional L
        dimensions are used to color the same mesh with different values.
        Type:
        :   3-tuple of array
    axis\_labels[#](#napari.layers.Surface.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimension names of the layer data.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    vertices[#](#napari.layers.Surface.vertices "Link to this definition")
    :   Vertices of mesh triangles.
        Type:
        :   (N, D) array
    faces[#](#napari.layers.Surface.faces "Link to this definition")
    :   Indices of mesh triangles.
        Type:
        :   (M, 3) array of [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    vertex\_values[#](#napari.layers.Surface.vertex_values "Link to this definition")
    :   Values used to color vertices.
        Type:
        :   (K0, …, KL, N) array
    features[#](#napari.layers.Surface.features "Link to this definition")
    :   Features table where each row corresponds to a vertex and each column
        is a feature.
        Type:
        :   DataFrame-like
    feature\_defaults[#](#napari.layers.Surface.feature_defaults "Link to this definition")
    :   Stores the default value of each feature in a table with one row.
        Type:
        :   DataFrame-like
    colormap[#](#napari.layers.Surface.colormap "Link to this definition")
    :   Colormap to use for luminance images. If a string must be the name
        of a supported colormap from vispy or matplotlib. If a tuple the
        first value must be a string to assign as a name to a colormap and
        the second item must be a Colormap. If a dict the key must be a
        string to assign as a name to a colormap and the value must be a
        Colormap.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [napari.utils.Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap"), [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)"), [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    contrast\_limits[#](#napari.layers.Surface.contrast_limits "Link to this definition")
    :   Color limits to be used for determining the colormap bounds for
        luminance images. If not passed is calculated as the min and max of
        the image.
        Type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") (2,)
    shading[#](#napari.layers.Surface.shading "Link to this definition")
    :   One of a list of preset shading modes that determine the lighting model
        using when rendering the surface.
        * `'none'`
        * `'flat'`
        * `'smooth'`
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    gamma[#](#napari.layers.Surface.gamma "Link to this definition")
    :   Gamma correction for determining colormap linearity.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    wireframe[#](#napari.layers.Surface.wireframe "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether and how to display the edges of the surface mesh with a wireframe.
        Type:
        :   SurfaceWireframe
    normals[#](#napari.layers.Surface.normals "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether and how to display the face and vertex normals of the surface mesh.
        Type:
        :   SurfaceNormals
    units[#](#napari.layers.Surface.units "Link to this definition")
    :   Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of pint.Unit
    Notes
    \_data\_view(M, 2) or (M, 3) array
    :   The coordinates of the vertices given the viewed dimensions.
    \_view\_faces(P, 3) array
    :   The integer indices of the vertices that form the triangles
        in the currently viewed slice.
    \_colorbararray
    :   Colorbar for current colormap.

    | [`create`](#napari.layers.Surface.create "napari.layers.Surface.create")(data[, meta, layer\_type]) | Create layer from data of type layer\_type. |

    | [`get_ray_intersections`](#napari.layers.Surface.get_ray_intersections "napari.layers.Surface.get_ray_intersections")(position, ...[, world]) | Get the start and end point for the ray extending from a point through the data bounding box. |

    | [`get_value`](#napari.layers.Surface.get_value "napari.layers.Surface.get_value")(position, \*[, view\_direction, ...]) | Value of the data at a position. |

    | [`refresh`](#napari.layers.Surface.refresh "napari.layers.Surface.refresh")([event, thumbnail, data\_displayed, ...]) | Refresh all layer data based on current view slice. |

    | [`save`](#napari.layers.Surface.save "napari.layers.Surface.save")(path[, plugin]) | Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin. |

    Details
    property affine: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.affine "Link to this definition")
    :   Extra affine transform to go from physical to world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.transforms.Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#id0 "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    bind\_key(*key\_bind: [KeyBinding](https://app-model.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/types/#app_model.types.KeyBinding "(in App Model v0.0.0)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [EllipsisType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/types.html#types.EllipsisType "(in Python v3.14)")*, *func=<object object>*, *\**, *overwrite=False*)[#](#napari.layers.Surface.bind_key "Link to this definition")
    :   Bind a key combination to a keymap.
        Parameters:
        :   * **keymap** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *str: callable*) – Keymap to modify.
            * **key\_bind** (*keybinding-like* *or* *...*) – Key combination.
              `...` acts as a wildcard if no key combinations can be matched
              in the keymap (this will overwrite all key combinations
              further down the lookup chain).
            * **func** (*callable**,* *None**, or* *...*) – Callable to bind to the key combination.
              If `None` is passed, unbind instead.
              `...` acts as a blocker, effectively unbinding the key
              combination for all keymaps further down the lookup chain.
            * **overwrite** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *keyword-only**,* *optional*) – Whether to overwrite the key combination if it already exists.
        Returns:
        :   **unbound** – Callable unbound by this operation, if any.
        Return type:
        :   callable or None
        Notes
        Key combinations are represented in the form `[modifier-]key`,
        e.g. `a`, `Control-c`, or `Control-Alt-Delete`.
        Valid modifiers are Control, Alt, Shift, and Meta.
        Letters will always be read as upper-case.
        Due to the native implementation of the key system, Shift pressed in
        certain key combinations may yield inconsistent or unexpected results.
        Therefore, it is not recommended to use Shift with non-letter keys. On OSX,
        Control is swapped with Meta such that pressing Command reads as Control.
        Special keys include Shift, Control, Alt, Meta, Up, Down, Left, Right,
        PageUp, PageDown, Insert, Delete, Home, End, Escape, Backspace, F1,
        F2, F3, F4, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10, F11, F12, Space, Enter, and Tab
        Functions take in only one argument: the parent that the function
        was bound to.
        By default, all functions are assumed to work on key presses only,
        but can be denoted to work on release too by separating the function
        into two statements with the yield keyword:
        ```
        @viewer.bind_key('h')
        def hello_world(viewer):
            # on key press
            viewer.status = 'hello world!'
            yield
            # on key release
            viewer.status = 'goodbye world :('
        ```
        To create a keymap that will block others, `` bind_key(..., ...)` ``.
    property blending: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.blending "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed.
        Blending.OPAQUE
        :   Allows for only the top layer to be visible and corresponds to
            depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False, blend=False.
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity
            and corresponds to depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False,
            blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT\_NO\_DEPTH
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity, but
            no depth testing is performed. Corresponds to `depth_test=False`,
            cull\_face=False, blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.ADDITIVE
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together with
            different colors and opacity. Useful for creating overlays. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one’), and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.MINIMUM
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together such that
            the minimum of each RGB component and alpha are selected.
            Useful for creating overlays with inverted colormaps. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_equation=(‘min’).
        Type:
        :   Blending mode
    click\_plane\_from\_click\_data(*click\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray, np.ndarray][#](#napari.layers.Surface.click_plane_from_click_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data
        coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera.
        Parameters:
        :   * **click\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – click position in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – view direction in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – dimensions of the data array currently in view.
        Returns:
        :   **click\_plane** – tuple of (plane\_position, plane\_normal) in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
    property colorbar[#](#napari.layers.Surface.colorbar "Link to this definition")
    :   Color Bar overlay.

    property contrast\_limits[#](#id2 "Link to this definition")
    :   Limits to use for the colormap.
        Type:

    property contrast\_limits\_range[#](#napari.layers.Surface.contrast_limits_range "Link to this definition")

    classmethod create(*data: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *meta: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *layer\_type: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.create "Link to this definition")
    :   Create layer from data of type layer\_type.
        Primarily intended for usage by reader plugin hooks and creating a
        layer from an unwrapped layer data tuple.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*Any*) – Data in a format that is valid for the corresponding layer\_type.
            * **meta** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dict of keyword arguments that will be passed to the corresponding
              layer constructor. If any keys in meta are not valid for the
              corresponding layer type, an exception will be raised.
            * **layer\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Type of layer to add. Must be the (case insensitive) name of a
              Layer subclass. If not provided, the layer is assumed to
              be “image”, unless data.dtype is one of (np.int32, np.uint32,
              np.int64, np.uint64), in which case it is assumed to be “labels”.
        Raises:
        :   * [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If `layer_type` is not one of the recognized layer types.
            * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If any keyword arguments in `meta` are unexpected for the
              corresponding add\_\* method for this layer\_type.
        Examples
        A typical use case might be to upack a tuple of layer data with a
        specified layer\_type.
        ```
        >>> data = (
        ...     np.random.random((10, 2)) * 20,
        ...     {'face_color': 'blue'},
        ...     'points',
        ... )
        >>> Layer.create(*data)
        ```
    property cursor: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.cursor "Link to this definition")
    :   String identifying cursor displayed over canvas.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property cursor\_size: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.cursor_size "Link to this definition")
    :   Size of cursor if custom. None yields default size.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    data\_to\_world(*position*)[#](#napari.layers.Surface.data_to_world "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from data coordinates to world coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in data coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Position in world coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")
    property editable: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.editable "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the current layer data is editable from the viewer.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property extent: Extent[#](#napari.layers.Surface.extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Extent of layer in data and world coordinates.
        For image-like layers, these coordinates are the locations of the
        pixels in Layer.data which are treated like sample points that are
        centered in the rendered version of those pixels.
        For other layers, these coordinates are the points or vertices stored
        in Layer.data.
        Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
    property feature\_defaults: pd.DataFrame[#](#id3 "Link to this definition")
    :   Dataframe-like with one row of feature default values.
        See features for more details on the type of this property.
    property features: pd.DataFrame[#](#id4 "Link to this definition")
    :   Dataframe-like features table.
        It is an implementation detail that this is a pandas.DataFrame. In the future,
        we will target the currently-in-development Data API dataframe protocol [1].
        This will enable us to use alternate libraries such as xarray or cuDF for
        additional features without breaking existing usage of this.
        If you need to specifically rely on the pandas API, please coerce this to a
        pandas.DataFrame using features\_to\_pandas\_dataframe.
        References
    get\_ray\_intersections(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Surface.get_ray_intersections "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the start and end point for the ray extending
        from a point through the data bounding box.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** – the position of the point in nD coordinates. World vs. data
              is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – a unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD coordinates.
              World vs. data is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – a list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – True if the provided coordinates are in world coordinates.
              Default value is True.
        Returns:
        :   * **start\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point closest to the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
            * **end\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point farthest from the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
    get\_status(*position: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *value: Any | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Surface.get_status "Link to this definition")
    :   Status message information of the data at a coordinate position.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
            * **value** (*Any*) – Pre-computed value. In some cases,
        Returns:
        :   **status\_dict** – Dictionary containing a information that can be used as a status update.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    get\_value(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.get_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Value of the data at a position.
        If the layer is not visible, return None.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:
        :   **value** – Value of the data. If the layer is not visible return None.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)"), None

        TRANSFORM allows for manipulation of the layer transform.

    property mouse\_pan: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.mouse_pan "Link to this definition")

    projected\_distance\_from\_mouse\_drag(*start\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *end\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *vector: np.ndarray*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Surface.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse
        clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   * **start\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – Starting point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **end\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – End point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – Vector defining the plane normal of the plane onto which the drag
              vector is projected.
            * **vector** (*np.ndarray*) – (3,) unit vector or (n, 3) array thereof on which to project the drag
              vector from start\_event to end\_event. This argument is defined in data
              coordinates.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – (3,) list of currently displayed dimensions
        Returns:
        :   **projected\_distance**
        Return type:
        :   (1, ) or (n, ) np.ndarray of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property projection\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Surface.projection_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Mode of projection of the thick slice onto the viewed dimensions.
        The sliced data is described by an n-dimensional bounding box (“thick slice”),
        which needs to be projected onto the visible dimensions to be visible.
        The projection mode controls the projection logic.
    refresh(*event: [Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *thumbnail: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *data\_displayed: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *highlight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *extent: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.refresh "Link to this definition")
    :   Refresh all layer data based on current view slice.
    reset\_contrast\_limits(*mode=None*)[#](#napari.layers.Surface.reset_contrast_limits "Link to this definition")
    :   Scale contrast limits to data range
    reset\_contrast\_limits\_range(*mode: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['data', 'slice'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.reset_contrast_limits_range "Link to this definition")
    :   Scale contrast limits range to data type if dtype is an integer,
        or use the current maximum data range otherwise.
        Parameters:
        :   **mode** (*'data'* *or* *'slice'*) – If range should be calculated on whole data, or only visible slice.
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float64]][#](#napari.layers.Surface.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   Rotation matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    save(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Surface.save "Link to this definition")
    :   Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to open. Extensions may be used to
              specify output format (provided a plugin is available for the
              requested format).
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If `None` then all plugins
              corresponding to appropriate hook specification will be looped
              through to find the first one that can save the data.
        Returns:
        :   File paths of any files that were written.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property scale: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Surface.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property scale\_factor[#](#napari.layers.Surface.scale_factor "Link to this definition")
    :   Conversion factor from canvas coordinates to image coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property shear: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Surface.shear "Link to this definition")
    :   Shear matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property thumbnail: npt.NDArray[np.uint8][#](#napari.layers.Surface.thumbnail "Link to this definition")
    :   Integer array of thumbnail for the layer
        Type:
        :   array
    property translate: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Surface.translate "Link to this definition")
    :   Factors to shift the layer by in units of world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property unique\_id: [Hashable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Hashable "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.unique_id "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique ID of the layer.
        This is guaranteed to be unique to this specific layer instance
        over the lifetime of the program.
    property units: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[pint.Unit, ...][#](#id5 "Link to this definition")
    :   List of units for the layer.
    property visible: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Surface.visible "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the visual is currently being displayed.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    world\_to\_data(*position: npt.ArrayLike*) → np.ndarray[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], np.dtype[np.floating]][#](#napari.layers.Surface.world_to_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from world coordinates to data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in world coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Position in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")
