* napari.layers.Tracks
# napari.layers.Tracks[#](#napari-layers-tracks "Link to this heading")
class napari.layers.Tracks(*data*, *\**, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='additive'*, *cache=True*, *color\_by='track\_id'*, *colormap='turbo'*, *colormaps\_dict=None*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *features=None*, *graph=None*, *head\_length: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*, *hide\_completed\_tracks: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *metadata=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *projection\_mode='none'*, *properties=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *tail\_length: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 30*, *tail\_width: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/tracks/tracks.html#Tracks)[#](#napari.layers.Tracks "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Layer`](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")
    Tracks layer.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*array* *(**N**,* *D+1**)*) – Coordinates for N points in D+1 dimensions. ID,T,(Z),Y,X. The first
          axis is the integer ID of the track. D is either 3 or 4 for planar
          or volumetric timeseries respectively.
        * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
          Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
          provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
          If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
        * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
          alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
          {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, and ‘additive’}.
        * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
          Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        * **color\_by** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Track property (from property keys) by which to color vertices.
        * **colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Default colormap to use to set vertex colors. Specialized colormaps,
          relating to specified properties can be passed to the layer via
          colormaps\_dict.
        * **colormaps\_dict** (*dict {str: napari.utils.Colormap}*) – Optional dictionary mapping each property to a colormap for that
          property. This allows each property to be assigned a specific colormap,
          rather than having a global colormap for everything.
        * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
          Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
          Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
        * **features** (*Dataframe-like*) – Features table where each row corresponds to a point and each column
          is a feature.
        * **graph** (*dict {int: list}*) – Graph representing associations between tracks. Dictionary defines the
          mapping between a track ID and the parents of the track. This can be
          one (the track has one parent, and the parent has >=1 child) in the
          case of track splitting, or more than one (the track has multiple
          parents, but only one child) in the case of track merging.
          See examples/tracks\_3d\_with\_graph.py
        * **head\_length** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Length of the positive (forward in time) tails in units of time.
        * **hide\_completed\_tracks** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, tracks that have completed before the current time point are not
          displayed, regardless of the value of tail\_length.
        * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
        * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
          be projected onto the viewed dimenions.
        * **properties** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}**,* *DataFrame*) – Properties for each point. Each property should be an array of length N,
          where N is the number of points.
        * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          np.degrees if needed.
        * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **tail\_length** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Length of the positive (backward in time) tails in units of time.
        * **tail\_width** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Width of the track tails in pixels.
        * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
        * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
          If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
        * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `as_layer_data_tuple`() |  |
    | [`bind_key`](#napari.layers.Tracks.bind_key "napari.layers.Tracks.bind_key")(key\_bind[, func, overwrite]) | Bind a key combination to a keymap. |
    | `block_update_properties`() |  |
    | [`click_plane_from_click_data`](#napari.layers.Tracks.click_plane_from_click_data "napari.layers.Tracks.click_plane_from_click_data")(click\_position, ...) | Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera. |
    | [`create`](#napari.layers.Tracks.create "napari.layers.Tracks.create")(data[, meta, layer\_type]) | Create layer from data of type layer\_type. |
    | [`data_to_world`](#napari.layers.Tracks.data_to_world "napari.layers.Tracks.data_to_world")(position) | Convert from data coordinates to world coordinates. |
    | [`get_ray_intersections`](#napari.layers.Tracks.get_ray_intersections "napari.layers.Tracks.get_ray_intersections")(position, ...[, world]) | Get the start and end point for the ray extending from a point through the data bounding box. |
    | `get_source_str`() |  |
    | [`get_status`](#napari.layers.Tracks.get_status "napari.layers.Tracks.get_status")([position, view\_direction, ...]) | Status message information of the data at a coordinate position. |
    | [`get_value`](#napari.layers.Tracks.get_value "napari.layers.Tracks.get_value")(position, \*[, view\_direction, ...]) | Value of the data at a position. |
    | [`projected_distance_from_mouse_drag`](#napari.layers.Tracks.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "napari.layers.Tracks.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag")(...) | Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates. |
    | [`refresh`](#napari.layers.Tracks.refresh "napari.layers.Tracks.refresh")([event, thumbnail, data\_displayed, ...]) | Refresh all layer data based on current view slice. |
    | [`save`](#napari.layers.Tracks.save "napari.layers.Tracks.save")(path[, plugin]) | Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin. |
    | `set_view_slice`() |  |
    | `update_highlight_visibility`(visible) |  |
    | `update_transform_box_visibility`(visible) |  |
    | [`world_to_data`](#napari.layers.Tracks.world_to_data "napari.layers.Tracks.world_to_data")(position) | Convert from world coordinates to data coordinates. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `ModeCallable` |  |
    | [`affine`](#napari.layers.Tracks.affine "napari.layers.Tracks.affine") | Extra affine transform to go from physical to world coordinates. |
    | [`axis_labels`](#napari.layers.Tracks.axis_labels "napari.layers.Tracks.axis_labels") | tuple of axis labels for the layer. |
    | [`blending`](#napari.layers.Tracks.blending "napari.layers.Tracks.blending") | Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed. |
    | `bounding_box` |  |
    | `class_keymap` |  |
    | `color_by` |  |
    | `colormap` |  |
    | `colormaps_dict` |  |
    | [`current_time`](#napari.layers.Tracks.current_time "napari.layers.Tracks.current_time") | current time according to the first dimension |
    | [`cursor`](#napari.layers.Tracks.cursor "napari.layers.Tracks.cursor") | String identifying cursor displayed over canvas. |
    | [`cursor_size`](#napari.layers.Tracks.cursor_size "napari.layers.Tracks.cursor_size") | Size of cursor if custom. |
    | [`data`](#napari.layers.Tracks.data "napari.layers.Tracks.data") | Coordinates for N points in D+1 dimensions. |
    | [`display_graph`](#napari.layers.Tracks.display_graph "napari.layers.Tracks.display_graph") | display the graph edges |
    | [`display_id`](#napari.layers.Tracks.display_id "napari.layers.Tracks.display_id") | display the track id |
    | [`display_tail`](#napari.layers.Tracks.display_tail "napari.layers.Tracks.display_tail") | display the track tail |
    | [`editable`](#napari.layers.Tracks.editable "napari.layers.Tracks.editable") | Whether the current layer data is editable from the viewer. |
    | `experimental_clipping_planes` |  |
    | [`extent`](#napari.layers.Tracks.extent "napari.layers.Tracks.extent") | Extent of layer in data and world coordinates. |
    | [`features`](#napari.layers.Tracks.features "napari.layers.Tracks.features") | Dataframe-like features table. |
    | [`graph`](#napari.layers.Tracks.graph "napari.layers.Tracks.graph") | Graph representing associations between tracks. |
    | [`graph_connex`](#napari.layers.Tracks.graph_connex "napari.layers.Tracks.graph_connex") | vertex connections for drawing the graph |
    | [`graph_times`](#napari.layers.Tracks.graph_times "napari.layers.Tracks.graph_times") | time points associated with each graph vertex |
    | `head_length` |  |
    | [`help`](#napari.layers.Tracks.help "napari.layers.Tracks.help") | displayed in status bar bottom right. |
    | [`hide_completed_tracks`](#napari.layers.Tracks.hide_completed_tracks "napari.layers.Tracks.hide_completed_tracks") | If True, tracks that have completed before the current time point are not displayed, regardless of the value of tail\_length. |
    | `keymap` |  |
    | [`loaded`](#napari.layers.Tracks.loaded "napari.layers.Tracks.loaded") | Whether the layer has been loaded into memory. |
    | [`locked`](#napari.layers.Tracks.locked "napari.layers.Tracks.locked") | which UI operations are locked on this layer. |
    | [`metadata`](#napari.layers.Tracks.metadata "napari.layers.Tracks.metadata") | Key/value map for user-stored data. |
    | [`mode`](#napari.layers.Tracks.mode "napari.layers.Tracks.mode") | Interactive mode |
    | [`mouse_pan`](#napari.layers.Tracks.mouse_pan "napari.layers.Tracks.mouse_pan") | Determine if canvas interactive panning is enabled with the mouse. |
    | [`mouse_zoom`](#napari.layers.Tracks.mouse_zoom "napari.layers.Tracks.mouse_zoom") | Determine if canvas interactive zooming is enabled with the mouse. |
    | [`name`](#napari.layers.Tracks.name "napari.layers.Tracks.name") | Unique name of the layer. |
    | `name_overlay` |  |
    | [`ndim`](#napari.layers.Tracks.ndim "napari.layers.Tracks.ndim") | Number of dimensions in the data. |
    | [`opacity`](#napari.layers.Tracks.opacity "napari.layers.Tracks.opacity") | Opacity value between 0.0 and 1.0. |
    | [`projection_mode`](#napari.layers.Tracks.projection_mode "napari.layers.Tracks.projection_mode") | Mode of projection of the thick slice onto the viewed dimensions. |
    | [`properties`](#napari.layers.Tracks.properties "napari.layers.Tracks.properties") | Properties for each track. |
    | [`properties_to_color_by`](#napari.layers.Tracks.properties_to_color_by "napari.layers.Tracks.properties_to_color_by") | track properties that can be used for coloring etc... |
    | [`rotate`](#napari.layers.Tracks.rotate "napari.layers.Tracks.rotate") | Rotation matrix in world coordinates. |
    | [`scale`](#napari.layers.Tracks.scale "napari.layers.Tracks.scale") | Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates. |
    | [`scale_factor`](#napari.layers.Tracks.scale_factor "napari.layers.Tracks.scale_factor") | Conversion factor from canvas coordinates to image coordinates. |
    | [`shear`](#napari.layers.Tracks.shear "napari.layers.Tracks.shear") | Shear matrix in world coordinates. |
    | `source` |  |
    | [`tail_length`](#napari.layers.Tracks.tail_length "napari.layers.Tracks.tail_length") | Width for all vectors in pixels. |
    | [`tail_width`](#napari.layers.Tracks.tail_width "napari.layers.Tracks.tail_width") | Width for all vectors in pixels. |
    | [`thumbnail`](#napari.layers.Tracks.thumbnail "napari.layers.Tracks.thumbnail") | Integer array of thumbnail for the layer |
    | [`track_colors`](#napari.layers.Tracks.track_colors "napari.layers.Tracks.track_colors") | Return current vertex colors. |
    | [`track_connex`](#napari.layers.Tracks.track_connex "napari.layers.Tracks.track_connex") | vertex connections for drawing track lines |
    | [`track_labels`](#napari.layers.Tracks.track_labels "napari.layers.Tracks.track_labels") | return track labels at the current time |
    | [`track_times`](#napari.layers.Tracks.track_times "napari.layers.Tracks.track_times") | time points associated with each track vertex |
    | [`translate`](#napari.layers.Tracks.translate "napari.layers.Tracks.translate") | Factors to shift the layer by in units of world coordinates. |
    | [`unique_id`](#napari.layers.Tracks.unique_id "napari.layers.Tracks.unique_id") | Unique ID of the layer. |
    | [`units`](#napari.layers.Tracks.units "napari.layers.Tracks.units") | List of units for the layer. |
    | [`use_fade`](#napari.layers.Tracks.use_fade "napari.layers.Tracks.use_fade") | toggle whether we fade the tail of the track, depending on whether the time dimension is displayed |
    | [`visible`](#napari.layers.Tracks.visible "napari.layers.Tracks.visible") | Whether the visual is currently being displayed. |
    Details
    property affine: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.affine "Link to this definition")
    :   Extra affine transform to go from physical to world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.transforms.Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    bind\_key(*key\_bind: [KeyBinding](https://app-model.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/types/#app_model.types.KeyBinding "(in App Model v0.0.0)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [EllipsisType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/types.html#types.EllipsisType "(in Python v3.14)")*, *func=<object object>*, *\**, *overwrite=False*)[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.bind_key "Link to this definition")
    :   Bind a key combination to a keymap.
        Parameters:
        :   * **keymap** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *str: callable*) – Keymap to modify.
            * **key\_bind** (*keybinding-like* *or* *...*) – Key combination.
              `...` acts as a wildcard if no key combinations can be matched
              in the keymap (this will overwrite all key combinations
              further down the lookup chain).
            * **func** (*callable**,* *None**, or* *...*) – Callable to bind to the key combination.
              If `None` is passed, unbind instead.
              `...` acts as a blocker, effectively unbinding the key
              combination for all keymaps further down the lookup chain.
            * **overwrite** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *keyword-only**,* *optional*) – Whether to overwrite the key combination if it already exists.
        Returns:
        :   **unbound** – Callable unbound by this operation, if any.
        Return type:
        :   callable or None
        Notes
        Key combinations are represented in the form `[modifier-]key`,
        e.g. `a`, `Control-c`, or `Control-Alt-Delete`.
        Valid modifiers are Control, Alt, Shift, and Meta.
        Letters will always be read as upper-case.
        Due to the native implementation of the key system, Shift pressed in
        certain key combinations may yield inconsistent or unexpected results.
        Therefore, it is not recommended to use Shift with non-letter keys. On OSX,
        Control is swapped with Meta such that pressing Command reads as Control.
        Special keys include Shift, Control, Alt, Meta, Up, Down, Left, Right,
        PageUp, PageDown, Insert, Delete, Home, End, Escape, Backspace, F1,
        F2, F3, F4, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10, F11, F12, Space, Enter, and Tab
        Functions take in only one argument: the parent that the function
        was bound to.
        By default, all functions are assumed to work on key presses only,
        but can be denoted to work on release too by separating the function
        into two statements with the yield keyword:
        ```
        @viewer.bind_key('h')
        def hello_world(viewer):
            # on key press
            viewer.status = 'hello world!'
            yield
            # on key release
            viewer.status = 'goodbye world :('
        ```
        To create a keymap that will block others, `` bind_key(..., ...)` ``.
    property blending: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.blending "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed.
        Blending.OPAQUE
        :   Allows for only the top layer to be visible and corresponds to
            depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False, blend=False.
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity
            and corresponds to depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False,
            blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT\_NO\_DEPTH
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity, but
            no depth testing is performed. Corresponds to `depth_test=False`,
            cull\_face=False, blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.ADDITIVE
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together with
            different colors and opacity. Useful for creating overlays. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one’), and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.MINIMUM
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together such that
            the minimum of each RGB component and alpha are selected.
            Useful for creating overlays with inverted colormaps. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_equation=(‘min’).
        Type:
        :   Blending mode
    click\_plane\_from\_click\_data(*click\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray, np.ndarray][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.click_plane_from_click_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data
        coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera.
        Parameters:
        :   * **click\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – click position in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – view direction in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – dimensions of the data array currently in view.
        Returns:
        :   **click\_plane** – tuple of (plane\_position, plane\_normal) in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
    classmethod create(*data: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *meta: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *layer\_type: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.create "Link to this definition")
    :   Create layer from data of type layer\_type.
        Primarily intended for usage by reader plugin hooks and creating a
        layer from an unwrapped layer data tuple.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*Any*) – Data in a format that is valid for the corresponding layer\_type.
            * **meta** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dict of keyword arguments that will be passed to the corresponding
              layer constructor. If any keys in meta are not valid for the
              corresponding layer type, an exception will be raised.
            * **layer\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Type of layer to add. Must be the (case insensitive) name of a
              Layer subclass. If not provided, the layer is assumed to
              be “image”, unless data.dtype is one of (np.int32, np.uint32,
              np.int64, np.uint64), in which case it is assumed to be “labels”.
        Raises:
        :   * [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If `layer_type` is not one of the recognized layer types.
            * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If any keyword arguments in `meta` are unexpected for the
              corresponding add\_\* method for this layer\_type.
        Examples
        A typical use case might be to upack a tuple of layer data with a
        specified layer\_type.
        ```
        >>> data = (
        ...     np.random.random((10, 2)) * 20,
        ...     {'face_color': 'blue'},
        ...     'points',
        ... )
        >>> Layer.create(*data)
        ```

    property extent: Extent[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Extent of layer in data and world coordinates.
        For image-like layers, these coordinates are the locations of the
        pixels in Layer.data which are treated like sample points that are
        centered in the rendered version of those pixels.
        For other layers, these coordinates are the points or vertices stored
        in Layer.data.
        Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
    property features: pd.DataFrame[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.features "Link to this definition")
    :   Dataframe-like features table.
        It is an implementation detail that this is a pandas.DataFrame. In the future,
        we will target the currently-in-development Data API dataframe protocol [1].
        This will enable us to use alternate libraries such as xarray or cuDF for
        additional features without breaking existing usage of this.
        If you need to specifically rely on the pandas API, please coerce this to a
        pandas.DataFrame using features\_to\_pandas\_dataframe.
        References
    get\_ray\_intersections(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.get_ray_intersections "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the start and end point for the ray extending
        from a point through the data bounding box.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** – the position of the point in nD coordinates. World vs. data
              is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – a unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD coordinates.
              World vs. data is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – a list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – True if the provided coordinates are in world coordinates.
              Default value is True.
        Returns:
        :   * **start\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point closest to the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
            * **end\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point farthest from the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
    get\_status(*position: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *value: Any | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.get_status "Link to this definition")
    :   Status message information of the data at a coordinate position.
        Parameters:

            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
            * **value** (*Any*) – Pre-computed value. In some cases,
        Returns:
        :   **status\_dict** – Dictionary containing a information that can be used as a status update.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    get\_value(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.get_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Value of the data at a position.
        If the layer is not visible, return None.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:

        Return type:

    :   Number of dimensions in the data.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    property opacity: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.opacity "Link to this definition")
    :   Opacity value between 0.0 and 1.0.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    projected\_distance\_from\_mouse\_drag(*start\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *end\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *vector: np.ndarray*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse
        clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   * **start\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – Starting point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **end\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – End point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – Vector defining the plane normal of the plane onto which the drag
              vector is projected.
            * **vector** (*np.ndarray*) – (3,) unit vector or (n, 3) array thereof on which to project the drag
              vector from start\_event to end\_event. This argument is defined in data
              coordinates.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – (3,) list of currently displayed dimensions
        Returns:
        :   **projected\_distance**
        Return type:
        :   (1, ) or (n, ) np.ndarray of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property projection\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.projection_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Mode of projection of the thick slice onto the viewed dimensions.
        The sliced data is described by an n-dimensional bounding box (“thick slice”),
        which needs to be projected onto the visible dimensions to be visible.
        The projection mode controls the projection logic.
    property properties: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.properties "Link to this definition")
    :   Properties for each track.
        Type:
        :   dict {str
        Type:
        :   np.ndarray (N,)}
    property properties\_to\_color\_by: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.properties_to_color_by "Link to this definition")
    :   track properties that can be used for coloring etc…
    refresh(*event: [Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *thumbnail: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *data\_displayed: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *highlight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *extent: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.refresh "Link to this definition")
    :   Refresh all layer data based on current view slice.
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float64]][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   Rotation matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    save(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.save "Link to this definition")
    :   Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to open. Extensions may be used to
              specify output format (provided a plugin is available for the
              requested format).
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If `None` then all plugins
              corresponding to appropriate hook specification will be looped
              through to find the first one that can save the data.
        Returns:
        :   File paths of any files that were written.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property scale: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property scale\_factor[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.scale_factor "Link to this definition")
    :   Conversion factor from canvas coordinates to image coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property shear: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.shear "Link to this definition")
    :   Shear matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property tail\_length: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.tail_length "Link to this definition")
    :   Width for all vectors in pixels.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property tail\_width: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.tail_width "Link to this definition")
    :   Width for all vectors in pixels.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property thumbnail: npt.NDArray[np.uint8][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.thumbnail "Link to this definition")
    :   Integer array of thumbnail for the layer
        Type:
        :   array
    property track\_colors: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.track_colors "Link to this definition")
    :   Return current vertex colors.
    property track\_connex: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.track_connex "Link to this definition")
    :   vertex connections for drawing track lines
    property track\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.track_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   return track labels at the current time
    property track\_times: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.track_times "Link to this definition")
    :   time points associated with each track vertex
    property translate: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.translate "Link to this definition")
    :   Factors to shift the layer by in units of world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property unique\_id: [Hashable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Hashable "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.unique_id "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique ID of the layer.
        This is guaranteed to be unique to this specific layer instance
        over the lifetime of the program.
    property units: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[pint.Unit, ...][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.units "Link to this definition")
    :   List of units for the layer.
    property use\_fade: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.use_fade "Link to this definition")
    :   toggle whether we fade the tail of the track, depending on whether
        the time dimension is displayed
    property visible: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Tracks.visible "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the visual is currently being displayed.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    world\_to\_data(*position: npt.ArrayLike*) → np.ndarray[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], np.dtype[np.floating]][#](#napari.layers.Tracks.world_to_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from world coordinates to data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in world coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Position in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")

* napari.layers.Vectors
# napari.layers.Vectors[#](#napari-layers-vectors "Link to this heading")
class napari.layers.Vectors(*data=None*, *\**, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *cache=True*, *edge\_color='red'*, *edge\_color\_cycle=None*, *edge\_colormap='viridis'*, *edge\_contrast\_limits=None*, *edge\_width=1*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *feature\_defaults=None*, *features=None*, *length=1*, *metadata=None*, *name=None*, *ndim=None*, *opacity=0.7*, *out\_of\_slice\_display=False*, *projection\_mode='all'*, *properties=None*, *property\_choices=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *vector\_style='triangle'*, *visible=True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/vectors/vectors.html#Vectors)[#](#napari.layers.Vectors "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Layer`](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")
    Vectors layer renders lines onto the canvas.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*(**N**,* *2**,* *D**) or* *(**N1**,* *N2**,* *...**,* *ND**,* *D**)* *array*) – An (N, 2, D) array is interpreted as “coordinate-like” data and a
          list of N vectors with start point and projections of the vector in
          D dimensions. An (N1, N2, …, ND, D) array is interpreted as
          “image-like” data where there is a length D vector of the
          projections at each pixel.
        * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
          Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
          provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
          If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
        * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
          alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
          {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, and ‘additive’}.
        * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
          Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        * **edge\_color** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Color of all of the vectors.
        * **edge\_color\_cycle** (*np.ndarray**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to edge\_color if a
          categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
        * **edge\_colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")) – Colormap to set vector color if a continuous attribute is used to set edge\_color.
        * **edge\_contrast\_limits** (*None**,* *(*[*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
          of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
          The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
          (property.min(), property.max())
        * **edge\_width** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Width for all vectors in pixels.
        * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
          Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
          Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
        * **feature\_defaults** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Any**] or* *DataFrame*) – The default value of each feature in a table with one row.
        * **features** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**] or* *DataFrame*) – Features table where each row corresponds to a vector and each column
          is a feature.
        * **length** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Multiplicative factor on projections for length of all vectors.
        * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
        * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Number of dimensions for vectors. When data is not None, ndim must be D.
          An empty vectors layer can be instantiated with arbitrary ndim.
        * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        * **out\_of\_slice\_display** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, renders vectors not just in central plane but also slightly out of slice
          according to specified point marker size.
        * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
          be projected onto the viewed dimenions.
        * **properties** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}**,* *DataFrame*) – Properties for each vector. Each property should be an array of length N,
          where N is the number of vectors.
        * **property\_choices** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}*) – possible values for each property.
        * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          np.degrees if needed.
        * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
        * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
          If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
        * **vector\_style** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset display modes that determines how vectors are displayed.
          Allowed values are {‘line’, ‘triangle’, and ‘arrow’}.
        * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
    data[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.data "Link to this definition")
    :   The start point and projections of N vectors in D dimensions.
        Type:
        :   (N, 2, D) array
    axis\_labels[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimension names of the layer data.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    features[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.features "Link to this definition")
    :   Features table where each row corresponds to a vector and each column
        is a feature.
        Type:
        :   Dataframe-like
    feature\_defaults[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.feature_defaults "Link to this definition")
    :   Stores the default value of each feature in a table with one row.
        Type:
        :   DataFrame-like
    properties[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.properties "Link to this definition")
    :   Properties for each vector. Each property should be an array of length N,
        where N is the number of vectors.
        Type:
        :   dict {str: array (N,)}, DataFrame
    edge\_width[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.edge_width "Link to this definition")
    :   Width for all vectors in pixels.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    vector\_style[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.vector_style "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how vectors are displayed.
        * `VectorStyle.LINE`:
          :   Vectors are displayed as lines.
        * `VectorStyle.TRIANGLE`:
          :   Vectors are displayed as triangles.
        * `VectorStyle.ARROW`:
          :   Vectors are displayed as arrows.
        Type:
        :   VectorStyle
    length[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.length "Link to this definition")
    :   Multiplicative factor on projections for length of all vectors.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    edge\_color[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.edge_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Color of all of the vectors.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    edge\_color\_cycle[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.edge_color_cycle "Link to this definition")
    :   Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to edge\_color if a
        categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
        Type:
        :   np.ndarray, [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    edge\_colormap[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.edge_colormap "Link to this definition")
    :   Colormap to set vector color if a continuous attribute is used to set edge\_color.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [napari.utils.Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")
    edge\_contrast\_limits[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.edge_contrast_limits "Link to this definition")
    :   clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
        of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
        The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
        (property.min(), property.max())
        Type:
        :   None, ([float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"))
    out\_of\_slice\_display[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.out_of_slice_display "Link to this definition")
    :   If True, renders vectors not just in central plane but also slightly out of slice
        according to specified point marker size.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    units[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.units "Link to this definition")
    :   Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of pint.Unit
    Notes
    \_view\_data(M, 2, 2) array
    :   The start point and projections of N vectors in 2D for vectors whose
        start point is in the currently viewed slice.
    \_view\_face\_color(M, 4) np.ndarray
    :   colors for the M in view vectors
    \_view\_indices(1, M) array
    :   indices for the M in view vectors
    \_view\_alphas(M,) or float
    :   relative opacity for the M in view vectors
    \_property\_choicesdict {str: array (N,)}
    :   Possible values for the properties in Vectors.properties.
    \_max\_vectors\_thumbnailint
    :   The maximum number of vectors that will ever be used to render the
        thumbnail. If more vectors are present then they are randomly
        subsampled.

    Details
    property affine: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.affine "Link to this definition")
    :   Extra affine transform to go from physical to world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.transforms.Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#id0 "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    bind\_key(*key\_bind: [KeyBinding](https://app-model.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/types/#app_model.types.KeyBinding "(in App Model v0.0.0)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [EllipsisType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/types.html#types.EllipsisType "(in Python v3.14)")*, *func=<object object>*, *\**, *overwrite=False*)[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.bind_key "Link to this definition")
    :   Bind a key combination to a keymap.
        Parameters:
        :   * **keymap** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *str: callable*) – Keymap to modify.
            * **key\_bind** (*keybinding-like* *or* *...*) – Key combination.
              `...` acts as a wildcard if no key combinations can be matched
              in the keymap (this will overwrite all key combinations
              further down the lookup chain).
            * **func** (*callable**,* *None**, or* *...*) – Callable to bind to the key combination.
              If `None` is passed, unbind instead.
              `...` acts as a blocker, effectively unbinding the key
              combination for all keymaps further down the lookup chain.
            * **overwrite** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *keyword-only**,* *optional*) – Whether to overwrite the key combination if it already exists.
        Returns:
        :   **unbound** – Callable unbound by this operation, if any.
        Return type:
        :   callable or None
        Notes
        Key combinations are represented in the form `[modifier-]key`,
        e.g. `a`, `Control-c`, or `Control-Alt-Delete`.
        Valid modifiers are Control, Alt, Shift, and Meta.
        Letters will always be read as upper-case.
        Due to the native implementation of the key system, Shift pressed in
        certain key combinations may yield inconsistent or unexpected results.
        Therefore, it is not recommended to use Shift with non-letter keys. On OSX,
        Control is swapped with Meta such that pressing Command reads as Control.
        Special keys include Shift, Control, Alt, Meta, Up, Down, Left, Right,
        PageUp, PageDown, Insert, Delete, Home, End, Escape, Backspace, F1,
        F2, F3, F4, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10, F11, F12, Space, Enter, and Tab
        Functions take in only one argument: the parent that the function
        was bound to.
        By default, all functions are assumed to work on key presses only,
        but can be denoted to work on release too by separating the function
        into two statements with the yield keyword:
        ```
        @viewer.bind_key('h')
        def hello_world(viewer):
            # on key press
            viewer.status = 'hello world!'
            yield
            # on key release
            viewer.status = 'goodbye world :('
        ```
        To create a keymap that will block others, `` bind_key(..., ...)` ``.
    property blending: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.blending "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how RGB and alpha values get mixed.
        Blending.OPAQUE
        :   Allows for only the top layer to be visible and corresponds to
            depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False, blend=False.
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity
            and corresponds to depth\_test=True, cull\_face=False,
            blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.TRANSLUCENT\_NO\_DEPTH
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended with different opacity, but
            no depth testing is performed. Corresponds to `depth_test=False`,
            cull\_face=False, blend=True, blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one\_minus\_src\_alpha’),
            and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.ADDITIVE
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together with
            different colors and opacity. Useful for creating overlays. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_func=(‘src\_alpha’, ‘one’), and blend\_equation=(‘func\_add’).
        Blending.MINIMUM
        :   Allows for multiple layers to be blended together such that
            the minimum of each RGB component and alpha are selected.
            Useful for creating overlays with inverted colormaps. It
            corresponds to depth\_test=False, cull\_face=False, blend=True,
            blend\_equation=(‘min’).
        Type:
        :   Blending mode
    click\_plane\_from\_click\_data(*click\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray, np.ndarray][#](#napari.layers.Vectors.click_plane_from_click_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate a (point, normal) plane parallel to the canvas in data
        coordinates, centered on the centre of rotation of the camera.
        Parameters:
        :   * **click\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – click position in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – view direction in world coordinates from mouse event.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – dimensions of the data array currently in view.
        Returns:
        :   **click\_plane** – tuple of (plane\_position, plane\_normal) in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
    classmethod create(*data: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *meta: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *layer\_type: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.create "Link to this definition")
    :   Create layer from data of type layer\_type.
        Primarily intended for usage by reader plugin hooks and creating a
        layer from an unwrapped layer data tuple.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*Any*) – Data in a format that is valid for the corresponding layer\_type.
            * **meta** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dict of keyword arguments that will be passed to the corresponding
              layer constructor. If any keys in meta are not valid for the
              corresponding layer type, an exception will be raised.
            * **layer\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Type of layer to add. Must be the (case insensitive) name of a
              Layer subclass. If not provided, the layer is assumed to
              be “image”, unless data.dtype is one of (np.int32, np.uint32,
              np.int64, np.uint64), in which case it is assumed to be “labels”.
        Raises:
        :   * [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If `layer_type` is not one of the recognized layer types.
            * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If any keyword arguments in `meta` are unexpected for the
              corresponding add\_\* method for this layer\_type.
        Examples
        A typical use case might be to upack a tuple of layer data with a
        specified layer\_type.
        ```
        >>> data = (
        ...     np.random.random((10, 2)) * 20,
        ...     {'face_color': 'blue'},
        ...     'points',
        ... )
        >>> Layer.create(*data)
        ```

    :   start point and projections of vectors.

    property edge\_color\_cycle: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[#](#id3 "Link to this definition")
    :   Color cycle for edge\_color.
        Can be a list of colors defined by name, RGB or RGBA
        Type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)"), np.ndarray
    property edge\_color\_mode: ColorMode[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.edge_color_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Edge color setting mode
        DIRECT (default mode) allows each vector to be set arbitrarily
        CYCLE allows the color to be set via a color cycle over an attribute
        COLORMAP allows color to be set via a color map over an attribute
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property edge\_colormap: [Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.colormaps.colormap.Colormap")[#](#id4 "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the colormap to be applied to a property to get the edge color.
