        Return type:
        :   [napari.utils.Colormap](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")
    property edge\_contrast\_limits: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#id5 "Link to this definition")
    :   contrast limits for mapping
        the edge\_color colormap property to 0 and 1
        Type:
        :   None, ([float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"))
    property edge\_width: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id6 "Link to this definition")
    :   Width for all vectors in pixels.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property editable: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.editable "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the current layer data is editable from the viewer.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property extent: Extent[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Extent of layer in data and world coordinates.
        For image-like layers, these coordinates are the locations of the
        pixels in Layer.data which are treated like sample points that are
        centered in the rendered version of those pixels.
        For other layers, these coordinates are the points or vertices stored
        in Layer.data.
        Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
    property feature\_defaults[#](#id7 "Link to this definition")
    :   Dataframe-like with one row of feature default values.
        See features for more details on the type of this property.
    property features[#](#id8 "Link to this definition")
    :   Dataframe-like features table.
        It is an implementation detail that this is a pandas.DataFrame. In the future,
        we will target the currently-in-development Data API dataframe protocol [1].
        This will enable us to use alternate libraries such as xarray or cuDF for
        additional features without breaking existing usage of this.
        If you need to specifically rely on the pandas API, please coerce this to a
        pandas.DataFrame using features\_to\_pandas\_dataframe.
        References
    get\_ray\_intersections(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), np.ndarray | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Vectors.get_ray_intersections "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the start and end point for the ray extending
        from a point through the data bounding box.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** – the position of the point in nD coordinates. World vs. data
              is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – a unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD coordinates.
              World vs. data is set by the world keyword argument.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – a list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – True if the provided coordinates are in world coordinates.
              Default value is True.
        Returns:
        :   * **start\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point closest to the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
            * **end\_point** (*np.ndarray*) – The point on the axis-aligned data bounding box that the cursor click
              intersects with. This is the point farthest from the camera.
              The point is the full nD coordinates of the layer data.
              If the click does not intersect the axis-aligned data bounding box,
              None is returned.
    get\_status(*position: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *value: Any | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Vectors.get_status "Link to this definition")
    :   Status message information of the data at a coordinate position.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
            * **value** (*Any*) – Pre-computed value. In some cases,
        Returns:
        :   **status\_dict** – Dictionary containing a information that can be used as a status update.
        Return type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    get\_value(*position: npt.ArrayLike*, *\**, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *world: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.get_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Value of the data at a position.
        If the layer is not visible, return None.
        Parameters:
        :   * **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Position in either data or world coordinates.
            * **view\_direction** (*Optional**[**np.ndarray**]*) – A unit vector giving the direction of the ray in nD world coordinates.
              The default value is None.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*Optional**[**List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]*) – A list of the dimensions currently being displayed in the viewer.
              The default value is None.
            * **world** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True the position is taken to be in world coordinates
              and converted into data coordinates. False by default.
        Returns:
        :   **value** – Value of the data. If the layer is not visible return None.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)"), None
    property help: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.help "Link to this definition")
    :   displayed in status bar bottom right.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property length: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id9 "Link to this definition")
    :   Multiplicative factor for length of all vectors.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property loaded: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.loaded "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the layer has been loaded into memory.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property locked: [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.base._base_constants.LayerLock")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.locked "Link to this definition")
    :   which UI operations are locked on this layer.
        `bool(layer.locked)` is truthy when any lock flag is set. Setting
        `True`/`False` is equivalent to `LayerLock.ALL`/`LayerLock.NONE`.
        Type:
        :   [LayerLock](napari.layers.LayerLock.html#napari.layers.LayerLock "napari.layers.LayerLock")
    property metadata: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.metadata "Link to this definition")
    :   Key/value map for user-stored data.
    property mode: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Interactive mode
        Interactive mode. The normal, default mode is PAN\_ZOOM, which
        allows for normal interactivity with the canvas.
        TRANSFORM allows for manipulation of the layer transform.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property mouse\_pan: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.mouse_pan "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive panning is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property mouse\_zoom: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.mouse_zoom "Link to this definition")
    :   Determine if canvas interactive zooming is enabled with the mouse.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    property name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.name "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique name of the layer.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property ndim: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Number of dimensions in the data.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    property opacity: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.opacity "Link to this definition")
    :   Opacity value between 0.0 and 1.0.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property out\_of\_slice\_display: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id10 "Link to this definition")
    :   renders vectors slightly out of slice.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    projected\_distance\_from\_mouse\_drag(*start\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *end\_position: npt.ArrayLike*, *view\_direction: npt.ArrayLike*, *vector: np.ndarray*, *dims\_displayed: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.projected_distance_from_mouse_drag "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate the length of the projection of a line between two mouse
        clicks onto a vector (or array of vectors) in data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   * **start\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – Starting point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **end\_position** (*np.ndarray*) – End point of the drag vector in data coordinates
            * **view\_direction** (*np.ndarray*) – Vector defining the plane normal of the plane onto which the drag
              vector is projected.
            * **vector** (*np.ndarray*) – (3,) unit vector or (n, 3) array thereof on which to project the drag
              vector from start\_event to end\_event. This argument is defined in data
              coordinates.
            * **dims\_displayed** (*List**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – (3,) list of currently displayed dimensions
        Returns:
        :   **projected\_distance**
        Return type:
        :   (1, ) or (n, ) np.ndarray of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property projection\_mode[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.projection_mode "Link to this definition")
    :   Mode of projection of the thick slice onto the viewed dimensions.
        The sliced data is described by an n-dimensional bounding box (“thick slice”),
        which needs to be projected onto the visible dimensions to be visible.
        The projection mode controls the projection logic.
    property properties: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")][#](#id11 "Link to this definition")
    :   Annotations for each point
        Type:
        :   dict {str
        Type:
        :   array (N,)}, DataFrame
    refresh(*event: [Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\**, *thumbnail: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *data\_displayed: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *highlight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *extent: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.refresh "Link to this definition")
    :   Refresh all layer data based on current view slice.
    refresh\_colors(*update\_color\_mapping: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/layers/vectors/vectors.html#Vectors.refresh_colors)[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.refresh_colors "Link to this definition")
    :   Calculate and update edge colors if using a cycle or color map
        Parameters:
        :   **update\_color\_mapping** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If set to True, the function will recalculate the color cycle map
            or colormap (whichever is being used). If set to False, the function
            will use the current color cycle map or color map. For example, if you
            are adding/modifying vectors and want them to be colored with the same
            mapping as the other vectors (i.e., the new vectors shouldn’t affect
            the color cycle map or colormap), set update\_color\_mapping=False.
            Default value is False.
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float64]][#](#napari.layers.Vectors.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   Rotation matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    save(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.layers.Vectors.save "Link to this definition")
    :   Save this layer to `path` with default (or specified) plugin.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to open. Extensions may be used to
              specify output format (provided a plugin is available for the
              requested format).
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If `None` then all plugins
              corresponding to appropriate hook specification will be looped
              through to find the first one that can save the data.
        Returns:
        :   File paths of any files that were written.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    property scale: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Anisotropy factors to scale data into world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property scale\_factor[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.scale_factor "Link to this definition")
    :   Conversion factor from canvas coordinates to image coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    property shear: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.shear "Link to this definition")
    :   Shear matrix in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property thumbnail: npt.NDArray[np.uint8][#](#napari.layers.Vectors.thumbnail "Link to this definition")
    :   Integer array of thumbnail for the layer
        Type:
        :   array
    property translate: npt.NDArray[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.translate "Link to this definition")
    :   Factors to shift the layer by in units of world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   array
    property unique\_id: [Hashable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Hashable "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.unique_id "Link to this definition")
    :   Unique ID of the layer.
        This is guaranteed to be unique to this specific layer instance
        over the lifetime of the program.
    property units: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[pint.Unit, ...][#](#id12 "Link to this definition")
    :   List of units for the layer.
    property vector\_style: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id13 "Link to this definition")
    :   Determines how vectors are displayed.
        VectorStyle.LINE
        :   Displays vectors as rectangular lines.
        VectorStyle.TRIANGLE
        :   Displays vectors as triangles.
        VectorStyle.ARROW
        :   Displays vectors as arrows.
        Type:
        :   Vectors display mode
    property visible: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.layers.Vectors.visible "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether the visual is currently being displayed.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    world\_to\_data(*position: npt.ArrayLike*) → np.ndarray[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], np.dtype[np.floating]][#](#napari.layers.Vectors.world_to_data "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert from world coordinates to data coordinates.
        Parameters:
        :   **position** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *1D array*) – Position in world coordinates. If longer then the
            number of dimensions of the layer, the later
            dimensions will be used.
        Returns:
        :   Position in data coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")

v3.14)"), [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['graph', 'image', 'labels', 'points', 'shapes', 'surface', 'tracks', 'vectors']]]][[source]](../_modules/napari/types.html#image_reader_to_layerdata_reader)[#](#napari.types.image_reader_to_layerdata_reader "Link to this definition")
:   Convert a PathLike -> ArrayLike function to a PathLike -> LayerData.
    Deprecated since version 0.7.1: This helper is deprecated and will be removed in 0.8.0.
    Parameters:
    :   **func** (*Callable**[**[**PathLike**]**,* *ArrayLike**]*) – A function that accepts a string or list of strings, and returns an
        ArrayLike.
    Returns:
    :   **reader\_function** – A function that accepts a string or list of strings, and returns data
        as a list of LayerData: List[Tuple[ArrayLike]]
    Return type:
    :   Callable[[PathLike], List[LayerData]]

# napari.types.SampleDict[#](#napari-types-sampledict "Link to this heading")
class napari.types.SampleDict[[source]](../_modules/napari/types.html#SampleDict)[#](#napari.types.SampleDict "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `TypedDict`
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`clear`](#napari.types.SampleDict.clear "napari.types.SampleDict.clear")() |  |
    | [`copy`](#napari.types.SampleDict.copy "napari.types.SampleDict.copy")() |  |
    | [`fromkeys`](#napari.types.SampleDict.fromkeys "napari.types.SampleDict.fromkeys")(iterable[, value]) | Create a new dictionary with keys from iterable and values set to value. |
    | [`get`](#napari.types.SampleDict.get "napari.types.SampleDict.get")(key[, default]) | Return the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default. |
    | [`items`](#napari.types.SampleDict.items "napari.types.SampleDict.items")() |  |
    | [`keys`](#napari.types.SampleDict.keys "napari.types.SampleDict.keys")() |  |
    | [`pop`](#napari.types.SampleDict.pop "napari.types.SampleDict.pop")(k[,d]) | If the key is not found, return the default if given; otherwise, raise a KeyError. |
    | [`popitem`](#napari.types.SampleDict.popitem "napari.types.SampleDict.popitem")() | Remove and return a (key, value) pair as a 2-tuple. |
    | [`setdefault`](#napari.types.SampleDict.setdefault "napari.types.SampleDict.setdefault")(key[, default]) | Insert key with a value of default if key is not in the dictionary. |
    | [`update`](#napari.types.SampleDict.update "napari.types.SampleDict.update")([E, ]\*\*F) | If E is present and has a .keys() method, then does: for k in E: D[k] = E[k] If E is present and lacks a .keys() method, then does: for k, v in E: D[k] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k in F: D[k] = F[k] |
    | [`values`](#napari.types.SampleDict.values "napari.types.SampleDict.values")() |  |
    Attributes
    Details
    clear() → None.  Remove all items from D.[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.clear "Link to this definition")
    copy() → a shallow copy of D[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.copy "Link to this definition")
    classmethod fromkeys(*iterable*, *value=None*, */*)[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.fromkeys "Link to this definition")
    :   Create a new dictionary with keys from iterable and values set to value.
    get(*key*, *default=None*, */*)[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.get "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default.
    items() → a set-like object providing a view on D's items[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.items "Link to this definition")
    keys() → a set-like object providing a view on D's keys[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.keys "Link to this definition")
    pop(*k*[, *d*]) → v, remove specified key and return the corresponding value.[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   If the key is not found, return the default if given; otherwise,
        raise a KeyError.
    popitem()[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.popitem "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove and return a (key, value) pair as a 2-tuple.
        Pairs are returned in LIFO (last-in, first-out) order.
        Raises KeyError if the dict is empty.
    setdefault(*key*, *default=None*, */*)[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.setdefault "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert key with a value of default if key is not in the dictionary.
        Return the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default.
    update([*E*, ]*\*\*F*) → None.  Update D from dict/iterable E and F.[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.update "Link to this definition")
    :   If E is present and has a .keys() method, then does: for k in E: D[k] = E[k]
        If E is present and lacks a .keys() method, then does: for k, v in E: D[k] = v
        In either case, this is followed by: for k in F: D[k] = F[k]
    values() → an object providing a view on D's values[#](#napari.types.SampleDict.values "Link to this definition")

# napari.utils[#](#module-napari.utils "Link to this heading")
Classes
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`Colormap`](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap") | Colormap that relates intensity values to colors. |
| [`CyclicLabelColormap`](napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.html#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap "napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap") | Color cycle with a background value. |
| [`DirectLabelColormap`](napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.html#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap "napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap") | Colormap using a direct mapping from labels to color using a dict. |
| [`NotebookScreenshot`](napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot.html#napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot "napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot") | Display a napari screenshot in a jupyter notebook. |
| [`cancelable_progress`](napari.utils.cancelable_progress.html#napari.utils.cancelable_progress "napari.utils.cancelable_progress") | This class inherits from progress, providing the additional ability to cancel expensive executions. |
| [`progress`](napari.utils.progress.html#napari.utils.progress "napari.utils.progress") | This class inherits from tqdm and provides an interface for progress bars in the napari viewer. |
Functions
napari.utils.nbscreenshot(*viewer*, *\**, *canvas\_only=False*, *alt\_text=None*) → [NotebookScreenshot](napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot.html#napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot "napari.utils.notebook_display.NotebookScreenshot")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/notebook_display.html#nbscreenshot)[#](#napari.utils.nbscreenshot "Link to this definition")
:   Display a napari screenshot in a Jupyter notebook.
    This is a convenience wrapper around NotebookScreenshot for the common
    use case of returning a rich display object directly from a notebook cell.
    Parameters:
    :   * **viewer** ([*napari.Viewer*](napari.Viewer.html#napari.Viewer "napari.Viewer")) – The napari viewer.
        * **canvas\_only** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – If False include the napari viewer frame in the screenshot,
          and if True then take screenshot of just the image display canvas.
          By default, False.
        * **alt\_text** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Image description alternative text, for screenreader accessibility.
          Good alt-text describes the image and any text within the image
          in no more than three short, complete sentences.
          By default, None.
    Returns:
    :   A rich display object that renders as a PNG in notebook frontends.
    Return type:
    :   [NotebookScreenshot](napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot.html#napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot "napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot")
    Examples
    ```
    >>> import napari
    >>> from napari.utils import nbscreenshot
    >>> from skimage.data import chelsea
    ```
    ```
    >>> viewer = napari.Viewer()
    >>> viewer.add_image(chelsea(), name='chelsea-the-cat')
    >>> nbscreenshot(viewer)
    >>> # screenshot just the canvas without the napari viewer framing it
    >>> nbscreenshot(viewer, canvas_only=True)
    ```
napari.utils.progrange(*\*args: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\*\*kwargs: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [progress](napari.utils.progress.html#napari.utils.progress "napari.utils.progress.progress")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#progrange)[#](#napari.utils.progrange "Link to this definition")
:   Shorthand for `progress(range(*args), **kwargs)`.
    Adds tqdm based progress bar to napari viewer, if it
    exists, and returns the wrapped range object.
    Returns:
    :   wrapped range object
    Return type:
    :   [progress](napari.utils.progress.html#napari.utils.progress "napari.utils.progress")
napari.utils.resize\_dask\_cache(*nbytes: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *mem\_fraction: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → Cache[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/_dask_utils.html#resize_dask_cache)[#](#napari.utils.resize_dask_cache "Link to this definition")
:   Create or resize the dask cache used for opportunistic caching.
    The cache object is an instance of a `Cache`, (which
    wraps a `cachey.Cache`).
    See [Dask opportunistic caching](https://docs.dask.org/en/latest/caching.html)
    Parameters:
    :   * **nbytes** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The desired size of the cache, in bytes. If `None`, the cache size
          will autodetermined as fraction of the total memory in the system,
          using `mem_fraction`. If `nbytes` is 0. The cache is turned off.
          by default, cache size is autodetermined using `mem_fraction`.
        * **mem\_fraction** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The fraction (from 0 to 1) of total memory to use for the dask cache.
    Returns:
    :   **dask\_cache** – An instance of a Dask Cache
    Return type:
    :   dask.cache.Cache
    Examples
    ```
    >>> from napari.utils import resize_dask_cache
    >>> cache = resize_dask_cache()  # use 25% of total memory by default
    ```
    ```
    >>> # dask.Cache wraps cachey.Cache
    >>> assert isinstance(cache.cache, cachey.Cache)
    ```
    ```
    >>> # useful attributes
    >>> cache.cache.available_bytes  # full size of cache
    >>> cache.cache.total_bytes   # currently used bytes
    ```
napari.utils.sys\_info(*as\_html: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/info.html#sys_info)[#](#napari.utils.sys_info "Link to this definition")
:   Gathers relevant module versions for troubleshooting purposes.
    Parameters:
    :   **as\_html** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – if True, info will be returned as HTML, suitable for a QTextEdit widget

* napari.utils.Colormap
# napari.utils.Colormap[#](#napari-utils-colormap "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.Colormap(*colors, display\_name: str | None = None, \*, name: str = 'custom', interpolation: ~napari.utils.colormaps.colormap.ColormapInterpolationMode = ColormapInterpolationMode.LINEAR, controls: ~napari.utils.events.custom\_types.Array = <factory>, nan\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue = ColorValue([0., 0., 0., 0.], dtype=float32), high\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue | None = None, low\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue | None = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/colormaps/colormap.html#Colormap)[#](#napari.utils.Colormap "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`EventedModel`](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel")
    Colormap that relates intensity values to colors.
    colors[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.colors "Link to this definition")
    :   Data used in the colormap.
        Type:
        :   array, shape (N, 4)
    name[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.name "Link to this definition")
    :   Name of the colormap.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    \_display\_name[#](#napari.utils.Colormap._display_name "Link to this definition")
    :   Display name of the colormap.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    controls[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.controls "Link to this definition")
    :   Control points of the colormap.
        Type:
        :   array, shape (N,) or (N+1,)
    interpolation[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.interpolation "Link to this definition")
    :   Colormap interpolation mode, either ‘linear’ or
        ‘zero’. If ‘linear’, ncontrols = ncolors (one
        color per control point). If ‘zero’, ncontrols
        = ncolors+1 (one color per bin).
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    nan\_color[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.nan_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Mapping for NaN values. Equivalent to matplotlib’s bad\_color
        Type:
        :   ColorValue
    high\_color[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.high_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Mapping for values equal to or greater than 1.
        Type:
        :   ColorValue
    low\_color[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.low_color "Link to this definition")
    :   Mapping for values equal to or less than 0.
        Type:
        :   ColorValue
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `construct`([\_fields\_set]) |  |

    | `dict`(\*[, include, exclude, by\_alias, ...]) |  |
    | [`enums_as_values`](#napari.utils.Colormap.enums_as_values "napari.utils.Colormap.enums_as_values")([as\_values]) | Temporarily override how enums are retrieved. |
    | `from_orm`(obj) |  |
    | `json`(\*[, include, exclude, by\_alias, ...]) |  |
    | `map`(values) |  |
    | [`model_construct`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_construct "napari.utils.Colormap.model_construct")([\_fields\_set]) | Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data. |
    | [`model_copy`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_copy "napari.utils.Colormap.model_copy")(\*[, update, deep]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_dump`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_dump "napari.utils.Colormap.model_dump")(\*[, mode, include, exclude, ...]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_dump_json`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_dump_json "napari.utils.Colormap.model_dump_json")(\*[, indent, ensure\_ascii, ...]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_json_schema`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_json_schema "napari.utils.Colormap.model_json_schema")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | Generate a JSON schema for this model. |
    | [`model_parametrized_name`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_parametrized_name "napari.utils.Colormap.model_parametrized_name")(params) | Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes. |
    | [`model_post_init`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_post_init "napari.utils.Colormap.model_post_init")(context, /) | This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes. |
    | [`model_rebuild`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_rebuild "napari.utils.Colormap.model_rebuild")(\*[, force, raise\_errors, ...]) | Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model. |
    | [`model_validate`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate "napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate")(obj, \*[, strict, extra, ...]) | Validate a pydantic model instance. |
    | [`model_validate_json`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate_json "napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate_json")(json\_data, \*[, strict, ...]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_validate_strings`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate_strings "napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate_strings")(obj, \*[, strict, ...]) | Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model. |

    | [`model_extra`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_extra "napari.utils.Colormap.model_extra") | Get extra fields set during validation. |
    | `model_fields` |  |
    | [`model_fields_set`](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_fields_set "napari.utils.Colormap.model_fields_set") | Returns the set of fields that have been explicitly set on this model instance. |
    Details
    copy(*\**, *include: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *exclude: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *update: Dict[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Any] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → Self[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! warning “Deprecated”
        :   This method is now deprecated; use model\_copy instead.
        If you need include or exclude, use:
        `` `python {test="skip" lint="skip"}
        data = self.model_dump(include=include, exclude=exclude, round_trip=True)
        data = {**data, **(update or {})}
        copied = self.model_validate(data)
        ` ``
        Parameters:
        :   * **include** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to include in the copied model.
            * **exclude** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to exclude in the copied model.
            * **update** – Optional dictionary of field-value pairs to override field values in the copied model.
            * **deep** – If True, the values of fields that are Pydantic models will be deep-copied.
        Returns:
        :   A copy of the model with included, excluded and updated fields as specified.
    enums\_as\_values(*as\_values: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*)[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.enums_as_values "Link to this definition")
    :   Temporarily override how enums are retrieved.
        Parameters:
        :   **as\_values** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether enums should be shown as values (or as enum objects),
            by default True
    model\_config = {'arbitrary\_types\_allowed': True, 'json\_encoders': {<class 'app\_model.types.\_keys.\_keybindings.KeyBinding'>: <function <lambda>>, <class 'napari.layers.utils.color\_encoding.ColorEncoding'>: <function StyleEncoding.\_json\_encode>, <class 'napari.layers.utils.string\_encoding.StringEncoding'>: <function StyleEncoding.\_json\_encode>, <class 'napari.settings.\_fields.Version'>: <function Version.\_json\_encode>, <class 'numpy.ndarray'>: <function <lambda>>}, 'validate\_assignment': True, 'validate\_default': True}[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_config "Link to this definition")
    :   Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].
    classmethod model\_construct(*\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*values: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_construct "Link to this definition")
    :   Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data.
        Creates a new model setting \_\_dict\_\_ and \_\_pydantic\_fields\_set\_\_ from trusted or pre-validated data.
        Default values are respected, but no other validation is performed.
        !!! note
        :   model\_construct() generally respects the model\_config.extra setting on the provided model.
            That is, if model\_config.extra == ‘allow’, then all extra passed values are added to the model instance’s \_\_dict\_\_
            and \_\_pydantic\_extra\_\_ fields. If model\_config.extra == ‘ignore’ (the default), then all extra passed values are ignored.
            Because no validation is performed with a call to model\_construct(), having model\_config.extra == ‘forbid’ does not result in
            an error if extra values are passed, but they will be ignored.
        Parameters:
        :   * **\_fields\_set** – A set of field names that were originally explicitly set during instantiation. If provided,
              this is directly used for the [model\_fields\_set][pydantic.BaseModel.model\_fields\_set] attribute.
              Otherwise, the field names from the values argument will be used.
            * **values** – Trusted or pre-validated data dictionary.
        Returns:
        :   A new instance of the Model class with validated data.
    model\_copy(*\**, *update: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_copy "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_copy](../concepts/models.md#model-copy)
        Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! note
        :   The underlying instance’s [\_\_dict\_\_][object.\_\_dict\_\_] attribute is copied. This
            might have unexpected side effects if you store anything in it, on top of the model
            fields (e.g. the value of [cached properties][functools.cached\_property]).
        Parameters:
        :   * **update** – Values to change/add in the new model. Note: the data is not validated
              before creating the new model. You should trust this data.
            * **deep** – Set to True to make a deep copy of the model.
        Returns:
        :   New model instance.
    model\_dump(*\**, *mode: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['json', 'python'] | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_dump "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump](../concepts/serialization.md#python-mode)
        Generate a dictionary representation of the model, optionally specifying which fields to include or exclude.
        Parameters:
        :   * **mode** – The mode in which to\_python should run.
              If mode is ‘json’, the output will only contain JSON serializable types.
              If mode is ‘python’, the output may contain non-JSON-serializable Python objects.
            * **include** – A set of fields to include in the output.
            * **exclude** – A set of fields to exclude from the output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias in the dictionary key if defined.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary representation of the model.
    model\_dump\_json(*\**, *indent: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *polymorphic\_serialization: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_dump_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump\_json](../concepts/serialization.md#json-mode)
        Generates a JSON representation of the model using Pydantic’s to\_json method.
        Parameters:
        :   * **indent** – Indentation to use in the JSON output. If None is passed, the output will be compact.
            * **ensure\_ascii** – If True, the output is guaranteed to have all incoming non-ASCII characters escaped.
              If False (the default), these characters will be output as-is.
            * **include** – Field(s) to include in the JSON output.
            * **exclude** – Field(s) to exclude from the JSON output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to serialize using field aliases.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A JSON string representation of the model.
    property model\_extra: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_extra "Link to this definition")
    :   Get extra fields set during validation.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary of extra fields, or None if config.extra is not set to “allow”.
    property model\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_fields_set "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns the set of fields that have been explicitly set on this model instance.
        Returns:
        :   A set of strings representing the fields that have been set,
            :   i.e. that were not filled from defaults.
    classmethod model\_json\_schema(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_json_schema "Link to this definition")
    :   Generate a JSON schema for this model.
        This is required to prevent from mail formated docstring break docs build.
    classmethod model\_parametrized\_name(*params: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], ...]*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_parametrized_name "Link to this definition")
    :   Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes.
        This method can be overridden to achieve a custom naming scheme for generic BaseModels.
        Parameters:
        :   **params** – Tuple of types of the class. Given a generic class
            Model with 2 type variables and a concrete model Model[str, int],
            the value (str, int) would be passed to params.
        Returns:
        :   String representing the new class where params are passed to cls as type variables.
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – Raised when trying to generate concrete names for non-generic models.
    model\_post\_init(*context: Any*, */*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_post_init "Link to this definition")
    :   This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes.
        It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.
        Parameters:
        :   * **self** – The BaseModel instance.
            * **context** – The context.
    classmethod model\_rebuild(*\**, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *raise\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *\_parent\_namespace\_depth: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *\_types\_namespace: MappingNamespace | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_rebuild "Link to this definition")
    :   Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model.
        This may be necessary when one of the annotations is a ForwardRef which could not be resolved during
        the initial attempt to build the schema, and automatic rebuilding fails.
        Parameters:
        :   * **force** – Whether to force the rebuilding of the model schema, defaults to False.
            * **raise\_errors** – Whether to raise errors, defaults to True.
            * **\_parent\_namespace\_depth** – The depth level of the parent namespace, defaults to 2.
            * **\_types\_namespace** – The types namespace, defaults to None.
        Returns:
        :   Returns None if the schema is already “complete” and rebuilding was not required.
            If rebuilding \_was\_ required, returns True if rebuilding was successful, otherwise False.
    classmethod model\_validate(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *from\_attributes: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate a pydantic model instance.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **from\_attributes** – Whether to extract data from object attributes.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If the object could not be validated.
        Returns:
        :   The validated model instance.
    classmethod model\_validate\_json(*json\_data: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytearray](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytearray "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [JSON Parsing](../concepts/json.md#json-parsing)
        Validate the given JSON data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **json\_data** – The JSON data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If json\_data is not a JSON string or the object could not be validated.
    classmethod model\_validate\_strings(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.model_validate_strings "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object containing string data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
    reset()[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.reset "Link to this definition")
    :   Reset the state of the model to default values.
    update(*values: [EventedModel](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*, *recurse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.Colormap.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update a model in place.
        Parameters:
        :   * **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.events.EventedModel*](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.EventedModel")) – Values to update the model with. If an EventedModel is passed it is
              first converted to a dictionary. The keys of this dictionary must
              be found as attributes on the current model.
            * **recurse** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, recursively update fields that are EventedModels.
              Otherwise, just update the immediate fields of this EventedModel,
              which is useful when the declared field type (e.g. `Union`) can have
              different realized types with different fields.

# napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap[#](#napari-utils-cycliclabelcolormap "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap(*colors, display\_name: str | None = None, \*, name: str = 'custom', interpolation: ~typing.Literal[ColormapInterpolationMode.ZERO] = ColormapInterpolationMode.ZERO, controls: ~napari.utils.events.custom\_types.Array = <factory>, nan\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue = ColorValue([0., 0., 0., 0.], dtype=float32), high\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue | None = None, low\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue | None = None, use\_selection: bool = False, selection: int = 0, background\_value: int = 0, seed: float = 0.5*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/colormaps/colormap.html#CyclicLabelColormap)[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `LabelColormapBase`
    Color cycle with a background value.
    colors[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.colors "Link to this definition")
    :   Colors to be used for mapping.
        For values above the number of colors,
        the colors will be cycled.
        Type:
        :   ColorArray
    use\_selection[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.use_selection "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether map only selected label.
        If True only selected label will be mapped to not transparent color.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    selection[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.selection "Link to this definition")
    :   The selected label.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    background\_value[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.background_value "Link to this definition")
    :   Which value should be treated as a background
        and mapped to transparent color.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    interpolation[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.interpolation "Link to this definition")
    :   required by implementation, please do not set value
        Type:
        :   Literal[‘zero’]
    seed[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.seed "Link to this definition")
    :   seed used for random color generation. Used for reproducibility.
        It will be removed in the future release.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")

    copy(*\**, *include: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *exclude: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *update: Dict[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Any] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → Self[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! warning “Deprecated”
        :   This method is now deprecated; use model\_copy instead.
        If you need include or exclude, use:
        `` `python {test="skip" lint="skip"}
        data = self.model_dump(include=include, exclude=exclude, round_trip=True)
        data = {**data, **(update or {})}
        copied = self.model_validate(data)
        ` ``
        Parameters:
        :   * **include** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to include in the copied model.
            * **exclude** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to exclude in the copied model.
            * **update** – Optional dictionary of field-value pairs to override field values in the copied model.
            * **deep** – If True, the values of fields that are Pydantic models will be deep-copied.
        Returns:
        :   A copy of the model with included, excluded and updated fields as specified.

    classmethod model\_construct(*\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*values: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_construct "Link to this definition")
    :   Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data.
        Creates a new model setting \_\_dict\_\_ and \_\_pydantic\_fields\_set\_\_ from trusted or pre-validated data.
        Default values are respected, but no other validation is performed.
        !!! note
        :   model\_construct() generally respects the model\_config.extra setting on the provided model.
            That is, if model\_config.extra == ‘allow’, then all extra passed values are added to the model instance’s \_\_dict\_\_
            and \_\_pydantic\_extra\_\_ fields. If model\_config.extra == ‘ignore’ (the default), then all extra passed values are ignored.
            Because no validation is performed with a call to model\_construct(), having model\_config.extra == ‘forbid’ does not result in
            an error if extra values are passed, but they will be ignored.
        Parameters:
        :   * **\_fields\_set** – A set of field names that were originally explicitly set during instantiation. If provided,
              this is directly used for the [model\_fields\_set][pydantic.BaseModel.model\_fields\_set] attribute.
              Otherwise, the field names from the values argument will be used.
            * **values** – Trusted or pre-validated data dictionary.
        Returns:
        :   A new instance of the Model class with validated data.
    model\_copy(*\**, *update: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_copy "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_copy](../concepts/models.md#model-copy)
        Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! note
        :   The underlying instance’s [\_\_dict\_\_][object.\_\_dict\_\_] attribute is copied. This
            might have unexpected side effects if you store anything in it, on top of the model
            fields (e.g. the value of [cached properties][functools.cached\_property]).
        Parameters:
        :   * **update** – Values to change/add in the new model. Note: the data is not validated
              before creating the new model. You should trust this data.
            * **deep** – Set to True to make a deep copy of the model.
        Returns:
        :   New model instance.

    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump](../concepts/serialization.md#python-mode)
        Generate a dictionary representation of the model, optionally specifying which fields to include or exclude.
        Parameters:
        :   * **mode** – The mode in which to\_python should run.
              If mode is ‘json’, the output will only contain JSON serializable types.
              If mode is ‘python’, the output may contain non-JSON-serializable Python objects.
            * **include** – A set of fields to include in the output.
            * **exclude** – A set of fields to exclude from the output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias in the dictionary key if defined.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary representation of the model.
