[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *serialize\_as\_any: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *polymorphic\_serialization: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_dump_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump\_json](../concepts/serialization.md#json-mode)
        Generates a JSON representation of the model using Pydantic’s to\_json method.
        Parameters:
        :   * **indent** – Indentation to use in the JSON output. If None is passed, the output will be compact.
            * **ensure\_ascii** – If True, the output is guaranteed to have all incoming non-ASCII characters escaped.
              If False (the default), these characters will be output as-is.
            * **include** – Field(s) to include in the JSON output.
            * **exclude** – Field(s) to exclude from the JSON output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to serialize using field aliases.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A JSON string representation of the model.
    property model\_extra: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_extra "Link to this definition")
    :   Get extra fields set during validation.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary of extra fields, or None if config.extra is not set to “allow”.
    property model\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_fields_set "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns the set of fields that have been explicitly set on this model instance.
        Returns:
        :   A set of strings representing the fields that have been set,
            :   i.e. that were not filled from defaults.
    classmethod model\_json\_schema(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_json_schema "Link to this definition")
    :   Generate a JSON schema for this model.
        This is required to prevent from mail formated docstring break docs build.
    classmethod model\_parametrized\_name(*params: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], ...]*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_parametrized_name "Link to this definition")
    :   Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes.
        This method can be overridden to achieve a custom naming scheme for generic BaseModels.
        Parameters:
        :   **params** – Tuple of types of the class. Given a generic class
            Model with 2 type variables and a concrete model Model[str, int],
            the value (str, int) would be passed to params.
        Returns:
        :   String representing the new class where params are passed to cls as type variables.
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – Raised when trying to generate concrete names for non-generic models.
    model\_post\_init(*context: Any*, */*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_post_init "Link to this definition")
    :   This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes.
        It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.
        Parameters:
        :   * **self** – The BaseModel instance.
            * **context** – The context.
    classmethod model\_rebuild(*\**, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *raise\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *\_parent\_namespace\_depth: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *\_types\_namespace: MappingNamespace | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_rebuild "Link to this definition")
    :   Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model.
        This may be necessary when one of the annotations is a ForwardRef which could not be resolved during
        the initial attempt to build the schema, and automatic rebuilding fails.
        Parameters:
        :   * **force** – Whether to force the rebuilding of the model schema, defaults to False.
            * **raise\_errors** – Whether to raise errors, defaults to True.
            * **\_parent\_namespace\_depth** – The depth level of the parent namespace, defaults to 2.
            * **\_types\_namespace** – The types namespace, defaults to None.
        Returns:
        :   Returns None if the schema is already “complete” and rebuilding was not required.
            If rebuilding \_was\_ required, returns True if rebuilding was successful, otherwise False.
    classmethod model\_validate(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *from\_attributes: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.model_validate "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate a pydantic model instance.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **from\_attributes** – Whether to extract data from object attributes.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If the object could not be validated.
        Returns:
        :   The validated model instance.

    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [JSON Parsing](../concepts/json.md#json-parsing)
        Validate the given JSON data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **json\_data** – The JSON data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.

            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.

        Raises:

            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.

    :   Reset the state of the model to default values.
    shuffle(*seed: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/colormaps/colormap.html#CyclicLabelColormap.shuffle)[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.shuffle "Link to this definition")
    :   Shuffle the colormap colors.

    update(*values: [EventedModel](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*, *recurse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update a model in place.
        Parameters:
        :   * **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.events.EventedModel*](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.EventedModel")) – Values to update the model with. If an EventedModel is passed it is
              first converted to a dictionary. The keys of this dictionary must
              be found as attributes on the current model.
            * **recurse** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, recursively update fields that are EventedModels.
              Otherwise, just update the immediate fields of this EventedModel,
              which is useful when the declared field type (e.g. `Union`) can have
              different realized types with different fields.

# napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap[#](#napari-utils-directlabelcolormap "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap(*\*args, colors: ~napari.utils.color.ColorArray, name: str = 'custom', interpolation: ~typing.Literal[ColormapInterpolationMode.ZERO] = ColormapInterpolationMode.ZERO, controls: ~napari.utils.events.custom\_types.Array = <factory>, nan\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue = ColorValue([0., 0., 0., 0.], dtype=float32), high\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue | None = None, low\_color: ~napari.utils.color.ColorValue | None = None, use\_selection: bool = False, selection: int = 0, background\_value: int = 0, color\_dict: ~collections.defaultdict[int | None, ~typing.Annotated[~numpy.ndarray, FieldInfo(annotation=NoneType, required=False, default\_factory=<lambda>)]] = <factory>*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/colormaps/colormap.html#DirectLabelColormap)[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `LabelColormapBase`
    Colormap using a direct mapping from labels to color using a dict.
    color\_dict[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.color_dict "Link to this definition")
    :   The dictionary mapping labels to colors.
        Type:
        :   dict from int to (3,) or (4,) array
    use\_selection[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.use_selection "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether to map only the selected label to a color.
        If True only selected label will be not transparent.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    selection[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.selection "Link to this definition")
    :   The selected label.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    colors[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.colors "Link to this definition")
    :   Exist because of implementation details. Please do not use it.
        Type:
        :   ColorArray

    copy(*\**, *include: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *exclude: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *update: Dict[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Any] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → Self[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! warning “Deprecated”
        :   This method is now deprecated; use model\_copy instead.
        If you need include or exclude, use:
        `` `python {test="skip" lint="skip"}
        data = self.model_dump(include=include, exclude=exclude, round_trip=True)
        data = {**data, **(update or {})}
        copied = self.model_validate(data)
        ` ``
        Parameters:
        :   * **include** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to include in the copied model.
            * **exclude** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to exclude in the copied model.
            * **update** – Optional dictionary of field-value pairs to override field values in the copied model.
            * **deep** – If True, the values of fields that are Pydantic models will be deep-copied.
        Returns:
        :   A copy of the model with included, excluded and updated fields as specified.
    enums\_as\_values(*as\_values: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*)[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.enums_as_values "Link to this definition")
    :   Temporarily override how enums are retrieved.

    :   Map values to colors.

    :   Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].
    classmethod model\_construct(*\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*values: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_construct "Link to this definition")
    :   Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data.
        Creates a new model setting \_\_dict\_\_ and \_\_pydantic\_fields\_set\_\_ from trusted or pre-validated data.
        Default values are respected, but no other validation is performed.
        !!! note
        :   model\_construct() generally respects the model\_config.extra setting on the provided model.
            That is, if model\_config.extra == ‘allow’, then all extra passed values are added to the model instance’s \_\_dict\_\_
            and \_\_pydantic\_extra\_\_ fields. If model\_config.extra == ‘ignore’ (the default), then all extra passed values are ignored.
            Because no validation is performed with a call to model\_construct(), having model\_config.extra == ‘forbid’ does not result in
            an error if extra values are passed, but they will be ignored.
        Parameters:
        :   * **\_fields\_set** – A set of field names that were originally explicitly set during instantiation. If provided,
              this is directly used for the [model\_fields\_set][pydantic.BaseModel.model\_fields\_set] attribute.
              Otherwise, the field names from the values argument will be used.
            * **values** – Trusted or pre-validated data dictionary.
        Returns:
        :   A new instance of the Model class with validated data.
    model\_copy(*\**, *update: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_copy "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_copy](../concepts/models.md#model-copy)
        Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! note
        :   The underlying instance’s [\_\_dict\_\_][object.\_\_dict\_\_] attribute is copied. This
            might have unexpected side effects if you store anything in it, on top of the model
            fields (e.g. the value of [cached properties][functools.cached\_property]).
        Parameters:
        :   * **update** – Values to change/add in the new model. Note: the data is not validated
              before creating the new model. You should trust this data.
            * **deep** – Set to True to make a deep copy of the model.
        Returns:
        :   New model instance.

    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump](../concepts/serialization.md#python-mode)
        Generate a dictionary representation of the model, optionally specifying which fields to include or exclude.
        Parameters:
        :   * **mode** – The mode in which to\_python should run.
              If mode is ‘json’, the output will only contain JSON serializable types.
              If mode is ‘python’, the output may contain non-JSON-serializable Python objects.
            * **include** – A set of fields to include in the output.
            * **exclude** – A set of fields to exclude from the output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias in the dictionary key if defined.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary representation of the model.

v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_dump_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump\_json](../concepts/serialization.md#json-mode)
        Generates a JSON representation of the model using Pydantic’s to\_json method.
        Parameters:
        :   * **indent** – Indentation to use in the JSON output. If None is passed, the output will be compact.
            * **ensure\_ascii** – If True, the output is guaranteed to have all incoming non-ASCII characters escaped.
              If False (the default), these characters will be output as-is.
            * **include** – Field(s) to include in the JSON output.
            * **exclude** – Field(s) to exclude from the JSON output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to serialize using field aliases.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A JSON string representation of the model.
    property model\_extra: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_extra "Link to this definition")
    :   Get extra fields set during validation.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary of extra fields, or None if config.extra is not set to “allow”.
    property model\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_fields_set "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns the set of fields that have been explicitly set on this model instance.
        Returns:
        :   A set of strings representing the fields that have been set,
            :   i.e. that were not filled from defaults.
    classmethod model\_json\_schema(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_json_schema "Link to this definition")
    :   Generate a JSON schema for this model.
        This is required to prevent from mail formated docstring break docs build.
    classmethod model\_parametrized\_name(*params: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], ...]*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_parametrized_name "Link to this definition")
    :   Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes.
        This method can be overridden to achieve a custom naming scheme for generic BaseModels.
        Parameters:
        :   **params** – Tuple of types of the class. Given a generic class
            Model with 2 type variables and a concrete model Model[str, int],
            the value (str, int) would be passed to params.
        Returns:
        :   String representing the new class where params are passed to cls as type variables.
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – Raised when trying to generate concrete names for non-generic models.
    model\_post\_init(*context: Any*, */*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_post_init "Link to this definition")
    :   This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes.
        It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.
        Parameters:
        :   * **self** – The BaseModel instance.
            * **context** – The context.
    classmethod model\_rebuild(*\**, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *raise\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *\_parent\_namespace\_depth: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *\_types\_namespace: MappingNamespace | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_rebuild "Link to this definition")
    :   Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model.
        This may be necessary when one of the annotations is a ForwardRef which could not be resolved during
        the initial attempt to build the schema, and automatic rebuilding fails.
        Parameters:
        :   * **force** – Whether to force the rebuilding of the model schema, defaults to False.
            * **raise\_errors** – Whether to raise errors, defaults to True.
            * **\_parent\_namespace\_depth** – The depth level of the parent namespace, defaults to 2.
            * **\_types\_namespace** – The types namespace, defaults to None.
        Returns:
        :   Returns None if the schema is already “complete” and rebuilding was not required.
            If rebuilding \_was\_ required, returns True if rebuilding was successful, otherwise False.
    classmethod model\_validate(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *from\_attributes: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_validate "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate a pydantic model instance.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **from\_attributes** – Whether to extract data from object attributes.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If the object could not be validated.
        Returns:
        :   The validated model instance.
    classmethod model\_validate\_json(*json\_data: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytearray](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytearray "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_validate_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [JSON Parsing](../concepts/json.md#json-parsing)
        Validate the given JSON data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **json\_data** – The JSON data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If json\_data is not a JSON string or the object could not be validated.
    classmethod model\_validate\_strings(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.model_validate_strings "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object containing string data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
    reset()[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.reset "Link to this definition")
    :   Reset the state of the model to default values.
    update(*values: [EventedModel](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*, *recurse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update a model in place.
        Parameters:
        :   * **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.events.EventedModel*](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.EventedModel")) – Values to update the model with. If an EventedModel is passed it is
              first converted to a dictionary. The keys of this dictionary must
              be found as attributes on the current model.
            * **recurse** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, recursively update fields that are EventedModels.
              Otherwise, just update the immediate fields of this EventedModel,
              which is useful when the declared field type (e.g. `Union`) can have
              different realized types with different fields.

* napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot
# napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot[#](#napari-utils-notebookscreenshot "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot(*viewer*, *\**, *canvas\_only=False*, *alt\_text=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/notebook_display.html#NotebookScreenshot)[#](#napari.utils.NotebookScreenshot "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`object`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")
    Display a napari screenshot in a jupyter notebook.
    Functions returning an object with a \_repr\_png\_() method
    will be displayed as a rich image in a jupyter notebook.
    <https://ipython.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api/generated/IPython.display.html>
    Parameters:
    :   * **viewer** ([*napari.Viewer*](napari.Viewer.html#napari.Viewer "napari.Viewer")) – The napari viewer.
        * **canvas\_only** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – If False include the napari viewer frame in the screenshot,
          and if True then take screenshot of just the image display canvas.
          By default, False.
        * **alt\_text** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Image description alternative text, for screenreader accessibility.
          Good alt-text describes the image and any text within the image
          in no more than three short, complete sentences.
          By default, None.
    Examples
    ```
    >>> import napari
    >>> from napari.utils import nbscreenshot
    >>> from skimage.data import chelsea
    ```
    ```
    >>> viewer = napari.Viewer()
    >>> viewer.add_image(chelsea(), name='chelsea-the-cat')
    >>> nbscreenshot(viewer)
    # screenshot just the canvas without the napari viewer framing it
    >>> nbscreenshot(viewer, canvas_only=True)
    ```
    Methods
    Details

<!-- Source: https://napari.org/stable/api/napari.utils.cancelable_progress.html -->
* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* [napari.utils](napari.utils.html)
* napari.utils.cancelable\_progress
# napari.utils.cancelable\_progress[#](#napari-utils-cancelable-progress "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.cancelable\_progress(*\*\_*, *\*\*\_\_*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#cancelable_progress)[#](#napari.utils.cancelable_progress "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`progress`](napari.utils.progress.html#napari.utils.progress "napari.utils.progress.progress")
    This class inherits from progress, providing the additional
    ability to cancel expensive executions. When progress is
    canceled by the user in the napari UI, two things will happen:
    Firstly, the is\_canceled attribute will become True, and the
    for loop will terminate after the current iteration, regardless
    of whether or not the iterator had more items.
    Secondly, cancel\_callback will be called, allowing the computation
    to close resources, repair state, etc.
    See napari.utils.progress and tqdm.tqdm API for valid args and kwargs:
    <https://tqdm.github.io/docs/tqdm/>
    Examples
    ```
    >>> def long_running(steps=10, delay=0.1):
    ...     def on_cancel():
    ...         print("Canceled operation")
    ...     for i in cancelable_progress(range(steps), cancel_callback=on_cancel):
    ...         sleep(delay)
    ```
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`cancel`](#napari.utils.cancelable_progress.cancel "napari.utils.cancelable_progress.cancel")() | Cancels the execution of the underlying computation. |
    | `clear`([nolock]) | Clear current bar display. |
    | `close`() | Close progress object and emit event. |
    | `display`([msg, pos]) | Update the display and emit eta event. |
    | `external_write_mode`([file, nolock]) | Disable tqdm within context and refresh tqdm when exits. |
    | `format_interval`(t) | Formats a number of seconds as a clock time, [H:]MM:SS |
    | `format_meter`(n, total, elapsed[, ncols, ...]) | Return a string-based progress bar given some parameters |
    | `format_num`(n) | Intelligent scientific notation (.3g). |
    | `format_sizeof`(num[, suffix, divisor]) | Formats a number (greater than unity) with SI Order of Magnitude prefixes. |
    | `get_lock`() | Get the global lock. |
    | `increment_with_overflow`() | Update if not exceeding total, else set indeterminate range. |
    | `moveto`(n) |  |
    | `pandas`(\*\*tqdm\_kwargs) | Registers the current tqdm class with |
    | `refresh`([nolock, lock\_args]) | Force refresh the display of this bar. |
    | `reset`([total]) | Resets to 0 iterations for repeated use. |
    | `set_description`(desc) | Update progress description and emit description event. |
    | `set_description_str`([desc, refresh]) | Set/modify description without ': ' appended. |
    | `set_lock`(lock) | Set the global lock. |
    | `set_postfix`([ordered\_dict, refresh]) | Set/modify postfix (additional stats) with automatic formatting based on datatype. |
    | `set_postfix_str`([s, refresh]) | Postfix without dictionary expansion, similar to prefix handling. |
    | `status_printer`(file) | Manage the printing and in-place updating of a line of characters. |
    | `unpause`() | Restart tqdm timer from last print time. |
    | `update`([n]) | Update progress value by n and emit value event |
    | `wrapattr`(stream, method[, total, bytes]) | stream : file-like object. method : str, "read" or "write". The result of read() and the first argument of write() should have a len(). |
    | `write`(s[, file, end, nolock]) | Print a message via tqdm (without overlap with bars). |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `format_dict` | Public API for read-only member access. |
    | `monitor` |  |
    | `monitor_interval` |  |
    | `total` |  |
    Details
    cancel() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#cancelable_progress.cancel)[#](#napari.utils.cancelable_progress.cancel "Link to this definition")
    :   Cancels the execution of the underlying computation.
        Note that the current iteration will be allowed to complete, however
        future iterations will not be run.

<!-- Source: https://napari.org/stable/api/napari.utils.progress.html -->
* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* [napari.utils](napari.utils.html)
* napari.utils.progress
# napari.utils.progress[#](#napari-utils-progress "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.progress(*\*\_*, *\*\*\_\_*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#progress)[#](#napari.utils.progress "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `tqdm`
    This class inherits from tqdm and provides an interface for
    progress bars in the napari viewer. Progress bars can be created
    directly by wrapping an iterable or by providing a total number
    of expected updates.
    While this interface is primarily designed to be displayed in
    the viewer, it can also be used without a viewer open, in which
    case it behaves identically to tqdm and produces the progress
    bar in the terminal.
    See tqdm.tqdm API for valid args and kwargs:
    <https://tqdm.github.io/docs/tqdm/>
    Examples
    ```
    >>> def long_running(steps=10, delay=0.1):
    ...     for i in progress(range(steps)):
    ...         sleep(delay)
    ```
    it can also be used as a context manager:
    ```
    >>> def long_running(steps=10, repeats=4, delay=0.1):
    ...     with progress(range(steps)) as pbr:
    ...         for i in pbr:
    ...             sleep(delay)
    ```
    or equivalently, using the progrange shorthand
    ```
    with progrange(steps) as pbr:
        for i in pbr:
            sleep(delay)
    ```
    For manual updates:
    ```
    >>> def manual_updates(total):
    ...     pbr = progress(total=total)
    ...     sleep(10)
    ...     pbr.set_description("Step 1 Complete")
    ...     pbr.update(1)
    ...     # must call pbr.close() when using outside for loop
    ...     # or context manager
    ...     pbr.close()
    ```
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `clear`([nolock]) | Clear current bar display. |
    | `close`() | Close progress object and emit event. |
    | `display`([msg, pos]) | Update the display and emit eta event. |
    | `external_write_mode`([file, nolock]) | Disable tqdm within context and refresh tqdm when exits. |
    | `format_interval`(t) | Formats a number of seconds as a clock time, [H:]MM:SS |
    | `format_meter`(n, total, elapsed[, ncols, ...]) | Return a string-based progress bar given some parameters |
    | `format_num`(n) | Intelligent scientific notation (.3g). |
    | `format_sizeof`(num[, suffix, divisor]) | Formats a number (greater than unity) with SI Order of Magnitude prefixes. |
    | `get_lock`() | Get the global lock. |
    | `increment_with_overflow`() | Update if not exceeding total, else set indeterminate range. |
    | `moveto`(n) |  |
    | `pandas`(\*\*tqdm\_kwargs) | Registers the current tqdm class with |
    | `refresh`([nolock, lock\_args]) | Force refresh the display of this bar. |
    | `reset`([total]) | Resets to 0 iterations for repeated use. |
    | `set_description`(desc) | Update progress description and emit description event. |
    | `set_description_str`([desc, refresh]) | Set/modify description without ': ' appended. |
    | `set_lock`(lock) | Set the global lock. |
    | `set_postfix`([ordered\_dict, refresh]) | Set/modify postfix (additional stats) with automatic formatting based on datatype. |
    | `set_postfix_str`([s, refresh]) | Postfix without dictionary expansion, similar to prefix handling. |
    | `status_printer`(file) | Manage the printing and in-place updating of a line of characters. |
    | `unpause`() | Restart tqdm timer from last print time. |
    | `update`([n]) | Update progress value by n and emit value event |
    | `wrapattr`(stream, method[, total, bytes]) | stream : file-like object. method : str, "read" or "write". The result of read() and the first argument of write() should have a len(). |
    | `write`(s[, file, end, nolock]) | Print a message via tqdm (without overlap with bars). |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `format_dict` | Public API for read-only member access. |
    | `monitor` |  |
    | `monitor_interval` |  |
    | `total` |  |
    Details
    close() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#progress.close)[#](#napari.utils.progress.close "Link to this definition")
    :   Close progress object and emit event.
    display(*msg: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *pos: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#progress.display)[#](#napari.utils.progress.display "Link to this definition")
    :   Update the display and emit eta event.
    increment\_with\_overflow() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#progress.increment_with_overflow)[#](#napari.utils.progress.increment_with_overflow "Link to this definition")
    :   Update if not exceeding total, else set indeterminate range.
    set\_description(*desc: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#progress.set_description)[#](#napari.utils.progress.set_description "Link to this definition")
    :   Update progress description and emit description event.
    update(*n: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 1*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/progress.html#progress.update)[#](#napari.utils.progress.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update progress value by n and emit value event

* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* napari.window
# napari.window[#](#module-napari.window "Link to this heading")
The Window class is the primary entry to the napari GUI.
Currently, this module is just a stub file that will simply pass through the
[`napari._qt.qt_main_window.Window`](napari.window.Window.html#napari.window.Window "napari._qt.qt_main_window.Window") class. In the future, this module
could serve to define a window Protocol that a backend would need to implement
to server as a graphical user interface for napari.
Classes
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`Window`](napari.window.Window.html#napari.window.Window "napari.window.Window") | Application window that contains the menu bar and viewer. |
[previous
napari.utils.progress](napari.utils.progress.html "previous page")
[next
napari.window.Window](napari.window.Window.html "next page")
[Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/napari/docs/blob/main/docs/api/napari.window.rst)

# napari.window.Window[#](#napari-window-window "Link to this heading")
class napari.window.Window(*viewer: [Viewer](napari.Viewer.html#napari.Viewer "napari.Viewer")*, *\**, *show: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *show\_welcome\_screen: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window)[#](#napari.window.Window "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`object`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")
    Application window that contains the menu bar and viewer.
    Parameters:
    :   **viewer** ([*napari.components.ViewerModel*](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel")) – Contained viewer widget.
    file\_menu[#](#napari.window.Window.file_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   File menu.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMenu
    help\_menu[#](#napari.window.Window.help_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   Help menu.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMenu
    main\_menu[#](#napari.window.Window.main_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   Main menubar.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMainWindow.menuBar
    view\_menu[#](#napari.window.Window.view_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   View menu.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMenu
    window\_menu[#](#napari.window.Window.window_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   Window menu.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMenu
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`activate`](#napari.window.Window.activate "napari.window.Window.activate")() | Make the viewer the currently active window. |
    | [`add_dock_widget`](#napari.window.Window.add_dock_widget "napari.window.Window.add_dock_widget")(widget, Widget], \*, name, ...) | Convenience method to add a QDockWidget to the main window. |
    | [`add_function_widget`](#napari.window.Window.add_function_widget "napari.window.Window.add_function_widget")(function, \*[, ...]) | Turn a function into a dock widget via magicgui. |
    | [`add_plugin_dock_widget`](#napari.window.Window.add_plugin_dock_widget "napari.window.Window.add_plugin_dock_widget")(plugin\_name[, ...]) | Add plugin dock widget if not already added. |
    | [`clipboard`](#napari.window.Window.clipboard "napari.window.Window.clipboard")([flash, canvas\_only]) | Copy screenshot of current viewer to the clipboard. |
    | [`close`](#napari.window.Window.close "napari.window.Window.close")() | Close the viewer window and cleanup sub-widgets. |
    | [`export_figure`](#napari.window.Window.export_figure "napari.window.Window.export_figure")([path, scale, flash]) | Export an image of the full extent of the displayed layer data. |
    | [`export_rois`](#napari.window.Window.export_rois "napari.window.Window.export_rois")(rois[, paths, scale]) | Export the given rectangular rois to specified file paths. |
    | [`geometry`](#napari.window.Window.geometry "napari.window.Window.geometry")() | Get the geometry of the widget |
    | [`remove_dock_widget`](#napari.window.Window.remove_dock_widget "napari.window.Window.remove_dock_widget")(widget[, menu]) | Removes specified dock widget. |
    | [`resize`](#napari.window.Window.resize "napari.window.Window.resize")(width, height) | Resize the window. |
    | [`screenshot`](#napari.window.Window.screenshot "napari.window.Window.screenshot")([path, size, scale, flash, ...]) | Take currently displayed viewer and convert to an image array. |
    | [`set_geometry`](#napari.window.Window.set_geometry "napari.window.Window.set_geometry")(left, top, width, height) | Set the geometry of the widget |
    | [`show`](#napari.window.Window.show "napari.window.Window.show")(\*[, block]) | Resize, show, and bring forward the window. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`dock_widgets`](#napari.window.Window.dock_widgets "napari.window.Window.dock_widgets") | Read-only mapping of widgets docked in napari window. |
    | `qt_viewer` |  |
    Details
    activate()[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.activate)[#](#napari.window.Window.activate "Link to this definition")
    :   Make the viewer the currently active window.
    add\_dock\_widget(*widget: Union[QWidget, Widget], \*, name: str = '', area: str | None = None, allowed\_areas: Sequence[str] | None = None, shortcut=<object object>, add\_vertical\_stretch=True, tabify: bool = False, menu: QMenu | None = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.add_dock_widget)[#](#napari.window.Window.add_dock_widget "Link to this definition")
    :   Convenience method to add a QDockWidget to the main window.
        If name is not provided a generic name will be addded to avoid
        saveState warnings on close.
        Parameters:
        :   * **widget** (*QWidget*) – widget will be added as QDockWidget’s main widget.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of dock widget to appear in window menu.
            * **area** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Side of the main window to which the new dock widget will be added.
              Must be in {‘left’, ‘right’, ‘top’, ‘bottom’}
            * **allowed\_areas** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**,* *optional*) – Areas, relative to the main window, that the widget is allowed dock.
              Each item in list must be in {‘left’, ‘right’, ‘top’, ‘bottom’}
              By default, all areas are allowed.
            * **shortcut** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Keyboard shortcut to appear in dropdown menu.
            * **add\_vertical\_stretch** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) –
              Whether to add stretch to the bottom of vertical widgets (pushing
              widgets up towards the top of the allotted area, instead of letting
              them distribute across the vertical space). By default, True.
              Deprecated since version 0.4.8: The shortcut parameter is deprecated since version 0.4.8, please use
              the action and shortcut manager APIs. The new action manager and
              shortcut API allow user configuration and localization.
            * **tabify** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to tabify dock widget or not.
            * **menu** (*QMenu**,* *optional*) – Menu bar to add toggle action to. If None nothing added to menu.
        Returns:
        :   **dock\_widget** – dock\_widget that can pass viewer events.
        Return type:
        :   QtViewerDockWidget
    add\_function\_widget(*function*, *\**, *magic\_kwargs=None*, *name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = ''*, *area=None*, *allowed\_areas=None*, *shortcut=<object object>*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.add_function_widget)[#](#napari.window.Window.add_function_widget "Link to this definition")
    :   Turn a function into a dock widget via magicgui.
        Parameters:
        :   * **function** (*callable*) – Function that you want to add.
            * **magic\_kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Keyword arguments to `magicgui.magicgui()` that
              can be used to specify widget.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of dock widget to appear in window menu.
            * **area** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Side of the main window to which the new dock widget will be added.
              Must be in {‘left’, ‘right’, ‘top’, ‘bottom’}. If not provided the
              default will be determined by the widget.layout, with ‘vertical’
              layouts appearing on the right, otherwise on the bottom.
            * **allowed\_areas** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**,* *optional*) – Areas, relative to main window, that the widget is allowed dock.
              Each item in list must be in {‘left’, ‘right’, ‘top’, ‘bottom’}
              By default, only provided areas is allowed.
            * **shortcut** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Keyboard shortcut to appear in dropdown menu.
        Returns:
        :   **dock\_widget** – dock\_widget that can pass viewer events.
        Return type:
        :   QtViewerDockWidget
    add\_plugin\_dock\_widget(*plugin\_name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *widget\_name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *tabify: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[QtViewerDockWidget, [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.add_plugin_dock_widget)[#](#napari.window.Window.add_plugin_dock_widget "Link to this definition")
    :   Add plugin dock widget if not already added.
        Parameters:
        :   * **plugin\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of a plugin providing a widget
            * **widget\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of a widget provided by plugin\_name. If None, and the
              specified plugin provides only a single widget, that widget will be
              returned, otherwise a ValueError will be raised, by default None
            * **tabify** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to tabify dock widget or not.
        Returns:
        :   A 2-tuple containing (the DockWidget instance, the plugin widget
            instance).
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")
    clipboard(*flash=True*, *canvas\_only=False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.clipboard)[#](#napari.window.Window.clipboard "Link to this definition")
    :   Copy screenshot of current viewer to the clipboard.
        Parameters:
        :   * **flash** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to indicate whether flash animation should be shown after
              the screenshot was captured.
            * **canvas\_only** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, screenshot shows only the image display canvas, and
              if False include the napari viewer frame in the screenshot,
              By default, True.
    close()[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.close)[#](#napari.window.Window.close "Link to this definition")
    :   Close the viewer window and cleanup sub-widgets.
    property dock\_widgets: Mapping[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), QWidget | Widget][#](#napari.window.Window.dock_widgets "Link to this definition")
    :   Read-only mapping of widgets docked in napari window.
        Notes
        This mapping returns the *inner* widget contained in each dock widget
        (a Qt `QWidget` or a `magicgui.widgets.Widget`), not the
        `QtViewerDockWidget`
        wrapper.
        If you need to control the dock widget itself (for example, to show or
        raise a docked widget tab), access the wrapper via the Qt parent:
        ```
        >>> name = "My widget"
        >>> widget = viewer.window.dock_widgets[name]
        >>> qt_widget = widget.native if hasattr(widget, "native") else widget
        >>> dock_widget = qt_widget.parent()  # QtViewerDockWidget / QDockWidget
        >>> dock_widget.show(); dock_widget.raise_()
        ```
    export\_figure(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *scale: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1*, *flash=True*) → np.ndarray[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.export_figure)[#](#napari.window.Window.export_figure "Link to this definition")
    :   Export an image of the full extent of the displayed layer data.
        This function finds a tight boundary around the data, resets the view
        around that boundary (and, when scale=1, such that 1 captured pixel is
        equivalent to one data pixel), takes a screenshot, then restores the
        previous zoom and canvas sizes.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Filename for saving screenshot image.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the
              screenshot. By default, a scale of 1.
            * **flash** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to indicate whether flash animation should be shown after
              the screenshot was captured.
              By default, True.
        Returns:
        :   **image** – Numpy array of type ubyte and shape (h, w, 4). Index [0, 0] is the
            upper-left corner of the rendered region.
        Return type:
        :   array
    export\_rois(*rois: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray]*, *paths: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | Path | [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | Path] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *scale: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1.0*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.export_rois)[#](#napari.window.Window.export_rois "Link to this definition")
    :   Export the given rectangular rois to specified file paths.
        For each shape, moves the camera to the center of the shape
        and adjust the canvas size to fit the shape.
        Note: The shape height and width can be of type float.
        However, the canvas size only accepts a tuple of integers.
        This can result in slight misalignment.
        Parameters:
        :   * **rois** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[**np.ndarray**]*) – A list of arrays with each being of shape (4, 2) representing
              a rectangular roi.
            * **paths** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Path**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Path**]**,* *optional*) – Where to save the rois. If a string or a Path, a directory will
              be created if it does not exist yet and screenshots will be
              saved with filename roi\_{n}.png where n is the nth roi. If
              paths is a list of either string or paths, these need to be the
              full paths of where to store each individual roi. In this case
              the length of the list and the number of rois must match.
              If None, the screenshots will only be returned and not saved
              to disk.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the screenshot.
              By default, uses the displayed scale.
        Returns:
        :   **screenshot\_list** – The list with roi screenshots.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    geometry() → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.geometry)[#](#napari.window.Window.geometry "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the geometry of the widget
        Returns:
        :   * **left** (*int*) – X coordinate of the upper left border.
            * **top** (*int*) – Y coordinate of the upper left border.
            * **width** (*int*) – Width of the rectangle shape of the window.
            * **height** (*int*) – Height of the rectangle shape of the window.
    remove\_dock\_widget(*widget: QWidget*, *menu=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.remove_dock_widget)[#](#napari.window.Window.remove_dock_widget "Link to this definition")
    :   Removes specified dock widget.
        If a QDockWidget is not provided, the existing QDockWidgets will be
        searched for one whose inner widget (`.widget()`) is the provided
        `widget`.
        Parameters:
        :   * **widget** (*QWidget* *|* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – If widget == ‘all’, all docked widgets will be removed.
            * **menu** (*QMenu**,* *optional*) – Menu bar to remove toggle action from. If None nothing removed
              from menu.
    resize(*width*, *height*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.resize)[#](#napari.window.Window.resize "Link to this definition")
    :   Resize the window.
        Parameters:
        :   * **width** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Width in logical pixels.
            * **height** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Height in logical pixels.
    screenshot(*path=None*, *size=None*, *scale=None*, *flash=True*, *canvas\_only=False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.screenshot)[#](#napari.window.Window.screenshot "Link to this definition")
    :   Take currently displayed viewer and convert to an image array.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Path*) – Filename for saving screenshot image.
            * **size** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *(*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – Size (resolution) of the screenshot. By default, the currently displayed size.
              Only used if canvas\_only is True.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the screenshot.
              By default, the currently displayed resolution.
              Only used if canvas\_only is True.
            * **flash** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to indicate whether flash animation should be shown after
              the screenshot was captured.
            * **canvas\_only** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, screenshot shows only the image display canvas, and
              if False includes the napari viewer frame in the screenshot,
              By default, True.
        Returns:
        :   **image** – Numpy array of type ubyte and shape (h, w, 4). Index [0, 0] is the
            upper-left corner of the rendered region.
        Return type:
        :   array
    set\_geometry(*left*, *top*, *width*, *height*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.set_geometry)[#](#napari.window.Window.set_geometry "Link to this definition")
    :   Set the geometry of the widget
        Parameters:
        :   * **left** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – X coordinate of the upper left border.
            * **top** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Y coordinate of the upper left border.
            * **width** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Width of the rectangle shape of the window.
            * **height** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Height of the rectangle shape of the window.
    show(*\**, *block=False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.show)[#](#napari.window.Window.show "Link to this definition")
    :   Resize, show, and bring forward the window.
        Raises:
        :   [**RuntimeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#RuntimeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the viewer.window has already been closed and deleted.

napari.utils.transforms.shear\_matrix\_from\_angle(*angle*, *ndim=3*, *axes=(-1, 0)*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transform_utils.html#shear_matrix_from_angle)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.shear_matrix_from_angle "Link to this definition")
:   Create a shear matrix from an angle.
    Parameters:
    :   * **angle** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Angle in degrees.
        * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Dimensionality of the shear matrix
        * **axes** (*2-tuple* *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Location of the angle in the shear matrix.
          Default is the lower left value.
    Returns:
    :   **matrix** – Shear matrix with ones along the main diagonal
    Return type:
    :   np.ndarray
