<!-- Source: https://napari.org/stable/api/napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html -->
* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* [napari.utils.transforms](napari.utils.transforms.html)
* napari.utils.transforms.Affine
# napari.utils.transforms.Affine[#](#napari-utils-transforms-affine "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.transforms.Affine(*scale=(1.0, 1.0)*, *translate=(0.0, 0.0)*, *\**, *affine\_matrix=None*, *axis\_labels: Sequence[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *linear\_matrix=None*, *name=None*, *ndim=None*, *rotate=None*, *shear=None*, *units: Sequence[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | pint.Unit] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Affine)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Transform`](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Transform")
    n-dimensional affine transformation class.
    The affine transform can be represented as a n+1 dimensional
    transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates [[1]](#id3), an n
    dimensional matrix and a length n translation vector, or be
    composed and decomposed from scale, rotate, and shear
    transformations defined in the following order:
    rotate \* shear \* scale + translate
    The affine\_matrix representation can be used for easy compatibility
    with other libraries that can generate affine transformations.
    Parameters:
    :   * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          np.degrees if needed.
        * **scale** (*1-D array*) – A 1-D array of factors to scale each axis by. Scale is broadcast to 1
          in leading dimensions, so that, for example, a scale of [4, 18, 34] in
          3D can be used as a scale of [1, 4, 18, 34] in 4D without modification.
          An empty translation vector implies no scaling.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **translate** (*1-D array*) – A 1-D array of factors to shift each axis by. Translation is broadcast
          to 0 in leading dimensions, so that, for example, a translation of
          [4, 18, 34] in 3D can be used as a translation of [0, 4, 18, 34] in 4D
          without modification. An empty translation vector implies no
          translation.
        * **linear\_matrix** (*n-D array**,* *optional*) – (N, N) matrix with linear transform. If provided then scale, rotate,
          and shear values are ignored.
        * **affine\_matrix** (*n-D array**,* *optional*) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates [[1]](#id3).
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
          AffineTransform object. If provided then translate, scale, rotate, and
          shear values are ignored.
        * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – The dimensionality of the transform. If None, this is inferred from the
          other parameters.
        * **name** (*string*) – A string name for the transform.
    References
    [1]
    ([1](#id1),[2](#id2))
    <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Homogeneous_coordinates>.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`compose`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.compose "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.compose")(...) | Return the composite of this transform and the provided one. |
    | [`expand_dims`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.expand_dims "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.expand_dims")(axes) | Return a transform with added axes for non-visible dimensions. |
    | [`replace_slice`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.replace_slice "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.replace_slice")(axes, transform) | Returns a transform where the transform at the indicated n dimensions is replaced with another n-dimensional transform |
    | [`set_slice`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.set_slice "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.set_slice")(axes) | Return a transform subset to the visible dimensions. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`affine_matrix`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.affine_matrix "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.affine_matrix") | Return the affine matrix for the transform. |
    | [`axis_labels`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.axis_labels "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.axis_labels") | tuple of axis labels for the layer. |
    | `changed` |  |
    | [`inverse`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.inverse "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.inverse") | Return the inverse transform. |
    | [`linear_matrix`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.linear_matrix "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.linear_matrix") | Return the linear matrix of the transform. |
    | [`ndim`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.ndim "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.ndim") | Dimensionality of the transform. |
    | [`physical_scale`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.physical_scale "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.physical_scale") | Return the scale of the transform, with units. |
    | [`rotate`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.rotate "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.rotate") | Return the rotation of the transform. |
    | [`scale`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.scale "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.scale") | Return the scale of the transform. |
    | [`shear`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.shear "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.shear") | Return the shear of the transform. |
    | [`translate`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.translate "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.translate") | Return the translation of the transform. |
    | [`units`](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.units "napari.utils.transforms.Affine.units") | List of units for the layer. |
    Details
    property affine\_matrix: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.affine_matrix "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the affine matrix for the transform.
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    compose(*transform: [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")*) → [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Affine.compose)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.compose "Link to this definition")
    compose(*transform: [Transform](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Transform")*) → [Transform](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Transform")
    :   Return the composite of this transform and the provided one.
    expand\_dims(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Affine.expand_dims)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.expand_dims "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform with added axes for non-visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Location of axes to expand the current transform with. Passing a
            list allows expansion to occur at specific locations and for
            expand\_dims to be like an inverse to the set\_slice method.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Transform](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.Transform")
    property inverse: [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.inverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the inverse transform.
    property linear\_matrix: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.linear_matrix "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the linear matrix of the transform.
    property ndim: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimensionality of the transform.
    property physical\_scale: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[Quantity, ...][#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.physical_scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the scale of the transform, with units.
    replace\_slice(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *transform: [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")*) → [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Affine.replace_slice)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.replace_slice "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns a transform where the transform at the indicated n dimensions is replaced with another n-dimensional transform
        Parameters:
        :   * **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Axes where the transform will be replaced
            * **transform** ([*Affine*](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – The transform that will be inserted. Must have as many dimension as len(axes)
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[float64]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the rotation of the transform.
    property scale: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the scale of the transform.
    set\_slice(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Affine.set_slice)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.set_slice "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform subset to the visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Axes to subset the current transform with.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Affine](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property shear: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.shear "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the shear of the transform.
    property translate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.Affine.translate "Link to this definition")

* napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine
# napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine[#](#napari-utils-transforms-compositeaffine "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine(*scale=(1, 1)*, *translate=(0, 0)*, *\**, *axis\_labels=None*, *rotate=None*, *shear=None*, *ndim=None*, *name=None*, *units=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#CompositeAffine)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Affine`](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")
    n-dimensional affine transformation composed from more basic components.
    Composition is in the following order
    rotate \* shear \* scale + translate
    Parameters:
    :   * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          np.degrees if needed.
        * **scale** (*1-D array*) – A 1-D array of factors to scale each axis by. Scale is broadcast to 1
          in leading dimensions, so that, for example, a scale of [4, 18, 34] in
          3D can be used as a scale of [1, 4, 18, 34] in 4D without modification.
          An empty translation vector implies no scaling.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
          ones along the main diagonal.
        * **translate** (*1-D array*) – A 1-D array of factors to shift each axis by. Translation is broadcast
          to 0 in leading dimensions, so that, for example, a translation of
          [4, 18, 34] in 3D can be used as a translation of [0, 4, 18, 34] in 4D
          without modification. An empty translation vector implies no
          translation.
        * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – The dimensionality of the transform. If None, this is inferred from the
          other parameters.
        * **name** (*string*) – A string name for the transform.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`compose`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.compose "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.compose")(transform) | Return the composite of this transform and the provided one. |
    | [`expand_dims`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.expand_dims "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.expand_dims")(axes) | Return a transform with added axes for non-visible dimensions. |
    | [`replace_slice`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.replace_slice "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.replace_slice")(axes, transform) | Returns a transform where the transform at the indicated n dimensions is replaced with another n-dimensional transform |
    | [`set_slice`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.set_slice "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.set_slice")(axes) | Return a transform subset to the visible dimensions. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`affine_matrix`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.affine_matrix "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.affine_matrix") | Return the affine matrix for the transform. |
    | [`axis_labels`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.axis_labels "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.axis_labels") | tuple of axis labels for the layer. |
    | `changed` |  |
    | [`inverse`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.inverse "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.inverse") | Return the inverse transform. |
    | [`linear_matrix`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.linear_matrix "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.linear_matrix") | Return the linear matrix of the transform. |
    | [`ndim`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.ndim "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.ndim") | Dimensionality of the transform. |
    | [`physical_scale`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.physical_scale "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.physical_scale") | Return the scale of the transform, with units. |
    | [`rotate`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.rotate "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.rotate") | Return the rotation of the transform. |
    | [`scale`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.scale "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.scale") | Return the scale of the transform. |
    | [`shear`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.shear "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.shear") | Return the shear of the transform. |
    | [`translate`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.translate "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.translate") | Return the translation of the transform. |
    | [`units`](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.units "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.units") | List of units for the layer. |
    Details
    property affine\_matrix[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.affine_matrix "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the affine matrix for the transform.
    property axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   tuple of axis labels for the layer.
    compose(*transform*)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.compose "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the composite of this transform and the provided one.
    expand\_dims(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [CompositeAffine](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#CompositeAffine.expand_dims)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.expand_dims "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform with added axes for non-visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Location of axes to expand the current transform with. Passing a
            list allows expansion to occur at specific locations and for
            expand\_dims to be like an inverse to the set\_slice method.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Transform](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.Transform")
    property inverse: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.inverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the inverse transform.
    property linear\_matrix[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.linear_matrix "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the linear matrix of the transform.
    property ndim: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimensionality of the transform.
    property physical\_scale: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[Quantity, ...][#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.physical_scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the scale of the transform, with units.
    replace\_slice(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *transform: [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")*) → [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.replace_slice "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns a transform where the transform at the indicated n dimensions is replaced with another n-dimensional transform
        Parameters:
        :   * **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Axes where the transform will be replaced
            * **transform** ([*Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – The transform that will be inserted. Must have as many dimension as len(axes)
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property rotate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.rotate "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the rotation of the transform.
    property scale: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.scale "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the scale of the transform.
    set\_slice(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [CompositeAffine](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine "napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#CompositeAffine.set_slice)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.set_slice "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform subset to the visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Axes to subset the current transform with.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Affine](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")
    property shear: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.shear "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the shear of the transform.
    property translate: [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)"), ...], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[\_ScalarT]][#](#napari.utils.transforms.CompositeAffine.translate "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the translation of the transform.

* [API](index.html)

* napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate
# napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate[#](#napari-utils-transforms-scaletranslate "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate(*scale=(1.0,)*, *translate=(0.0,)*, *\**, *name=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#ScaleTranslate)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Transform`](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Transform")
    n-dimensional scale and translation (shift) class.
    Scaling is always applied before translation.
    Parameters:
    :   * **scale** (*1-D array*) – A 1-D array of factors to scale each axis by. Scale is broadcast to 1
          in leading dimensions, so that, for example, a scale of [4, 18, 34] in
          3D can be used as a scale of [1, 4, 18, 34] in 4D without modification.
          An empty translation vector implies no scaling.
        * **translate** (*1-D array*) – A 1-D array of factors to shift each axis by. Translation is broadcast
          to 0 in leading dimensions, so that, for example, a translation of
          [4, 18, 34] in 3D can be used as a translation of [0, 4, 18, 34] in 4D
          without modification. An empty translation vector implies no
          translation.
        * **name** (*string*) – A string name for the transform.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`compose`](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.compose "napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.compose")(transform) | Return the composite of this transform and the provided one. |
    | [`expand_dims`](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.expand_dims "napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.expand_dims")(axes) | Return a transform with added axes for non-visible dimensions. |
    | [`set_slice`](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.set_slice "napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.set_slice")(axes) | Return a transform subset to the visible dimensions. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `changed` |  |
    | [`inverse`](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.inverse "napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.inverse") | Return the inverse transform. |
    Details
    compose(*transform: [Transform](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Transform")*) → [Transform](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Transform")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#ScaleTranslate.compose)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.compose "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the composite of this transform and the provided one.
    expand\_dims(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [ScaleTranslate](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate "napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#ScaleTranslate.expand_dims)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.expand_dims "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform with added axes for non-visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Location of axes to expand the current transform with. Passing a
            list allows expansion to occur at specific locations and for
            expand\_dims to be like an inverse to the set\_slice method.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Transform](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.Transform")
    property inverse: [ScaleTranslate](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.ScaleTranslate")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.inverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the inverse transform.
    set\_slice(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [ScaleTranslate](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate "napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#ScaleTranslate.set_slice)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.ScaleTranslate.set_slice "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform subset to the visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Axes to subset the current transform with.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Transform](napari.utils.transforms.Transform.html#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.Transform")

# napari.utils.transforms.Transform[#](#napari-utils-transforms-transform "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.transforms.Transform(*func=<function identity>*, *inverse=None*, *name=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Transform)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`object`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")
    Base transform class.
    Defaults to the identity transform.
    Parameters:
    :   * **func** (*callable**,* *Coords -> Coords*) – A function converting an NxD array of coordinates to NxD’.
        * **name** (*string*) – A string name for the transform.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`compose`](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform.compose "napari.utils.transforms.Transform.compose")(transform) | Return the composite of this transform and the provided one. |
    | [`expand_dims`](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform.expand_dims "napari.utils.transforms.Transform.expand_dims")(axes) | Return a transform with added axes for non-visible dimensions. |
    | [`set_slice`](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform.set_slice "napari.utils.transforms.Transform.set_slice")(axes) | Return a transform subset to the visible dimensions. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `changed` |  |
    | `inverse` |  |
    Details
    compose(*transform: [Transform](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Transform")*) → [Transform](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.Transform")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Transform.compose)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform.compose "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the composite of this transform and the provided one.
    expand\_dims(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [Transform](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.Transform")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Transform.expand_dims)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform.expand_dims "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform with added axes for non-visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Location of axes to expand the current transform with. Passing a
            list allows expansion to occur at specific locations and for
            expand\_dims to be like an inverse to the set\_slice method.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Transform](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.Transform")
    set\_slice(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [Transform](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.Transform")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#Transform.set_slice)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform.set_slice "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform subset to the visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Axes to subset the current transform with.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform.
        Return type:
        :   [Transform](#napari.utils.transforms.Transform "napari.utils.transforms.Transform")

    :   Return a shallow copy of the list.
    count(*value*) → integer -- return number of occurrences of value[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.count "Link to this definition")
    expand\_dims(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [TransformChain](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain "napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#TransformChain.expand_dims)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.expand_dims "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform chain with added axes for non-visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Location of axes to expand the current transform with. Passing a
            list allows expansion to occur at specific locations and for
            expand\_dims to be like an inverse to the set\_slice method.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform chain.
        Return type:
        :   [TransformChain](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain "napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain")
    extend(*values*)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.extend "Link to this definition")
    :   S.extend(iterable) – extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable
    index(*value: \_L*, *start: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*, *stop: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.index "Link to this definition")
    :   Return first index of value.
        Parameters:
        :   * **value** (*Any*) – A value to lookup. If type(value) is in the lookups functions
              provided for this class, then values in the list will be searched
              using the corresponding lookup converter function.
            * **start** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The starting index to search, by default 0
            * **stop** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The ending index to search, by default None
        Returns:
        :   The index of the value
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the value is not present
    insert(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.insert "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert `value` before index.
    property inverse: [TransformChain](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain "napari.utils.transforms.transforms.TransformChain")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.inverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the inverse transform chain.
    move(*src\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.move "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert object at `src_index` before `dest_index`.
        Both indices refer to the list prior to any object removal
        (pre-move space).
    move\_multiple(*sources: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [slice](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.move_multiple "Link to this definition")
    :   Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination.
        Note, if dest\_index is higher than any of the sources, then
        the resulting position of the moved objects after the move operation
        is complete will be lower than dest\_index.
        Parameters:
        :   * **sources** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*slice*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – A sequence of indices
            * **dest\_index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The destination index. All sources will be inserted before this
              index (in pre-move space), by default 0… which has the effect of
              “bringing to front” everything in `sources`, or acting as a
              “reorder” method if `sources` contains all indices.
        Returns:
        :   The number of successful move operations completed.
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the destination index is a slice, or any of the source indices
            are not `int` or `slice`.
    pop([*index*]) → item -- remove and return item at index (default last).[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range.
    remove(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   S.remove(value) – remove first occurrence of value.
        Raise ValueError if the value is not present.
    reverse() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.reverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Reverse list *IN PLACE*.
    set\_slice(*axes: Sequence[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]*) → [TransformChain](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain "napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/transforms/transforms.html#TransformChain.set_slice)[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.set_slice "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a transform chain subset to the visible dimensions.
        Parameters:
        :   **axes** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Axes to subset the current transform chain with.
        Returns:
        :   Resulting transform chain.
        Return type:
        :   [TransformChain](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain "napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain")
    property simplified: \_T[#](#napari.utils.transforms.TransformChain.simplified "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the composite of the transforms inside the transform chain.
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the transform chain is empty.

<!-- Source: https://napari.org/stable/api/napari.components.html -->
* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* napari.components
# napari.components[#](#module-napari.components "Link to this heading")
napari.components provides the public-facing models for widgets
and other utilities that the user will be able to programmatically interact
with.
## Classes[#](#classes "Link to this heading")
Dims
:   Current indices along each data dimension, together with which dimensions
    are being displayed, projected, sliced…
LayerList
:   List of layers currently present in the viewer.
ViewerModel
:   Data viewer displaying the currently rendered scene and
    layer-related controls.
Classes
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`Camera`](napari.components.Camera.html#napari.components.Camera "napari.components.Camera") | Camera object modeling position and view of the camera. |
| [`Dims`](napari.components.Dims.html#napari.components.Dims "napari.components.Dims") | Dimensions object modeling slicing and displaying. |
| [`LayerList`](napari.components.LayerList.html#napari.components.LayerList "napari.components.LayerList") | List-like layer collection with built-in reordering and callback hooks. |
| [`ViewerModel`](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel") | Viewer containing the rendered scene, layers, and controlling elements including dimension sliders, and control bars for color limits. |
[previous
napari.plugins](napari.plugins.html "previous page")
[next
napari.components.Camera](napari.components.Camera.html "next page")

# napari.components.Camera[#](#napari-components-camera "Link to this heading")
class napari.components.Camera(*\**, *center: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] = (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)*, *zoom: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1.0*, *angles: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] = (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)*, *perspective: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*, *mouse\_pan: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *mouse\_zoom: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *orientation: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[DepthAxisOrientation, VerticalAxisOrientation, HorizontalAxisOrientation] = (DepthAxisOrientation.TOWARDS, VerticalAxisOrientation.DOWN, HorizontalAxisOrientation.RIGHT)*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/camera.html#Camera)[#](#napari.components.Camera "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`EventedModel`](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel")
    Camera object modeling position and view of the camera.
    center[#](#napari.components.Camera.center "Link to this definition")
    :   Center of rotation for the camera.
        In 2D viewing the last two values are used.
        Type:
        :   3-tuple
    zoom[#](#napari.components.Camera.zoom "Link to this definition")
    :   Scale from canvas pixels to world pixels.
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    angles[#](#napari.components.Camera.angles "Link to this definition")
    :   Euler angles of camera in 3D viewing (rx, ry, rz), in degrees.
        Only used during 3D viewing.
        Euler angles in 3D do not uniquely represent an orientation, so
        different angle triplets can produce the same view.
        Stored or returned angle values may differ from those that were set,
        while still representing an equivalent camera orientation.
        Type:
        :   3-tuple
    perspective[#](#napari.components.Camera.perspective "Link to this definition")
    :   Perspective (aka “field of view” in vispy) of the camera (if 3D).
        Type:
        :   [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    mouse\_pan[#](#napari.components.Camera.mouse_pan "Link to this definition")
    :   If the camera interactive panning with the mouse is enabled or not.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    mouse\_zoom[#](#napari.components.Camera.mouse_zoom "Link to this definition")
    :   If the camera interactive zooming with the mouse is enabled or not.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`calculate_nd_up_direction`](#napari.components.Camera.calculate_nd_up_direction "napari.components.Camera.calculate_nd_up_direction")(ndim, dims\_displayed) | Calculate the nD up direction vector of the camera. |
    | [`calculate_nd_view_direction`](#napari.components.Camera.calculate_nd_view_direction "napari.components.Camera.calculate_nd_view_direction")(ndim, dims\_displayed) | Calculate the nD view direction vector of the camera. |
    | `construct`([\_fields\_set]) |  |
    | [`copy`](#napari.components.Camera.copy "napari.components.Camera.copy")(\*[, include, exclude, update, deep]) | Returns a copy of the model. |
    | `dict`(\*[, include, exclude, by\_alias, ...]) |  |
    | [`enums_as_values`](#napari.components.Camera.enums_as_values "napari.components.Camera.enums_as_values")([as\_values]) | Temporarily override how enums are retrieved. |
    | `from_orm`(obj) |  |
    | `json`(\*[, include, exclude, by\_alias, ...]) |  |
    | [`model_construct`](#napari.components.Camera.model_construct "napari.components.Camera.model_construct")([\_fields\_set]) | Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data. |
    | [`model_copy`](#napari.components.Camera.model_copy "napari.components.Camera.model_copy")(\*[, update, deep]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_dump`](#napari.components.Camera.model_dump "napari.components.Camera.model_dump")(\*[, mode, include, exclude, ...]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_dump_json`](#napari.components.Camera.model_dump_json "napari.components.Camera.model_dump_json")(\*[, indent, ensure\_ascii, ...]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_json_schema`](#napari.components.Camera.model_json_schema "napari.components.Camera.model_json_schema")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | Generate a JSON schema for this model. |
    | [`model_parametrized_name`](#napari.components.Camera.model_parametrized_name "napari.components.Camera.model_parametrized_name")(params) | Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes. |
    | [`model_post_init`](#napari.components.Camera.model_post_init "napari.components.Camera.model_post_init")(context, /) | This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes. |
    | [`model_rebuild`](#napari.components.Camera.model_rebuild "napari.components.Camera.model_rebuild")(\*[, force, raise\_errors, ...]) | Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model. |
    | [`model_validate`](#napari.components.Camera.model_validate "napari.components.Camera.model_validate")(obj, \*[, strict, extra, ...]) | Validate a pydantic model instance. |
    | [`model_validate_json`](#napari.components.Camera.model_validate_json "napari.components.Camera.model_validate_json")(json\_data, \*[, strict, ...]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_validate_strings`](#napari.components.Camera.model_validate_strings "napari.components.Camera.model_validate_strings")(obj, \*[, strict, ...]) | Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model. |
    | `parse_file`(path, \*[, content\_type, ...]) |  |
    | `parse_obj`(obj) |  |

    copy(*\**, *include: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *exclude: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *update: Dict[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Any] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → Self[#](#napari.components.Camera.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! warning “Deprecated”
        :   This method is now deprecated; use model\_copy instead.
        If you need include or exclude, use:
        `` `python {test="skip" lint="skip"}
        data = self.model_dump(include=include, exclude=exclude, round_trip=True)
        data = {**data, **(update or {})}
        copied = self.model_validate(data)
        ` ``
        Parameters:
        :   * **include** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to include in the copied model.
            * **exclude** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to exclude in the copied model.
            * **update** – Optional dictionary of field-value pairs to override field values in the copied model.
            * **deep** – If True, the values of fields that are Pydantic models will be deep-copied.
        Returns:
        :   A copy of the model with included, excluded and updated fields as specified.

    classmethod model\_construct(*\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*values: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_construct "Link to this definition")
    :   Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data.
        Creates a new model setting \_\_dict\_\_ and \_\_pydantic\_fields\_set\_\_ from trusted or pre-validated data.
        Default values are respected, but no other validation is performed.
        !!! note
        :   model\_construct() generally respects the model\_config.extra setting on the provided model.
            That is, if model\_config.extra == ‘allow’, then all extra passed values are added to the model instance’s \_\_dict\_\_
            and \_\_pydantic\_extra\_\_ fields. If model\_config.extra == ‘ignore’ (the default), then all extra passed values are ignored.
            Because no validation is performed with a call to model\_construct(), having model\_config.extra == ‘forbid’ does not result in
            an error if extra values are passed, but they will be ignored.
        Parameters:
        :   * **\_fields\_set** – A set of field names that were originally explicitly set during instantiation. If provided,
              this is directly used for the [model\_fields\_set][pydantic.BaseModel.model\_fields\_set] attribute.
              Otherwise, the field names from the values argument will be used.
            * **values** – Trusted or pre-validated data dictionary.
        Returns:
        :   A new instance of the Model class with validated data.
    model\_copy(*\**, *update: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_copy "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_copy](../concepts/models.md#model-copy)
        Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! note
        :   The underlying instance’s [\_\_dict\_\_][object.\_\_dict\_\_] attribute is copied. This
            might have unexpected side effects if you store anything in it, on top of the model
            fields (e.g. the value of [cached properties][functools.cached\_property]).
        Parameters:
        :   * **update** – Values to change/add in the new model. Note: the data is not validated
              before creating the new model. You should trust this data.
            * **deep** – Set to True to make a deep copy of the model.
        Returns:
        :   New model instance.

    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump](../concepts/serialization.md#python-mode)
        Generate a dictionary representation of the model, optionally specifying which fields to include or exclude.
        Parameters:
        :   * **mode** – The mode in which to\_python should run.
              If mode is ‘json’, the output will only contain JSON serializable types.
              If mode is ‘python’, the output may contain non-JSON-serializable Python objects.
            * **include** – A set of fields to include in the output.
            * **exclude** – A set of fields to exclude from the output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias in the dictionary key if defined.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary representation of the model.

v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_dump_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump\_json](../concepts/serialization.md#json-mode)
        Generates a JSON representation of the model using Pydantic’s to\_json method.
        Parameters:
        :   * **indent** – Indentation to use in the JSON output. If None is passed, the output will be compact.
            * **ensure\_ascii** – If True, the output is guaranteed to have all incoming non-ASCII characters escaped.
              If False (the default), these characters will be output as-is.
            * **include** – Field(s) to include in the JSON output.
            * **exclude** – Field(s) to exclude from the JSON output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to serialize using field aliases.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A JSON string representation of the model.
    property model\_extra: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_extra "Link to this definition")
    :   Get extra fields set during validation.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary of extra fields, or None if config.extra is not set to “allow”.
    property model\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.components.Camera.model_fields_set "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns the set of fields that have been explicitly set on this model instance.
        Returns:
        :   A set of strings representing the fields that have been set,
            :   i.e. that were not filled from defaults.
    classmethod model\_json\_schema(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.components.Camera.model_json_schema "Link to this definition")
    :   Generate a JSON schema for this model.
        This is required to prevent from mail formated docstring break docs build.
    classmethod model\_parametrized\_name(*params: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], ...]*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_parametrized_name "Link to this definition")
    :   Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes.
        This method can be overridden to achieve a custom naming scheme for generic BaseModels.
        Parameters:
        :   **params** – Tuple of types of the class. Given a generic class
            Model with 2 type variables and a concrete model Model[str, int],
            the value (str, int) would be passed to params.
        Returns:
        :   String representing the new class where params are passed to cls as type variables.
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – Raised when trying to generate concrete names for non-generic models.
    model\_post\_init(*context: Any*, */*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_post_init "Link to this definition")
    :   This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes.
        It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.
        Parameters:
        :   * **self** – The BaseModel instance.
            * **context** – The context.
    classmethod model\_rebuild(*\**, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *raise\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *\_parent\_namespace\_depth: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *\_types\_namespace: MappingNamespace | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_rebuild "Link to this definition")
    :   Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model.
        This may be necessary when one of the annotations is a ForwardRef which could not be resolved during
        the initial attempt to build the schema, and automatic rebuilding fails.
        Parameters:
        :   * **force** – Whether to force the rebuilding of the model schema, defaults to False.
            * **raise\_errors** – Whether to raise errors, defaults to True.
            * **\_parent\_namespace\_depth** – The depth level of the parent namespace, defaults to 2.
            * **\_types\_namespace** – The types namespace, defaults to None.
        Returns:
        :   Returns None if the schema is already “complete” and rebuilding was not required.
            If rebuilding \_was\_ required, returns True if rebuilding was successful, otherwise False.
    classmethod model\_validate(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *from\_attributes: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_validate "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate a pydantic model instance.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **from\_attributes** – Whether to extract data from object attributes.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If the object could not be validated.
        Returns:
        :   The validated model instance.
    classmethod model\_validate\_json(*json\_data: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytearray](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytearray "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_validate_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [JSON Parsing](../concepts/json.md#json-parsing)
        Validate the given JSON data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **json\_data** – The JSON data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If json\_data is not a JSON string or the object could not be validated.
    classmethod model\_validate\_strings(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.model_validate_strings "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object containing string data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
    reset()[#](#napari.components.Camera.reset "Link to this definition")
    :   Reset the state of the model to default values.
    set\_view\_direction(*view\_direction: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *up\_direction: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")] = (0, -1, 0)*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/camera.html#Camera.set_view_direction)[#](#napari.components.Camera.set_view_direction "Link to this definition")
    :   Set camera angles from direction vectors.
        Both the view direction and the up direction are specified in 3D scene
        coordinates, the world coordinate system for three currently displayed
        dimensions.
        The provided up direction must not be parallel to the provided
        view direction. The provided up direction does not need to be orthogonal
        to the view direction. The final up direction will be a vector orthogonal
        to the view direction, aligned with the provided up direction.
        Parameters:
        :   * **view\_direction** (*3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – The desired view direction vector in 3D scene coordinates, the world
              coordinate system for three currently displayed dimensions.
            * **up\_direction** (*3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – A direction vector which will point upwards on the canvas. Defaults
              to (0, -1, 0) unless the view direction is parallel to the y-axis,
              in which case will default to (-1, 0, 0).
    property up\_direction: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.components.Camera.up_direction "Link to this definition")
    :   3D direction vector pointing up on the canvas.
        Up direction is calculated from the Euler angles and returned as a
        3-tuple. This direction is in 3D scene coordinates, the world coordinate
        system for three currently displayed dimensions.
    update(*values: [EventedModel](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*, *recurse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Camera.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update a model in place.
        Parameters:
        :   * **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.events.EventedModel*](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.EventedModel")) – Values to update the model with. If an EventedModel is passed it is
              first converted to a dictionary. The keys of this dictionary must
              be found as attributes on the current model.
            * **recurse** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, recursively update fields that are EventedModels.
              Otherwise, just update the immediate fields of this EventedModel,
              which is useful when the declared field type (e.g. `Union`) can have
              different realized types with different fields.
    property view\_direction: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.components.Camera.view_direction "Link to this definition")
    :   3D view direction vector of the camera.
        View direction is calculated from the Euler angles and returned as a
        3-tuple. This direction is in 3D scene coordinates, the world coordinate
        system for three currently displayed dimensions.
