# napari.components.Dims[#](#napari-components-dims "Link to this heading")
class napari.components.Dims(*\**, *ndim: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *ndisplay: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")[2, 3] = 2*, *order: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] = ()*, *axis\_labels: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] = ()*, *rollable: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] = ()*, *range: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[RangeTuple, ...] = ()*, *margin\_left: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] = ()*, *margin\_right: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] = ()*, *point: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] = ()*, *units: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[Unit, ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *last\_used: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/dims.html#Dims)[#](#napari.components.Dims "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`EventedModel`](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel")
    Dimensions object modeling slicing and displaying.
    Parameters:
    :   * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Number of dimensions.
        * **ndisplay** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Number of displayed dimensions.
        * **range** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – List of tuples (min, max, step), one for each dimension in world
          coordinates space. Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
        * **point** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *floats*) – Dims position in world coordinates for each dimension.
        * **margin\_left** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *floats*) – Left margin in world pixels of the slice for each dimension.
        * **margin\_right** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *floats*) – Right margin in world pixels of the slice for each dimension.
        * **order** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Tuple of ordering the dimensions, where the last dimensions are rendered.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Tuple of labels for each dimension.
        * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Shared world units for each dimension.
          If `None`, no additional unit conversion is applied.
        * **last\_used** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Dimension which was last interacted with.
    ndim[#](#napari.components.Dims.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Number of dimensions.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    ndisplay[#](#napari.components.Dims.ndisplay "Link to this definition")
    :   Number of displayed dimensions.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    range[#](#napari.components.Dims.range "Link to this definition")
    :   List of tuples (min, max, step), one for each dimension in world
        coordinates space. Lower and upper bounds are inclusive.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of 3-tuple of [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")
    point[#](#napari.components.Dims.point "Link to this definition")
    :   Dims position in world coordinates for each dimension.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of floats
    margin\_left[#](#napari.components.Dims.margin_left "Link to this definition")
    :   Left margin (=thickness) in world pixels of the slice for each dimension.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of floats
    margin\_right[#](#napari.components.Dims.margin_right "Link to this definition")
    :   Right margin (=thickness) in world pixels of the slice for each dimension.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of floats
    order[#](#napari.components.Dims.order "Link to this definition")
    :   Tuple of ordering the dimensions, where the last dimensions are rendered.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    axis\_labels[#](#napari.components.Dims.axis_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Tuple of labels for each dimension.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    units[#](#napari.components.Dims.units "Link to this definition")
    :   Shared world units for each dimension.
        If `None`, no additional unit conversion is applied.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of pint.Unit or None
    last\_used[#](#napari.components.Dims.last_used "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimension which was last used.
        Tuple the slider position for each dims slider, in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    current\_step[#](#napari.components.Dims.current_step "Link to this definition")
    :   Current step for each dimension (same as point, but in slider coordinates).
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    nsteps[#](#napari.components.Dims.nsteps "Link to this definition")
    :   Number of steps available to each slider. These are calculated from
        the `range`.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    thickness[#](#napari.components.Dims.thickness "Link to this definition")
    :   Thickness of the slice (sum of both margins) for each dimension in world coordinates.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of floats
    displayed[#](#napari.components.Dims.displayed "Link to this definition")
    :   List of dimensions that are displayed. These are calculated from the
        `order` and `ndisplay`.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    not\_displayed[#](#napari.components.Dims.not_displayed "Link to this definition")
    :   List of dimensions that are not displayed. These are calculated from the
        `order` and `ndisplay`.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    displayed\_order[#](#napari.components.Dims.displayed_order "Link to this definition")
    :   Order of only displayed dimensions. These are calculated from the
        `displayed` dimensions.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    rollable[#](#napari.components.Dims.rollable "Link to this definition")
    :   Tuple of axis roll state. If True the axis is rollable.
        Type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") of [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")

    Details
    copy(*\**, *include: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *exclude: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *update: Dict[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Any] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → Self[#](#napari.components.Dims.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! warning “Deprecated”
        :   This method is now deprecated; use model\_copy instead.
        If you need include or exclude, use:
        `` `python {test="skip" lint="skip"}
        data = self.model_dump(include=include, exclude=exclude, round_trip=True)
        data = {**data, **(update or {})}
        copied = self.model_validate(data)
        ` ``
        Parameters:
        :   * **include** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to include in the copied model.
            * **exclude** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to exclude in the copied model.
            * **update** – Optional dictionary of field-value pairs to override field values in the copied model.
            * **deep** – If True, the values of fields that are Pydantic models will be deep-copied.
        Returns:
        :   A copy of the model with included, excluded and updated fields as specified.
    property displayed: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#id0 "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimensions that are displayed.
        Type:
        :   Tuple

    model\_config = {'arbitrary\_types\_allowed': True, 'json\_encoders': {<class 'app\_model.types.\_keys.\_keybindings.KeyBinding'>: <function <lambda>>, <class 'napari.layers.utils.color\_encoding.ColorEncoding'>: <function StyleEncoding.\_json\_encode>, <class 'napari.layers.utils.string\_encoding.StringEncoding'>: <function StyleEncoding.\_json\_encode>, <class 'napari.settings.\_fields.Version'>: <function Version.\_json\_encode>, <class 'numpy.ndarray'>: <function <lambda>>}, 'validate\_assignment': True, 'validate\_default': True}[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_config "Link to this definition")
    :   Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].
    classmethod model\_construct(*\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*values: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_construct "Link to this definition")
    :   Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data.
        Creates a new model setting \_\_dict\_\_ and \_\_pydantic\_fields\_set\_\_ from trusted or pre-validated data.
        Default values are respected, but no other validation is performed.
        !!! note
        :   model\_construct() generally respects the model\_config.extra setting on the provided model.
            That is, if model\_config.extra == ‘allow’, then all extra passed values are added to the model instance’s \_\_dict\_\_
            and \_\_pydantic\_extra\_\_ fields. If model\_config.extra == ‘ignore’ (the default), then all extra passed values are ignored.
            Because no validation is performed with a call to model\_construct(), having model\_config.extra == ‘forbid’ does not result in
            an error if extra values are passed, but they will be ignored.
        Parameters:
        :   * **\_fields\_set** – A set of field names that were originally explicitly set during instantiation. If provided,
              this is directly used for the [model\_fields\_set][pydantic.BaseModel.model\_fields\_set] attribute.
              Otherwise, the field names from the values argument will be used.
            * **values** – Trusted or pre-validated data dictionary.
        Returns:
        :   A new instance of the Model class with validated data.
    model\_copy(*\**, *update: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_copy "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_copy](../concepts/models.md#model-copy)
        Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! note
        :   The underlying instance’s [\_\_dict\_\_][object.\_\_dict\_\_] attribute is copied. This
            might have unexpected side effects if you store anything in it, on top of the model
            fields (e.g. the value of [cached properties][functools.cached\_property]).
        Parameters:
        :   * **update** – Values to change/add in the new model. Note: the data is not validated
              before creating the new model. You should trust this data.
            * **deep** – Set to True to make a deep copy of the model.
        Returns:
        :   New model instance.

    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump](../concepts/serialization.md#python-mode)
        Generate a dictionary representation of the model, optionally specifying which fields to include or exclude.
        Parameters:
        :   * **mode** – The mode in which to\_python should run.
              If mode is ‘json’, the output will only contain JSON serializable types.
              If mode is ‘python’, the output may contain non-JSON-serializable Python objects.
            * **include** – A set of fields to include in the output.
            * **exclude** – A set of fields to exclude from the output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias in the dictionary key if defined.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary representation of the model.
    model\_dump\_json(*\**, *indent: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")     :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump\_json](../concepts/serialization.md#json-mode)
        Generates a JSON representation of the model using Pydantic’s to\_json method.
        Parameters:
        :   * **indent** – Indentation to use in the JSON output. If None is passed, the output will be compact.
            * **ensure\_ascii** – If True, the output is guaranteed to have all incoming non-ASCII characters escaped.
              If False (the default), these characters will be output as-is.
            * **include** – Field(s) to include in the JSON output.
            * **exclude** – Field(s) to exclude from the JSON output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to serialize using field aliases.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A JSON string representation of the model.
    property model\_extra: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_extra "Link to this definition")
    :   Get extra fields set during validation.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary of extra fields, or None if config.extra is not set to “allow”.
    property model\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.components.Dims.model_fields_set "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns the set of fields that have been explicitly set on this model instance.
        Returns:
        :   A set of strings representing the fields that have been set,
            :   i.e. that were not filled from defaults.
    classmethod model\_json\_schema(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.components.Dims.model_json_schema "Link to this definition")
    :   Generate a JSON schema for this model.
        This is required to prevent from mail formated docstring break docs build.
    classmethod model\_parametrized\_name(*params: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], ...]*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_parametrized_name "Link to this definition")
    :   Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes.
        This method can be overridden to achieve a custom naming scheme for generic BaseModels.
        Parameters:
        :   **params** – Tuple of types of the class. Given a generic class
            Model with 2 type variables and a concrete model Model[str, int],
            the value (str, int) would be passed to params.
        Returns:
        :   String representing the new class where params are passed to cls as type variables.
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – Raised when trying to generate concrete names for non-generic models.
    model\_post\_init(*context: Any*, */*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_post_init "Link to this definition")
    :   This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes.
        It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.
        Parameters:
        :   * **self** – The BaseModel instance.
            * **context** – The context.
    classmethod model\_rebuild(*\**, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *raise\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *\_parent\_namespace\_depth: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *\_types\_namespace: MappingNamespace | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_rebuild "Link to this definition")
    :   Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model.
        This may be necessary when one of the annotations is a ForwardRef which could not be resolved during
        the initial attempt to build the schema, and automatic rebuilding fails.
        Parameters:
        :   * **force** – Whether to force the rebuilding of the model schema, defaults to False.
            * **raise\_errors** – Whether to raise errors, defaults to True.
            * **\_parent\_namespace\_depth** – The depth level of the parent namespace, defaults to 2.
            * **\_types\_namespace** – The types namespace, defaults to None.
        Returns:
        :   Returns None if the schema is already “complete” and rebuilding was not required.
            If rebuilding \_was\_ required, returns True if rebuilding was successful, otherwise False.
    classmethod model\_validate(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *from\_attributes: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_validate "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate a pydantic model instance.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **from\_attributes** – Whether to extract data from object attributes.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If the object could not be validated.
        Returns:
        :   The validated model instance.
    classmethod model\_validate\_json(*json\_data: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytearray](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytearray "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_validate_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [JSON Parsing](../concepts/json.md#json-parsing)
        Validate the given JSON data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **json\_data** – The JSON data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If json\_data is not a JSON string or the object could not be validated.
    classmethod model\_validate\_strings(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.model_validate_strings "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model.

            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.

            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.

    :   Transpose displayed dimensions.

        The order of the displayed is taken from Dims.order.
    update(*values: [EventedModel](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*, *recurse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.Dims.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update a model in place.
        Parameters:
        :   * **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.events.EventedModel*](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.EventedModel")) – Values to update the model with. If an EventedModel is passed it is
              first converted to a dictionary. The keys of this dictionary must
              be found as attributes on the current model.
            * **recurse** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, recursively update fields that are EventedModels.
              Otherwise, just update the immediate fields of this EventedModel,
              which is useful when the declared field type (e.g. `Union`) can have
              different realized types with different fields.

* napari.components.LayerList
# napari.components.LayerList[#](#napari-components-layerlist "Link to this heading")
class napari.components.LayerList(*data=()*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList)[#](#napari.components.LayerList "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`SelectableEventedList`](napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.html#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList "napari.utils.events.containers._selectable_list.SelectableEventedList")[[`Layer`](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")]
    List-like layer collection with built-in reordering and callback hooks.
    Parameters:
    :   **data** (*iterable*) – Iterable of napari.layer.Layer
    Events:
    :   * **inserting** (*(index: int)*) – emitted before an item is inserted at `index`
        * **inserted** (*(index: int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is inserted at `index`
        * **removing** (*(index: int)*) – emitted before an item is removed at `index`
        * **removed** (*(index: int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is removed at `index`
        * **moving** (*(index: int, new\_index: int)*) – emitted before an item is moved from `index` to `new_index`
        * **moved** (*(index: int, new\_index: int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is moved from `index` to `new_index`
        * **changed** (*(index: int, old\_value: T, value: T)*) – emitted when item at `index` is changed from `old_value` to
          `value`
        * **changed <OVERLOAD>** (*(index: slice, old\_value: List[\_T], value: List[\_T])*) – emitted when items at ``` index``es are changed from ``old_value ``` to
          `value`
        * **reordered** (*(value: self)*) – emitted when the list is reordered (eg. moved/reversed).
        * **renamed** (*(index: int)*) – emitted when a layer in the list is renamed, providing the index
          of the layer.
        * **selection.events.changed** (*(added: Set[\_T], removed: Set[\_T])*) – emitted when the set changes, includes item(s) that have been added
          and/or removed from the set.
        * **selection.events.active** (*(value: \_T)*) – emitted when the current item has changed.
        * **selection.events.\_current** (*(value: \_T)*) – emitted when the current item has changed. (Private event)
        * **units** (*(value: tuple[pint.Unit, …] | None)*) – emitted when the global overriding units for the layer list is changed.
    Notes
    Note that `changed` events are only emitted when an element of the
    list changes, *not* when the list itself changes (for example when items
    are added or removed). For example, `layerlist.append(layer)` will emit
    an `inserted` event. `layerlist[idx] = layer` *will* emit a `changed`
    event.
    However, the layerlist does not have a way of detecting when an object in
    the list is modified in-place. Therefore, although
    `layerlist[idx].scale = [2, 1, 1]` changes the *value* of the layer at
    position `idx`, a `changed` event will not be emitted.
    Examples
    ```
    >>> import napari
    >>> from skimage.data import astronaut
    >>> viewer = napari.Viewer()
    >>> event_list = []
    ```
    Connect to the event list:
    ```
    >>> viewer.layers.events.connect(event_list.append)
    <built-in method append of list object at 0x7fc225764780>
    ```
    ```
    >>> viewer.add_image(astronaut())
    <Image layer 'Image' at 0x7f0fe7fa9e90>
    >>> viewer.add_points()
    <Points layer 'Points' at 0x7f102f962350>
    >>> viewer.layers
    [<Image layer 'Image' at 0x7f0fe7fa9e90>, <Points layer 'Points' at 0x7f102f962350>]
    ```
    Inspecting the list of events, we see:
    ```
    >>> event_list[0].type
    'inserting'
    >>> viewer.layers.pop(1)
    <Points layer 'Points' at 0x7f102f962350>
    >>> event_list[-1].type
    'removed'
    ```
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`append`](#napari.components.LayerList.append "napari.components.LayerList.append")(value) | S.append(value) -- append value to the end of the sequence |
    | `batched_update`() |  |
    | [`clear`](#napari.components.LayerList.clear "napari.components.LayerList.clear")() | Remove all layers from viewer. |
    | [`copy`](#napari.components.LayerList.copy "napari.components.LayerList.copy")() | Return a shallow copy of the list. |
    | [`count`](#napari.components.LayerList.count "napari.components.LayerList.count")(value) |  |
    | [`extend`](#napari.components.LayerList.extend "napari.components.LayerList.extend")(values) | S.extend(iterable) -- extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable |
    | [`get_extent`](#napari.components.LayerList.get_extent "napari.components.LayerList.get_extent")(layers) | Return extent for a given layer list. |
    | [`index`](#napari.components.LayerList.index "napari.components.LayerList.index")(value[, start, stop]) | Return first index of value. |
    | [`insert`](#napari.components.LayerList.insert "napari.components.LayerList.insert")(index, value) | Insert `value` before index. |
    | [`link_layers`](#napari.components.LayerList.link_layers "napari.components.LayerList.link_layers")([layers, attributes]) | Links the selected layers. |
    | [`move`](#napari.components.LayerList.move "napari.components.LayerList.move")(src\_index[, dest\_index]) | Insert object at `src_index` before `dest_index`. |
    | [`move_multiple`](#napari.components.LayerList.move_multiple "napari.components.LayerList.move_multiple")(sources[, dest\_index]) | Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination. |
    | [`move_selected`](#napari.components.LayerList.move_selected "napari.components.LayerList.move_selected")(index, insert) | Reorder list by moving the item at index and inserting it at the insert index. |
    | [`pop`](#napari.components.LayerList.pop "napari.components.LayerList.pop")([index]) | Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range. |
    | [`remove`](#napari.components.LayerList.remove "napari.components.LayerList.remove")(value) | S.remove(value) -- remove first occurrence of value. |
    | [`remove_selected`](#napari.components.LayerList.remove_selected "napari.components.LayerList.remove_selected")() | Remove selected layers from LayerList, but first unlink them. |
    | [`reverse`](#napari.components.LayerList.reverse "napari.components.LayerList.reverse")() | Reverse list *IN PLACE*. |
    | [`save`](#napari.components.LayerList.save "napari.components.LayerList.save")(path, \*[, selected, plugin, \_writer]) | Save all or only selected layers to a path using writer plugins. |
    | [`select_all`](#napari.components.LayerList.select_all "napari.components.LayerList.select_all")() | Select all items in the list. |
    | [`select_next`](#napari.components.LayerList.select_next "napari.components.LayerList.select_next")([step, shift]) | Selects next item from list. |
    | [`select_previous`](#napari.components.LayerList.select_previous "napari.components.LayerList.select_previous")([shift]) | Selects previous item from list. |
    | [`toggle_selected_visibility`](#napari.components.LayerList.toggle_selected_visibility "napari.components.LayerList.toggle_selected_visibility")() | Toggle visibility of selected layers |
    | [`unlink_layers`](#napari.components.LayerList.unlink_layers "napari.components.LayerList.unlink_layers")([layers, attributes]) | Unlinks previously linked layers. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`extent`](#napari.components.LayerList.extent "napari.components.LayerList.extent") | Extent of layers in data and world coordinates. |
    | [`ndim`](#napari.components.LayerList.ndim "napari.components.LayerList.ndim") | Maximum dimensionality of layers. |
    | [`selection`](#napari.components.LayerList.selection "napari.components.LayerList.selection") | Get current selection. |
    | [`units`](#napari.components.LayerList.units "napari.components.LayerList.units") | Units of layers in world coordinates. |
    Details
    append(*value*)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.append "Link to this definition")
    :   S.append(value) – append value to the end of the sequence
    clear()[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList.clear)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.clear "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove all layers from viewer.
    copy() → [TypedMutableSequence](napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.html#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence "napari.utils.events.containers._typed.TypedMutableSequence")[\_T][#](#napari.components.LayerList.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a shallow copy of the list.
    count(*value*) → integer -- return number of occurrences of value[#](#napari.components.LayerList.count "Link to this definition")
    extend(*values*)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.extend "Link to this definition")
    :   S.extend(iterable) – extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable
    property extent: LayerListExtent[#](#napari.components.LayerList.extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Extent of layers in data and world coordinates.
        Extent bounds are inclusive. See Layer.extent for a detailed explanation
        of how extents are calculated.
    get\_extent(*layers: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")]*) → LayerListExtent[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList.get_extent)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.get_extent "Link to this definition")
    :   Return extent for a given layer list.
        Extent bounds are inclusive. This function is useful for calculating the extent
        of a subset of layers when preparing and updating some supplementary layers.
        For example see the cross Vectors layer in the multiple\_viewer\_widget example.
        Parameters:
        :   **layers** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*Layer*](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.Layer")) – list of layers for which extent should be calculated
        Returns:
        :   **extent** – extent for selected layers
        Return type:
        :   LayerListExtent
    index(*value: \_L*, *start: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*, *stop: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.index "Link to this definition")
    :   Return first index of value.
        Parameters:
        :   * **value** (*Any*) – A value to lookup. If type(value) is in the lookups functions
              provided for this class, then values in the list will be searched
              using the corresponding lookup converter function.
            * **start** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The starting index to search, by default 0
            * **stop** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The ending index to search, by default None
        Returns:
        :   The index of the value
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the value is not present
    insert(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *value: [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList.insert)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.insert "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert `value` before index.
    link\_layers(*layers: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *attributes: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] = ()*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList.link_layers)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.link_layers "Link to this definition")
    :   Links the selected layers.
        Once layers are linked, any action performed on one layer will be
        performed on all linked layers at the same time.
    move(*src\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.move "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert object at `src_index` before `dest_index`.
        Both indices refer to the list prior to any object removal
        (pre-move space).
    move\_multiple(*sources: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [slice](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.move_multiple "Link to this definition")
    :   Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination.
        Note, if dest\_index is higher than any of the sources, then
        the resulting position of the moved objects after the move operation
        is complete will be lower than dest\_index.
        Parameters:
        :   * **sources** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*slice*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – A sequence of indices
            * **dest\_index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The destination index. All sources will be inserted before this
              index (in pre-move space), by default 0… which has the effect of
              “bringing to front” everything in `sources`, or acting as a
              “reorder” method if `sources` contains all indices.
        Returns:
        :   The number of successful move operations completed.
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the destination index is a slice, or any of the source indices
            are not `int` or `slice`.
    move\_selected(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *insert: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.move_selected "Link to this definition")
    :   Reorder list by moving the item at index and inserting it
        at the insert index. If additional items are selected these will
        get inserted at the insert index too. This allows for rearranging
        the list based on dragging and dropping a selection of items, where
        index is the index of the primary item being dragged, and insert is
        the index of the drop location, and the selection indicates if
        multiple items are being dragged. If the moved layer is not selected
        select it.
        This method is deprecated. Please use layers.move\_multiple
        with layers.selection instead.
        Parameters:
        :   * **index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Index of primary item to be moved
            * **insert** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Index that item(s) will be inserted at
    property ndim: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.ndim "Link to this definition")
    :   Maximum dimensionality of layers.
        Defaults to 2 if no data is present.
        Returns:
        :   **ndim**
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    pop([*index*]) → item -- remove and return item at index (default last).[#](#napari.components.LayerList.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range.
    remove(*value*)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   S.remove(value) – remove first occurrence of value.
        Raise ValueError if the value is not present.
    remove\_selected()[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList.remove_selected)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.remove_selected "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove selected layers from LayerList, but first unlink them.
    reverse() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.reverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Reverse list *IN PLACE*.
    save(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *selected: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\_writer: WriterContribution | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList.save)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.save "Link to this definition")
    :   Save all or only selected layers to a path using writer plugins.
        If `plugin` is provided, we attempt to write with that plugin.
        If `plugin` is not provided, we call the first compatible writer for
        the given layer(s) and path.
        If any errors occur during writing, they are raised. If the given `plugin`
        does not provide a compatible writer or does not write any files, or
        if there are no compatible writers available, a warning will be issued.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to open. Extensions may be used to
              specify output format (provided a plugin is available for the
              requested format).
            * **selected** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Optional flag to only save selected layers. False by default.
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If None then the first compatible
              writer (if one exists), will be used.
            * **\_writer** (*WriterContribution**,* *optional*) – private: npe2 specific writer override.
        Returns:
        :   File paths of any files that were written.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    select\_all() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.select_all "Link to this definition")
    :   Select all items in the list.
    select\_next(*step: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 1*, *shift: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.select_next "Link to this definition")
    :   Selects next item from list.
    select\_previous(*shift: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.select_previous "Link to this definition")
    :   Selects previous item from list.
    property selection: [Selection](napari.utils.events.Selection.html#napari.utils.events.Selection "napari.utils.events.containers._selection.Selection")[\_S][#](#napari.components.LayerList.selection "Link to this definition")
    :   Get current selection.
    toggle\_selected\_visibility()[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList.toggle_selected_visibility)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.toggle_selected_visibility "Link to this definition")
    :   Toggle visibility of selected layers
    property units: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[Unit, ...] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.LayerList.units "Link to this definition")
    :   Units of layers in world coordinates.
        Returns:
        :   **units** – Units of layers in world coordinates.
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[pint.Unit, …] or None
    unlink\_layers(*layers: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *attributes: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] = ()*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/layerlist.html#LayerList.unlink_layers)[#](#napari.components.LayerList.unlink_layers "Link to this definition")
    :   Unlinks previously linked layers.
        Changes to one of the layer’s properties no longer result in the same
        changes to the previously linked layers.

# napari.components.ViewerModel[#](#napari-components-viewermodel "Link to this heading")
class napari.components.ViewerModel(*title='napari'*, *ndisplay=2*, *order=()*, *axis\_labels=()*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/viewer_model.html#ViewerModel)[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `KeymapProvider`, `MousemapProviderPydantic`, [`EventedModel`](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel")
    Viewer containing the rendered scene, layers, and controlling elements
    including dimension sliders, and control bars for color limits.
    Parameters:
    :   * **title** (*string*) – The title of the viewer window.
        * **ndisplay** (*{2**,* *3}*) – Number of displayed dimensions.
        * **order** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Order in which dimensions are displayed where the last two or last
          three dimensions correspond to row x column or plane x row x column if
          ndisplay is 2 or 3.
        * **axis\_labels** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Dimension names.
    camera[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.camera "Link to this definition")
    :   The camera object modeling the position and view.
        Type:
        :   [napari.components.camera.Camera](napari.components.Camera.html#napari.components.Camera "napari.components.camera.Camera")
    cursor[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.cursor "Link to this definition")
    :   The cursor object containing the position and properties of the cursor.
        Type:
        :   napari.components.cursor.Cursor
    dims[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.dims "Link to this definition")
    :   Contains axes, indices, dimensions and sliders.
        Type:
        :   napari.components.dims.Dimensions
    grid[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.grid "Link to this definition")
    :   Gridcanvas allowing for the current implementation of a gridview of the canvas.
        Type:
        :   napari.components.grid.Gridcanvas
    help[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.help "Link to this definition")
    :   A help message of the viewer model
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    layers[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.layers "Link to this definition")
    :   List of contained layers.
        Type:
        :   [napari.components.layerlist.LayerList](napari.components.LayerList.html#napari.components.LayerList "napari.components.layerlist.LayerList")
    mouse\_over\_canvas[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.mouse_over_canvas "Link to this definition")
    :   Indicating whether the mouse cursor is on the viewer canvas.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    theme[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.theme "Link to this definition")
    :   Name of the Napari theme of the viewer
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    title[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.title "Link to this definition")
    :   The title of the viewer model
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    tooltip[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.tooltip "Link to this definition")
    :   A tooltip showing extra information on the cursor
        Type:
        :   napari.components.tooltip.Tooltip
    window[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.window "Link to this definition")
    :   Parent window.
        Type:
        :   [napari.\_qt.qt\_main\_window.Window](napari.window.Window.html#napari.window.Window "napari._qt.qt_main_window.Window")
    \_canvas\_size[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel._canvas_size "Link to this definition")
    :   The canvas size following the Numpy convention of height x width
        Type:
        :   Tuple[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")]
    \_ctx[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel._ctx "Link to this definition")
    :   Viewer object context mapping.
        Type:
        :   Mapping
    \_layer\_slicer[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel._layer_slicer "Link to this definition")
    :   A layer slicer object controlling the creation of a slice
        Type:
        :   napari.components.\_layer\_slicer.\_Layer\_Slicer
    \_overlays[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel._overlays "Link to this definition")
    :   An EventedDict with as keys the string names of different napari overlays and as values the napari.Overlay
        objects.
        Type:
        :   [napari.utils.events.containers.\_evented\_dict.EventedDict](napari.utils.events.EventedDict.html#napari.utils.events.EventedDict "napari.utils.events.containers._evented_dict.EventedDict")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Overlay]

    | --- | --- |

    | [`add_labels`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.add_labels "napari.components.ViewerModel.add_labels")(data, \*[, affine, axis\_labels, ...]) | Add a Labels layer to the layer list. |

    | [`add_surface`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.add_surface "napari.components.ViewerModel.add_surface")(data, \*[, affine, axis\_labels, ...]) | Add a Surface layer to the layer list. |

    | `from_orm`(obj) |  |
    | [`json`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.json "napari.components.ViewerModel.json")(\*\*kwargs) | Serialize to json. |
    | [`model_construct`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_construct "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_construct")([\_fields\_set]) | Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data. |
    | [`model_copy`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_copy "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_copy")(\*[, update, deep]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_dump`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_dump "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_dump")(\*\*kwargs) | Convert to a dictionary. |
    | [`model_dump_json`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_dump_json "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_dump_json")(\*[, indent, ensure\_ascii, ...]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_json_schema`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_json_schema "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_json_schema")(\*args, \*\*kwargs) | Generate a JSON schema for this model. |
    | [`model_parametrized_name`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_parametrized_name "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_parametrized_name")(params) | Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes. |
    | [`model_post_init`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_post_init "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_post_init")(context, /) | This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes. |
    | [`model_rebuild`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_rebuild "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_rebuild")(\*[, force, raise\_errors, ...]) | Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model. |
    | [`model_validate`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate")(obj, \*[, strict, extra, ...]) | Validate a pydantic model instance. |
    | [`model_validate_json`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate_json "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate_json")(json\_data, \*[, strict, ...]) | !!! abstract "Usage Documentation" |
    | [`model_validate_strings`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate_strings "napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate_strings")(obj, \*[, strict, ...]) | Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model. |
    | [`open`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.open "napari.components.ViewerModel.open")(path, \*[, stack, plugin, layer\_type]) | Open a path or list of paths with plugins, and add layers to viewer. |
    | [`open_sample`](#napari.components.ViewerModel.open_sample "napari.components.ViewerModel.open_sample")(plugin, sample[, reader\_plugin]) | Open sample from plugin and add it to the viewer. |

    Details
    add\_image(*data=None*, *\**, *channel\_axis=None*, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *attenuation=0.05*, *blending=None*, *cache=True*, *colormap=None*, *contrast\_limits=None*, *custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d=None*, *depiction='volume'*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *gamma=1.0*, *interpolation2d='nearest'*, *interpolation3d='linear'*, *iso\_threshold=None*, *metadata=None*, *multiscale=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *plane=None*, *projection\_mode='mean'*, *rendering='mip'*, *rgb=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*) → [Image](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.image.image.Image") | [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Image](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.image.image.Image")][[source]](../_modules/napari/components/viewer_model.html#ViewerModel.add_image)[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.add_image "Link to this definition")
    :   Add one or more Image layers to the layer list.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*array* *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *array*) – Image data. Can be N >= 2 dimensional. If the last dimension has length
              3 or 4 can be interpreted as RGB or RGBA if rgb is True. If a
              list and arrays are decreasing in shape then the data is treated as
              a multiscale image. Please note multiscale rendering is only
              supported in 2D. In 3D, only the lowest resolution scale is
              displayed.
            * **channel\_axis** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Axis to expand image along. If provided, each channel in the data
              will be added as an individual image layer. In channel\_axis mode,
              other parameters MAY be provided as lists. The Nth value of the list
              will be applied to the Nth channel in the data. If a single value
              is provided, it will be broadcast to all Layers.
              All parameters except data, rgb, and multiscale can be provided as
              list of values. If a list is provided, it must be the same length as
              the axis that is being expanded as channels.
            * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
              The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
              the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a
              napari Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on
              top of the provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
            * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Dimension names of the layer data.
              If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
            * **attenuation** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Attenuation rate for attenuated maximum intensity projection.
            * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
              alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
              {‘translucent’, ‘translucent\_no\_depth’, ‘additive’, ‘minimum’, ‘opaque’}.
            * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon
              retrieval. Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
            * **colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")*,* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *these types*) – Colormaps to use for luminance images. If a string, it can be the name
              of a supported colormap from vispy or matplotlib or the name of
              a vispy color or a hexadecimal RGB color representation.
              If a tuple, the first value must be a string to assign as a name to a
              colormap and the second item must be a Colormap. If a dict, the key must
              be a string to assign as a name to a colormap and the value must be a
              Colormap.
            * **contrast\_limits** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *(**2**,**)*) – Intensity value limits to be used for determining the minimum and maximum colormap bounds for
              luminance images. If not passed, they will be calculated as the min and max intensity value of
              the image.
            * **custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d** (*np.ndarray*) – Convolution kernel used with the ‘custom’ interpolation mode in 2D rendering.
            * **depiction** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – 3D Depiction mode. Must be one of {‘volume’, ‘plane’}.
              The default value is ‘volume’.
            * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
              Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
              Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
            * **gamma** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Gamma correction for determining colormap linearity; defaults to 1.
            * **interpolation2d** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Interpolation mode used by vispy for rendering 2d data.
              Must be one of our supported modes.
              (for list of supported modes see Interpolation enum)
              ‘custom’ is a special mode for 2D interpolation in which a regular grid
              of samples is taken from the texture around a position using ‘linear’
              interpolation before being multiplied with a custom interpolation kernel
              (provided with ‘custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d’).
            * **interpolation3d** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Same as ‘interpolation2d’ but for 3D rendering.
            * **iso\_threshold** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Threshold for isosurface.
            * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
            * **multiscale** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the data is a multiscale image or not. Multiscale data is
              represented by a list of array-like image data. If not specified by
              the user and if the data is a list of arrays that decrease in shape,
              then it will be taken to be multiscale. The first image in the list
              should be the largest. Please note multiscale rendering is only
              supported in 2D. In 3D, only the lowest resolution scale is
              displayed.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
            * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
            * **plane** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *SlicingPlane*) – Properties defining plane rendering in 3D. Properties are defined in
              data coordinates. Valid dictionary keys are
              {‘position’, ‘normal’, ‘thickness’, and ‘enabled’}.
            * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions, but inside the thick Dims slice will
              be projected onto the viewed dimensions. Must fit to cls.\_projectionclass
            * **rendering** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Rendering mode used by vispy. Must be one of our supported
              modes. If a list then must be same length as the axis that is being
              expanded as channels.
            * **rgb** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether the image is RGB or RGBA if rgb. If not
              specified by user, but the last dimension of the data has length 3 or 4,
              it will be set as True. If False, the image is interpreted as a
              luminance image.
            * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *n-D array* *or* *list.*) – If a float, convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
              angle. If 3-tuple, convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
              pitch, roll convention. Otherwise, assume an nD rotation. Angles are
              assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
              ‘np.degrees’ if needed.
            * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
            * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *list.*) – A vector of shear values for an upper triangular n-D shear matrix.
            * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
            * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
              If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
            * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
        Returns:
        :   **layer** – The newly-created image layer or list of image layers.
        Return type:
        :   [`napari.layers.Image`](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.Image") or list
    add\_labels(*data*, *\**, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *cache=True*, *colormap=None*, *depiction='volume'*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *features=None*, *iso\_gradient\_mode='fast'*, *metadata=None*, *multiscale=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=0.7*, *plane=None*, *projection\_mode='none'*, *properties=None*, *rendering='iso\_categorical'*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*) → [Labels](napari.layers.Labels.html#napari.layers.Labels "napari.layers.labels.labels.Labels")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.add_labels "Link to this definition")
