v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_dump_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump\_json](../concepts/serialization.md#json-mode)
        Generates a JSON representation of the model using Pydantic’s to\_json method.
        Parameters:
        :   * **indent** – Indentation to use in the JSON output. If None is passed, the output will be compact.
            * **ensure\_ascii** – If True, the output is guaranteed to have all incoming non-ASCII characters escaped.
              If False (the default), these characters will be output as-is.
            * **include** – Field(s) to include in the JSON output.
            * **exclude** – Field(s) to exclude from the JSON output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to serialize using field aliases.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A JSON string representation of the model.
    property model\_extra: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_extra "Link to this definition")
    :   Get extra fields set during validation.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary of extra fields, or None if config.extra is not set to “allow”.
    property model\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_fields_set "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns the set of fields that have been explicitly set on this model instance.
        Returns:
        :   A set of strings representing the fields that have been set,
            :   i.e. that were not filled from defaults.
    classmethod model\_json\_schema(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_json_schema "Link to this definition")
    :   Generate a JSON schema for this model.
        This is required to prevent from mail formated docstring break docs build.
    classmethod model\_parametrized\_name(*params: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], ...]*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_parametrized_name "Link to this definition")
    :   Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes.
        This method can be overridden to achieve a custom naming scheme for generic BaseModels.
        Parameters:
        :   **params** – Tuple of types of the class. Given a generic class
            Model with 2 type variables and a concrete model Model[str, int],
            the value (str, int) would be passed to params.
        Returns:
        :   String representing the new class where params are passed to cls as type variables.
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – Raised when trying to generate concrete names for non-generic models.
    model\_post\_init(*context: Any*, */*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_post_init "Link to this definition")
    :   This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes.
        It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.
        Parameters:
        :   * **self** – The BaseModel instance.
            * **context** – The context.
    classmethod model\_rebuild(*\**, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *raise\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *\_parent\_namespace\_depth: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *\_types\_namespace: MappingNamespace | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_rebuild "Link to this definition")
    :   Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model.
        This may be necessary when one of the annotations is a ForwardRef which could not be resolved during
        the initial attempt to build the schema, and automatic rebuilding fails.
        Parameters:
        :   * **force** – Whether to force the rebuilding of the model schema, defaults to False.
            * **raise\_errors** – Whether to raise errors, defaults to True.
            * **\_parent\_namespace\_depth** – The depth level of the parent namespace, defaults to 2.
            * **\_types\_namespace** – The types namespace, defaults to None.
        Returns:
        :   Returns None if the schema is already “complete” and rebuilding was not required.
            If rebuilding \_was\_ required, returns True if rebuilding was successful, otherwise False.
    classmethod model\_validate(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *from\_attributes: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate a pydantic model instance.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **from\_attributes** – Whether to extract data from object attributes.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If the object could not be validated.
        Returns:
        :   The validated model instance.
    classmethod model\_validate\_json(*json\_data: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytearray](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytearray "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [JSON Parsing](../concepts/json.md#json-parsing)
        Validate the given JSON data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **json\_data** – The JSON data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If json\_data is not a JSON string or the object could not be validated.
    classmethod model\_validate\_strings(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.model_validate_strings "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object containing string data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
    open(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Path](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html#pathlib.Path "(in Python v3.14)") | [Sequence](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Sequence "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Path](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html#pathlib.Path "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *\**, *stack: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Path](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html#pathlib.Path "(in Python v3.14)")]] = False*, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = 'napari'*, *layer\_type: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['graph', 'image', 'labels', 'points', 'shapes', 'surface', 'tracks', 'vectors'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")][[source]](../_modules/napari/components/viewer_model.html#ViewerModel.open)[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.open "Link to this definition")
    :   Open a path or list of paths with plugins, and add layers to viewer.
        A list of paths will be handed one-by-one to the napari\_get\_reader hook
        if stack is False, otherwise the full list is passed to each plugin
        hook.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL (or a list of any) to open.
            * **stack** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]**,* *optional*) – If a list of strings is passed as `path` and `stack` is `True`, then the
              entire list will be passed to plugins. It is then up to individual
              plugins to know how to handle a list of paths. If `stack` is
              `False`, then the `path` list is broken up and passed to plugin
              readers one by one. by default False.
              If the stack option is a list of lists containing individual paths,
              the inner lists are passedto the reader and will be stacked.
            * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of a plugin to use, by default builtins. If provided, will
              force `path` to be read with the specified `plugin`.
              If None, `plugin` will be read from preferences or inferred if just
              one reader is compatible.
              If the requested plugin cannot read `path`, an exception will be raised.
            * **layer\_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – If provided, will force data read from `path` to be passed to the
              corresponding `add_<layer_type>` method (along with any
              additional) `kwargs` provided to this function. This *may*
              result in exceptions if the data returned from the path is not
              compatible with the layer\_type.
            * **\*\*kwargs** – All other keyword arguments will be passed on to the respective
              `add_layer` method.
        Returns:
        :   **layers** – A list of any layers that were added to the viewer.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    open\_sample(*plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *sample: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *reader\_plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")][[source]](../_modules/napari/components/viewer_model.html#ViewerModel.open_sample)[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.open_sample "Link to this definition")
    :   Open sample from plugin and add it to the viewer.
        To see all available samples registered by plugins, use
        `napari.plugins.available_samples()`
        Parameters:
        :   * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – name of a plugin providing a sample
            * **sample** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – name of the sample
            * **reader\_plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – reader plugin to use, passed to `viewer.open`. Only used if the
              sample data is an URI (Uniform Resource Identifier). By default None.
            * **\*\*kwargs** – additional kwargs will be passed to the sample data loader provided
              by plugin. Use of `**kwargs` may raise an error if the kwargs do
              not match the sample data loader.
        Returns:
        :   **layers** – A list of any layers that were added to the viewer.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**KeyError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#KeyError "(in Python v3.14)") – If plugin does not provide a sample named sample.
    reset()[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.reset "Link to this definition")
    :   Reset the state of the model to default values.
    reset\_view(*\**, *margin: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 0.05*, *reset\_camera\_angle: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/viewer_model.html#ViewerModel.reset_view)[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.reset_view "Link to this definition")
    :   Reset the camera and fit the current layers to the canvas.
        Resets the angles of the camera, adjust the camera zoom,
        and centers the view so that all layers are visible,
        accounting for the current grid mode and margin.
        Parameters:
        :   * **margin** (*float in* *[**0**,* *1**)*) – Margin as fraction of the canvas, showing blank space around the
              data. Default is 0.05 (5% of the canvas).
            * **reset\_camera\_angle** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether to reset the camera angles to (0, 0, 0) before fitting
              to view. Default is True.
    update(*values: [EventedModel](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*, *recurse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update a model in place.
        Parameters:
        :   * **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.events.EventedModel*](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.EventedModel")) – Values to update the model with. If an EventedModel is passed it is
              first converted to a dictionary. The keys of this dictionary must
              be found as attributes on the current model.
            * **recurse** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, recursively update fields that are EventedModels.
              Otherwise, just update the immediate fields of this EventedModel,
              which is useful when the declared field type (e.g. `Union`) can have
              different realized types with different fields.
    update\_status\_from\_cursor()[[source]](../_modules/napari/components/viewer_model.html#ViewerModel.update_status_from_cursor)[#](#napari.components.ViewerModel.update_status_from_cursor "Link to this definition")
    :   Update the status and tooltip from the cursor position.

Functions
napari.qt.create\_worker(*func: FunctionType | GeneratorType*, *\*args*, *\_start\_thread: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\_connect: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Callable | Sequence[Callable]] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\_progress: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | v3.14)")[[GeneratorWorker](napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker "napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker") | [FunctionWorker](napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker "napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\_ignore\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [FunctionWorker](napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker "napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker") | [GeneratorWorker](napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker "napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker")[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qthreading.html#create_worker)[#](#napari.qt.create_worker "Link to this definition")
:   Convenience function to start a function in another thread.
    By default, uses `Worker`, but a custom `WorkerBase` subclass may
    be provided. If so, it must be a subclass of `Worker`, which
    defines a standard set of signals and a run method.
    Parameters:
    :   * **func** (*Callable*) – The function to call in another thread.
        * **\_start\_thread** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether to immediaetly start the thread. If False, the returned worker
          must be manually started with `worker.start()`. by default it will be
          `False` if the `_connect` argument is `None`, otherwise `True`.
        * **\_connect** (*Dict**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Union**[**Callable**,* *Sequence**]**]**,* *optional*) – A mapping of `"signal_name"` -> `callable` or list of `callable`:
          callback functions to connect to the various signals offered by the
          worker class. by default None
        * **\_progress** (*Union**[*[*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Dict**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Union**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]**]**,* *optional*) – Can be True, to provide indeterminate progress bar, or dictionary.
          If dict, requires mapping of ‘total’ to number of expected yields.
          If total is not provided, progress bar will be indeterminate. Will connect
          progress bar update to yields and display this progress in the viewer.
          Can also take a mapping of ‘desc’ to the progress bar description.
          Progress bar will become indeterminate when number of yields exceeds ‘total’.
          By default None.
        * **\_worker\_class** (*Type**[*[*WorkerBase*](napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.html#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase")*]**,* *optional*) – The :class`WorkerBase` to instantiate, by default
          `FunctionWorker` will be used if `func` is a regular function,
          and `GeneratorWorker` will be used if it is a generator.
        * **\_ignore\_errors** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – If `False` (the default), errors raised in the other thread will be
          reraised in the main thread (makes debugging significantly easier).
        * **\*args** – will be passed to `func`
        * **\*\*kwargs** – will be passed to `func`
    Returns:
    :   **worker** – An instantiated worker. If `_start_thread` was `False`, the worker
        will have a .start() method that can be used to start the thread.
    Return type:
    :   [WorkerBase](napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.html#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase")
    Raises:
    :   * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If a worker\_class is provided that is not a subclass of WorkerBase.
        * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If \_connect is provided and is not a dict of `{str: callable}`
        * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If \_progress is provided and function is not a generator
    Examples
    ```
    def long_function(duration):
        import time
        time.sleep(duration)
    worker = create_worker(long_function, 10)
    ```
napari.qt.get\_current\_stylesheet(*extra: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_resources.html#get_current_stylesheet)[#](#napari.qt.get_current_stylesheet "Link to this definition")
:   Return the current stylesheet base on settings. This is wrapper around
    [`get_stylesheet()`](#napari.qt.get_stylesheet "napari.qt.get_stylesheet") that takes the current theme base on settings.
    Parameters:
    :   **extra** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Additional paths to QSS files to include in stylesheet, by default None
    Returns:
    :   **css** – The combined stylesheet.
    Return type:
    :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
napari.qt.get\_qapp(*\**, *app\_name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *app\_version: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *icon: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *org\_name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *org\_domain: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *app\_id: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *ipy\_interactive: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → QApplication[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_event_loop.html#get_qapp)[#](#napari.qt.get_qapp "Link to this definition")
:   Get or create the Qt QApplication.
    There is only one global QApplication instance, which can be retrieved by
    calling get\_qapp, (or by using QApplication.instance())
    Parameters:
    :   * **app\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Set app name (if creating for the first time), by default ‘napari’
        * **app\_version** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Set app version (if creating for the first time), by default \_\_version\_\_
        * **icon** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Set app icon (if creating for the first time), by default
          NAPARI\_ICON\_PATH
        * **org\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Set organization name (if creating for the first time), by default
          ‘napari’
        * **org\_domain** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Set organization domain (if creating for the first time), by default
          ‘napari.org’
        * **app\_id** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Set organization domain (if creating for the first time). Will be
          passed to set\_app\_id (which may also be called independently), by
          default NAPARI\_APP\_ID
        * **ipy\_interactive** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Use the IPython Qt event loop (‘%gui qt’ magic) if running in an
          interactive IPython terminal.
    Returns:
    :   [description]
    Return type:
    :   QApplication
    Notes
    Substitutes QApplicationWithTracing when the NAPARI\_PERFMON env variable
    is set.
napari.qt.get\_stylesheet(*theme\_id: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra\_variables: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_resources.html#get_stylesheet)[#](#napari.qt.get_stylesheet "Link to this definition")
:   Combine all qss files into single, possibly pre-themed, style string.
    Parameters:
    :   * **theme\_id** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Theme to apply to the stylesheet. If no theme is provided, the returned
          stylesheet will still have `{{ template_variables }}` that need to be
          replaced using the `napari.utils.theme.template()` function prior
          to using the stylesheet.
        * **extra** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Additional paths to QSS files to include in stylesheet, by default None
        * **extra\_variables** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dictionary of variables values that replace default theme values.
          For example: { ‘font\_size’: ‘14pt’}
    Returns:
    :   **css** – The combined stylesheet.
    Return type:
    :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
napari.qt.run(*\**, *force=False*, *gui\_exceptions=False*, *max\_loop\_level=1*, *\_func\_name='run'*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_event_loop.html#run)[#](#napari.qt.run "Link to this definition")
:   Start the Qt Event Loop
    Parameters:
    :   * **force** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Force the application event\_loop to start, even if there are no top
          level widgets to show.
        * **gui\_exceptions** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether to show uncaught exceptions in the GUI. By default they will be
          shown in the console that launched the event loop.
        * **max\_loop\_level** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The maximum allowable “loop level” for the execution thread. Every
          time QApplication.exec\_() is called, Qt enters the event loop,
          increments app.thread().loopLevel(), and waits until exit() is called.
          This function will prevent calling exec\_() if the application already
          has at least `max_loop_level` event loops running. By default, 1.
        * **\_func\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – name of calling function, by default ‘run’. This is here to
          provide functions a way to inject their name into the
          warning message.
    Raises:
    :   [**RuntimeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#RuntimeError "(in Python v3.14)") – (To avoid confusion) if no widgets would be shown upon starting the
        event loop.
napari.qt.thread\_worker(*function: Callable | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *start\_thread: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *connect: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Callable | Sequence[Callable]] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *progress: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *worker\_class: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[FunctionWorker](napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker "napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker") | [GeneratorWorker](napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker "napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *ignore\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qthreading.html#thread_worker)[#](#napari.qt.thread_worker "Link to this definition")
:   Decorator that runs a function in a separate thread when called.
    When called, the decorated function returns a `WorkerBase`. See
    [`create_worker()`](#napari.qt.create_worker "napari.qt.create_worker") for additional keyword arguments that can be used
    when calling the function.
    The returned worker will have these signals:
    * *started*: emitted when the work is started
    * *finished*: emitted when the work is finished
    * *returned*: emitted with return value
    * *errored*: emitted with error object on Exception
    It will also have a `worker.start()` method that can be used to start
    execution of the function in another thread. (useful if you need to connect
    callbacks to signals prior to execution)
    If the decorated function is a generator, the returned worker will also
    provide these signals:
    * *yielded*: emitted with yielded values
    * *paused*: emitted when a running job has successfully paused
    * *resumed*: emitted when a paused job has successfully resumed
    * *aborted*: emitted when a running job is successfully aborted
    And these methods:
    * *quit*: ask the thread to quit
    * *toggle\_paused*: toggle the running state of the thread.
    * *send*: send a value into the generator. (This requires that your
      :   decorator function uses the `value = yield` syntax)
    Parameters:
    :   * **function** (*callable*) – Function to call in another thread. For communication between threads
          may be a generator function.
        * **start\_thread** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether to immediaetly start the thread. If False, the returned worker
          must be manually started with `worker.start()`. by default it will be
          `False` if the `_connect` argument is `None`, otherwise `True`.
        * **connect** (*Dict**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Union**[**Callable**,* *Sequence**]**]**,* *optional*) – A mapping of `"signal_name"` -> `callable` or list of `callable`:
          callback functions to connect to the various signals offered by the
          worker class. by default None
        * **progress** (*Union**[*[*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Dict**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Union**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]**]**,* *optional*) – Can be True, to provide indeterminate progress bar, or dictionary.
          If dict, requires mapping of ‘total’ to number of expected yields.
          If total is not provided, progress bar will be indeterminate. Will connect
          progress bar update to yields and display this progress in the viewer.
          Can also take a mapping of ‘desc’ to the progress bar description.
          Progress bar will become indeterminate when number of yields exceeds ‘total’.
          By default None. Must be used in conjunction with a generator function.
        * **worker\_class** (*Type**[*[*WorkerBase*](napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.html#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase")*]**,* *optional*) – The :class`WorkerBase` to instantiate, by default
          `FunctionWorker` will be used if `func` is a regular function,
          and `GeneratorWorker` will be used if it is a generator.
        * **ignore\_errors** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – If `False` (the default), errors raised in the other thread will be
          reraised in the main thread (makes debugging significantly easier).
    Returns:
    :   function that creates a worker, puts it in a new thread and returns
        the worker instance.
    Return type:
    :   callable
    Examples
    ```
    @thread_worker
    def long_function(start, end):
        # do work, periodically yielding
        i = start
        while i <= end:
            time.sleep(0.1)
            yield i
        # do teardown
        return 'anything'
    # call the function to start running in another thread.
    worker = long_function()
    # connect signals here if desired... or they may be added using the
    # `connect` argument in the `@thread_worker` decorator... in which
    # case the worker will start immediately when long_function() is called
    worker.start()
    ```

# napari.qt.QtViewer[#](#napari-qt-qtviewer "Link to this heading")
class napari.qt.QtViewer(*viewer: [ViewerModel](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel")*, *show\_welcome\_screen: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *canvas\_class: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[VispyCanvas] = <class 'napari.\_vispy.canvas.VispyCanvas'>*, *tips: Sequence[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_viewer.html#QtViewer)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `QSplitter`
    Qt view for the napari Viewer model.
    Parameters:
    :   * **viewer** ([*napari.components.ViewerModel*](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel")) – Napari viewer containing the rendered scene, layers, and controls.
        * **show\_welcome\_screen** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Flag to show a welcome message when no layers are present in the
          canvas. Default is False.
        * **canvas\_class** (*napari.\_vispy.canvas.VispyCanvas*) – The VispyCanvas class providing the Vispy SceneCanvas. Users can also
          have a custom canvas here.
        * **tips** (*Sequence**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]* *|* *None**,* *optional*) – Custom welcome-screen tips. By default, the built-in napari tips are
          used.
    canvas[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.canvas "Link to this definition")
    :   The VispyCanvas class providing the Vispy SceneCanvas. Users can also
        have a custom canvas here.
        Type:
        :   napari.\_vispy.canvas.VispyCanvas
    dims[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.dims "Link to this definition")
    :   Dimension sliders; Qt View for Dims model.
        Type:
        :   napari.qt\_dims.QtDims
    show\_welcome\_screen[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.show_welcome_screen "Link to this definition")
    :   Boolean indicating whether to show the welcome screen.
        Type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    viewer[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.viewer "Link to this definition")
    :   Napari viewer containing the rendered scene, layers, and controls.
        Type:
        :   [napari.components.ViewerModel](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel")
    \_key\_map\_handler[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer._key_map_handler "Link to this definition")
    :   KeymapHandler handling the calling functionality when keys are pressed that have a callback function mapped
        Type:
        :   napari.utils.key\_bindings.KeymapHandler
    \_qt\_poll[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer._qt_poll "Link to this definition")
    :   A QtPoll object required for the monitor.
        Type:
        :   Optional[napari.\_qt.experimental.qt\_poll.QtPoll]
    \_remote\_manager[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer._remote_manager "Link to this definition")
    :   A remote manager processing commands from remote clients and sending out messages when polled.
        Type:
        :   napari.components.experimental.remote.RemoteManager

    add\_to\_console\_backlog(*variables*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_viewer.html#QtViewer.add_to_console_backlog)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.add_to_console_backlog "Link to this definition")
    :   Save variables for pushing to console when it is instantiated.
        This function will create weakrefs when possible to avoid holding on to
        too much memory unnecessarily.
        Parameters:
        :   **variables** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *list/tuple* *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – The variables to inject into the console’s namespace. If a dict, a
            simple update is done. If a str, the string is assumed to have
            variable names separated by spaces. A list/tuple of str can also
            be used to give the variable names. If just the variable names are
            give (list/tuple/str) then the variable values looked up in the
            callers frame.

    dragEnterEvent(*event*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_viewer.html#QtViewer.dragEnterEvent)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.dragEnterEvent "Link to this definition")
    :   Ignore event if not dragging & dropping a file or URL to open.
        Using event.ignore() here allows the event to pass through the
        parent widget to its child widget, otherwise the parent widget
        would catch the event and not pass it on to the child widget.
        Parameters:
        :   **event** (*qtpy.QtCore.QDragEvent*) – Event from the Qt context.

    dropEvent(*event*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_viewer.html#QtViewer.dropEvent)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.dropEvent "Link to this definition")
    :   Add local files and web URLS with drag and drop.
        For each file, attempt to open with existing associated reader
        (if available). If no reader is associated or opening fails,
        and more than one reader is available, open dialog and ask
        user to choose among available readers. User can choose to persist
        this choice.
        Parameters:
        :   **event** (*qtpy.QtCore.QDropEvent*) – Event from the Qt context.

    event(*self*, *a0: QEvent | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.event "Link to this definition")

    export\_figure(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *scale: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1*, *flash=True*) → [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_viewer.html#QtViewer.export_figure)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.export_figure "Link to this definition")
    :   Export an image of the full extent of the displayed layer data.
        This function finds a tight boundary around the data, resets the view
        around that boundary (and, when scale=1, such that 1 captured pixel is
        equivalent to one data pixel), takes a screenshot, then restores the
        previous zoom and canvas sizes.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Filename for saving screenshot image.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the
              screenshot. By default, a scale of 1.
            * **flash** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to indicate whether flash animation should be shown after
              the screenshot was captured.
              By default, True.
        Returns:
        :   **image** – Numpy array of type ubyte and shape (h, w, 4). Index [0, 0] is the
            upper-left corner of the rendered region.
        Return type:
        :   array
    export\_rois(*rois: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")]*, *paths: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Path](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html#pathlib.Path "(in Python v3.14)") | [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Path](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html#pathlib.Path "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *scale: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1.0*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_viewer.html#QtViewer.export_rois)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.export_rois "Link to this definition")
    :   Export the given rectangular rois to specified file paths.
        For each shape, moves the camera to the center of the shape
        and adjust the canvas size to fit the shape.
        Note: The shape height and width can be of type float.
        However, the canvas size only accepts a tuple of integers.
        This can result in slight misalignment.
        Parameters:
        :   * **rois** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[**np.ndarray**]*) – A list of arrays with each being of shape (4, 2) representing
              a rectangular roi.
            * **paths** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Path**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Path**]**,* *optional*) – Where to save the rois. If a string or a Path, a directory will
              be created if it does not exist yet and screenshots will be
              saved with filename roi\_{n}.png where n is the nth roi. If
              paths is a list of either string or paths, these need to be the
              full paths of where to store each individual roi. In this case
              the length of the list and the number of rois must match.
              If None, the screenshots will only be returned and not saved
              to disk.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the screenshot.
              By default, uses the displayed scale.
        Returns:
        :   **screenshot\_list** – The list with roi screenshots.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    find(*a0: PyQt5.sip.voidptr*) → QWidget | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.find "Link to this definition")

    resize\_canvas(*size: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")*, *scale: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_viewer.html#QtViewer.resize_canvas)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.resize_canvas "Link to this definition")
    :   Temporarily, safely, resize the canvas
        Parameters:
        :   * **size** (*(*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – New canvas size in pixels. Often calculated based on data size.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factor to modify final canvas size.

    screenshot(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *flash: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *size: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *scale: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1.0*, *fit\_to\_data\_extent: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [ndarray](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray "(in NumPy v2.4)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")[4]], [dtype](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.dtype.html#numpy.dtype "(in NumPy v2.4)")[uint8]][[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_viewer.html#QtViewer.screenshot)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewer.screenshot "Link to this definition")
    :   Take currently displayed screen and convert to an image array.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Filename for saving screenshot image.
            * **flash** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to indicate whether flash animation should be shown after
              the screenshot was captured.
            * **size** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – Size (resolution height x width) of the screenshot.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the screenshot.
              By default, the currently displayed resolution.
            * **fit\_to\_data\_extent** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Tightly fit the canvas around the data to prevent margins from
              showing in the screenshot. If False, a screenshot of the currently
              visible canvas will be generated.
        Returns:
        :   **image** – Numpy array of type ubyte and shape (h, w, 4). Index [0, 0] is the
            upper-left corner of the rendered region.
        Return type:
        :   array

# napari.qt.QtViewerButtons[#](#napari-qt-qtviewerbuttons "Link to this heading")
class napari.qt.QtViewerButtons(*viewer: [ViewerModel](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/widgets/qt_viewer_buttons.html#QtViewerButtons)[#](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `QFrame`
    Button controls for the napari viewer.
    Parameters:
    :   **viewer** ([*napari.components.ViewerModel*](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel")) – Napari viewer containing the rendered scene, layers, and controls.
    consoleButton[#](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.consoleButton "Link to this definition")
    :   Button to open iPython console within napari.
        Type:
        :   QtViewerPushButton
    rollDimsButton[#](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.rollDimsButton "Link to this definition")
    :   Button to roll orientation of spatial dimensions in the napari viewer.
        Type:
        :   QtViewerPushButton
    transposeDimsButton[#](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.transposeDimsButton "Link to this definition")
    :   Button to transpose dimensions in the napari viewer.
        Type:
        :   QtViewerPushButton
    resetViewButton[#](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.resetViewButton "Link to this definition")
    :   Button resetting the view of the rendered scene.
        Type:
        :   QtViewerPushButton
    gridViewButton[#](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.gridViewButton "Link to this definition")
    :   Button to toggle grid view mode of layers on and off.
        Type:
        :   QtViewerPushButton
    ndisplayButton[#](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.ndisplayButton "Link to this definition")
    :   Button to toggle number of displayed dimensions.
        Type:
        :   QtViewerPushButton
    viewer[#](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.viewer "Link to this definition")
    :   Napari viewer containing the rendered scene, layers, and controls.
        Type:
        :   [napari.components.ViewerModel](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel")
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`acceptDrops`](#napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.acceptDrops "napari.qt.QtViewerButtons.acceptDrops")(self) |  |
