# napari.qt.Window[#](#napari-qt-window "Link to this heading")
class napari.qt.Window(*viewer: [Viewer](napari.Viewer.html#napari.Viewer "napari.Viewer")*, *\**, *show: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *show\_welcome\_screen: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window)[#](#napari.qt.Window "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`object`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")
    Application window that contains the menu bar and viewer.
    Parameters:
    :   **viewer** ([*napari.components.ViewerModel*](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.ViewerModel")) – Contained viewer widget.
    file\_menu[#](#napari.qt.Window.file_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   File menu.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMenu
    help\_menu[#](#napari.qt.Window.help_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   Help menu.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMenu
    main\_menu[#](#napari.qt.Window.main_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   Main menubar.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMainWindow.menuBar
    view\_menu[#](#napari.qt.Window.view_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   View menu.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMenu
    window\_menu[#](#napari.qt.Window.window_menu "Link to this definition")
    :   Window menu.
        Type:
        :   qtpy.QtWidgets.QMenu
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`activate`](#napari.qt.Window.activate "napari.qt.Window.activate")() | Make the viewer the currently active window. |
    | [`add_dock_widget`](#napari.qt.Window.add_dock_widget "napari.qt.Window.add_dock_widget")(widget, Widget], \*, name, ...) | Convenience method to add a QDockWidget to the main window. |
    | [`add_function_widget`](#napari.qt.Window.add_function_widget "napari.qt.Window.add_function_widget")(function, \*[, ...]) | Turn a function into a dock widget via magicgui. |
    | [`add_plugin_dock_widget`](#napari.qt.Window.add_plugin_dock_widget "napari.qt.Window.add_plugin_dock_widget")(plugin\_name[, ...]) | Add plugin dock widget if not already added. |
    | [`clipboard`](#napari.qt.Window.clipboard "napari.qt.Window.clipboard")([flash, canvas\_only]) | Copy screenshot of current viewer to the clipboard. |
    | [`close`](#napari.qt.Window.close "napari.qt.Window.close")() | Close the viewer window and cleanup sub-widgets. |
    | [`export_figure`](#napari.qt.Window.export_figure "napari.qt.Window.export_figure")([path, scale, flash]) | Export an image of the full extent of the displayed layer data. |
    | [`export_rois`](#napari.qt.Window.export_rois "napari.qt.Window.export_rois")(rois[, paths, scale]) | Export the given rectangular rois to specified file paths. |
    | [`geometry`](#napari.qt.Window.geometry "napari.qt.Window.geometry")() | Get the geometry of the widget |
    | [`remove_dock_widget`](#napari.qt.Window.remove_dock_widget "napari.qt.Window.remove_dock_widget")(widget[, menu]) | Removes specified dock widget. |
    | [`resize`](#napari.qt.Window.resize "napari.qt.Window.resize")(width, height) | Resize the window. |
    | [`screenshot`](#napari.qt.Window.screenshot "napari.qt.Window.screenshot")([path, size, scale, flash, ...]) | Take currently displayed viewer and convert to an image array. |
    | [`set_geometry`](#napari.qt.Window.set_geometry "napari.qt.Window.set_geometry")(left, top, width, height) | Set the geometry of the widget |
    | [`show`](#napari.qt.Window.show "napari.qt.Window.show")(\*[, block]) | Resize, show, and bring forward the window. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`dock_widgets`](#napari.qt.Window.dock_widgets "napari.qt.Window.dock_widgets") | Read-only mapping of widgets docked in napari window. |
    | `qt_viewer` |  |
    Details
    activate()[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.activate)[#](#napari.qt.Window.activate "Link to this definition")
    :   Make the viewer the currently active window.
    add\_dock\_widget(*widget: Union[QWidget, Widget], \*, name: str = '', area: str | None = None, allowed\_areas: Sequence[str] | None = None, shortcut=<object object>, add\_vertical\_stretch=True, tabify: bool = False, menu: QMenu | None = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.add_dock_widget)[#](#napari.qt.Window.add_dock_widget "Link to this definition")
    :   Convenience method to add a QDockWidget to the main window.
        If name is not provided a generic name will be addded to avoid
        saveState warnings on close.
        Parameters:
        :   * **widget** (*QWidget*) – widget will be added as QDockWidget’s main widget.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of dock widget to appear in window menu.
            * **area** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Side of the main window to which the new dock widget will be added.
              Must be in {‘left’, ‘right’, ‘top’, ‘bottom’}
            * **allowed\_areas** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**,* *optional*) – Areas, relative to the main window, that the widget is allowed dock.
              Each item in list must be in {‘left’, ‘right’, ‘top’, ‘bottom’}
              By default, all areas are allowed.
            * **shortcut** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Keyboard shortcut to appear in dropdown menu.
            * **add\_vertical\_stretch** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) –
              Whether to add stretch to the bottom of vertical widgets (pushing
              widgets up towards the top of the allotted area, instead of letting
              them distribute across the vertical space). By default, True.
              Deprecated since version 0.4.8: The shortcut parameter is deprecated since version 0.4.8, please use
              the action and shortcut manager APIs. The new action manager and
              shortcut API allow user configuration and localization.
            * **tabify** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to tabify dock widget or not.
            * **menu** (*QMenu**,* *optional*) – Menu bar to add toggle action to. If None nothing added to menu.
        Returns:
        :   **dock\_widget** – dock\_widget that can pass viewer events.
        Return type:
        :   QtViewerDockWidget
    add\_function\_widget(*function*, *\**, *magic\_kwargs=None*, *name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = ''*, *area=None*, *allowed\_areas=None*, *shortcut=<object object>*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.add_function_widget)[#](#napari.qt.Window.add_function_widget "Link to this definition")
    :   Turn a function into a dock widget via magicgui.
        Parameters:
        :   * **function** (*callable*) – Function that you want to add.
            * **magic\_kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Keyword arguments to `magicgui.magicgui()` that
              can be used to specify widget.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of dock widget to appear in window menu.
            * **area** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Side of the main window to which the new dock widget will be added.
              Must be in {‘left’, ‘right’, ‘top’, ‘bottom’}. If not provided the
              default will be determined by the widget.layout, with ‘vertical’
              layouts appearing on the right, otherwise on the bottom.
            * **allowed\_areas** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**,* *optional*) – Areas, relative to main window, that the widget is allowed dock.
              Each item in list must be in {‘left’, ‘right’, ‘top’, ‘bottom’}
              By default, only provided areas is allowed.
            * **shortcut** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Keyboard shortcut to appear in dropdown menu.
        Returns:
        :   **dock\_widget** – dock\_widget that can pass viewer events.
        Return type:
        :   QtViewerDockWidget
    add\_plugin\_dock\_widget(*plugin\_name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *widget\_name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *tabify: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[QtViewerDockWidget, [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.add_plugin_dock_widget)[#](#napari.qt.Window.add_plugin_dock_widget "Link to this definition")
    :   Add plugin dock widget if not already added.
        Parameters:
        :   * **plugin\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of a plugin providing a widget
            * **widget\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of a widget provided by plugin\_name. If None, and the
              specified plugin provides only a single widget, that widget will be
              returned, otherwise a ValueError will be raised, by default None
            * **tabify** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to tabify dock widget or not.
        Returns:
        :   A 2-tuple containing (the DockWidget instance, the plugin widget
            instance).
        Return type:
        :   [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")
    clipboard(*flash=True*, *canvas\_only=False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.clipboard)[#](#napari.qt.Window.clipboard "Link to this definition")
    :   Copy screenshot of current viewer to the clipboard.
        Parameters:
        :   * **flash** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to indicate whether flash animation should be shown after
              the screenshot was captured.
            * **canvas\_only** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, screenshot shows only the image display canvas, and
              if False include the napari viewer frame in the screenshot,
              By default, True.
    close()[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.close)[#](#napari.qt.Window.close "Link to this definition")
    :   Close the viewer window and cleanup sub-widgets.
    property dock\_widgets: Mapping[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), QWidget | Widget][#](#napari.qt.Window.dock_widgets "Link to this definition")
    :   Read-only mapping of widgets docked in napari window.
        Notes
        This mapping returns the *inner* widget contained in each dock widget
        (a Qt `QWidget` or a `magicgui.widgets.Widget`), not the
        `QtViewerDockWidget`
        wrapper.
        If you need to control the dock widget itself (for example, to show or
        raise a docked widget tab), access the wrapper via the Qt parent:
        ```
        >>> name = "My widget"
        >>> widget = viewer.window.dock_widgets[name]
        >>> qt_widget = widget.native if hasattr(widget, "native") else widget
        >>> dock_widget = qt_widget.parent()  # QtViewerDockWidget / QDockWidget
        >>> dock_widget.show(); dock_widget.raise_()
        ```
    export\_figure(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *scale: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1*, *flash=True*) → np.ndarray[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.export_figure)[#](#napari.qt.Window.export_figure "Link to this definition")
    :   Export an image of the full extent of the displayed layer data.
        This function finds a tight boundary around the data, resets the view
        around that boundary (and, when scale=1, such that 1 captured pixel is
        equivalent to one data pixel), takes a screenshot, then restores the
        previous zoom and canvas sizes.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Filename for saving screenshot image.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the
              screenshot. By default, a scale of 1.
            * **flash** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to indicate whether flash animation should be shown after
              the screenshot was captured.
              By default, True.
        Returns:
        :   **image** – Numpy array of type ubyte and shape (h, w, 4). Index [0, 0] is the
            upper-left corner of the rendered region.
        Return type:
        :   array
    export\_rois(*rois: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[np.ndarray]*, *paths: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | Path | [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | Path] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *scale: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") = 1.0*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.export_rois)[#](#napari.qt.Window.export_rois "Link to this definition")
    :   Export the given rectangular rois to specified file paths.
        For each shape, moves the camera to the center of the shape
        and adjust the canvas size to fit the shape.
        Note: The shape height and width can be of type float.
        However, the canvas size only accepts a tuple of integers.
        This can result in slight misalignment.
        Parameters:
        :   * **rois** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[**np.ndarray**]*) – A list of arrays with each being of shape (4, 2) representing
              a rectangular roi.
            * **paths** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Path**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Path**]**,* *optional*) – Where to save the rois. If a string or a Path, a directory will
              be created if it does not exist yet and screenshots will be
              saved with filename roi\_{n}.png where n is the nth roi. If
              paths is a list of either string or paths, these need to be the
              full paths of where to store each individual roi. In this case
              the length of the list and the number of rois must match.
              If None, the screenshots will only be returned and not saved
              to disk.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the screenshot.
              By default, uses the displayed scale.
        Returns:
        :   **screenshot\_list** – The list with roi screenshots.
        Return type:
        :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
    geometry() → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.geometry)[#](#napari.qt.Window.geometry "Link to this definition")
    :   Get the geometry of the widget
        Returns:
        :   * **left** (*int*) – X coordinate of the upper left border.
            * **top** (*int*) – Y coordinate of the upper left border.
            * **width** (*int*) – Width of the rectangle shape of the window.
            * **height** (*int*) – Height of the rectangle shape of the window.
    remove\_dock\_widget(*widget: QWidget*, *menu=None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.remove_dock_widget)[#](#napari.qt.Window.remove_dock_widget "Link to this definition")
    :   Removes specified dock widget.
        If a QDockWidget is not provided, the existing QDockWidgets will be
        searched for one whose inner widget (`.widget()`) is the provided
        `widget`.
        Parameters:
        :   * **widget** (*QWidget* *|* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – If widget == ‘all’, all docked widgets will be removed.
            * **menu** (*QMenu**,* *optional*) – Menu bar to remove toggle action from. If None nothing removed
              from menu.
    resize(*width*, *height*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.resize)[#](#napari.qt.Window.resize "Link to this definition")
    :   Resize the window.
        Parameters:
        :   * **width** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Width in logical pixels.
            * **height** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Height in logical pixels.
    screenshot(*path=None*, *size=None*, *scale=None*, *flash=True*, *canvas\_only=False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.screenshot)[#](#napari.qt.Window.screenshot "Link to this definition")
    :   Take currently displayed viewer and convert to an image array.
        Parameters:
        :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Path*) – Filename for saving screenshot image.
            * **size** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *(*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – Size (resolution) of the screenshot. By default, the currently displayed size.
              Only used if canvas\_only is True.
            * **scale** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factor used to increase resolution of canvas for the screenshot.
              By default, the currently displayed resolution.
              Only used if canvas\_only is True.
            * **flash** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Flag to indicate whether flash animation should be shown after
              the screenshot was captured.
            * **canvas\_only** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, screenshot shows only the image display canvas, and
              if False includes the napari viewer frame in the screenshot,
              By default, True.
        Returns:
        :   **image** – Numpy array of type ubyte and shape (h, w, 4). Index [0, 0] is the
            upper-left corner of the rendered region.
        Return type:
        :   array
    set\_geometry(*left*, *top*, *width*, *height*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.set_geometry)[#](#napari.qt.Window.set_geometry "Link to this definition")
    :   Set the geometry of the widget
        Parameters:
        :   * **left** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – X coordinate of the upper left border.
            * **top** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Y coordinate of the upper left border.
            * **width** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Width of the rectangle shape of the window.
            * **height** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Height of the rectangle shape of the window.
    show(*\**, *block=False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.html#Window.show)[#](#napari.qt.Window.show "Link to this definition")
    :   Resize, show, and bring forward the window.
        Raises:
        :   [**RuntimeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#RuntimeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the viewer.window has already been closed and deleted.

Python v3.14)") = None*, *\_ignore\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [FunctionWorker](napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker "napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker") | [GeneratorWorker](napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker "napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker")[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qthreading.html#create_worker)[#](#napari.qt.threading.create_worker "Link to this definition")
:   Convenience function to start a function in another thread.
    By default, uses `Worker`, but a custom `WorkerBase` subclass may
    be provided. If so, it must be a subclass of `Worker`, which
    defines a standard set of signals and a run method.
    Parameters:
    :   * **func** (*Callable*) – The function to call in another thread.
        * **\_start\_thread** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether to immediaetly start the thread. If False, the returned worker
          must be manually started with `worker.start()`. by default it will be
          `False` if the `_connect` argument is `None`, otherwise `True`.
        * **\_connect** (*Dict**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Union**[**Callable**,* *Sequence**]**]**,* *optional*) – A mapping of `"signal_name"` -> `callable` or list of `callable`:
          callback functions to connect to the various signals offered by the
          worker class. by default None
        * **\_progress** (*Union**[*[*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Dict**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Union**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]**]**,* *optional*) – Can be True, to provide indeterminate progress bar, or dictionary.
          If dict, requires mapping of ‘total’ to number of expected yields.
          If total is not provided, progress bar will be indeterminate. Will connect
          progress bar update to yields and display this progress in the viewer.
          Can also take a mapping of ‘desc’ to the progress bar description.
          Progress bar will become indeterminate when number of yields exceeds ‘total’.
          By default None.
        * **\_worker\_class** (*Type**[*[*WorkerBase*](napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.html#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase")*]**,* *optional*) – The :class`WorkerBase` to instantiate, by default
          [`FunctionWorker`](napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker "napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker") will be used if `func` is a regular function,
          and [`GeneratorWorker`](napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker "napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker") will be used if it is a generator.
        * **\_ignore\_errors** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – If `False` (the default), errors raised in the other thread will be
          reraised in the main thread (makes debugging significantly easier).
        * **\*args** – will be passed to `func`
        * **\*\*kwargs** – will be passed to `func`
    Returns:
    :   **worker** – An instantiated worker. If `_start_thread` was `False`, the worker
        will have a .start() method that can be used to start the thread.
    Return type:
    :   [WorkerBase](napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.html#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase")
    Raises:
    :   * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If a worker\_class is provided that is not a subclass of WorkerBase.
        * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If \_connect is provided and is not a dict of `{str: callable}`
        * [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If \_progress is provided and function is not a generator
    Examples
    ```
    def long_function(duration):
        import time
        time.sleep(duration)
    worker = create_worker(long_function, 10)
    ```
napari.qt.threading.thread\_worker(*function: Callable | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *start\_thread: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *connect: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Callable | Sequence[Callable]] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *progress: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *worker\_class: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[FunctionWorker](napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker "napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker") | [GeneratorWorker](napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker "napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *ignore\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qthreading.html#thread_worker)[#](#napari.qt.threading.thread_worker "Link to this definition")
:   Decorator that runs a function in a separate thread when called.
    When called, the decorated function returns a [`WorkerBase`](napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.html#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase"). See
    [`create_worker()`](#napari.qt.threading.create_worker "napari.qt.threading.create_worker") for additional keyword arguments that can be used
    when calling the function.
    The returned worker will have these signals:
    * *started*: emitted when the work is started
    * *finished*: emitted when the work is finished
    * *returned*: emitted with return value
    * *errored*: emitted with error object on Exception
    It will also have a `worker.start()` method that can be used to start
    execution of the function in another thread. (useful if you need to connect
    callbacks to signals prior to execution)
    If the decorated function is a generator, the returned worker will also
    provide these signals:
    * *yielded*: emitted with yielded values
    * *paused*: emitted when a running job has successfully paused
    * *resumed*: emitted when a paused job has successfully resumed
    * *aborted*: emitted when a running job is successfully aborted
    And these methods:
    * *quit*: ask the thread to quit
    * *toggle\_paused*: toggle the running state of the thread.
    * *send*: send a value into the generator. (This requires that your
      :   decorator function uses the `value = yield` syntax)
    Parameters:
    :   * **function** (*callable*) – Function to call in another thread. For communication between threads
          may be a generator function.
        * **start\_thread** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether to immediaetly start the thread. If False, the returned worker
          must be manually started with `worker.start()`. by default it will be
          `False` if the `_connect` argument is `None`, otherwise `True`.
        * **connect** (*Dict**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Union**[**Callable**,* *Sequence**]**]**,* *optional*) – A mapping of `"signal_name"` -> `callable` or list of `callable`:
          callback functions to connect to the various signals offered by the
          worker class. by default None
        * **progress** (*Union**[*[*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Dict**[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Union**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*]**]**]**,* *optional*) – Can be True, to provide indeterminate progress bar, or dictionary.
          If dict, requires mapping of ‘total’ to number of expected yields.
          If total is not provided, progress bar will be indeterminate. Will connect
          progress bar update to yields and display this progress in the viewer.
          Can also take a mapping of ‘desc’ to the progress bar description.
          Progress bar will become indeterminate when number of yields exceeds ‘total’.
          By default None. Must be used in conjunction with a generator function.
        * **worker\_class** (*Type**[*[*WorkerBase*](napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.html#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase")*]**,* *optional*) – The :class`WorkerBase` to instantiate, by default
          [`FunctionWorker`](napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker "napari.qt.threading.FunctionWorker") will be used if `func` is a regular function,
          and [`GeneratorWorker`](napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker.html#napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker "napari.qt.threading.GeneratorWorker") will be used if it is a generator.
        * **ignore\_errors** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – If `False` (the default), errors raised in the other thread will be
          reraised in the main thread (makes debugging significantly easier).
    Returns:
    :   function that creates a worker, puts it in a new thread and returns
        the worker instance.
    Return type:
    :   callable
    Examples
    ```
    @thread_worker
    def long_function(start, end):
        # do work, periodically yielding
        i = start
        while i <= end:
            time.sleep(0.1)
            yield i
        # do teardown
        return 'anything'
    # call the function to start running in another thread.
    worker = long_function()
    # connect signals here if desired... or they may be added using the
    # `connect` argument in the `@thread_worker` decorator... in which
    # case the worker will start immediately when long_function() is called
    worker.start()
    ```

# napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase[#](#napari-qt-threading-workerbase "Link to this heading")
class napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase(*func: Callable[\_P*, *\_R] | None=None*, *SignalsClass: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[WorkerBaseSignals](napari.qt.threading.WorkerBaseSignals.html#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBaseSignals "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBaseSignals")] = <class 'superqt.utils.\_qthreading.WorkerBaseSignals'>*)[[source]](../_modules/superqt/utils/_qthreading.html#WorkerBase)[#](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `QRunnable`, [`Generic`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generic "(in Python v3.14)")[`_R`]
    Base class for creating a Worker that can run in another thread.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`autoDelete`](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.autoDelete "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.autoDelete")(self) |  |
    | [`await_workers`](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.await_workers "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.await_workers")([msecs]) | Ask all workers to quit, and wait up to msec for quit. |
    | [`create`](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.create "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.create")(functionToRun) |  |
    | [`quit`](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.quit "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.quit")() | Send a request to abort the worker. |
    | [`run`](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.run "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.run")() | Start the worker. |
    | [`setAutoDelete`](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.setAutoDelete "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.setAutoDelete")(self, \_autoDelete) |  |
    | [`start`](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.start "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.start")() | Start this worker in a thread and add it to the global threadpool. |
    | [`work`](#napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.work "napari.qt.threading.WorkerBase.work")() | Main method to execute the worker. |

# napari.utils.events[#](#module-napari.utils.events "Link to this heading")
Classes
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`EmitterGroup`](napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.html#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup") | EmitterGroup instances manage a set of related `EventEmitters`. |
| [`Event`](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.Event") | Class describing events that occur and can be reacted to with callbacks. |
| [`EventEmitter`](napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.html#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter") | Encapsulates a list of event callbacks. |
| [`EventedDict`](napari.utils.events.EventedDict.html#napari.utils.events.EventedDict "napari.utils.events.EventedDict") | Mutable dictionary that emits events when altered. |
| [`EventedList`](napari.utils.events.EventedList.html#napari.utils.events.EventedList "napari.utils.events.EventedList") | Mutable Sequence that emits events when altered. |
| [`EventedModel`](napari.utils.events.EventedModel.html#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.EventedModel") | A Model subclass that emits an event whenever a field value is changed. |
| [`EventedSet`](napari.utils.events.EventedSet.html#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.EventedSet") | An unordered collection of unique elements. |
| [`NestableEventedList`](napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.html#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList") | Nestable Mutable Sequence that emits recursive events when altered. |
| [`SelectableEventedList`](napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.html#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList") | List model that also supports selection. |
| [`Selection`](napari.utils.events.Selection.html#napari.utils.events.Selection "napari.utils.events.Selection") | A model of selected items, with `active` and `current` item. |
| [`SupportsEvents`](napari.utils.events.SupportsEvents.html#napari.utils.events.SupportsEvents "napari.utils.events.SupportsEvents") |  |
| [`TypedMutableSequence`](napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.html#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence") | List mixin that enforces item type, and enables custom indexing. |
Functions

        * **listener** (*Object*) – Any object that has been connected to.

* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* [napari.utils.events](napari.utils.events.html)
* napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup
# napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup[#](#napari-utils-events-emittergroup "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup(*source: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *auto\_connect: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *\_connect\_children: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *\*\*emitters: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")] | [EventEmitter](napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.html#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "napari.utils.events.event.EventEmitter") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EmitterGroup)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`EventEmitter`](napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.html#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "napari.utils.events.event.EventEmitter")
    EmitterGroup instances manage a set of related
    `EventEmitters`.
    Its primary purpose is to provide organization for objects
    that make use of multiple emitters and to reduce the boilerplate code
    needed to initialize those emitters with default connections.
    EmitterGroup instances are usually stored as an ‘events’ attribute on
    objects that use multiple emitters. For example:
    ```
     EmitterGroup  EventEmitter
             |       |
    Canvas.events.mouse_press
    Canvas.events.resized
    Canvas.events.key_press
    ```
    EmitterGroup is also a subclass of `EventEmitters`,
    allowing it to emit its own events.
    Any callback that connects directly to the EmitterGroup will
    receive *all* of the events generated by the group’s emitters.
    When connecting a first callback to an EmitterGroup, the group will
    check if a source object has a ` first\_callback\_connect ` method, and if so,
    it will call it. When the last callback is disconnected, the group will
    check if a source object has the ` last\_callback\_disconnect ` method, and
    if so, it will call it.
    Parameters:
    :   * **source** ([*object*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")) – The object that the generated events apply to.
        * **auto\_connect** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If *auto\_connect* is True, then one connection will
          be made for each emitter that looks like
          `emitter.connect((source, 'on_' + event_name))`.
          This provides a simple mechanism for automatically connecting a large
          group of emitters to default callbacks. By default, false.
        * **emitters** (*keyword arguments*) – See the `add` method.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`add`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.add "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.add")([auto\_connect]) | Add one or more EventEmitter instances to this emitter group. Each keyword argument may be specified as either an EventEmitter instance or an Event subclass, in which case an EventEmitter will be generated automatically::. |
    | [`block`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.block "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.block")([callback]) | Block this emitter. |
    | [`block_all`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.block_all "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.block_all")() | Block all emitters in this group by increase counter of semaphores for each event emitter |
    | [`blocked`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocked "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocked")([callback]) | Return boolean indicating whether the emitter is blocked for the given callback. |
    | [`blocker`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocker "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocker")([callback]) | Return an EventBlocker to be used in 'with' statements |
    | [`blocker_all`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocker_all "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocker_all")() | Return an EventBlockerAll to be used in 'with' statements |
    | [`connect`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.connect "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.connect")(callback[, ref, position, before, after]) | Connect the callback to the event group. |
    | [`disconnect`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.disconnect "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.disconnect")([callback]) | Disconnect the callback from this group. |
    | [`unblock`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.unblock "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.unblock")([callback]) | Unblock this emitter. |
    | [`unblock_all`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.unblock_all "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.unblock_all")() | Unblock all emitters in this group, by decrease counter of semaphores for each event emitter. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`callback_refs`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.callback_refs "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.callback_refs") | The set of callback references |
    | [`callbacks`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.callbacks "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.callbacks") | The set of callbacks |
    | [`emitters`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.emitters "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.emitters") | List of current emitters in this group. |
    | [`ignore_callback_errors`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.ignore_callback_errors "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.ignore_callback_errors") | Whether exceptions during callbacks will be caught by the emitter |
    | [`print_callback_errors`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.print_callback_errors "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.print_callback_errors") | Print a message and stack trace if a callback raises an exception |
    | [`source`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.source "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.source") | The object that events generated by this emitter apply to |
    Details
    add(*auto\_connect: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*kwargs: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")] | [EventEmitter](napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.html#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "napari.utils.events.event.EventEmitter") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EmitterGroup.add)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.add "Link to this definition")
    :   Add one or more EventEmitter instances to this emitter group.
        Each keyword argument may be specified as either an EventEmitter
        instance or an Event subclass, in which case an EventEmitter will be
        generated automatically:
        ```
        # This statement:
        group.add(mouse_press=MouseEvent,
                  mouse_release=MouseEvent)
        # ..is equivalent to this statement:
        group.add(mouse_press=EventEmitter(group.source, 'mouse_press',
                                           MouseEvent),
                  mouse_release=EventEmitter(group.source, 'mouse_press',
                                             MouseEvent))
        ```
    block(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.block "Link to this definition")
    :   Block this emitter. Any attempts to emit an event while blocked
        will be silently ignored. If *callback* is given, then the emitter
        is only blocked for that specific callback.
        Calls to block are cumulative; the emitter must be unblocked the same
        number of times as it is blocked.
    block\_all()[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EmitterGroup.block_all)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.block_all "Link to this definition")
    :   Block all emitters in this group by increase counter of semaphores for each event emitter
    blocked(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocked "Link to this definition")
    :   Return boolean indicating whether the emitter is blocked for
        the given callback.
    blocker(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocker "Link to this definition")
    :   Return an EventBlocker to be used in ‘with’ statements
        Notes
        For example, one could do:
        ```
        with emitter.blocker():
            pass  # ..do stuff; no events will be emitted..
        ```
    blocker\_all() → EventBlockerAll[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EmitterGroup.blocker_all)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.blocker_all "Link to this definition")
    :   Return an EventBlockerAll to be used in ‘with’ statements
        Notes
        For example, one could do:
        ```
        with emitter.blocker_all():
            pass  # ..do stuff; no events will be emitted..
        ```
    property callback\_refs: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.callback_refs "Link to this definition")
    :   The set of callback references
    property callbacks: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[ReferenceType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/weakref.html#weakref.ReferenceType "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")], ...][#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.callbacks "Link to this definition")
    :   The set of callbacks
    connect(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *after: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EmitterGroup.connect)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.connect "Link to this definition")
    :   Connect the callback to the event group. The callback will receive
        events from *all* of the emitters in the group.
        If this is the first callback to be connected to the group, then
        EventEmitter connects to all sub-emitters in the group.
        It also cheks if the source has a first\_callback\_connect
        method, and if so, it calls it.
    disconnect(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EmitterGroup.disconnect)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.disconnect "Link to this definition")
    :   Disconnect the callback from this group. See
        [`connect()`](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.connect "napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.connect") and `` EventEmitter.connect()`() `` for
        more information.
        If this is the last callback to be disconnected from the group, then
        EventEmitter disconnects from all sub-emitters in the group.
        If the source has a last\_callback\_disconnect method,
        then it is called too.
    property emitters: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [EventEmitter](napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.html#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "napari.utils.events.event.EventEmitter")][#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.emitters "Link to this definition")
    :   List of current emitters in this group.
    property ignore\_callback\_errors[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.ignore_callback_errors "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether exceptions during callbacks will be caught by the emitter
        This allows it to continue invoking other callbacks if an error
        occurs.
    property print\_callback\_errors: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.print_callback_errors "Link to this definition")
    :   Print a message and stack trace if a callback raises an exception
        Valid values are “first” (only show first instance), “reminders” (show
        complete first instance, then counts), “always” (always show full
        traceback), or “never”.
        This assumes ignore\_callback\_errors=True. These will be raised as
        warnings, so ensure that the vispy logging level is set to at
        least “warning”.
    property source: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.source "Link to this definition")
    :   The object that events generated by this emitter apply to
    unblock(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.unblock "Link to this definition")
    :   Unblock this emitter. See `event.EventEmitter.block()`.
        Note: Use of `unblock(None)` only reverses the effect of
        `block(None)`; it does not unblock callbacks that were explicitly
        blocked using `block(callback)`.
    unblock\_all()[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EmitterGroup.unblock_all)[#](#napari.utils.events.EmitterGroup.unblock_all "Link to this definition")
    :   Unblock all emitters in this group, by decrease counter of semaphores for each event emitter.
        if block is called twice and unblock is called once, then events will be still blocked.
        See [Semaphore (programming)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semaphore_(programming)).

* [API](index.html)

* napari.utils.events.Event
# napari.utils.events.Event[#](#napari-utils-events-event "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.Event(*type\_name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *native: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*kwargs: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#Event)[#](#napari.utils.events.Event "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`object`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")
    Class describing events that occur and can be reacted to with callbacks.
    Each event instance contains information about a single event that has
    occurred such as a key press, mouse motion, timer activation, etc.
    Subclasses: `KeyEvent`, `MouseEvent`, `TouchEvent`,
    `StylusEvent`
    The creation of events and passing of events to the appropriate callback
    functions is the responsibility of [`EventEmitter`](napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.html#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter") instances.
    Note that each event object has an attribute for each of the input
    arguments listed below.
    Parameters:
    :   * **type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – String indicating the event type (e.g. mouse\_press, key\_release)
        * **native** ([*object*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)") *(**optional**)*) – The native GUI event object
        * **\*\*kwargs** (*keyword arguments*) – All extra keyword arguments become attributes of the event object.
    Methods
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`blocked`](#napari.utils.events.Event.blocked "napari.utils.events.Event.blocked") | This boolean property indicates whether the event will be delivered to event callbacks. |
    | [`handled`](#napari.utils.events.Event.handled "napari.utils.events.Event.handled") | This boolean property indicates whether the event has already been acted on by an event handler. |
    | `native` |  |
    | [`source`](#napari.utils.events.Event.source "napari.utils.events.Event.source") | The object that the event applies to (i.e. the source of the event). |
    | [`sources`](#napari.utils.events.Event.sources "napari.utils.events.Event.sources") | List of objects that the event applies to (i.e. are or have been a source of the event). |
    | `type` |  |
    Details
    property blocked: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Event.blocked "Link to this definition")
    :   This boolean property indicates whether the event will be delivered
        to event callbacks. If it is set to True, then no further callbacks
        will receive the event. When possible, it is recommended to use
        Event.handled rather than Event.blocked.
    property handled: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Event.handled "Link to this definition")
    :   This boolean property indicates whether the event has already been
        acted on by an event handler. Since many handlers may have access to
        the same events, it is recommended that each check whether the event
        has already been handled as well as set handled=True if it decides to
        act on the event.
    property source: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Event.source "Link to this definition")
    :   The object that the event applies to (i.e. the source of the event).
    property sources: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.events.Event.sources "Link to this definition")
    :   List of objects that the event applies to (i.e. are or have
        been a source of the event). Can contain multiple objects in case
        the event traverses a hierarchy of objects.

# napari.utils.events.EventEmitter[#](#napari-utils-events-eventemitter "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.EventEmitter(*source: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *type\_name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *event\_class: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")] = <class 'napari.utils.events.event.Event'>*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EventEmitter)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`object`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")
    Encapsulates a list of event callbacks.
    Each instance of EventEmitter represents the source of a stream of similar
    events, such as mouse click events or timer activation events. For
    example, the following diagram shows the propagation of a mouse click
    event to the list of callbacks that are registered to listen for that
    event:
    ```
    User clicks    |Canvas creates
    mouse on       |MouseEvent:                |'mouse_press' EventEmitter:         |callbacks in sequence: # noqa
    Canvas         |                           |                                    |  # noqa
                -->|event = MouseEvent(...) -->|Canvas.events.mouse_press(event) -->|callback1(event)  # noqa
                   |                           |                                 -->|callback2(event)  # noqa
                   |                           |                                 -->|callback3(event)  # noqa
    ```
    Callback functions may be added or removed from an EventEmitter using
    `connect()` or
    `disconnect()`.
    Calling an instance of EventEmitter will cause each of its callbacks
    to be invoked in sequence. All callbacks are invoked with a single
    argument which will be an instance of `Event`.
    EventEmitters are generally created by an EmitterGroup instance.
    Parameters:
    :   * **source** ([*object*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")) – The object that the generated events apply to. All emitted Events will
          have their .source property set to this value.
        * **type\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *None*) – String indicating the event type (e.g. mouse\_press, key\_release)
        * **event\_class** (*subclass* *of* [*Event*](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.Event")) – The class of events that this emitter will generate.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`block`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.block "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.block")([callback]) | Block this emitter. |
    | [`blocked`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.blocked "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.blocked")([callback]) | Return boolean indicating whether the emitter is blocked for the given callback. |
    | [`blocker`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.blocker "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.blocker")([callback]) | Return an EventBlocker to be used in 'with' statements |
    | [`connect`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.connect "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.connect")(callback[, ref, position, before, ...]) | Connect this emitter to a new callback. |
    | [`disconnect`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.disconnect "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.disconnect")([callback]) | Disconnect a callback from this emitter. |
    | [`unblock`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.unblock "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.unblock")([callback]) | Unblock this emitter. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`callback_refs`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.callback_refs "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.callback_refs") | The set of callback references |
    | [`callbacks`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.callbacks "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.callbacks") | The set of callbacks |
    | [`ignore_callback_errors`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.ignore_callback_errors "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.ignore_callback_errors") | Whether exceptions during callbacks will be caught by the emitter |
    | [`print_callback_errors`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.print_callback_errors "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.print_callback_errors") | Print a message and stack trace if a callback raises an exception |
    | [`source`](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.source "napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.source") | The object that events generated by this emitter apply to |
    Details
    block(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EventEmitter.block)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.block "Link to this definition")
    :   Block this emitter. Any attempts to emit an event while blocked
        will be silently ignored. If *callback* is given, then the emitter
        is only blocked for that specific callback.
        Calls to block are cumulative; the emitter must be unblocked the same
        number of times as it is blocked.
    blocked(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EventEmitter.blocked)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.blocked "Link to this definition")
    :   Return boolean indicating whether the emitter is blocked for
        the given callback.
    blocker(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EventEmitter.blocker)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.blocker "Link to this definition")
    :   Return an EventBlocker to be used in ‘with’ statements
        Notes
        For example, one could do:
        ```
        with emitter.blocker():
            pass  # ..do stuff; no events will be emitted..
        ```
    property callback\_refs: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), ...][#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.callback_refs "Link to this definition")
    :   The set of callback references
    property callbacks: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[ReferenceType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/weakref.html#weakref.ReferenceType "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")], ...][#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.callbacks "Link to this definition")
    :   The set of callbacks
    connect(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[ReferenceType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/weakref.html#weakref.ReferenceType "(in Python [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[ReferenceType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/weakref.html#weakref.ReferenceType "(in Python [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [EventEmitter](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "napari.utils.events.event.EventEmitter")*, *ref: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *position: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['first', 'last'] = 'last'*, *before: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *after: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")]] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *until: [EventEmitter](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter "napari.utils.events.event.EventEmitter") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EventEmitter.connect)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.connect "Link to this definition")
    :   Connect this emitter to a new callback.
        Parameters:
        :   * **callback** (*function* *|* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")) – *callback* may be either a callable object or a tuple
              (object, attr\_name) where object.attr\_name will point to a
              callable object. Note that only a weak reference to `object`
              will be kept.
            * **ref** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") *|* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Reference used to identify the callback in `before`/`after`.
              If True, the callback ref will automatically determined (see
              Notes). If False, the callback cannot be referred to by a string.
              If str, the given string will be used. Note that if `ref`
              is not unique in `callback_refs`, an error will be thrown.
            * **position** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – If `'first'`, the first eligible position is used (that
              meets the before and after criteria), `'last'` will use
              the last position.
            * **before** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *|* *callback* *|* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *callback* *|* *None*) – List of callbacks that the current callback should precede.
              Can be None if no before-criteria should be used.
            * **after** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *|* *callback* *|* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *callback* *|* *None*) – List of callbacks that the current callback should follow.
              Can be None if no after-criteria should be used.
            * **until** (*optional eventEmitter*) – if provided, when the event until is emitted, callback
              will be disconnected from this emitter.
        Notes
        If `ref=True`, the callback reference will be determined from:
        > 1. If `callback` is `tuple`, the second element in the tuple.
        > 2. The `__name__` attribute.
        > 3. The `__class__.__name__` attribute.
        The current list of callback refs can be obtained using
        `event.callback_refs`. Callbacks can be referred to by either
        their string reference (if given), or by the actual callback that
        was attached (e.g., `(canvas, 'swap_buffers')`).
        If the specified callback is already connected, then the request is
        ignored.
        If before is None and after is None (default), the new callback will
        be added to the beginning of the callback list. Thus the
        callback that is connected \_last\_ will be the \_first\_ to receive
        events from the emitter.
    disconnect(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[ReferenceType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/weakref.html#weakref.ReferenceType "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") | [object](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EventEmitter.disconnect)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.disconnect "Link to this definition")
    :   Disconnect a callback from this emitter.
        If no callback is specified, then *all* callbacks are removed.
        If the callback was not already connected, then the call does nothing.
    property ignore\_callback\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.ignore_callback_errors "Link to this definition")
    :   Whether exceptions during callbacks will be caught by the emitter
        This allows it to continue invoking other callbacks if an error
        occurs.
    property print\_callback\_errors: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.print_callback_errors "Link to this definition")
    :   Print a message and stack trace if a callback raises an exception
        Valid values are “first” (only show first instance), “reminders” (show
        complete first instance, then counts), “always” (always show full
        traceback), or “never”.
        This assumes ignore\_callback\_errors=True. These will be raised as
        warnings, so ensure that the vispy logging level is set to at
        least “warning”.
    property source: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.source "Link to this definition")
    :   The object that events generated by this emitter apply to
    unblock(*callback: [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[[Event](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/event.html#EventEmitter.unblock)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.unblock "Link to this definition")
    :   Unblock this emitter. See `event.EventEmitter.block()`.
        Note: Use of `unblock(None)` only reverses the effect of
        `block(None)`; it does not unblock callbacks that were explicitly
        blocked using `block(callback)`.
