* napari.utils.events.EventedDict
# napari.utils.events.EventedDict[#](#napari-utils-events-eventeddict "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.EventedDict(*data: Mapping[\_K, \_T] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *basetype: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] | Sequence[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]] = ()*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_dict.html#EventedDict)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `TypedMutableMapping`[`_K`, `_T`]
    Mutable dictionary that emits events when altered.
    This class is designed to behave exactly like builtin `dict`, but
    will emit events before and after all mutations (addition, removal, and
    changing).
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*Mapping**,* *optional*) – Dictionary to initialize the class with.
        * **basetype** ([*type*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *sequence* *of* *types**,* *optional*) – Type of the element in the dictionary.
    Events:
    :   * **changing (key** (*K)*) – emitted before an item at `key` is changed
        * **changed (key** (*K, old\_value: T, value: T)*) – emitted when item at `key` is changed from `old_value` to `value`
        * **adding (key** (*K)*) – emitted before an item is added to the dictionary with `key`
        * **added (key** (*K, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` was added to the dictionary with `key`
        * **removing (key** (*K)*) – emitted before `key` is removed from the dictionary
        * **removed (key** (*K, value: T)*) – emitted after `key` was removed from the dictionary
        * **updated (key, K, value** (*T)*) – emitted after `value` of `key` was changed. Only implemented by
          subclasses to give them an option to trigger some update after `value`
          was changed and this class did not register it. This can be useful if
          the `basetype` is not an evented object.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`clear`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.clear "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.clear")() |  |
    | [`copy`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.copy "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.copy")() | Return a shallow copy of the dictionary. |
    | [`first_callback_connect`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.first_callback_connect "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.first_callback_connect")() | When the first callback is connected to self.events, connect to all child emitters. |
    | [`get`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.get "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.get")(k[,d]) |  |
    | [`items`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.items "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.items")() |  |
    | [`key`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.key "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.key")(value) | Return first instance of value. |
    | [`keys`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.keys "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.keys")() |  |
    | [`last_callback_disconnect`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.last_callback_disconnect "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.last_callback_disconnect")() | When the last callback is disconnected from self.events, disconnect from all child emitters. |
    | [`pop`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.pop "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.pop")(k[,d]) | If key is not found, d is returned if given, otherwise KeyError is raised. |
    | [`popitem`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.popitem "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.popitem")() | as a 2-tuple; but raise KeyError if D is empty. |
    | [`setdefault`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.setdefault "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.setdefault")(k[,d]) |  |
    | [`update`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.update "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.update")([E, ]\*\*F) | If E present and has a .keys() method, does: for k in E: D[k] = E[k] If E present and lacks .keys() method, does: for (k, v) in E: D[k] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D[k] = v |
    | [`values`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.values "napari.utils.events.EventedDict.values")() |  |
    Attributes
    Details
    clear() → None.  Remove all items from D.[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.clear "Link to this definition")
    copy() → TypedMutableMapping[\_K, \_T][#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a shallow copy of the dictionary.
    first\_callback\_connect()[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_dict.html#EventedDict.first_callback_connect)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.first_callback_connect "Link to this definition")
    :   When the first callback is connected to self.events,
        connect to all child emitters.
    get(*k*[, *d*]) → D[k] if k in D, else d.  d defaults to None.[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.get "Link to this definition")
    items() → a set-like object providing a view on D's items[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.items "Link to this definition")
    key(*value: \_T*) → \_K | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_dict.html#EventedDict.key)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.key "Link to this definition")
    :   Return first instance of value.
    keys() → a set-like object providing a view on D's keys[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.keys "Link to this definition")
    last\_callback\_disconnect()[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_dict.html#EventedDict.last_callback_disconnect)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.last_callback_disconnect "Link to this definition")
    :   When the last callback is disconnected from self.events, disconnect
        from all child emitters.
    pop(*k*[, *d*]) → v, remove specified key and return the corresponding value.[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   If key is not found, d is returned if given, otherwise KeyError is raised.
    popitem() → (k, v), remove and return some (key, value) pair[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.popitem "Link to this definition")
    :   as a 2-tuple; but raise KeyError if D is empty.
    setdefault(*k*[, *d*]) → D.get(k,d), also set D[k]=d if k not in D[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.setdefault "Link to this definition")
    update([*E*, ]*\*\*F*) → None.  Update D from mapping/iterable E and F.[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.update "Link to this definition")
    :   If E present and has a .keys() method, does: for k in E: D[k] = E[k]
        If E present and lacks .keys() method, does: for (k, v) in E: D[k] = v
        In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D[k] = v
    values() → an object providing a view on D's values[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.values "Link to this definition")
[previous
napari.utils.events.EventEmitter](napari.utils.events.EventEmitter.html "previous page")
[next
napari.utils.events.EventedList](napari.utils.events.EventedList.html "next page")
On this page
* [`EventedDict`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict)
  + [`EventedDict.clear()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.clear)
  + [`EventedDict.copy()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.copy)
  + [`EventedDict.first_callback_connect()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.first_callback_connect)
  + [`EventedDict.get()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.get)
  + [`EventedDict.items()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.items)
  + [`EventedDict.key()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.key)
  + [`EventedDict.keys()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.keys)
  + [`EventedDict.last_callback_disconnect()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.last_callback_disconnect)
  + [`EventedDict.pop()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.pop)
  + [`EventedDict.popitem()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.popitem)
  + [`EventedDict.setdefault()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.setdefault)
  + [`EventedDict.update()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.update)
  + [`EventedDict.values()`](#napari.utils.events.EventedDict.values)
[Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/napari/docs/blob/main/docs/api/napari.utils.events.EventedDict.rst)

* napari.utils.events.EventedList
# napari.utils.events.EventedList[#](#napari-utils-events-eventedlist "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.EventedList(*data: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*, *\**, *basetype: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] | [Sequence](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Sequence "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]] = ()*, *lookup: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_L], [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[\_T], \_T | \_L]] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_list.html#EventedList)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`TypedMutableSequence`](napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.html#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence "napari.utils.events.containers._typed.TypedMutableSequence")[`_T`]
    Mutable Sequence that emits events when altered.
    This class is designed to behave exactly like the builtin `list`, but
    will emit events before and after all mutations (insertion, removal,
    setting, and moving).
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*iterable**,* *optional*) – Elements to initialize the list with.
        * **basetype** ([*type*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *sequence* *of* *types**,* *optional*) – Type of the elements in the list.
        * **lookup** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *Type**[**L**]* *: function**(*[*object*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")*)* *-> L*) – Mapping between a type, and a function that converts items in the list
          to that type.
    Events:
    :   * **inserting (index** (*int)*) – emitted before an item is inserted at `index`
        * **inserted (index** (*int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is inserted at `index`
        * **removing (index** (*int)*) – emitted before an item is removed at `index`
        * **removed (index** (*int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is removed at `index`
        * **moving (index** (*int, new\_index: int)*) – emitted before an item is moved from `index` to `new_index`
        * **moved (index** (*int, new\_index: int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is moved from `index` to `new_index`
        * **changed (index** (*int, old\_value: T, value: T)*) – emitted when item at `index` is changed from `old_value` to `value`
        * **changed <OVERLOAD> (index** (*slice, old\_value: List[\_T], value: List[\_T])*) – emitted when item at `index` is changed from `old_value` to `value`
        * **reordered (value** (*self)*) – emitted when the list is reordered (eg. moved/reversed).
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`append`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.append "napari.utils.events.EventedList.append")(value) | S.append(value) -- append value to the end of the sequence |
    | [`clear`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.clear "napari.utils.events.EventedList.clear")() |  |
    | [`copy`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.copy "napari.utils.events.EventedList.copy")() | Return a shallow copy of the list. |
    | [`count`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.count "napari.utils.events.EventedList.count")(value) |  |
    | [`extend`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.extend "napari.utils.events.EventedList.extend")(values) | S.extend(iterable) -- extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable |
    | [`index`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.index "napari.utils.events.EventedList.index")(value[, start, stop]) | Return first index of value. |
    | [`insert`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.insert "napari.utils.events.EventedList.insert")(index, value) | Insert `value` before index. |
    | [`move`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.move "napari.utils.events.EventedList.move")(src\_index[, dest\_index]) | Insert object at `src_index` before `dest_index`. |
    | [`move_multiple`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.move_multiple "napari.utils.events.EventedList.move_multiple")(sources[, dest\_index]) | Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination. |
    | [`pop`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.pop "napari.utils.events.EventedList.pop")([index]) | Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range. |
    | [`remove`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.remove "napari.utils.events.EventedList.remove")(value) | S.remove(value) -- remove first occurrence of value. |
    | [`reverse`](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.reverse "napari.utils.events.EventedList.reverse")() | Reverse list *IN PLACE*. |
    Attributes
    Details
    append(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.append "Link to this definition")
    :   S.append(value) – append value to the end of the sequence
    clear() → None -- remove all items from S[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.clear "Link to this definition")
    copy() → [TypedMutableSequence](napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.html#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence "napari.utils.events.containers._typed.TypedMutableSequence")[\_T][#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a shallow copy of the list.
    count(*value*) → integer -- return number of occurrences of value[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.count "Link to this definition")
    extend(*values*)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.extend "Link to this definition")
    :   S.extend(iterable) – extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable
    index(*value: \_L*, *start: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*, *stop: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.index "Link to this definition")
    :   Return first index of value.
        Parameters:
        :   * **value** (*Any*) – A value to lookup. If type(value) is in the lookups functions
              provided for this class, then values in the list will be searched
              using the corresponding lookup converter function.
            * **start** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The starting index to search, by default 0
            * **stop** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The ending index to search, by default None
        Returns:
        :   The index of the value
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the value is not present
    insert(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_list.html#EventedList.insert)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.insert "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert `value` before index.
    move(*src\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_list.html#EventedList.move)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.move "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert object at `src_index` before `dest_index`.
        Both indices refer to the list prior to any object removal
        (pre-move space).
    move\_multiple(*sources: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [slice](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_list.html#EventedList.move_multiple)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.move_multiple "Link to this definition")
    :   Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination.
        Note, if dest\_index is higher than any of the sources, then
        the resulting position of the moved objects after the move operation
        is complete will be lower than dest\_index.
        Parameters:
        :   * **sources** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*slice*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – A sequence of indices
            * **dest\_index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The destination index. All sources will be inserted before this
              index (in pre-move space), by default 0… which has the effect of
              “bringing to front” everything in `sources`, or acting as a
              “reorder” method if `sources` contains all indices.
        Returns:
        :   The number of successful move operations completed.
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the destination index is a slice, or any of the source indices
            are not `int` or `slice`.
    pop([*index*]) → item -- remove and return item at index (default last).[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range.
    remove(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   S.remove(value) – remove first occurrence of value.
        Raise ValueError if the value is not present.
    reverse() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_evented_list.html#EventedList.reverse)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedList.reverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Reverse list *IN PLACE*.

# napari.utils.events.EventedModel[#](#napari-utils-events-eventedmodel "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.EventedModel[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/evented_model.html#EventedModel)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`BaseModel`](https://pydantic.dev/docs/validation/latest/api/pydantic/base_model/#pydantic.BaseModel "(in Pydantic v0.0.0)")
    A Model subclass that emits an event whenever a field value is changed.
    Note: As per the standard pydantic behavior, default Field values are
    not validated (#4138) and should be correctly typed.

    Details
    copy(*\**, *include: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *exclude: AbstractSetIntStr | MappingIntStrAny | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *update: Dict[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), Any] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → Self[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! warning “Deprecated”
        :   This method is now deprecated; use model\_copy instead.
        If you need include or exclude, use:
        `` `python {test="skip" lint="skip"}
        data = self.model_dump(include=include, exclude=exclude, round_trip=True)
        data = {**data, **(update or {})}
        copied = self.model_validate(data)
        ` ``
        Parameters:
        :   * **include** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to include in the copied model.
            * **exclude** – Optional set or mapping specifying which fields to exclude in the copied model.
            * **update** – Optional dictionary of field-value pairs to override field values in the copied model.
            * **deep** – If True, the values of fields that are Pydantic models will be deep-copied.
        Returns:
        :   A copy of the model with included, excluded and updated fields as specified.
    enums\_as\_values(*as\_values: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/evented_model.html#EventedModel.enums_as_values)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.enums_as_values "Link to this definition")
    :   Temporarily override how enums are retrieved.
        Parameters:
        :   **as\_values** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether enums should be shown as values (or as enum objects),
            by default True
    model\_config = {'arbitrary\_types\_allowed': True, 'json\_encoders': {<class 'app\_model.types.\_keys.\_keybindings.KeyBinding'>: <function <lambda>>, <class 'napari.layers.utils.color\_encoding.ColorEncoding'>: <function StyleEncoding.\_json\_encode>, <class 'napari.layers.utils.string\_encoding.StringEncoding'>: <function StyleEncoding.\_json\_encode>, <class 'napari.settings.\_fields.Version'>: <function Version.\_json\_encode>, <class 'numpy.ndarray'>: <function <lambda>>}, 'validate\_assignment': True, 'validate\_default': True}[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_config "Link to this definition")
    :   Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].
    classmethod model\_construct(*\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*values: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_construct "Link to this definition")
    :   Creates a new instance of the Model class with validated data.
        Creates a new model setting \_\_dict\_\_ and \_\_pydantic\_fields\_set\_\_ from trusted or pre-validated data.
        Default values are respected, but no other validation is performed.
        !!! note
        :   model\_construct() generally respects the model\_config.extra setting on the provided model.
            That is, if model\_config.extra == ‘allow’, then all extra passed values are added to the model instance’s \_\_dict\_\_
            and \_\_pydantic\_extra\_\_ fields. If model\_config.extra == ‘ignore’ (the default), then all extra passed values are ignored.
            Because no validation is performed with a call to model\_construct(), having model\_config.extra == ‘forbid’ does not result in
            an error if extra values are passed, but they will be ignored.
        Parameters:
        :   * **\_fields\_set** – A set of field names that were originally explicitly set during instantiation. If provided,
              this is directly used for the [model\_fields\_set][pydantic.BaseModel.model\_fields\_set] attribute.
              Otherwise, the field names from the values argument will be used.
            * **values** – Trusted or pre-validated data dictionary.
        Returns:
        :   A new instance of the Model class with validated data.
    model\_copy(*\**, *update: [Mapping](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Mapping "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *deep: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_copy "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_copy](../concepts/models.md#model-copy)
        Returns a copy of the model.
        !!! note
        :   The underlying instance’s [\_\_dict\_\_][object.\_\_dict\_\_] attribute is copied. This
            might have unexpected side effects if you store anything in it, on top of the model
            fields (e.g. the value of [cached properties][functools.cached\_property]).
        Parameters:
        :   * **update** – Values to change/add in the new model. Note: the data is not validated
              before creating the new model. You should trust this data.
            * **deep** – Set to True to make a deep copy of the model.
        Returns:
        :   New model instance.

[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_dump "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump](../concepts/serialization.md#python-mode)
        Generate a dictionary representation of the model, optionally specifying which fields to include or exclude.
        Parameters:
        :   * **mode** – The mode in which to\_python should run.
              If mode is ‘json’, the output will only contain JSON serializable types.
              If mode is ‘python’, the output may contain non-JSON-serializable Python objects.
            * **include** – A set of fields to include in the output.
            * **exclude** – A set of fields to exclude from the output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias in the dictionary key if defined.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary representation of the model.
    model\_dump\_json(*\**, *indent: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_dump_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [model\_dump\_json](../concepts/serialization.md#json-mode)
        Generates a JSON representation of the model using Pydantic’s to\_json method.
        Parameters:
        :   * **indent** – Indentation to use in the JSON output. If None is passed, the output will be compact.
            * **ensure\_ascii** – If True, the output is guaranteed to have all incoming non-ASCII characters escaped.
              If False (the default), these characters will be output as-is.
            * **include** – Field(s) to include in the JSON output.
            * **exclude** – Field(s) to exclude from the JSON output.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the serializer.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to serialize using field aliases.
            * **exclude\_unset** – Whether to exclude fields that have not been explicitly set.
            * **exclude\_defaults** – Whether to exclude fields that are set to their default value.
            * **exclude\_none** – Whether to exclude fields that have a value of None.
            * **exclude\_computed\_fields** – Whether to exclude computed fields.
              While this can be useful for round-tripping, it is usually recommended to use the dedicated
              round\_trip parameter instead.
            * **round\_trip** – If True, dumped values should be valid as input for non-idempotent types such as Json[T].
            * **warnings** – How to handle serialization errors. False/”none” ignores them, True/”warn” logs errors,
              “error” raises a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError].
            * **fallback** – A function to call when an unknown value is encountered. If not provided,
              a [PydanticSerializationError][pydantic\_core.PydanticSerializationError] error is raised.
            * **serialize\_as\_any** – Whether to serialize fields with duck-typing serialization behavior.
            * **polymorphic\_serialization** – Whether to use model and dataclass polymorphic serialization for this call.
        Returns:
        :   A JSON string representation of the model.
    property model\_extra: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_extra "Link to this definition")
    :   Get extra fields set during validation.
        Returns:
        :   A dictionary of extra fields, or None if config.extra is not set to “allow”.
    property model\_fields\_set: [set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_fields_set "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns the set of fields that have been explicitly set on this model instance.
        Returns:
        :   A set of strings representing the fields that have been set,
            :   i.e. that were not filled from defaults.
    classmethod model\_json\_schema(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/evented_model.html#EventedModel.model_json_schema)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_json_schema "Link to this definition")
    :   Generate a JSON schema for this model.
        This is required to prevent from mail formated docstring break docs build.
    classmethod model\_parametrized\_name(*params: [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")], ...]*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_parametrized_name "Link to this definition")
    :   Compute the class name for parametrizations of generic classes.
        This method can be overridden to achieve a custom naming scheme for generic BaseModels.
        Parameters:
        :   **params** – Tuple of types of the class. Given a generic class
            Model with 2 type variables and a concrete model Model[str, int],
            the value (str, int) would be passed to params.
        Returns:
        :   String representing the new class where params are passed to cls as type variables.
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – Raised when trying to generate concrete names for non-generic models.
    model\_post\_init(*context: Any*, */*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_post_init "Link to this definition")
    :   This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialize private attributes.
        It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.
        Parameters:
        :   * **self** – The BaseModel instance.
            * **context** – The context.
    classmethod model\_rebuild(*\**, *force: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *raise\_errors: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*, *\_parent\_namespace\_depth: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 2*, *\_types\_namespace: MappingNamespace | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_rebuild "Link to this definition")
    :   Try to rebuild the pydantic-core schema for the model.
        This may be necessary when one of the annotations is a ForwardRef which could not be resolved during
        the initial attempt to build the schema, and automatic rebuilding fails.
        Parameters:
        :   * **force** – Whether to force the rebuilding of the model schema, defaults to False.
            * **raise\_errors** – Whether to raise errors, defaults to True.
            * **\_parent\_namespace\_depth** – The depth level of the parent namespace, defaults to 2.
            * **\_types\_namespace** – The types namespace, defaults to None.
        Returns:
        :   Returns None if the schema is already “complete” and rebuilding was not required.
            If rebuilding \_was\_ required, returns True if rebuilding was successful, otherwise False.
    classmethod model\_validate(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *from\_attributes: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_validate "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate a pydantic model instance.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **from\_attributes** – Whether to extract data from object attributes.
            * **context** – Additional context to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If the object could not be validated.
        Returns:
        :   The validated model instance.
    classmethod model\_validate\_json(*json\_data: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes "(in Python v3.14)") | [bytearray](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytearray "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_validate_json "Link to this definition")
    :   !!! abstract “Usage Documentation”
        :   [JSON Parsing](../concepts/json.md#json-parsing)
        Validate the given JSON data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **json\_data** – The JSON data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
        Raises:
        :   **ValidationError** – If json\_data is not a JSON string or the object could not be validated.
    classmethod model\_validate\_strings(*obj: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *strict: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *extra: [Literal](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal "(in Python v3.14)")['allow', 'ignore', 'forbid'] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *context: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_alias: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *by\_name: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [Self](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Self "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.model_validate_strings "Link to this definition")
    :   Validate the given object with string data against the Pydantic model.
        Parameters:
        :   * **obj** – The object containing string data to validate.
            * **strict** – Whether to enforce types strictly.
            * **extra** – Whether to ignore, allow, or forbid extra data during model validation.
              See the [extra configuration value][pydantic.ConfigDict.extra] for details.
            * **context** – Extra variables to pass to the validator.
            * **by\_alias** – Whether to use the field’s alias when validating against the provided input data.
            * **by\_name** – Whether to use the field’s name when validating against the provided input data.
        Returns:
        :   The validated Pydantic model.
    reset()[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/evented_model.html#EventedModel.reset)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.reset "Link to this definition")
    :   Reset the state of the model to default values.
    update(*values: [EventedModel](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.evented_model.EventedModel") | [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*, *recurse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = True*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/evented_model.html#EventedModel.update)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update a model in place.
        Parameters:
        :   * **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.events.EventedModel*](#napari.utils.events.EventedModel "napari.utils.events.EventedModel")) – Values to update the model with. If an EventedModel is passed it is
              first converted to a dictionary. The keys of this dictionary must
              be found as attributes on the current model.
            * **recurse** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, recursively update fields that are EventedModels.
              Otherwise, just update the immediate fields of this EventedModel,
              which is useful when the declared field type (e.g. `Union`) can have
              different realized types with different fields.

# napari.utils.events.EventedSet[#](#napari-utils-events-eventedset "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.EventedSet(*data: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`MutableSet`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.MutableSet "(in Python v3.14)")[`_T`]
    An unordered collection of unique elements.
    Parameters:
    :   **data** (*iterable**,* *optional*) – Elements to initialize the set with.
    Events:
    :   **changed (added** (*Set[\_T], removed: Set[\_T])*) – Emitted when the set changes, includes item(s) that have been added
        and/or removed from the set.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`add`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.add "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.add")(value) | Add an element to the set, if not already present. |
    | [`clear`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.clear "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.clear")() | This is slow (creates N new iterators!) but effective. |
    | [`copy`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.copy "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.copy")() | Return a shallow copy of this set. |
    | [`difference`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.difference "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.difference")([others]) | Return set of all elements that are in this set but not other. |
    | [`difference_update`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.difference_update "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.difference_update")([others]) | Remove all elements of another set from this set. |
    | [`discard`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.discard "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.discard")(value) | Remove an element from a set if it is a member. |
    | [`intersection`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.intersection "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.intersection")([others]) | Return all elements that are in both sets as a new set. |
    | [`intersection_update`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.intersection_update "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.intersection_update")([others]) | Remove all elements of in this set that are not present in other. |
    | [`isdisjoint`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.isdisjoint "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.isdisjoint")(other) | Return True if two sets have a null intersection. |
    | [`issubset`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.issubset "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.issubset")(others) | Returns whether another set contains this set or not |
    | [`issuperset`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.issuperset "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.issuperset")(others) | Returns whether this set contains another set or not |
    | [`pop`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.pop "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.pop")() | Return the popped value. |
    | [`remove`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.remove "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.remove")(value) | Remove an element. |
    | [`symmetric_difference`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.symmetric_difference "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.symmetric_difference")(others) | Returns set of elements that are in exactly one of the sets |
    | [`symmetric_difference_update`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.symmetric_difference_update "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.symmetric_difference_update")(others) | Update set to the symmetric difference of itself and another. |
    | [`union`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.union "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.union")([others]) | Return a set containing the union of sets |
    | [`update`](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.update "napari.utils.events.EventedSet.update")([others]) | Update this set with the union of this set and others |
    Attributes
    Details
    add(*value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.add)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.add "Link to this definition")
    :   Add an element to the set, if not already present.
    clear() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.clear)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.clear "Link to this definition")
    :   This is slow (creates N new iterators!) but effective.

    :   Return a shallow copy of this set.
    difference(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [EventedSet](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.difference)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.difference "Link to this definition")
    :   Return set of all elements that are in this set but not other.
    difference\_update(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.difference_update)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.difference_update "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove all elements of another set from this set.
    discard(*value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.discard)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.discard "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove an element from a set if it is a member.
        If the element is not a member, do nothing.
    intersection(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [EventedSet](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.intersection)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.intersection "Link to this definition")
    :   Return all elements that are in both sets as a new set.
    intersection\_update(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.intersection_update)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.intersection_update "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove all elements of in this set that are not present in other.
    isdisjoint(*other*)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.isdisjoint "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if two sets have a null intersection.
    issubset(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.issubset)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.issubset "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns whether another set contains this set or not
    issuperset(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.issuperset)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.issuperset "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns whether this set contains another set or not
    pop()[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the popped value. Raise KeyError if empty.
    remove(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove an element. If not a member, raise a KeyError.
    symmetric\_difference(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]*) → [EventedSet](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.symmetric_difference)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.symmetric_difference "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns set of elements that are in exactly one of the sets
    symmetric\_difference\_update(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.symmetric_difference_update)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.symmetric_difference_update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update set to the symmetric difference of itself and another.
        This will remove any items in this set that are also in other, and
        add any items in others that are not present in this set.
    union(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [EventedSet](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.union)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.union "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a set containing the union of sets
    update(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_set.html#EventedSet.update)[#](#napari.utils.events.EventedSet.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update this set with the union of this set and others

<!-- Source: https://napari.org/stable/api/napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.html -->
* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* [napari.utils.events](napari.utils.events.html)
* napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList
# napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList[#](#napari-utils-events-nestableeventedlist "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList(*data: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*, *\**, *basetype: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] | [Sequence](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Sequence "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]] = ()*, *lookup: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_L], [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[\_T], \_T | \_L]] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_nested_list.html#NestableEventedList)[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`EventedList`](napari.utils.events.EventedList.html#napari.utils.events.EventedList "napari.utils.events.containers._evented_list.EventedList")[`_T`]
    Nestable Mutable Sequence that emits recursive events when altered.
    `NestableEventedList` instances can be indexed with a `tuple` of
    `int` (e.g. `mylist[0, 2, 1]`) to retrieve nested child objects.
    A key property of this class is that when new mutable sequences are added
    to the list, they are themselves converted to a `NestableEventedList`,
    and all of the `EventEmitter` objects in the child are connect to the
    parent object’s `_reemit_child_event` method (assuming the child has
    an attribute called `events` that is an instance of `EmitterGroup`).
    When `_reemit_child_event` receives an event from a child object, it
    remits the event, but changes any `index` keys in the event to a
    `NestedIndex` (a tuple of `int`) such that indices emitted by any given
    `NestableEventedList` are always relative to itself.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*iterable**,* *optional*) – Elements to initialize the list with. by default None.
        * **basetype** ([*type*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *sequence* *of* *types**,* *optional*) – Type of the elements in the list.
        * **lookup** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *Type**[**L**]* *: function**(*[*object*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")*)* *-> L*) – Mapping between a type, and a function that converts items in the list
          to that type.
    Events:
    :   * **types used** – Index = Union[int, Tuple[int, …]]
        * **inserting (index** (*Index)*) – emitted before an item is inserted at `index`
        * **inserted (index** (*Index, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is inserted at `index`
        * **removing (index** (*Index)*) – emitted before an item is removed at `index`
        * **removed (index** (*Index, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is removed at `index`
        * **moving (index** (*Index, new\_index: Index)*) – emitted before an item is moved from `index` to `new_index`
        * **moved (index** (*Index, new\_index: Index, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is moved from `index` to `new_index`
        * **changed (index** (*Index, old\_value: T, value: T)*) – emitted when item at `index` is changed from `old_value` to `value`
        * **changed <OVERLOAD> (index** (*slice, old\_value: list[\_T], value: list[\_T])*) – emitted when slice at `index` is changed from `old_value` to `value`
        * **reordered (value** (*self)*) – emitted when the list is reordered (eg. moved/reversed).
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`append`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.append "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.append")(value) | S.append(value) -- append value to the end of the sequence |
    | [`clear`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.clear "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.clear")() |  |
    | [`copy`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.copy "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.copy")() | Return a shallow copy of the list. |
    | [`count`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.count "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.count")(value) |  |
    | [`extend`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.extend "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.extend")(values) | S.extend(iterable) -- extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable |
    | [`has_index`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.has_index "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.has_index")(index) | Return true if index is valid for this nestable list. |
    | [`index`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.index "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.index")(value[, start, stop]) | Return first index of value. |
    | [`insert`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.insert "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.insert")(index, value) | Insert object before index. |
    | [`move`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.move "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.move")(src\_index[, dest\_index]) | Move a single item from `src_index` to `dest_index`. |
    | [`move_multiple`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.move_multiple "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.move_multiple")(sources[, dest\_index]) | Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination. |
    | [`pop`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.pop "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.pop")([index]) | Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range. |
    | [`remove`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.remove "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.remove")(value) | S.remove(value) -- remove first occurrence of value. |
    | [`reverse`](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.reverse "napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.reverse")() | Reverse list *IN PLACE*. |
    Attributes
    Details
    append(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.append "Link to this definition")
    :   S.append(value) – append value to the end of the sequence
    clear() → None -- remove all items from S[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.clear "Link to this definition")
    copy() → [TypedMutableSequence](napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.html#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence "napari.utils.events.containers._typed.TypedMutableSequence")[\_T][#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a shallow copy of the list.
    count(*value*) → integer -- return number of occurrences of value[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.count "Link to this definition")
    extend(*values*)[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.extend "Link to this definition")
    :   S.extend(iterable) – extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable
    has\_index(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)"), ...]*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_nested_list.html#NestableEventedList.has_index)[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.has_index "Link to this definition")
    :   Return true if index is valid for this nestable list.
    index(*value: \_L*, *start: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*, *stop: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.index "Link to this definition")
    :   Return first index of value.
        Parameters:
        :   * **value** (*Any*) – A value to lookup. If type(value) is in the lookups functions
              provided for this class, then values in the list will be searched
              using the corresponding lookup converter function.
            * **start** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The starting index to search, by default 0
            * **stop** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The ending index to search, by default None
        Returns:
        :   The index of the value
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the value is not present
    insert(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_nested_list.html#NestableEventedList.insert)[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.insert "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert object before index.
    move(*src\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [slice](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)"), ...]*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [slice](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)"), ...] = (0,)*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_nested_list.html#NestableEventedList.move)[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.move "Link to this definition")
    :   Move a single item from `src_index` to `dest_index`.
        Parameters:
        :   * **src\_index** (*Union**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *NestedIndex**]*) – The index of the object to move
            * **dest\_index** (*Union**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *NestedIndex**]**,* *optional*) – The destination. Object will be inserted before `dest_index.`,
              by default, will insert at the front of the root list.
        Returns:
        :   Whether the operation completed successfully
        Return type:
        :   [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the terminal source is a slice, or if the source is this root
            object
    move\_multiple(*sources: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [slice](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.move_multiple "Link to this definition")
    :   Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination.
        Note, if dest\_index is higher than any of the sources, then
        the resulting position of the moved objects after the move operation
        is complete will be lower than dest\_index.
        Parameters:
        :   * **sources** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*slice*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – A sequence of indices
            * **dest\_index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The destination index. All sources will be inserted before this
              index (in pre-move space), by default 0… which has the effect of
              “bringing to front” everything in `sources`, or acting as a
              “reorder” method if `sources` contains all indices.
        Returns:
        :   The number of successful move operations completed.
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the destination index is a slice, or any of the source indices
            are not `int` or `slice`.
    pop([*index*]) → item -- remove and return item at index (default last).[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range.
    remove(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   S.remove(value) – remove first occurrence of value.
        Raise ValueError if the value is not present.
    reverse() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.NestableEventedList.reverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Reverse list *IN PLACE*.
