* napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList
# napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList[#](#napari-utils-events-selectableeventedlist "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList(*\*args: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\*\*kwargs: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selectable_list.html#SelectableEventedList)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: `Selectable`[`_T`], [`EventedList`](napari.utils.events.EventedList.html#napari.utils.events.EventedList "napari.utils.events.containers._evented_list.EventedList")[`_T`]
    List model that also supports selection.
    Events:
    :   * **inserting (index** (*int)*) – emitted before an item is inserted at `index`
        * **inserted (index** (*int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is inserted at `index`
        * **removing (index** (*int)*) – emitted before an item is removed at `index`
        * **removed (index** (*int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is removed at `index`
        * **moving (index** (*int, new\_index: int)*) – emitted before an item is moved from `index` to `new_index`
        * **moved (index** (*int, new\_index: int, value: T)*) – emitted after `value` is moved from `index` to `new_index`
        * **changed (index** (*int, old\_value: T, value: T)*) – emitted when item at `index` is changed from `old_value` to `value`
        * **changed <OVERLOAD> (index** (*slice, old\_value: List[\_T], value: List[\_T])*) – emitted when item at `index` is changed from `old_value` to `value`
        * **reordered (value** (*self)*) – emitted when the list is reordered (eg. moved/reversed).
        * **selection.changed (added** (*Set[\_T], removed: Set[\_T])*) – Emitted when the set changes, includes item(s) that have been added
          and/or removed from the set.
        * **selection.active (value** (*\_T)*) – emitted when the current item has changed.
        * **selection.\_current (value** (*\_T)*) – emitted when the current item has changed. (Private event)
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`append`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.append "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.append")(value) | S.append(value) -- append value to the end of the sequence |
    | [`clear`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.clear "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.clear")() |  |
    | [`copy`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.copy "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.copy")() | Return a shallow copy of the list. |
    | [`count`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.count "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.count")(value) |  |
    | [`extend`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.extend "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.extend")(values) | S.extend(iterable) -- extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable |
    | [`index`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.index "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.index")(value[, start, stop]) | Return first index of value. |
    | [`insert`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.insert "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.insert")(index, value) | Insert `value` before index. |
    | [`move`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move")(src\_index[, dest\_index]) | Insert object at `src_index` before `dest_index`. |
    | [`move_multiple`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move_multiple "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move_multiple")(sources[, dest\_index]) | Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination. |
    | [`move_selected`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move_selected "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move_selected")(index, insert) | Reorder list by moving the item at index and inserting it at the insert index. |
    | [`pop`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.pop "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.pop")([index]) | Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range. |
    | [`remove`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.remove "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.remove")(value) | S.remove(value) -- remove first occurrence of value. |
    | [`remove_selected`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.remove_selected "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.remove_selected")() | Remove selected items from list. |
    | [`reverse`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.reverse "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.reverse")() | Reverse list *IN PLACE*. |
    | [`select_all`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_all "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_all")() | Select all items in the list. |
    | [`select_next`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_next "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_next")([step, shift]) | Selects next item from list. |
    | [`select_previous`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_previous "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_previous")([shift]) | Selects previous item from list. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`selection`](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.selection "napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.selection") | Get current selection. |
    Details
    append(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.append "Link to this definition")
    :   S.append(value) – append value to the end of the sequence
    clear() → None -- remove all items from S[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.clear "Link to this definition")
    copy() → [TypedMutableSequence](napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.html#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence "napari.utils.events.containers._typed.TypedMutableSequence")[\_T][#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a shallow copy of the list.
    count(*value*) → integer -- return number of occurrences of value[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.count "Link to this definition")
    extend(*values*)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.extend "Link to this definition")
    :   S.extend(iterable) – extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable
    index(*value: \_L*, *start: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*, *stop: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.index "Link to this definition")
    :   Return first index of value.
        Parameters:
        :   * **value** (*Any*) – A value to lookup. If type(value) is in the lookups functions
              provided for this class, then values in the list will be searched
              using the corresponding lookup converter function.
            * **start** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The starting index to search, by default 0
            * **stop** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The ending index to search, by default None
        Returns:
        :   The index of the value
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the value is not present
    insert(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selectable_list.html#SelectableEventedList.insert)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.insert "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert `value` before index.
    move(*src\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move "Link to this definition")
    :   Insert object at `src_index` before `dest_index`.
        Both indices refer to the list prior to any object removal
        (pre-move space).
    move\_multiple(*sources: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [slice](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")]*, *dest\_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move_multiple "Link to this definition")
    :   Move a batch of sources indices, to a single destination.
        Note, if dest\_index is higher than any of the sources, then
        the resulting position of the moved objects after the move operation
        is complete will be lower than dest\_index.
        Parameters:
        :   * **sources** (*Sequence**[*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*slice*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#slice "(in Python v3.14)")*]*) – A sequence of indices
            * **dest\_index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The destination index. All sources will be inserted before this
              index (in pre-move space), by default 0… which has the effect of
              “bringing to front” everything in `sources`, or acting as a
              “reorder” method if `sources` contains all indices.
        Returns:
        :   The number of successful move operations completed.
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**TypeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the destination index is a slice, or any of the source indices
            are not `int` or `slice`.
    move\_selected(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *insert: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selectable_list.html#SelectableEventedList.move_selected)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.move_selected "Link to this definition")
    :   Reorder list by moving the item at index and inserting it
        at the insert index. If additional items are selected these will
        get inserted at the insert index too. This allows for rearranging
        the list based on dragging and dropping a selection of items, where
        index is the index of the primary item being dragged, and insert is
        the index of the drop location, and the selection indicates if
        multiple items are being dragged. If the moved layer is not selected
        select it.
        This method is deprecated. Please use layers.move\_multiple
        with layers.selection instead.
        Parameters:
        :   * **index** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Index of primary item to be moved
            * **insert** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Index that item(s) will be inserted at
    pop([*index*]) → item -- remove and return item at index (default last).[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range.
    remove(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   S.remove(value) – remove first occurrence of value.
        Raise ValueError if the value is not present.
    remove\_selected() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selectable_list.html#SelectableEventedList.remove_selected)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.remove_selected "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove selected items from list.
    reverse() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.reverse "Link to this definition")
    :   Reverse list *IN PLACE*.
    select\_all() → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selectable_list.html#SelectableEventedList.select_all)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_all "Link to this definition")
    :   Select all items in the list.
    select\_next(*step: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 1*, *shift: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selectable_list.html#SelectableEventedList.select_next)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_next "Link to this definition")
    :   Selects next item from list.
    select\_previous(*shift: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selectable_list.html#SelectableEventedList.select_previous)[#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.select_previous "Link to this definition")
    :   Selects previous item from list.
    property selection: [Selection](napari.utils.events.Selection.html#napari.utils.events.Selection "napari.utils.events.containers._selection.Selection")[\_S][#](#napari.utils.events.SelectableEventedList.selection "Link to this definition")
    :   Get current selection.

<!-- Source: https://napari.org/stable/api/napari.utils.events.Selection.html -->
* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* [napari.utils.events](napari.utils.events.html)
* napari.utils.events.Selection
# napari.utils.events.Selection[#](#napari-utils-events-selection "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.Selection(*data: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selection.html#Selection)[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`EventedSet`](napari.utils.events.EventedSet.html#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[`_T`]
    A model of selected items, with `active` and `current` item.
    There can only be one `active` and one `current` item, but there can be
    multiple selected items. An “active” item is defined as a single selected
    item (if multiple items are selected, there is no active item). The
    “current” item is mostly useful for (e.g.) keyboard actions: even with
    multiple items selected, you may only have one current item, and keyboard
    events (like up and down) can modify that current item. It’s possible to
    have a current item without an active item, but an active item will always
    be the current item.
    An item can be the current item and selected at the same time. Qt views
    will ensure that there is always a current item as keyboard navigation,
    for example, requires a current item.
    This pattern mimics current/selected items from Qt:
    <https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/model-view-programming.html#current-item-and-selected-items>
    Parameters:
    :   **data** (*iterable**,* *optional*) – Elements to initialize the set with.
    active[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.active "Link to this definition")
    :   The active item, if any. An active item is the one being edited.
        Type:
        :   Any, optional
    \_current[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection._current "Link to this definition")
    :   The current item, if any. This is used primarily by GUI views when
        handling mouse/key events.
        Type:
        :   Any, optional
    Events:
    :   * **changed (added** (*Set[\_T], removed: Set[\_T])*) – Emitted when the set changes, includes item(s) that have been added and/or removed from the set.
        * **active (value** (*\_T)*) – emitted when the current item has changed.
        * **\_current (value** (*\_T)*) – emitted when the current item has changed. (Private event)
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`add`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.add "napari.utils.events.Selection.add")(value) | Add an element to the set, if not already present. |
    | [`clear`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.clear "napari.utils.events.Selection.clear")([keep\_current]) | Clear the selection. |
    | [`copy`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.copy "napari.utils.events.Selection.copy")() | Return a shallow copy of this set. |
    | [`difference`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.difference "napari.utils.events.Selection.difference")([others]) | Return set of all elements that are in this set but not other. |
    | [`difference_update`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.difference_update "napari.utils.events.Selection.difference_update")([others]) | Remove all elements of another set from this set. |
    | [`discard`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.discard "napari.utils.events.Selection.discard")(value) | Remove an element from a set if it is a member. |
    | [`intersection`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.intersection "napari.utils.events.Selection.intersection")([others]) | Return all elements that are in both sets as a new set. |
    | [`intersection_update`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.intersection_update "napari.utils.events.Selection.intersection_update")([others]) | Remove all elements of in this set that are not present in other. |
    | [`isdisjoint`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.isdisjoint "napari.utils.events.Selection.isdisjoint")(other) | Return True if two sets have a null intersection. |
    | [`issubset`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.issubset "napari.utils.events.Selection.issubset")(others) | Returns whether another set contains this set or not |
    | [`issuperset`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.issuperset "napari.utils.events.Selection.issuperset")(others) | Returns whether this set contains another set or not |
    | [`pop`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.pop "napari.utils.events.Selection.pop")() | Return the popped value. |
    | [`remove`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.remove "napari.utils.events.Selection.remove")(value) | Remove an element. |
    | [`select_only`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.select_only "napari.utils.events.Selection.select_only")(obj) | Unselect everything but obj. |
    | [`symmetric_difference`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.symmetric_difference "napari.utils.events.Selection.symmetric_difference")(others) | Returns set of elements that are in exactly one of the sets |
    | [`symmetric_difference_update`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.symmetric_difference_update "napari.utils.events.Selection.symmetric_difference_update")(others) | Update set to the symmetric difference of itself and another. |
    | [`toggle`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.toggle "napari.utils.events.Selection.toggle")(obj) | Toggle selection state of obj. |
    | [`union`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.union "napari.utils.events.Selection.union")([others]) | Return a set containing the union of sets |
    | [`update`](#napari.utils.events.Selection.update "napari.utils.events.Selection.update")([others]) | Update this set with the union of this set and others |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`active`](#id0 "napari.utils.events.Selection.active") | Return the currently active item or None. |
    Details
    property active: \_T | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#id0 "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the currently active item or None.
    add(*value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.add "Link to this definition")
    :   Add an element to the set, if not already present.
    clear(*keep\_current: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selection.html#Selection.clear)[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.clear "Link to this definition")
    :   Clear the selection.
    copy() → [EventedSet](napari.utils.events.EventedSet.html#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a shallow copy of this set.
    difference(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [EventedSet](napari.utils.events.EventedSet.html#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.difference "Link to this definition")
    :   Return set of all elements that are in this set but not other.
    difference\_update(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.difference_update "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove all elements of another set from this set.
    discard(*value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.discard "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove an element from a set if it is a member.
        If the element is not a member, do nothing.
    intersection(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [EventedSet](napari.utils.events.EventedSet.html#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.intersection "Link to this definition")
    :   Return all elements that are in both sets as a new set.
    intersection\_update(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.intersection_update "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove all elements of in this set that are not present in other.
    isdisjoint(*other*)[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.isdisjoint "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if two sets have a null intersection.
    issubset(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.issubset "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns whether another set contains this set or not
    issuperset(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]*) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.issuperset "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns whether this set contains another set or not
    pop()[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the popped value. Raise KeyError if empty.
    remove(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove an element. If not a member, raise a KeyError.
    select\_only(*obj: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selection.html#Selection.select_only)[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.select_only "Link to this definition")
    :   Unselect everything but obj. Add to selection if not present.
    symmetric\_difference(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]*) → [EventedSet](napari.utils.events.EventedSet.html#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.symmetric_difference "Link to this definition")
    :   Returns set of elements that are in exactly one of the sets
    symmetric\_difference\_update(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.symmetric_difference_update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update set to the symmetric difference of itself and another.
        This will remove any items in this set that are also in other, and
        add any items in others that are not present in this set.
    toggle(*obj: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_selection.html#Selection.toggle)[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.toggle "Link to this definition")
    :   Toggle selection state of obj.
    union(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [EventedSet](napari.utils.events.EventedSet.html#napari.utils.events.EventedSet "napari.utils.events.containers._set.EventedSet")[\_T][#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.union "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a set containing the union of sets
    update(*others: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.events.Selection.update "Link to this definition")
    :   Update this set with the union of this set and others

* napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence
# napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence[#](#napari-utils-events-typedmutablesequence "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence(*data: [Iterable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Iterable "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] = ()*, *\**, *basetype: [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T] | [Sequence](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Sequence "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_T]] = ()*, *lookup: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")[[type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")[\_L], [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[\_T], \_T | \_L]] | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_typed.html#TypedMutableSequence)[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`MutableSequence`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.MutableSequence "(in Python v3.14)")[`_T`]
    List mixin that enforces item type, and enables custom indexing.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*iterable**,* *optional*) – Elements to initialize the list with.
        * **basetype** ([*type*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *sequence* *of* *types**,* *optional*) – Type of the elements in the list. If a basetype (or multiple) is
          provided, then a TypeError will be raised when attempting to add an
          item to this sequence if it is not an instance of one of the types in
          `basetype`.
        * **lookup** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *Type**[**L**]* *: function**(*[*object*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")*)* *-> L*) – Mapping between a type, and a function that converts items in the list
          to that type. This is used for custom indexing. For example, if a
          `lookup` of {str: lambda x: x.name} is provided, then you can index
          into the list using `list['frank']` and it will search for an object
          whos attribute `.name` equals `'frank'`.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`append`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.append "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.append")(value) | S.append(value) -- append value to the end of the sequence |
    | [`clear`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.clear "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.clear")() |  |
    | [`copy`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.copy "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.copy")() | Return a shallow copy of the list. |
    | [`count`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.count "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.count")(value) |  |
    | [`extend`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.extend "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.extend")(values) | S.extend(iterable) -- extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable |
    | [`index`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.index "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.index")(value[, start, stop]) | Return first index of value. |
    | [`insert`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.insert "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.insert")(index, value) | S.insert(index, value) -- insert value before index |
    | [`pop`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.pop "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.pop")([index]) | Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range. |
    | [`remove`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.remove "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.remove")(value) | S.remove(value) -- remove first occurrence of value. |
    | [`reverse`](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.reverse "napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.reverse")() | S.reverse() -- reverse *IN PLACE* |
    Details
    append(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.append "Link to this definition")
    :   S.append(value) – append value to the end of the sequence
    clear() → None -- remove all items from S[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.clear "Link to this definition")
    copy() → [TypedMutableSequence](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence "napari.utils.events.containers._typed.TypedMutableSequence")[\_T][[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_typed.html#TypedMutableSequence.copy)[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.copy "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a shallow copy of the list.
    count(*value*) → integer -- return number of occurrences of value[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.count "Link to this definition")
    extend(*values*)[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.extend "Link to this definition")
    :   S.extend(iterable) – extend sequence by appending elements from the iterable
    index(*value: \_L*, *start: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") = 0*, *stop: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_typed.html#TypedMutableSequence.index)[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.index "Link to this definition")
    :   Return first index of value.
        Parameters:
        :   * **value** (*Any*) – A value to lookup. If type(value) is in the lookups functions
              provided for this class, then values in the list will be searched
              using the corresponding lookup converter function.
            * **start** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The starting index to search, by default 0
            * **stop** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The ending index to search, by default None
        Returns:
        :   The index of the value
        Return type:
        :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
        Raises:
        :   [**ValueError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)") – If the value is not present
    insert(*index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *value: \_T*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/events/containers/_typed.html#TypedMutableSequence.insert)[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.insert "Link to this definition")
    :   S.insert(index, value) – insert value before index
    pop([*index*]) → item -- remove and return item at index (default last).[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.pop "Link to this definition")
    :   Raise IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range.
    remove(*value*)[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.remove "Link to this definition")
    :   S.remove(value) – remove first occurrence of value.
        Raise ValueError if the value is not present.
    reverse()[#](#napari.utils.events.TypedMutableSequence.reverse "Link to this definition")
    :   S.reverse() – reverse *IN PLACE*

# napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification[#](#napari-utils-notifications-errornotification "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification(*exception: [BaseException](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#BaseException "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/notifications.html#ErrorNotification)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Notification`](napari.utils.notifications.Notification.html#napari.utils.notifications.Notification "napari.utils.notifications.Notification")
    Notification at an Error severity level.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `as_html`() |  |
    | `as_text`() |  |
    | `from_exception`(exc, \*\*kwargs) |  |
    | `from_warning`(warning, \*\*kwargs) |  |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`blocked`](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.blocked "napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.blocked") | This boolean property indicates whether the event will be delivered to event callbacks. |
    | [`handled`](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.handled "napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.handled") | This boolean property indicates whether the event has already been acted on by an event handler. |
    | `message` |  |
    | `native` |  |
    | [`source`](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.source "napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.source") | The object that the event applies to (i.e. the source of the event). |
    | [`sources`](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.sources "napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.sources") | List of objects that the event applies to (i.e. are or have been a source of the event). |
    | `type` |  |
    Details
    property blocked: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.blocked "Link to this definition")
    :   This boolean property indicates whether the event will be delivered
        to event callbacks. If it is set to True, then no further callbacks
        will receive the event. When possible, it is recommended to use
        Event.handled rather than Event.blocked.
    property handled: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.handled "Link to this definition")
    :   This boolean property indicates whether the event has already been
        acted on by an event handler. Since many handlers may have access to
        the same events, it is recommended that each check whether the event
        has already been handled as well as set handled=True if it decides to
        act on the event.
    property source: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.source "Link to this definition")
    :   The object that the event applies to (i.e. the source of the event).
    property sources: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.notifications.ErrorNotification.sources "Link to this definition")
    :   List of objects that the event applies to (i.e. are or have
        been a source of the event). Can contain multiple objects in case
        the event traverses a hierarchy of objects.

# napari.utils.notifications.Notification[#](#napari-utils-notifications-notification "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.notifications.Notification(*message: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *severity: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [NotificationSeverity](napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.html#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity "napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity") = NotificationSeverity.WARNING*, *actions: [Sequence](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Sequence "(in Python v3.14)")[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)"), [Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")[[], [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")]]] = ()*, *\*\*kwargs*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/notifications.html#Notification)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Event`](napari.utils.events.Event.html#napari.utils.events.Event "napari.utils.events.event.Event")
    A Notifcation event. Usually created by [`NotificationManager`](napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager.html#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager "napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager").
    Parameters:
    :   * **message** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – The main message/payload of the notification.
        * **severity** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*NotificationSeverity*](napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.html#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity "napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity")*,* *optional*) – The severity of the notification, by default
          NotificationSeverity.WARNING.
        * **actions** (*sequence* *of* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Where each tuple is a (str, callable) 2-tuple where the first item
          is a name for the action (which may, for example, be put on a button),
          and the callable is a callback to perform when the action is triggered.
          (for example, one might show a traceback dialog). by default ()
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `from_exception`(exc, \*\*kwargs) |  |
    | `from_warning`(warning, \*\*kwargs) |  |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`blocked`](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification.blocked "napari.utils.notifications.Notification.blocked") | This boolean property indicates whether the event will be delivered to event callbacks. |
    | [`handled`](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification.handled "napari.utils.notifications.Notification.handled") | This boolean property indicates whether the event has already been acted on by an event handler. |
    | `message` |  |
    | `native` |  |
    | [`source`](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification.source "napari.utils.notifications.Notification.source") | The object that the event applies to (i.e. the source of the event). |
    | [`sources`](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification.sources "napari.utils.notifications.Notification.sources") | List of objects that the event applies to (i.e. are or have been a source of the event). |
    | `type` |  |
    Details
    property blocked: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification.blocked "Link to this definition")
    :   This boolean property indicates whether the event will be delivered
        to event callbacks. If it is set to True, then no further callbacks
        will receive the event. When possible, it is recommended to use
        Event.handled rather than Event.blocked.
    property handled: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification.handled "Link to this definition")
    :   This boolean property indicates whether the event has already been
        acted on by an event handler. Since many handlers may have access to
        the same events, it is recommended that each check whether the event
        has already been handled as well as set handled=True if it decides to
        act on the event.
    property source: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification.source "Link to this definition")
    :   The object that the event applies to (i.e. the source of the event).
    property sources: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.notifications.Notification.sources "Link to this definition")
    :   List of objects that the event applies to (i.e. are or have
        been a source of the event). Can contain multiple objects in case
        the event traverses a hierarchy of objects.

<!-- Source: https://napari.org/stable/api/napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager.html -->
* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* [napari.utils.notifications](napari.utils.notifications.html)
* napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager
# napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager[#](#napari-utils-notifications-notificationmanager "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/notifications.html#NotificationManager)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`object`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")
    A notification manager, to route all notifications through.
    Only one instance is in general available through napari; as we need
    notification to all flow to a single location that is registered with the
    sys.except\_hook and showwarning hook.
    This can and should be used a context manager; the context manager will
    properly re-entered, and install/remove hooks and keep them in a stack to
    restore them.
    While it might seem unnecessary to make it re-entrant; or to make the
    re-entrancy no-op; one need to consider that this could be used inside
    another context manager that modify except\_hook and showwarning.
    Currently the original except and show warnings hooks are not called; but
    this could be changed in the future; this poses some questions with the
    re-entrency of the hooks themselves.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `dispatch`(notification) |  |
    | [`install_hooks`](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager.install_hooks "napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager.install_hooks")() | Install a sys.excepthook, a showwarning hook and a threading.excepthook to display any message in the UI, storing the previous hooks to be restored if necessary. |
    | `receive_error`(exctype, value[, traceback, ...]) |  |
    | `receive_info`(message) |  |
    | `receive_thread_error`(args) |  |
    | `receive_warning`(message, category, filename, ...) |  |
    | [`restore_hooks`](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager.restore_hooks "napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager.restore_hooks")() | Remove hooks installed by install\_hooks and restore previous hooks. |
    Attributes
    Details
    install\_hooks()[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/notifications.html#NotificationManager.install_hooks)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager.install_hooks "Link to this definition")
    :   Install a sys.excepthook, a showwarning hook and a
        threading.excepthook to display any message in the UI,
        storing the previous hooks to be restored if necessary.
    restore\_hooks()[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/notifications.html#NotificationManager.restore_hooks)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationManager.restore_hooks "Link to this definition")
    :   Remove hooks installed by install\_hooks and restore previous hooks.

    | [`rindex`](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rindex "napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rindex")(sub[, start[, end]]) | Return the highest index in S where substring sub is found, such that sub is contained within S[start:end]. |
    | [`rjust`](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rjust "napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rjust")(width[, fillchar]) | Return a right-justified string of length width. |
    | [`rstrip`](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rstrip "napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rstrip")([chars]) | Return a copy of the string with trailing whitespace removed. |

    Details
    capitalize()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.capitalize "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a capitalized version of the string.
        More specifically, make the first character have upper case and the rest lower
        case.
    casefold()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.casefold "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a version of the string suitable for caseless comparisons.
    center(*width*, *fillchar=' '*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.center "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a centered string of length width.
        Padding is done using the specified fill character (default is a space).
    count(*sub*[, *start*[, *end*]]) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.count "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the number of non-overlapping occurrences of substring sub in
        string S[start:end]. Optional arguments start and end are
        interpreted as in slice notation.
    encode(*encoding='utf-8'*, *errors='strict'*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.encode "Link to this definition")
    :   Encode the string using the codec registered for encoding.
        encoding
        :   The encoding in which to encode the string.
        errors
        :   The error handling scheme to use for encoding errors.
            The default is ‘strict’ meaning that encoding errors raise a
            UnicodeEncodeError. Other possible values are ‘ignore’, ‘replace’ and
            ‘xmlcharrefreplace’ as well as any other name registered with
            codecs.register\_error that can handle UnicodeEncodeErrors.
    endswith(*suffix*[, *start*[, *end*]]) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.endswith "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if S ends with the specified suffix, False otherwise.
        With optional start, test S beginning at that position.
        With optional end, stop comparing S at that position.
        suffix can also be a tuple of strings to try.
    expandtabs(*tabsize=8*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.expandtabs "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a copy where all tab characters are expanded using spaces.
        If tabsize is not given, a tab size of 8 characters is assumed.
    find(*sub*[, *start*[, *end*]]) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.find "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the lowest index in S where substring sub is found,
        such that sub is contained within S[start:end]. Optional
        arguments start and end are interpreted as in slice notation.
        Return -1 on failure.
    format(*\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.format "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a formatted version of S, using substitutions from args and kwargs.
        The substitutions are identified by braces (‘{’ and ‘}’).
    format\_map(*mapping*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.format_map "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a formatted version of S, using substitutions from mapping.
        The substitutions are identified by braces (‘{’ and ‘}’).
    index(*sub*[, *start*[, *end*]]) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.index "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the lowest index in S where substring sub is found,
        such that sub is contained within S[start:end]. Optional
        arguments start and end are interpreted as in slice notation.
        Raises ValueError when the substring is not found.
    isalnum()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isalnum "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is an alpha-numeric string, False otherwise.
        A string is alpha-numeric if all characters in the string are alpha-numeric and
        there is at least one character in the string.
    isalpha()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isalpha "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is an alphabetic string, False otherwise.
        A string is alphabetic if all characters in the string are alphabetic and there
        is at least one character in the string.
    isascii()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isascii "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if all characters in the string are ASCII, False otherwise.
        ASCII characters have code points in the range U+0000-U+007F.
        Empty string is ASCII too.
    isdecimal()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isdecimal "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is a decimal string, False otherwise.
        A string is a decimal string if all characters in the string are decimal and
        there is at least one character in the string.
    isdigit()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isdigit "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is a digit string, False otherwise.
        A string is a digit string if all characters in the string are digits and there
        is at least one character in the string.
    isidentifier()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isidentifier "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is a valid Python identifier, False otherwise.
        Call keyword.iskeyword(s) to test whether string s is a reserved identifier,
        such as “def” or “class”.
    islower()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.islower "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is a lowercase string, False otherwise.
        A string is lowercase if all cased characters in the string are lowercase and
        there is at least one cased character in the string.
    isnumeric()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isnumeric "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is a numeric string, False otherwise.
        A string is numeric if all characters in the string are numeric and there is at
        least one character in the string.
    isprintable()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isprintable "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is printable, False otherwise.
        A string is printable if all of its characters are considered printable in
        repr() or if it is empty.
    isspace()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isspace "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is a whitespace string, False otherwise.
        A string is whitespace if all characters in the string are whitespace and there
        is at least one character in the string.
    istitle()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.istitle "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is a title-cased string, False otherwise.
        In a title-cased string, upper- and title-case characters may only
        follow uncased characters and lowercase characters only cased ones.
    isupper()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.isupper "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if the string is an uppercase string, False otherwise.
        A string is uppercase if all cased characters in the string are uppercase and
        there is at least one cased character in the string.
    join(*iterable*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.join "Link to this definition")
    :   Concatenate any number of strings.
        The string whose method is called is inserted in between each given string.
        The result is returned as a new string.
        Example: ‘.’.join([‘ab’, ‘pq’, ‘rs’]) -> ‘ab.pq.rs’
    ljust(*width*, *fillchar=' '*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.ljust "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a left-justified string of length width.
        Padding is done using the specified fill character (default is a space).
    lower()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.lower "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a copy of the string converted to lowercase.
    lstrip(*chars=None*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.lstrip "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a copy of the string with leading whitespace removed.
        If chars is given and not None, remove characters in chars instead.
    static maketrans()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.maketrans "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a translation table usable for str.translate().
        If there is only one argument, it must be a dictionary mapping Unicode
        ordinals (integers) or characters to Unicode ordinals, strings or None.
        Character keys will be then converted to ordinals.
        If there are two arguments, they must be strings of equal length, and
        in the resulting dictionary, each character in x will be mapped to the
        character at the same position in y. If there is a third argument, it
        must be a string, whose characters will be mapped to None in the result.
    partition(*sep*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.partition "Link to this definition")
    :   Partition the string into three parts using the given separator.
        This will search for the separator in the string. If the separator is found,
        returns a 3-tuple containing the part before the separator, the separator
        itself, and the part after it.
        If the separator is not found, returns a 3-tuple containing the original string
        and two empty strings.
    removeprefix(*prefix*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.removeprefix "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a str with the given prefix string removed if present.
        If the string starts with the prefix string, return string[len(prefix):].
        Otherwise, return a copy of the original string.
    removesuffix(*suffix*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.removesuffix "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a str with the given suffix string removed if present.
        If the string ends with the suffix string and that suffix is not empty,
        return string[:-len(suffix)]. Otherwise, return a copy of the original
        string.
    replace(*old*, *new*, *count=-1*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.replace "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a copy with all occurrences of substring old replaced by new.
        > count
        > :   Maximum number of occurrences to replace.
        >     -1 (the default value) means replace all occurrences.
        If the optional argument count is given, only the first count occurrences are
        replaced.
    rfind(*sub*[, *start*[, *end*]]) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rfind "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the highest index in S where substring sub is found,
        such that sub is contained within S[start:end]. Optional
        arguments start and end are interpreted as in slice notation.
        Return -1 on failure.
    rindex(*sub*[, *start*[, *end*]]) → [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rindex "Link to this definition")
    :   Return the highest index in S where substring sub is found,
        such that sub is contained within S[start:end]. Optional
        arguments start and end are interpreted as in slice notation.
        Raises ValueError when the substring is not found.
    rjust(*width*, *fillchar=' '*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rjust "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a right-justified string of length width.
        Padding is done using the specified fill character (default is a space).
    rpartition(*sep*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rpartition "Link to this definition")
    :   Partition the string into three parts using the given separator.
        This will search for the separator in the string, starting at the end. If
        the separator is found, returns a 3-tuple containing the part before the
        separator, the separator itself, and the part after it.
        If the separator is not found, returns a 3-tuple containing two empty strings
        and the original string.
    rsplit(*sep=None*, *maxsplit=-1*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rsplit "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a list of the substrings in the string, using sep as the separator string.
        > sep
        > :   The separator used to split the string.
        >
        >     When set to None (the default value), will split on any whitespace
        >     character (including n r t f and spaces) and will discard
        >     empty strings from the result.
        >
        > maxsplit
        > :   Maximum number of splits.
        >     -1 (the default value) means no limit.
        Splitting starts at the end of the string and works to the front.
    rstrip(*chars=None*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.rstrip "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a copy of the string with trailing whitespace removed.
        If chars is given and not None, remove characters in chars instead.
    split(*sep=None*, *maxsplit=-1*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.split "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a list of the substrings in the string, using sep as the separator string.
        > sep
        > :   The separator used to split the string.
        >
        >     When set to None (the default value), will split on any whitespace
        >     character (including n r t f and spaces) and will discard
        >     empty strings from the result.
        >
        > maxsplit
        > :   Maximum number of splits.
        >     -1 (the default value) means no limit.
        Splitting starts at the front of the string and works to the end.
        Note, str.split() is mainly useful for data that has been intentionally
        delimited. With natural text that includes punctuation, consider using
        the regular expression module.
    splitlines(*keepends=False*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.splitlines "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a list of the lines in the string, breaking at line boundaries.
        Line breaks are not included in the resulting list unless keepends is given and
        true.
    startswith(*prefix*[, *start*[, *end*]]) → [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.startswith "Link to this definition")
    :   Return True if S starts with the specified prefix, False otherwise.
        With optional start, test S beginning at that position.
        With optional end, stop comparing S at that position.
        prefix can also be a tuple of strings to try.
    strip(*chars=None*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.strip "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a copy of the string with leading and trailing whitespace removed.
        If chars is given and not None, remove characters in chars instead.
    swapcase()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.swapcase "Link to this definition")
    :   Convert uppercase characters to lowercase and lowercase characters to uppercase.
    title()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.title "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a version of the string where each word is titlecased.
        More specifically, words start with uppercased characters and all remaining
        cased characters have lower case.
    translate(*table*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.translate "Link to this definition")
    :   Replace each character in the string using the given translation table.
        > table
        > :   Translation table, which must be a mapping of Unicode ordinals to
        >     Unicode ordinals, strings, or None.
        The table must implement lookup/indexing via \_\_getitem\_\_, for instance a
        dictionary or list. If this operation raises LookupError, the character is
        left untouched. Characters mapped to None are deleted.
    upper()[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.upper "Link to this definition")
    :   Return a copy of the string converted to uppercase.
    zfill(*width*, */*)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.NotificationSeverity.zfill "Link to this definition")
    :   Pad a numeric string with zeros on the left, to fill a field of the given width.
        The string is never truncated.

# napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification[#](#napari-utils-notifications-warningnotification "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification(*warning: [Warning](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#Warning "(in Python v3.14)")*, *filename=None*, *lineno=None*, *\*args*, *\*\*kwargs*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/notifications.html#WarningNotification)[#](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`Notification`](napari.utils.notifications.Notification.html#napari.utils.notifications.Notification "napari.utils.notifications.Notification")
    Notification at a Warning severity level.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | `from_exception`(exc, \*\*kwargs) |  |
    | `from_warning`(warning, \*\*kwargs) |  |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`blocked`](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.blocked "napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.blocked") | This boolean property indicates whether the event will be delivered to event callbacks. |
    | [`handled`](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.handled "napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.handled") | This boolean property indicates whether the event has already been acted on by an event handler. |
    | `message` |  |
    | `native` |  |
    | [`source`](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.source "napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.source") | The object that the event applies to (i.e. the source of the event). |
    | [`sources`](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.sources "napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.sources") | List of objects that the event applies to (i.e. are or have been a source of the event). |
    | `type` |  |
    Details
    property blocked: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.blocked "Link to this definition")
    :   This boolean property indicates whether the event will be delivered
        to event callbacks. If it is set to True, then no further callbacks
        will receive the event. When possible, it is recommended to use
        Event.handled rather than Event.blocked.
    property handled: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.handled "Link to this definition")
    :   This boolean property indicates whether the event has already been
        acted on by an event handler. Since many handlers may have access to
        the same events, it is recommended that each check whether the event
        has already been handled as well as set handled=True if it decides to
        act on the event.
    property source: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.source "Link to this definition")
    :   The object that the event applies to (i.e. the source of the event).
    property sources: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.14)")][#](#napari.utils.notifications.WarningNotification.sources "Link to this definition")
    :   List of objects that the event applies to (i.e. are or have
        been a source of the event). Can contain multiple objects in case
        the event traverses a hierarchy of objects.

* [API](index.html)
* [Modules](modules.html)
* napari.utils.perf
# napari.utils.perf[#](#module-napari.utils.perf "Link to this heading")
Performance Monitoring.
The perfmon module lets you instrument your code and visualize its run-time
behavior and timings in Chrome’s Tracing GUI.
To enable perfmon define the env var NAPARI\_PERFMON as follows:
NAPARI\_PERFMON=1
:   Activates perfmon, trace using Debug -> Performance Trace menu.
NAPARI\_PERFMON=/path/to/config.json
:   Configure perfmon using the config.json configuration. See the
    PerfmonConfig docs for the spec of the config file.
## Chrome Tracing[#](#chrome-tracing "Link to this heading")
Chrome has a nice built-in performance tool called chrome://tracing. Chrome
can record traces of web applications. But the format is well-documented and
anyone can create the files and use the nice GUI. And other programs accept
the format including:
1) https://www.speedscope.app/ which does flamegraphs (Chrome doesn’t).
2) Qt Creator’s performance tools.
## Monkey Patching[#](#monkey-patching "Link to this heading")
The best way to add perf\_timers is using the perfmon config file. You can
list which methods or functions you want to time, and a perf\_timer will be
monkey-patched into each callable on startup. The monkey patching
is done only if perfmon is enabled.
## Trace On Start[#](#trace-on-start "Link to this heading")
Add a line to the config file like:
> “trace\_file\_on\_start”: “/Path/to/my/trace.json”
Perfmon will start tracing on startup. You must quit napari with the Quit
command for napari to write trace file. See PerfmonConfig docs.
## Manual Timing[#](#manual-timing "Link to this heading")
You can also manually add “perf\_timer” context objects and
“add\_counter\_event()” and “add\_instant\_event()” functions to your code. All
three of these should be removed before merging the PR into main. While
they have almost zero overhead when perfmon is disabled, it’s still better
not to leave them in the code. Think of them as similar to debug prints.
Classes
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`PerfEvent`](napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.html#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent "napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent") | A performance related event: timer, counter, etc. |
Functions
napari.utils.perf.add\_counter\_event(*name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\*\*kwargs: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/perf/_timers.html#add_counter_event)[#](#napari.utils.perf.add_counter_event "Link to this definition")
:   Add one counter event.
    Parameters:
    :   * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – The name of this event like “draw”.
        * **\*\*kwargs** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – The individual counters for this event.
    Notes
    For example add\_counter\_event(“draw”, triangles=5, squares=10).
napari.utils.perf.add\_instant\_event(*name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *\**, *category: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *process\_id: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *thread\_id: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\*\*kwargs: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/perf/_timers.html#add_instant_event)[#](#napari.utils.perf.add_instant_event "Link to this definition")
:   Add one instant event.
    Parameters:
    :   * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Add this event.
        * **category** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *|* *None*) – Comma separated categories such as “render,update”.
        * **process\_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *|* *None*) – The process id that produced the event.
        * **thread\_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") *|* *None*) – The thread id that produced the event.
        * **\*\*kwargs** – Arguments to display in the Args section of the Chrome Tracing GUI.
napari.utils.perf.block\_timer(*name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *category: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *print\_time: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *\**, *process\_id: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *thread\_id: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *phase: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = 'X'*, *\*\*kwargs: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*) → Generator[[PerfEvent](napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.html#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent "napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent"), [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/perf/_timers.html#block_timer)[#](#napari.utils.perf.block_timer "Link to this definition")
:   Time a block of code.
    block\_timer can be used when perfmon is disabled. Use perf\_timer instead
    if you want your timer to do nothing when perfmon is disabled.
    Notes
    Most of the time you should use the perfmon config file to monkey-patch
    perf\_timer’s into methods and functions. Then you do not need to use
    block\_timer or perf\_timer context objects explicitly at all.
    Parameters:
    :   * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – The name of this timer.
        * **category** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Comma separated categories such as “render,update”.
        * **print\_time** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Print the duration of the timer when it finishes.
        * **\*\*kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Additional keyword arguments for the “args” field of the event.
    Examples
    ```
    with block_timer("draw") as event:
        draw_stuff()
    print(f"The timer took {event.duration_ms} milliseconds.")
    ```
napari.utils.perf.perf\_timer(*name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *category: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *print\_time: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*, *\**, *process\_id: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *thread\_id: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *phase: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = 'X'*, *\*\*kwargs: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*) → Generator[[None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)"), [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/perf/_timers.html#perf_timer)[#](#napari.utils.perf.perf_timer "Link to this definition")
:   Do nothing when perfmon is disabled.
