# napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent[#](#napari-utils-perf-perfevent "Link to this heading")
class napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent(*name: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *start\_ns: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *end\_ns: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *category: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *process\_id: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *thread\_id: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *phase: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = 'X'*, *\*\*kwargs: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/perf/_event.html#PerfEvent)[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`object`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#object "(in Python v3.14)")
    A performance related event: timer, counter, etc.
    Parameters:
    :   * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – The name of this event like “draw”.
        * **start\_ns** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Start time in nanoseconds.
        * **end\_ns** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – End time in nanoseconds.
        * **category** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Comma separated categories such has “render,update”.
        * **process\_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – The process id that produced the event.
        * **thread\_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – The thread id that produced the event.
        * **phase** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – The Chrome Tracing “phase” such as “X”, “I”, “C”.
        * **\*\*kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Additional keyword arguments for the “args” field of the event.
    name[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.name "Link to this definition")
    :   The name of this event like “draw”.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    span[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.span "Link to this definition")
    :   The time span when the event happened.
        Type:
        :   Span
    category[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.category "Link to this definition")
    :   Comma separated categories such as “render,update”.
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    origin[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.origin "Link to this definition")
    :   The process and thread that produced the event.
        Type:
        :   Origin
    args[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.args "Link to this definition")
    :   Arbitrary keyword arguments for this event.
        Type:
        :   [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    phase[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.phase "Link to this definition")
    :   The Chrome Tracing phase (event type):
        :   “X” - Complete Events
            “I” - Instant Events
            “C” - Counter Events
        Type:
        :   [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    Notes
    The time stamps are from perf\_counter\_ns() and do not indicate time of
    day. The origin is arbitrary, but subtracting two counters results in a
    valid span of wall clock time. If start is the same as the end the
    event was instant.
    Google the phrase “Trace Event Format” for the full Chrome Tracing spec.
    Methods
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`update_end_ns`](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.update_end_ns "napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.update_end_ns")(end\_ns) | Update our end\_ns with this new end\_ns. |
    Attributes
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`duration_ms`](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_ms "napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_ms") | Duration in milliseconds. |
    | [`duration_ns`](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_ns "napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_ns") | Duration in nanoseconds. |
    | [`duration_us`](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_us "napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_us") | Duration in microseconds. |
    | [`start_ms`](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.start_ms "napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.start_ms") | Start time in milliseconds. |
    | [`start_us`](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.start_us "napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.start_us") | Start time in microseconds. |
    Details
    property duration\_ms: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_ms "Link to this definition")
    :   Duration in milliseconds.
    property duration\_ns: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_ns "Link to this definition")
    :   Duration in nanoseconds.
    property duration\_us: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.duration_us "Link to this definition")
    :   Duration in microseconds.
    property start\_ms: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.start_ms "Link to this definition")
    :   Start time in milliseconds.
    property start\_us: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.start_us "Link to this definition")
    :   Start time in microseconds.
    update\_end\_ns(*end\_ns: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*) → [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/perf/_event.html#PerfEvent.update_end_ns)[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.update_end_ns "Link to this definition")
    :   Update our end\_ns with this new end\_ns.
        end\_ns[#](#napari.utils.perf.PerfEvent.end_ns "Link to this definition")
        :   The new ending time in nanoseconds.
            Type:
            :   [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")

# napari[#](#module-napari "Link to this heading")
Classes
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [`Viewer`](napari.Viewer.html#napari.Viewer "napari.Viewer") | Napari ndarray viewer. |
Functions
napari.current\_viewer() → [Viewer](napari.Viewer.html#napari.Viewer "napari.viewer.Viewer") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/viewer.html#current_viewer)[#](#napari.current_viewer "Link to this definition")
:   Return the currently active napari viewer.
napari.imshow(*data*, *\**, *channel\_axis: [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *attenuation=0.05*, *blending=None*, *cache=True*, *colormap=None*, *contrast\_limits=None*, *custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d=None*, *depiction='volume'*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *gamma=1.0*, *interpolation2d='nearest'*, *interpolation3d='linear'*, *iso\_threshold=None*, *metadata=None*, *multiscale=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *plane=None*, *projection\_mode='mean'*, *rendering='mip'*, *rgb=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*, *viewer=None*, *title='napari'*, *ndisplay=2*, *order=()*, *show=True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Viewer](napari.Viewer.html#napari.Viewer "napari.viewer.Viewer"), [Image](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.image.image.Image")][[source]](../_modules/napari/view_layers.html#imshow)[#](#napari.imshow "Link to this definition")
napari.imshow(*data*, *\**, *channel\_axis: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *attenuation=0.05*, *blending=None*, *cache=True*, *colormap=None*, *contrast\_limits=None*, *custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d=None*, *depiction='volume'*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *gamma=1.0*, *interpolation2d='nearest'*, *interpolation3d='linear'*, *iso\_threshold=None*, *metadata=None*, *multiscale=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *plane=None*, *projection\_mode='mean'*, *rendering='mip'*, *rgb=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*, *viewer=None*, *title='napari'*, *ndisplay=2*, *order=()*, *show=True*) → [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Viewer](napari.Viewer.html#napari.Viewer "napari.viewer.Viewer"), [tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")[[Image](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.image.image.Image"), ...]]
:   Load data into an Image layer and return the Viewer and Layer.
    Parameters:
    :   * **data** (*array* *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *array*) – Image data. Can be N >= 2 dimensional. If the last dimension has length
          3 or 4 can be interpreted as RGB or RGBA if rgb is True. If a
          list and arrays are decreasing in shape then the data is treated as
          a multiscale image. Please note multiscale rendering is only
          supported in 2D. In 3D, only the lowest resolution scale is
          displayed.
        * **channel\_axis** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Axis to expand image along. If provided, each channel in the data
          will be added as an individual image layer. In channel\_axis mode,
          other parameters MAY be provided as lists. The Nth value of the list
          will be applied to the Nth channel in the data. If a single value
          is provided, it will be broadcast to all Layers.
          All parameters except data, rgb, and multiscale can be provided as
          list of values. If a list is provided, it must be the same length as
          the axis that is being expanded as channels.
        * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
          The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
          the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a
          napari Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on
          top of the provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
        * **attenuation** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Attenuation rate for attenuated maximum intensity projection.
        * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
          alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
          {‘translucent’, ‘translucent\_no\_depth’, ‘additive’, ‘minimum’, ‘opaque’}.
        * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon
          retrieval. Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
        * **colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")*,* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *these types*) – Colormaps to use for luminance images. If a string, it can be the name
          of a supported colormap from vispy or matplotlib or the name of
          a vispy color or a hexadecimal RGB color representation.
          If a tuple, the first value must be a string to assign as a name to a
          colormap and the second item must be a Colormap. If a dict, the key must
          be a string to assign as a name to a colormap and the value must be a
          Colormap.
        * **contrast\_limits** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *(**2**,**)*) – Intensity value limits to be used for determining the minimum and maximum colormap bounds for
          luminance images. If not passed, they will be calculated as the min and max intensity value of
          the image.
        * **custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d** (*np.ndarray*) – Convolution kernel used with the ‘custom’ interpolation mode in 2D rendering.
        * **depiction** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – 3D Depiction mode. Must be one of {‘volume’, ‘plane’}.
          The default value is ‘volume’.
        * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
          Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
          Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
        * **gamma** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Gamma correction for determining colormap linearity; defaults to 1.
        * **interpolation2d** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Interpolation mode used by vispy for rendering 2d data.
          Must be one of our supported modes.
          (for list of supported modes see Interpolation enum)
          ‘custom’ is a special mode for 2D interpolation in which a regular grid
          of samples is taken from the texture around a position using ‘linear’
          interpolation before being multiplied with a custom interpolation kernel
          (provided with ‘custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d’).
        * **interpolation3d** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Same as ‘interpolation2d’ but for 3D rendering.
        * **iso\_threshold** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Threshold for isosurface.
        * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
        * **multiscale** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the data is a multiscale image or not. Multiscale data is
          represented by a list of array-like image data. If not specified by
          the user and if the data is a list of arrays that decrease in shape,
          then it will be taken to be multiscale. The first image in the list
          should be the largest. Please note multiscale rendering is only
          supported in 2D. In 3D, only the lowest resolution scale is
          displayed.
        * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
        * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
        * **plane** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *SlicingPlane*) – Properties defining plane rendering in 3D. Properties are defined in
          data coordinates. Valid dictionary keys are
          {‘position’, ‘normal’, ‘thickness’, and ‘enabled’}.
        * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions, but inside the thick Dims slice will
          be projected onto the viewed dimensions. Must fit to cls.\_projectionclass
        * **rendering** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Rendering mode used by vispy. Must be one of our supported
          modes. If a list then must be same length as the axis that is being
          expanded as channels.
        * **rgb** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether the image is RGB or RGBA if rgb. If not
          specified by user, but the last dimension of the data has length 3 or 4,
          it will be set as True. If False, the image is interpreted as a
          luminance image.
        * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *n-D array* *or* *list.*) – If a float, convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
          angle. If 3-tuple, convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
          pitch, roll convention. Otherwise, assume an nD rotation. Angles are
          assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
          ‘np.degrees’ if needed.
        * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
        * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *list.*) – A vector of shear values for an upper triangular n-D shear matrix.
        * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
        * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
        * **viewer** (*Viewer object**,* *optional**,* *by default None.*)
        * **title** (*string**,* *optional*) – The title of the viewer window. By default ‘napari’.
        * **ndisplay** (*{2**,* *3}**,* *optional*) – Number of displayed dimensions. By default 2.
        * **order** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Order in which dimensions are displayed where the last two or last
          three dimensions correspond to row x column or plane x row x column if
          ndisplay is 2 or 3. By default None
        * **axis\_labels** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names. By default they are labeled with sequential numbers
        * **show** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether to show the viewer after instantiation. By default True.
    Returns:
    :   * **viewer** (*napari.Viewer*) – The created or passed viewer.
        * **layer(s)** (*napari.layers.Image or tuple of napari.layers.Image*) – The added layer(s). (May be more than one if the `channel_axis` keyword
          argument is given.
napari.run(*\**, *force=False*, *gui\_exceptions=False*, *max\_loop\_level=1*, *\_func\_name='run'*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/_qt/qt_event_loop.html#run)[#](#napari.run "Link to this definition")
:   Start the Qt Event Loop
    Parameters:
    :   * **force** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Force the application event\_loop to start, even if there are no top
          level widgets to show.
        * **gui\_exceptions** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether to show uncaught exceptions in the GUI. By default they will be
          shown in the console that launched the event loop.
        * **max\_loop\_level** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – The maximum allowable “loop level” for the execution thread. Every
          time QApplication.exec\_() is called, Qt enters the event loop,
          increments app.thread().loopLevel(), and waits until exit() is called.
          This function will prevent calling exec\_() if the application already
          has at least `max_loop_level` event loops running. By default, 1.
        * **\_func\_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – name of calling function, by default ‘run’. This is here to
          provide functions a way to inject their name into the
          warning message.
    Raises:
    :   [**RuntimeError**](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#RuntimeError "(in Python v3.14)") – (To avoid confusion) if no widgets would be shown upon starting the
        event loop.
napari.save\_layers(*path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*, *layers: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.Layer")]*, *\**, *plugin: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*, *\_writer: WriterContribution | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.14)") = None*) → [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")][[source]](../_modules/napari/plugins/io.html#save_layers)[#](#napari.save_layers "Link to this definition")
:   Write list of layers or individual layer to a path using writer plugins.
    If `plugin` is provided, will attempt to use a writer declared by that
    plugin, assuming it is compatible with the given layer(s) and `path`.
    Otherwise, will use first compatible writer.
    Parameters:
    :   * **path** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – A filepath, directory, or URL to write.
        * **layers** (*List**[*[*layers.Layer*](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.Layer")*]*) – Non-empty List of layers to be saved. Warns when the list
          of layers is empty.
        * **plugin** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Name of the plugin to use for saving. If None then the first compatible
          writer will be used.
    Returns:
    :   File paths of any files that were written.
    Return type:
    :   [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") of [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
napari.sys\_info(*as\_html: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*) → [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")[[source]](../_modules/napari/utils/info.html#sys_info)[#](#napari.sys_info "Link to this definition")
:   Gathers relevant module versions for troubleshooting purposes.
    Parameters:
    :   **as\_html** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – if True, info will be returned as HTML, suitable for a QTextEdit widget

# napari.Viewer[#](#napari-viewer "Link to this heading")
class napari.Viewer(*\**, *title='napari'*, *ndisplay=2*, *order=()*, *axis\_labels=()*, *show=True*, *show\_welcome\_screen=True*, *mouse\_move\_callbacks: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")] = <factory>*, *mouse\_wheel\_callbacks: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")] = <factory>*, *mouse\_drag\_callbacks: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")] = <factory>*, *mouse\_double\_click\_callbacks: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Callable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html#collections.abc.Callable "(in Python v3.14)")] = <factory>*, *camera: [Camera](napari.components.Camera.html#napari.components.Camera "napari.components.camera.Camera") = <factory>*, *cursor: Cursor = <factory>*, *dims: [Dims](napari.components.Dims.html#napari.components.Dims "napari.components.dims.Dims") = <factory>*, *grid: GridCanvas = <factory>*, *layers: [LayerList](napari.components.LayerList.html#napari.components.LayerList "napari.components.layerlist.LayerList") = <factory>*, *help: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = ''*, *status: str | dict[str*, *str]='Ready'*, *tooltip: Tooltip = <factory>*, *theme: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") = <factory>*, *mouse\_over\_canvas: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") = False*)[[source]](../_modules/napari/viewer.html#Viewer)[#](#napari.Viewer "Link to this definition")
:   Bases: [`ViewerModel`](napari.components.ViewerModel.html#napari.components.ViewerModel "napari.components.viewer_model.ViewerModel")
    Napari ndarray viewer.
    Parameters:
    :   * **title** (*string**,* *optional*) – The title of the viewer window. By default ‘napari’.
        * **ndisplay** (*{2**,* *3}**,* *optional*) – Number of displayed dimensions. By default 2.
        * **order** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Order in which dimensions are displayed where the last two or last
          three dimensions correspond to row x column or plane x row x column if
          ndisplay is 2 or 3. By default None
        * **axis\_labels** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names. By default they are labeled with sequential numbers
        * **show** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether to show the viewer after instantiation. By default True.

    | [`add_shapes`](#napari.Viewer.add_shapes "napari.Viewer.add_shapes")([data, ndim, affine, ...]) | Add a Shapes layer to the layer list. |

    | [`add_vectors`](#napari.Viewer.add_vectors "napari.Viewer.add_vectors")([data, affine, axis\_labels, ...]) | Add a Vectors layer to the layer list. |

    Details
    add\_image(*data=None*, *\**, *channel\_axis=None*, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *attenuation=0.05*, *blending=None*, *cache=True*, *colormap=None*, *contrast\_limits=None*, *custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d=None*, *depiction='volume'*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *gamma=1.0*, *interpolation2d='nearest'*, *interpolation3d='linear'*, *iso\_threshold=None*, *metadata=None*, *multiscale=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *plane=None*, *projection\_mode='mean'*, *rendering='mip'*, *rgb=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*) → [Image](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.image.image.Image") | [list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")[[Image](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.image.image.Image")][#](#napari.Viewer.add_image "Link to this definition")
    :   Add one or more Image layers to the layer list.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*array* *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *array*) – Image data. Can be N >= 2 dimensional. If the last dimension has length
              3 or 4 can be interpreted as RGB or RGBA if rgb is True. If a
              list and arrays are decreasing in shape then the data is treated as
              a multiscale image. Please note multiscale rendering is only
              supported in 2D. In 3D, only the lowest resolution scale is
              displayed.
            * **channel\_axis** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Axis to expand image along. If provided, each channel in the data
              will be added as an individual image layer. In channel\_axis mode,
              other parameters MAY be provided as lists. The Nth value of the list
              will be applied to the Nth channel in the data. If a single value
              is provided, it will be broadcast to all Layers.
              All parameters except data, rgb, and multiscale can be provided as
              list of values. If a list is provided, it must be the same length as
              the axis that is being expanded as channels.
            * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
              The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
              the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a
              napari Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on
              top of the provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
            * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Dimension names of the layer data.
              If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
            * **attenuation** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Attenuation rate for attenuated maximum intensity projection.
            * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
              alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
              {‘translucent’, ‘translucent\_no\_depth’, ‘additive’, ‘minimum’, ‘opaque’}.
            * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon
              retrieval. Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
            * **colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")*,* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *these types*) – Colormaps to use for luminance images. If a string, it can be the name
              of a supported colormap from vispy or matplotlib or the name of
              a vispy color or a hexadecimal RGB color representation.
              If a tuple, the first value must be a string to assign as a name to a
              colormap and the second item must be a Colormap. If a dict, the key must
              be a string to assign as a name to a colormap and the value must be a
              Colormap.
            * **contrast\_limits** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *(**2**,**)*) – Intensity value limits to be used for determining the minimum and maximum colormap bounds for
              luminance images. If not passed, they will be calculated as the min and max intensity value of
              the image.
            * **custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d** (*np.ndarray*) – Convolution kernel used with the ‘custom’ interpolation mode in 2D rendering.
            * **depiction** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – 3D Depiction mode. Must be one of {‘volume’, ‘plane’}.
              The default value is ‘volume’.
            * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
              Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
              Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
            * **gamma** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Gamma correction for determining colormap linearity; defaults to 1.
            * **interpolation2d** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Interpolation mode used by vispy for rendering 2d data.
              Must be one of our supported modes.
              (for list of supported modes see Interpolation enum)
              ‘custom’ is a special mode for 2D interpolation in which a regular grid
              of samples is taken from the texture around a position using ‘linear’
              interpolation before being multiplied with a custom interpolation kernel
              (provided with ‘custom\_interpolation\_kernel\_2d’).
            * **interpolation3d** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Same as ‘interpolation2d’ but for 3D rendering.
            * **iso\_threshold** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Threshold for isosurface.
            * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
            * **multiscale** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the data is a multiscale image or not. Multiscale data is
              represented by a list of array-like image data. If not specified by
              the user and if the data is a list of arrays that decrease in shape,
              then it will be taken to be multiscale. The first image in the list
              should be the largest. Please note multiscale rendering is only
              supported in 2D. In 3D, only the lowest resolution scale is
              displayed.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
            * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
            * **plane** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *SlicingPlane*) – Properties defining plane rendering in 3D. Properties are defined in
              data coordinates. Valid dictionary keys are
              {‘position’, ‘normal’, ‘thickness’, and ‘enabled’}.
            * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions, but inside the thick Dims slice will
              be projected onto the viewed dimensions. Must fit to cls.\_projectionclass
            * **rendering** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Rendering mode used by vispy. Must be one of our supported
              modes. If a list then must be same length as the axis that is being
              expanded as channels.
            * **rgb** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Whether the image is RGB or RGBA if rgb. If not
              specified by user, but the last dimension of the data has length 3 or 4,
              it will be set as True. If False, the image is interpreted as a
              luminance image.
            * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *n-D array* *or* *list.*) – If a float, convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
              angle. If 3-tuple, convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
              pitch, roll convention. Otherwise, assume an nD rotation. Angles are
              assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
              ‘np.degrees’ if needed.
            * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
            * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *list.*) – A vector of shear values for an upper triangular n-D shear matrix.
            * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
            * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
              If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
            * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
        Returns:
        :   **layer** – The newly-created image layer or list of image layers.
        Return type:
        :   [`napari.layers.Image`](napari.layers.Image.html#napari.layers.Image "napari.layers.Image") or list
    add\_labels(*data*, *\**, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *cache=True*, *colormap=None*, *depiction='volume'*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *features=None*, *iso\_gradient\_mode='fast'*, *metadata=None*, *multiscale=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=0.7*, *plane=None*, *projection\_mode='none'*, *properties=None*, *rendering='iso\_categorical'*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shear=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*) → [Labels](napari.layers.Labels.html#napari.layers.Labels "napari.layers.labels.labels.Labels")[#](#napari.Viewer.add_labels "Link to this definition")
    :   Add a Labels layer to the layer list.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*array* *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *array*) – Labels data as an array or multiscale. Must be integer type or bools.
              In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen automatically based on
              the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution scale is displayed by
              default. The resolution level can be locked via
              `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
              controls UI.
            * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
              The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
              the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
              Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
              provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
            * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
              If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
            * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
              alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
              {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, and ‘additive’}.
            * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
              Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
            * **colormap** ([*CyclicLabelColormap*](napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap.html#napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap "napari.utils.CyclicLabelColormap") *or* [*DirectLabelColormap*](napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap.html#napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap "napari.utils.DirectLabelColormap") *or* *None*) – Colormap to use for the labels. If None, a random colormap will be
              used.
            * **depiction** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – 3D Depiction mode. Must be one of {‘volume’, ‘plane’}.
              The default value is ‘volume’.
            * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
              Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
              Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
            * **features** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**] or* *DataFrame*) – Features table where each row corresponds to a label and each column
              is a feature. The first row corresponds to the background label.
            * **iso\_gradient\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Method for calulating the gradient (used to get the surface normal) in the
              ‘iso\_categorical’ rendering mode. Must be one of {‘fast’, ‘smooth’}.
              ‘fast’ uses a simple finite difference gradient in x, y, and z. ‘smooth’ uses an
              isotropic Sobel gradient, which is smoother but more computationally expensive.
              The default value is ‘fast’.
            * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
            * **multiscale** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the data is a multiscale image or not. Multiscale data is
              represented by a list of array like image data. If not specified by
              the user and if the data is a list of arrays that decrease in shape
              then it will be taken to be multiscale. The first image in the list
              should be the largest. In 2D, the displayed resolution is chosen
              automatically based on the viewport. In 3D, the lowest resolution
              scale is displayed by default. The resolution level can be locked
              via `locked_data_level` or the resolution control in the layer
              controls UI.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
            * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
            * **plane** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *SlicingPlane*) – Properties defining plane rendering in 3D. Properties are defined in
              data coordinates. Valid dictionary keys are
              {‘position’, ‘normal’, ‘thickness’, and ‘enabled’}.
            * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
              be projected onto the viewed dimensions
            * **properties** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}* *or* *DataFrame*) – Properties for each label. Each property should be an array of length
              N, where N is the number of labels, and the first property corresponds
              to background.
            * **rendering** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – 3D Rendering mode used by vispy. Must be one {‘translucent’, ‘iso\_categorical’}.
              ‘translucent’ renders without lighting. ‘iso\_categorical’ uses isosurface
              rendering to calculate lighting effects on labeled surfaces.
              The default value is ‘iso\_categorical’.
            * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
              angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
              pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
              assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
              np.degrees if needed.
            * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
            * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
              ones along the main diagonal.
            * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
            * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
              If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
            * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
        Returns:
        :   **layer** – The newly-created labels layer.
        Return type:
        :   [`napari.layers.Labels`](napari.layers.Labels.html#napari.layers.Labels "napari.layers.Labels")
    add\_layer(*layer: [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")*) → [Layer](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.base.base.Layer")[#](#napari.Viewer.add_layer "Link to this definition")
    :   Add a layer to the viewer.
        Parameters:
        :   **layer** ([`napari.layers.Layer`](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.Layer")) – Layer to add.
        Returns:
        :   **layer** – The layer that was added (same as input).
        Return type:
        :   [`napari.layers.Layer`](napari.layers.Layer.html#napari.layers.Layer "napari.layers.Layer") or list
    add\_points(*data=None*, *ndim=None*, *\**, *affine=None*, *antialiasing=1*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *border\_color='dimgray'*, *border\_color\_cycle=None*, *border\_colormap='viridis'*, *border\_contrast\_limits=None*, *border\_width=0.05*, *border\_width\_is\_relative=True*, *cache=True*, *canvas\_size\_limits=(2, 10000)*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *face\_color='white'*, *face\_color\_cycle=None*, *face\_colormap='viridis'*, *face\_contrast\_limits=None*, *feature\_defaults=None*, *features=None*, *metadata=None*, *n\_dimensional=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=1.0*, *out\_of\_slice\_display=False*, *projection\_mode='all'*, *properties=None*, *property\_choices=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shading='none'*, *shear=None*, *shown=True*, *size=10*, *symbol='o'*, *text=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*) → [Points](napari.layers.Points.html#napari.layers.Points "napari.layers.points.points.Points")[#](#napari.Viewer.add_points "Link to this definition")
    :   Add a Points layer to the layer list.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** (*array* *(**N**,* *D**)*) – Coordinates for N points in D dimensions.
            * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Number of dimensions for shapes. When data is not None, ndim must be D.
              An empty points layer can be instantiated with arbitrary ndim.
            * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
              The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
              the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
              Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
              provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
            * **antialiasing** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Amount of antialiasing in canvas pixels.
            * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
              If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
            * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
              alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
              {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, ‘translucent\_no\_depth’, ‘additive’, and ‘minimum’}.
            * **border\_color** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Color of the point marker border. Numeric color values should be RGB(A).
            * **border\_color\_cycle** (*np.ndarray**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to border\_color if a
              categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
            * **border\_colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")) – Colormap to set border\_color if a continuous attribute is used to set face\_color.
            * **border\_contrast\_limits** (*None**,* *(*[*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
              of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
              The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
              (property.min(), property.max())
            * **border\_width** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array*) – Width of the symbol border in pixels.
            * **border\_width\_is\_relative** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If enabled, border\_width is interpreted as a fraction of the point size.
            * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
              Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
            * **canvas\_size\_limits** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Lower and upper limits for the size of points in canvas pixels.
            * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
              Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
              Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
            * **face\_color** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Color of the point marker body. Numeric color values should be RGB(A).
            * **face\_color\_cycle** (*np.ndarray**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to face\_color if a
              categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
            * **face\_colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")) – Colormap to set face\_color if a continuous attribute is used to set face\_color.
            * **face\_contrast\_limits** (*None**,* *(*[*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
              of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
              The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
              (property.min(), property.max())
            * **feature\_defaults** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Any**] or* *DataFrame*) – The default value of each feature in a table with one row.
            * **features** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**] or* *DataFrame*) – Features table where each row corresponds to a point and each column
              is a feature.
            * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
            * **n\_dimensional** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – This property will soon be deprecated in favor of ‘out\_of\_slice\_display’.
              Use that instead.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer. If not provided then will be guessed using heuristics.
            * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
            * **out\_of\_slice\_display** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – If True, renders points not just in central plane but also slightly out of slice
              according to specified point marker size.
            * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
              be projected onto the viewed dimensions. Must fit to cls.\_projectionclass.
            * **properties** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}**,* *DataFrame*) – Properties for each point. Each property should be an array of length N,
              where N is the number of points.
            * **property\_choices** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}*) – possible values for each property.
            * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
              angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
              pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
              assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
              np.degrees if needed.
            * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
            * **shading** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Shading*) –
              Render lighting and shading on points. Options are:
              + ’none’
                No shading is added to the points.
              + ’spherical’
                Shading and depth buffer are changed to give a 3D spherical look to the points
            * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
              ones along the main diagonal.
            * **shown** (*1-D array* *of* [*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether to show each point.
            * **size** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array*) – Size of the point marker in data pixels. If given as a scalar, all points are made
              the same size. If given as an array, size must be the same or broadcastable
              to the same shape as the data.
            * **symbol** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array*) – Symbols to be used for the point markers. Must be one of the
              following: arrow, clobber, cross, diamond, disc, hbar, ring,
              square, star, tailed\_arrow, triangle\_down, triangle\_up, vbar, x.
            * **text** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Text to be displayed with the points. If text is set to a key in properties,
              the value of that property will be displayed. Multiple properties can be
              composed using f-string-like syntax (e.g., ‘{property\_1}, {float\_property:.2f}).
              A dictionary can be provided with keyword arguments to set the text values
              and display properties. See TextManager.\_\_init\_\_() for the valid keyword arguments.
              For example usage, see /napari/examples/add\_points\_with\_text.py.
            * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
            * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
              If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
            * **visible** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether the layer visual is currently being displayed.
        Returns:
        :   **layer** – The newly-created points layer.
        Return type:
        :   [`napari.layers.Points`](napari.layers.Points.html#napari.layers.Points "napari.layers.Points")
    add\_shapes(*data=None*, *ndim=None*, *\**, *affine=None*, *axis\_labels=None*, *blending='translucent'*, *cache=True*, *edge\_color='#777777'*, *edge\_color\_cycle=None*, *edge\_colormap='viridis'*, *edge\_contrast\_limits=None*, *edge\_width=1*, *experimental\_clipping\_planes=None*, *face\_color='white'*, *face\_color\_cycle=None*, *face\_colormap='viridis'*, *face\_contrast\_limits=None*, *feature\_defaults=None*, *features=None*, *metadata=None*, *name=None*, *opacity=0.7*, *projection\_mode='none'*, *properties=None*, *property\_choices=None*, *rotate=None*, *scale=None*, *shape\_type='rectangle'*, *shear=None*, *text=None*, *translate=None*, *units=None*, *visible=True*, *z\_index=0*) → [Shapes](napari.layers.Shapes.html#napari.layers.Shapes "napari.layers.shapes.shapes.Shapes")[#](#napari.Viewer.add_shapes "Link to this definition")
    :   Add a Shapes layer to the layer list.
        Parameters:
        :   * **data** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *array*) – List of shape data, where each element is an (N, D) array of the
              N vertices of a shape in D dimensions. Can be an 3-dimensional
              array if each shape has the same number of vertices.
            * **ndim** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")) – Number of dimensions for shapes. When data is not None, ndim must be D.
              An empty shapes layer can be instantiated with arbitrary ndim.
            * **affine** (*n-D array* *or* [*napari.utils.transforms.Affine*](napari.utils.transforms.Affine.html#napari.utils.transforms.Affine "napari.utils.transforms.Affine")) – (N+1, N+1) affine transformation matrix in homogeneous coordinates.
              The first (N, N) entries correspond to a linear transform and
              the final column is a length N translation vector and a 1 or a napari
              Affine transform object. Applied as an extra transform on top of the
              provided scale, rotate, and shear values.
            * **axis\_labels** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *optional*) – Dimension names of the layer data.
              If not provided, axis\_labels will be set to (…, ‘-2’, ‘-1’).
            * **blending** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – One of a list of preset blending modes that determines how RGB and
              alpha values of the layer visual get mixed. Allowed values are
              {‘opaque’, ‘translucent’, and ‘additive’}.
            * **cache** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")) – Whether slices of out-of-core datasets should be cached upon retrieval.
              Currently, this only applies to dask arrays.
            * **edge\_color** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like*) – If string can be any color name recognized by vispy or hex value if
              starting with #. If array-like must be 1-dimensional array with 3
              or 4 elements. If a list is supplied it must be the same length as
              the length of data and each element will be applied to each shape
              otherwise the same value will be used for all shapes.
            * **edge\_color\_cycle** (*np.ndarray**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to edge\_color if a
              categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
            * **edge\_colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")) – Colormap to set edge\_color if a continuous attribute is used to set face\_color.
            * **edge\_contrast\_limits** (*None**,* *(*[*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
              of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
              The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
              (property.min(), property.max())
            * **edge\_width** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)") *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Thickness of lines and edges. If a list is supplied it must be the
              same length as the length of data and each element will be
              applied to each shape otherwise the same value will be used for all
              shapes.
            * **experimental\_clipping\_planes** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *dicts**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)") *of* *ClippingPlane**, or* *ClippingPlaneList*) – Each dict defines a clipping plane in 3D in data coordinates.
              Valid dictionary keys are {‘position’, ‘normal’, and ‘enabled’}.
              Values on the negative side of the normal are discarded if the plane is enabled.
            * **face\_color** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like*) – If string can be any color name recognized by vispy or hex value if
              starting with #. If array-like must be 1-dimensional array with 3
              or 4 elements. If a list is supplied it must be the same length as
              the length of data and each element will be applied to each shape
              otherwise the same value will be used for all shapes.
            * **face\_color\_cycle** (*np.ndarray**,* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – Cycle of colors (provided as string name, RGB, or RGBA) to map to face\_color if a
              categorical attribute is used color the vectors.
            * **face\_colormap** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*napari.utils.Colormap*](napari.utils.Colormap.html#napari.utils.Colormap "napari.utils.Colormap")) – Colormap to set face\_color if a continuous attribute is used to set face\_color.
            * **face\_contrast\_limits** (*None**,* *(*[*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*)*) – clims for mapping the property to a color map. These are the min and max value
              of the specified property that are mapped to 0 and 1, respectively.
              The default value is None. If set the none, the clims will be set to
              (property.min(), property.max())
            * **feature\_defaults** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *Any**] or* *Dataframe-like*) – The default value of each feature in a table with one row.
            * **features** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *array-like**] or* *Dataframe-like*) – Features table where each row corresponds to a shape and each column
              is a feature.
            * **metadata** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Layer metadata.
            * **name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – Name of the layer.
            * **opacity** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Opacity of the layer visual, between 0.0 and 1.0.
            * **projection\_mode** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")) – How data outside the viewed dimensions but inside the thick Dims slice will
              be projected onto the viewed dimenions.
            * **properties** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}**,* *DataFrame*) – Properties for each shape. Each property should be an array of length N,
              where N is the number of shapes.
            * **property\_choices** (*dict {str: array* *(**N**,**)**}*) – possible values for each property.
            * **rotate** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*,* *3-tuple* *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")*, or* *n-D array.*) – If a float convert into a 2D rotation matrix using that value as an
              angle. If 3-tuple convert into a 3D rotation matrix, using a yaw,
              pitch, roll convention. Otherwise assume an nD rotation. Angles are
              assumed to be in degrees. They can be converted from radians with
              np.degrees if needed.
            * **scale** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Scale factors for the layer.
            * **shape\_type** (*string* *or* [*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")) – String of shape shape\_type, must be one of “{‘line’, ‘rectangle’,
              ‘ellipse’, ‘path’, ‘polygon’}”. If a list is supplied it must be
              the same length as the length of data and each element will be
              applied to each shape otherwise the same value will be used for all
              shapes.
            * **shear** (*1-D array* *or* *n-D array*) – Either a vector of upper triangular values, or an nD shear matrix with
              ones along the main diagonal.
            * **text** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")*,* [*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")) – Text to be displayed with the shapes. If text is set to a key in properties,
              the value of that property will be displayed. Multiple properties can be
              composed using f-string-like syntax (e.g., ‘{property\_1}, {float\_property:.2f}).
              A dictionary can be provided with keyword arguments to set the text values
              and display properties. See TextManager.\_\_init\_\_() for the valid keyword arguments.
              For example usage, see /napari/examples/add\_shapes\_with\_text.py.
            * **translate** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.14)")) – Translation values for the layer.
            * **units** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.14)") *of* [*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)") *or* *pint.Unit**,* *optional*) – Units of the layer data in world coordinates.
              If not provided, the default units are assumed to be pixels.
